Threat: any contributor who can land Markdown — outside PR author, importer of a malicious Ghost export, or anyone with write access to content/ — gains full stored XSS on every reader. Impact: account takeover via session theft on hosted blogs that share an origin with auth, defacement, malware drive-by. Mitigation: pipe rendered HTML through DOMPurify / sanitize-html with an opt-out flag (`unsafeHtml: true` per post for trusted authors), and explicitly forbid `<script>`, event handlers, `javascript:` / `data:` URLs in `href`/`src`. Document that the default is sanitised.

title: seo: Tag and author archive pages emit site-default meta description, title, and og:title
body: On example/dist/tag/news/index.html and example/dist/author/casper/index.html lines 5, 53-61: the <title> is 'Nectar Example' (not 'News — Nectar Example'), the description is the site description (not the tag/author bio), and og:title/og:description follow suit. This means every archive page is indexed under the same title and description — pure duplicate-content signal for Google. Verify by opening each file and inspecting <head>. Fix: in src/build/routes.ts `defaultMeta()`, for kind=tag use `${tag.name} | ${site.title}`; for kind=author use `${author.name} | ${site.title}`. Also feed `tag.description` / `author.bio` into the meta description.

title: seo: og:image and twitter:image emitted as relative URLs
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html line 59: `<meta property="og:image" content="/content/images/welcome-cover.svg">` and line 63 `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/content/images/welcome-cover.svg">`. Open Graph and Twitter Card validators require absolute URLs (https://...). Same issue in JSON-LD `image` field (line 64) and on about/index.html (lines 59-63). Verify with https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/ or Twitter's card validator. Fix: in src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts `computeMeta`, run `meta.image` through `absoluteUrl(site.url, image)` before emission.

title: seo: robots.txt is never emitted
body:
There is no /robots.txt file in example/dist/, and no code path in src/build/pipeline.ts or src/build/feeds.ts emits one. Without robots.txt, crawlers fall back to defaults but cannot discover the sitemap URL. Verify: `ls example/dist/robots.txt` returns nothing. Fix: add an `emitRobots()` step in pipeline.ts (or a `components.robots` component) that writes `User-agent: *
Allow: /
Sitemap: {site.url}/sitemap.xml
`. Allow override via config (`[components.robots]`) for staging environments that need Disallow: /.

title: subscribe-forms: `<form data-members-form>` posts to nonexistent `/members/api/send-magic-link`
body:
Source's `email-subscription.hbs` is `<form class="gh-form" data-members-form>` with no `action` attribute. Ghost's runtime JS binds to the `data-members-form` selector and POSTs to `/members/api/send-magic-link`. In Nectar build with no portal provider, this form will submit to the current URL — bad UX.

Fix (paired with the portal adapter): the build-time DOM transform sets `<form>.action` to the provider's endpoint and adjusts the input `name` if needed (e.g. Buttondown uses `email`; Mailchimp uses `EMAIL`).

title: subscribe-forms: `<input data-members-email>` field name not provider-aware
body: Same issue: the input is `<input … data-members-email>` without `name="email"`. Buttondown/Beehiiv expect `name="email"`; Mailchimp expects `name="EMAIL"`. Build-time transform must add the correct name attribute.

title: test: no golden test for Source theme rendering
body: Add a golden-HTML test that builds the example and diffs against committed expected output. Catches regressions in any helper.

title: test: no helper-level unit tests for any individual helper
body: Every helper in src/render/helpers/ needs at least one happy-path and one edge-case test.

title: testing: helper unit tests missing for most helpers
body: We have 6 test files total and none target the helpers in `src/render/helpers/*.ts`. Each helper (`date`, `t`, `url`, `social_url`, `foreach`, `is`, `match`, `has`, `get`, `excerpt`, `reading_time`, `pagination`, `navigation`, `ghost_head`, `ghost_foot`, `asset`, `img_url`, `body_class`, `post_class`) needs at least one positive and one negative test. Without this, every refactor risks silent theme regressions.

title: testing: no golden HTML snapshot tests
body: DESIGN.md §6 promises `tests/golden/`, but the directory doesn't exist. Add tiny theme + fixture + frozen `expected.html` snapshots and assert byte-identical output. The first regression we should catch is "helper output changed by mistake".

title: testing: no tests against real Ghost themes (Casper / Headline / Edition / Wave / Solo)
body: We claim Source theme compatibility but only run against the vendored copy. Add a `test-themes/` workspace that pulls Casper/Headline/Edition/Wave/Solo on CI (git-subtree or release tarball pin), runs `nectar build` against a canned content fixture, and asserts every page emitted has no `{{` leakage and parses as HTML. This is the contract test that defines our market.

title: theme-dev: no `nectar theme:lint` (gscan equivalent)
body: Ghost has `gscan`, a theme validator that catches deprecated helpers, missing required files, broken partial refs, missing `{{ghost_head}}`/`{{ghost_foot}}`. We need our own `nectar theme:lint <path>` that returns the same class of errors, otherwise themes drift and we get bug reports we can't reproduce. Wrap or vendor `@tryghost/gscan` if its rules map cleanly.

title: theme-dev: no theme dev guide / cookbook
body: Write `docs/theme-dev.md` covering layout inheritance (`{{!< default}}`), partial conventions, locale files, asset fingerprinting, helper authoring, package.json `config` block, and how to test against Nectar's golden HTML. Without this, third-party themes will not appear.

title: theme/casper: @site.comments_enabled / members_enabled / members_invite_only flags missing
body: default.hbs:44,52,60 and post.hbs:102 read `@site.comments_enabled`, `@site.members_enabled`, `@site.members_invite_only`. Our SiteData (src/content/model.ts) lacks these booleans, so the head/foot subscribe UI and CTA logic short-circuits to default branches. Add booleans to SiteData populated from config (e.g. config.members.enabled, comments.enabled).

title: theme/casper: {{access}} block / property unimplemented
body: post.hbs:105 and partials/post-card.hbs:4,22,51 read `{{#unless access}}` and `{{#if access}}` to gate paid content. Nectar does not provide an `access` boolean per post (true when the visitor can read it). Without it, Casper always renders the access-overlay (`.post-access-...`, padlock icon) and never the post body. Implement a default `access=true` on the post context when no auth, and `false` for posts whose visibility=members/paid.

title: theme/digest: {{#unless access}} on article-footer class for paid-only borderless layout
body: digest/partials/content.hbs:31 reads `{{#unless access}} no-border{{/unless}}`. Same `access` context requirement as Casper P0. Without it the class is always applied, leading to visible no-border state on non-paid posts.

title: theme/ease: {{#match @page.show_title_and_feature_image}} on page route
body: ease/page.hbs:8 uses `{{#match @page.show_title_and_feature_image}}`. Same fix as edition P0 — @page must always expose show_title_and_feature_image (default true).

title: theme/edition: @page.show_title_and_feature_image / @page context missing
body: partials/content.hbs:3 and page.hbs uses `{{#match @page.show_title_and_feature_image}}`. buildRootData() in src/render/engine.ts sets @page only when route.kind === 'page', but `show_title_and_feature_image` is a Page field. Page model has this boolean but @page only references the current page object; for post routes @page is undefined. Match-helper on undefined returns false → header collapses on posts. Expose @page.show_title_and_feature_image=true as a default for posts.

title: theme/edition: prev_post / next_post block helpers not registered
body: partials/content.hbs:59,90 use `{{#prev_post}}...{{/prev_post}}` and `{{#next_post}}`. Nectar does not register prev_post/next_post as block helpers; Post model has prev/next fields (src/content/model.ts:93-94) but no helper exposes them on the inline-block syntax Ghost uses. Implement prev_post/next_post block helpers that delegate to ctx.prev/next.

title: theme/edition: {{#contentFor "body_class"}} from index.hbs lost
body: index.hbs:23 emits `{{#contentFor 'body_class'}}...{{/contentFor}}` to inject `with-full-cover is-head-transparent` into the body class. default.hbs:14 reads `{{{block 'body_class'}}}`. Without contentFor/block support, the homepage cover styling never activates. Implement Ghost-compatible block/contentFor (see headline P0 issue).

title: theme/headline: {{contentFor}} / {{block}} content-blocks system unsupported
body: Although Headline itself does not use contentFor, sibling themes (Edition index.hbs:23, Wave default.hbs:11/138, Liebling default/post) rely on `{{#contentFor 'X'}} ... {{/contentFor}}` + `{{{block 'X'}}}`. Nectar's engine has no Ghost-compatible block/contentFor helpers, so the output of the child template's contentFor block is silently lost. Implement these as a pair (collect in pre-pass, expose via {{{block 'name'}}} in default.hbs). Required for Edition/Wave/Liebling and any future Casper variants.

title: theme/liebling: {{#contentFor "preload"}} / `styles` / `scripts` / `herobackground` block system
body: Liebling's post.hbs, page-newsletter.hbs, etc. heavily use contentFor/block. Without Nectar's block helper (see Headline P0), all asset preloads, page-specific stylesheets, and per-page scripts are dropped. P0 for Liebling support.

title: theme/london: dark-mode and infinite-scroll CSS depends on `.gh-card-image` Koenig markup
body: London's screen.css (not inspected) relies on Koenig card HTML emitted from markdown. Verify Nectar's markdown→HTML pipeline (src/content/markdown.ts) emits `kg-image-card`, `kg-gallery-card`, `kg-bookmark-card`, `kg-callout-card`, `kg-toggle-card`, `kg-button-card`, `kg-product-card` classes so that any Casper-family theme's CSS keys correctly. Cross-cutting across all themes.

title: theme/ruby: legacy post-navigation partial uses {{#prev_post}}/{{#next_post}} (see Edition P0)
body: ruby/partials/post-navigation.hbs requires prev_post / next_post block helpers. Same global fix as Edition/Bulletin/Solo/Digest.

title: theme/ruby: related-posts.hbs likely uses {{#get "posts" filter="tags:[{{post.tags}}]+id:-{{post.id}}"}}
body: Verify Ruby's related-posts.hbs filter syntax with array interpolation. Our get helper's filter handles `+` AND-joins and `[a,b]` lists; verify interpolation `{{post.tags}}` in a filter value produces a comma-joined slug list. blocks.ts:262 does `{{path.like.this}}` interpolation. `post.tags` is an array of Tag objects, not a comma-string — output would be `[object Object],[object Object]`. Need special-case to map tags→slugs joined by commas.

title: theme/solo: {{#prev_post}} / {{#next_post}} block helpers
body: post.hbs:63-66 and 73-76. Same as Edition/Bulletin/Journal: implement prev_post/next_post block helpers backed by post.prev/post.next.

title: theme/wave: {{block}} / {{contentFor}} for title/scripts/body_class/description
body: default.hbs:9-12,18,138 uses `{{block "title"}}`, `{{{block "description"}}}`, `{{{block "body_class"}}}`, `{{{block "scripts"}}}`. Nothing in our engine supports these. Until implemented, Wave's `<title>` and dynamic body classes resolve to empty strings on the `blog` route. Critical P0.

title: web-standards: No 404.html / error page emitted
body: src/cli/commands/serve.ts line 32-36 looks for dist/404.html but src/build/pipeline.ts never emits one (and theme.loader.ts merely lists 'error-404' as a known template name). Result: serve falls back to a hardcoded 'Not Found' string, and a static host (GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify) gets a default error page from the host instead of a branded one. Verify: `ls example/dist/404.html` returns nothing. Fix: in planRoutes(), when theme.templates['error-404'] exists, push a route with url=/404.html and template='error-404'. Otherwise, emit a default 404.html that includes the site header/footer and a link home.

title: a11y: <html lang> is hardcoded to 'en' — no per-page lang and no locale honoring
body: Every emitted page begins with `<html lang="en">` even though config.site.locale is plumbed through (it's used as RSS <language>). If a user sets site.locale = 'ja' in nectar.toml, the HTML lang attribute will still claim English. WCAG 3.1.1. Fix: in the layout default partial (or wherever <html lang="..."> is rendered), interpolate `site.locale` (defaulting to 'en'). Also surface `dir="rtl"` automatically for known RTL locales.

title: a11y: Author 'social link' wraps the page <h1> heading text
body:
example/dist/author/casper/index.html lines 98-100 emit `

Casper

`. The page title is therefore the linked text 'Casper'. This breaks the heading semantics (the h1 acts as both a heading and a navigation target) and means the page has no plain-text page title for accessible context. WCAG 2.4.6. Fix: render the h1 as plain text (Casper) and surface the social link as a separate below the heading.

title: a11y: Featured-image and card images lack width/height attributes
body: Every `` emitted by the card / article-image partials (e.g. example/dist/index.html lines 103-114, hello-nectar lines 127-136) sets src/srcset but omits intrinsic width and height. This causes Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) and prevents browsers from reserving space. Verify with Lighthouse Performance. Fix: store image dimensions in the content graph (read once at build with sharp or image-size) and pass them through the `gh-card-image` / `gh-article-image` helpers.

title: a11y: Featured/hero image is not lazy-loaded but other cards are — but the LCP image has no fetchpriority
body: On example/dist/index.html the featured card image (line 103) intentionally omits loading="lazy" while other cards have it. Good, but the LCP image is missing `fetchpriority="high"` and `decoding="async"`. Same on hello-nectar/index.html: the article-image (line 127-136) is the LCP element and has neither attribute. Fix: in card/article-image helpers, when index === 0 (or post.feature_image is the article hero), add fetchpriority="high" decoding="async"; on all other images add decoding="async" and loading="lazy".

title: a11y: Heading hierarchy skips levels on post page
body:
example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html: h1 'Hello, Nectar' (line 103), then an h4 'Casper' for author name (line 114), then h2s inside content. h4 follows h1 with nothing between. On the home, in-feed cards use

(line 118) but the section heading

is empty (line 98). WCAG 1.3.1 / 2.4.6. Verify with axe / Lighthouse. Fix: promote the author-name from h4 to a styled

/ (it is metadata, not a section heading), and ensure every card grid has a visible/visually-hidden h2 above it.

title: a11y: Inline contrast script writes class on <html> after paint, causing FOUC and assumes 6-digit hex only
body: Every page contains the inline script (example/dist/index.html lines 32-49) that reads --background-color from the document element and writes `has-light-text` / `has-dark-text` on <html>. Issues: (a) FOUC because <html> is bare until JS runs; (b) the script only handles 3- and 6-digit hex — `var(--background-color: rgb(…))` or oklch values silently parse to NaN and yield `has-NaN-text`; (c) the script blocks parsing in <head>. Fix: precompute the contrast at build time from `config.theme.custom.background_color` and emit either `<html class="has-dark-text">` or write the class in a `<style>` rule. Drop the inline script.

title: a11y: Navigation has no aria-current on active link
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts renders the `{{navigation}}` <ul> without an aria-current attribute, and `link_class` only toggles a CSS class. On example/dist/tag/news/index.html, the 'Tags' nav item points to /tag/news/ which is the current page, but no `aria-current="page"` is emitted. Verify by inspecting the <a> tags inside `<ul class="nav">` on each page. Fix: in navigation.ts, when the URL matches route.url, render `aria-current="page"` on the anchor (and ideally on the parent <li>).

title: a11y: No skip-to-content link emitted
body:
None of the audited pages emit a `Skip to content` (or equivalent) as the first focusable element. Keyboard users have to tab through the entire header (logo, search, burger, full nav, search again) on every page. WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks. Verify by tabbing through file:///.../example/dist/index.html. Fix: have the engine prepend a skip link to every page body, or render one in the default theme partials. The `
` element exists but has no id, so even a manually added skip link wouldn't have a target — also add id="main" to the primary
.

title: a11y: PhotoSwipe and pagination icon buttons have empty title attributes
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html lines 336-339 and 354-355 emit `<button class="pswp__button pswp__button--close" title="">` and four other PhotoSwipe action buttons with empty `title=""`. They have no aria-label, no text. Even though PhotoSwipe is a JS overlay these are present in the static markup and tools like axe / Lighthouse will flag them. Verify with `npx pa11y file://.../hello-nectar/index.html`. Fix: render localized titles via the {{t}} helper for each pswp button, or set aria-hidden="true" on the entire `.pswp` container (it already has aria-hidden=true on the wrapper but the inner buttons are still in the a11y tree because tabindex / focusability is enabled by JS).

title: a11y: Share button is an anchor with href="#/share" (button masquerading as link)
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html line 121: `<a href="#/share" class="gh-button gh-button-share">` with empty body. This is a button-like control (it triggers share JS) but uses an <a> with a non-route fragment, and its accessible name is empty. WCAG 2.4.4 link purpose, 4.1.2 name. Fix: render as `<button type="button" class="gh-button gh-button-share" aria-label="{{t "Share this post"}}">…</button>` and include an SVG icon with role="img" and a <title>.

title: a11y: Two <h2> elements emitted empty
body:
On the home page (example/dist/index.html lines 98 and 139) two headings render empty: `

` and `

`. Empty headings break screen reader heading navigation and fail axe rule 'empty-heading'. Same pattern at lines 175 of hello-nectar/index.html. Verify with `npx axe file://.../example/dist/index.html` or check Lighthouse Accessibility. Fix: only render the wrapper heading when a title string exists (`{{#if title}}

{{title}}

{{/if}}`).

title: a11y: about/index.html contains two consecutive <h1> tags
body:
example/dist/about/index.html line 100 emits `

About Nectar

`, and the body content (rendered Markdown) starts with `

What is Nectar?

` on line 116. Two h1s on one page is permitted under HTML5 sectioning but is considered a Lighthouse/SEO smell and confuses screen readers that announce the outline. Fix: in src/render/helpers/content.ts, downshift h1 in post/page body content to h2 (and adjust subsequent headings) before injecting into a layout whose title is already an h1. Document this in the markdown writing guide.

title: access: `<!-- members-only -->` marker in Markdown for paywall point
body: Ghost editor inserts a paywall card that splits the post HTML. For Markdown-authored content, support a magic line `<!-- members-only -->` that becomes the paywall split point. Above the line → public preview; below → gated. Render an interpolated CTA in place.

title: access: paywall CTA partial not provided
body:
When we truncate a members-only post, we need to insert SOMETHING in place of the cut content. Provide a built-in partial `nectar/paywall.hbs` themes can override: ```hbs

{{t "This post is for members only"}}

``` Allow override by placing `partials/paywall.hbs` in theme.

title: access: post.access boolean not exposed in post context
body: Add `access: false` to Post object exposed via context (always false in builds; corresponds to logged-out viewer). Themes' `{{#if access}}` would evaluate falsy; `{{#unless access}}` truthy. Matches Ghost's behavior for anonymous viewers.

title: analytics: no privacy-friendly view tracking out of the box
body: Many Ghost users use Plausible/Umami/Fathom alongside. Add `[components.analytics] provider = "plausible|umami|fathom|simpleanalytics|googleanalytics" site = "…"` that injects the right snippet into `{{ghost_head}}` automatically. Honour DNT and respect IP-anonymisation flags.

title: api: CORS — emit dist/_headers granting Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on /content/*.json
body:
Static JSON served from a different origin than the consumer SPA will be blocked by CORS unless headers are explicit.

Proposal: emit `dist/_headers` (Netlify format) and `dist/_headers.cf` (Cloudflare Pages format, identical syntax) granting:
```
/content/*
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
  Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS
  Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, Authorization
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=300
```
Make configurable via `[components.content_api] cors_origin = "*"`.

title: api: GET /content/posts/: emit static dist/content/posts.json shadow for @tryghost/content-api consumers
body:
Ghost behavior: `GET /ghost/api/content/posts/?key=...` returns `{ posts: Post[], meta: { pagination } }` of *all* published posts (paginated, default `limit=15`).

Nectar gap: we emit zero JSON; only HTML routes. External SPAs / mobile apps / Zapier / `@tryghost/content-api` SDK cannot read the catalog.

Proposal: during `nectar build`, after `planRoutes`, write `dist/content/posts.json` containing the full posts array projected to the Ghost Content API shape (fields per `src/content/model.ts:Post` minus internal `prev`/`next` link refs; serialize `tags`, `authors`, `primary_tag`, `primary_author` as embedded objects so `?include=tags,authors` is satisfied by default). One file, full dump — pagination shards handled in a follow-up issue. Gate behind `[components.content_api] enabled = true` (default on).

title: api: GET /content/settings/: emit dist/content/settings.json mirroring @site context
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/settings/` returns the site-wide settings — `title`, `description`, `logo`, `icon`, `cover_image`, `accent_color`, `locale`, `timezone`, `url`, `navigation`, `secondary_navigation`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `og_image`, `twitter_image`, `members_signup_access`, `paid_members_enabled`, `firstpromoter_account`, etc.

Nectar gap: site config exists at `nectar.toml [site]` and is exposed via `@site` context but never serialized.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/settings.json` shaped as `{ settings: SiteData }` matching Ghost's envelope. Members-related fields hardcoded to `false`/empty (see /content/tiers/ stub issue).

title: api: SDK smoke test fixture — add tests/sdk/content-api.test.ts running @tryghost/content-api against dist/
body:
We have no test verifying that an actual Ghost SDK consumer can talk to a `nectar build` output. Without this we'll regress on URL paths, payload shape, pagination keys, etc.

Proposal: in `tests/sdk/content-api.test.ts`, build the example site, spin a `Bun.serve` over `dist/`, point `new ContentAPI({ url, key, version: 'v5.0' })` at it, and assert:
- `posts.browse()` returns array + pagination meta
- `posts.read({slug})` returns single post
- `tags.browse()` returns tags with `count.posts`
- `authors.browse()` returns authors
- `settings.browse()` returns site settings

Gate behind a dev dep on `@tryghost/content-api`.

title: api: build flag --emit-content-api / config gate [components.content_api]
body:
All the JSON shadow work above should be gated, off-by-default during the unstable phase, on-by-default once stable.

Proposal: add to `nectar.toml`:
```
[components.content_api]
enabled = true
paginate_limit = 15
emit_per_resource_shards = true
emit_filtered_shards = ["tag", "author", "featured"]
cors_origin = "*"
```
Wire through `src/build/pipeline.ts` similar to existing `components.rss` / `components.sitemap` gating.

title: api: emit dist/content/posts/index.json *and* dist/content/posts.json — host directory-index variance
body:
Hosts differ on whether `/foo/` serves `/foo/index.html` or 404s without `index.html`. JSON has no equivalent default. We must emit redundantly:
- `dist/content/posts.json` (file at non-trailing-slash request)
- `dist/content/posts/index.json` (file at trailing-slash directory request, if host serves *.json indexes)

Proposal: emit both. Or pick one + ship a `_redirects` rewriting `/posts/` → `/posts.json`. Document.

title: api: meta.pagination shape — define canonical projector helper
body:
Ghost pagination envelope (must match exactly for `@tryghost/content-api` to parse):

```
{ "meta": { "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 15, "pages": 3, "total": 42, "next": 2, "prev": null } } }
```

Nectar gap: nothing emitting this today.

Proposal: introduce `src/build/api/pagination.ts` exporting `paginate<T>(items: T[], limit: number, page: number): { items: T[]; pagination: GhostPagination }`. All collection shadow emitters (posts/pages/tags/authors) share this helper. `limit='all'` produces `pages: 1, total: items.length, next: null, prev: null`.

title: api: no content transformer / shortcode pipeline
body: Hugo's shortcodes are why a lot of bloggers love it. Add a transformer hook (`beforeMarkdown`, `afterMarkdown`) so plugins can rewrite `:::callout` or `{{< youtube id >}}` blocks. Without this, every embed (Twitter, YouTube, gist) requires raw HTML in posts.

title: api: no custom helper registration mechanism
body: Theme authors and plugin authors need to inject Handlebars helpers (e.g. a `{{stripe_button}}` helper) without forking Nectar. Expose `engine.registerHelper(name, fn)` and a `[components.helpers]` config entry that imports a JS file from `node_modules`.

title: api: no custom-route plugin point
body: Plugins should be able to add routes (e.g. an `archive.html`, a `/feed/json/` JSON feed, a `/_redirects` Netlify file). Extend the route planner to accept `extraRoutes` from plugins. Sitemap/RSS should be re-implemented as built-in plugins so the API stays load-bearing.

title: api: search — map /content/search/ to Pagefind output OR provide a flat search.json fallback
body:
Ghost has a `/search/` endpoint (and `sodo-search` integration). It returns hits across posts/pages/tags/authors.

Nectar gap: no search emission.

Proposal: emit two artifacts:
1. `dist/content/search.json` — flat `{ posts: [{id, slug, title, excerpt, url}], tags: [...], authors: [...] }` suitable for client-side fuzzy search (lunr/Fuse/minisearch). Cheap.
2. Optional Pagefind integration — `[components.search] engine = "pagefind"` runs `pagefind` over `dist/` post-build, emitting `dist/pagefind/*`. Document the consumer JS snippet.

Document that Ghost's exact `/search/` endpoint shape is NOT replicated; this is a divergent search API.

title: api: types not exported from a stable entrypoint
body: Add `nectar/types` exporting Post/Page/Tag/Author/SiteData/NectarConfig.

title: build-pipeline: emitRss CDATA termination is naïve — "]]>" inside post.html would break the feed
body: src/build/feeds.ts line 36: `<content:encoded><![CDATA[${post.html}]]></content:encoded>`. If a post's HTML contains the literal string `]]>` (e.g. a code sample about XML CDATA), it terminates the CDATA section prematurely and corrupts the feed. Fix: split occurrences of `]]>` into `]]]]><![CDATA[>` per the XML spec.

title: build: RSS lacks atom:link self, channel image, language for content
body: Add `<atom:link href=".../rss.xml" rel="self" type="application/rss+xml"/>`, `<image>` if site.logo, `<lastBuildDate>`.

title: build: feeds.ts double-prefixes site URL when post.url is absolute
body: feeds.ts:emitRss does `base + new URL(post.url).pathname`. If post.url is already absolute, this is correct. But for the `<guid>`, Ghost uses a stable ID — ours regenerates per-build. Use post.id (e.g. `post-<slug>`).

title: build: no 404.html emitted
body: Most hosts serve `404.html` for unknown paths. Emit one from a theme template (`error-404.hbs` or `error.hbs`), fall back to a built-in.

title: build: no incremental builds; full rebuild every time
body: Watch mode and large sites need content/template invalidation. Hash inputs per route; skip rerender when hash matches manifest.

title: build: no robots.txt emitted
body: Default emit `User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: <site>/sitemap.xml`. Allow override via `static/robots.txt`.

title: build: no static/ passthrough directory
body: Many SSGs let users drop arbitrary files into `static/` and pass them through unchanged. We don't. Add `[content].static_dir`.

title: build: rm -rf outputDir destroys any user-managed files (e.g., a CNAME)
body: src/build/pipeline.ts deletes the output dir entirely. Users hosting on GitHub Pages keep CNAME and `_headers` etc. there. Either: (a) write a `.nectarignore`-aware preserve list, or (b) clean only files we wrote in the previous build (track via a manifest).

title: build: route output paths can collide silently (post slug == page slug)
body: A post 'about' and a page 'about' both emit `about/index.html`. Last one wins. Detect collisions and error with both file paths.

title: build: sequential rendering — N posts = N awaits in serial
body: pipeline.ts loops `for (route of routes) { engine.render(route); writeHtml(...) }`. Parallelise with `Promise.all` chunked at CPU count. For 1000-post sites this is the difference between 3s and 30s.

title: build: sitemap has no lastmod, changefreq, priority; single-file only
body: Per sitemap protocol, add lastmod (use updated_at). For >50k URLs Ghost splits into sitemap-posts/-pages/-tags/-authors with a sitemap index. Implement multi-file emission.

title: build: tag/author archive routes always exist — Ghost themes can suppress them via routes.yaml
body: Implement basic routes.yaml support: disabling tag or author collections; custom paths (e.g. `/category/` instead of `/tag/`).

title: ci: no Renovate / Dependabot config
body: Add `.github/renovate.json` (preferred — supports Bun) or `.github/dependabot.yml`. Group bun.lock minor updates, separate handlebars/marked majors, schedule weekly. Without this, security updates pile up silently.

title: ci: no matrix testing across OS + Bun versions
body: Bun behaves differently on Windows (path separators, Bun.Glob behaviour). Run the CI matrix across `{ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest}` × `{bun-latest, bun-1.3.x}` so we know our path normalisation (e.g. `theme/loader.ts` uses `\\` replacement) actually works on Windows.

title: ci: no security scanning (gitleaks, osv-scanner, codeql)
body: Add `gitleaks` (pre-commit + CI), `osv-scanner` for the lockfile, and CodeQL JS scanning. Particularly important because we will accept `codeinjection_head`/`codeinjection_foot` raw HTML from user content.

title: cli: `check` does not actually compile templates
body: We load templates as strings only. Compile them to surface syntax errors with file/line context.

title: cli: `check` does not validate frontmatter against schema
body: Add a strict schema check (required title, valid dates, known status enum, known visibility enum). Emit a JSON report.

title: cli: `import-ghost` does not download images from Ghost CDN
body: Image URLs in imported posts point to the original Ghost host. Add `--download-images` to fetch and rewrite paths.

title: cli: `import-ghost` does not preserve Mobiledoc / Lexical content
body: Only HTML import works. Add Lexical→HTML via `@tryghost/kg-lexical-html-renderer`. Mobiledoc via `@tryghost/kg-mobiledoc-html-renderer`.

title: cli: `nectar check` doesn't actually lint theme/content
body: Today `check` just calls `loadConfig`/`loadContent`/`loadTheme` and reports OK. It doesn't surface: missing required fields, dead asset references, unknown frontmatter keys, malformed dates, image files referenced but missing on disk, duplicate slugs, navigation pointing at non-existent pages. Make `check` a real linter that returns nonzero for warnings (with `--strict`).

title: cli: `nectar new` has no `--draft`, no `--open`, no `--date`
body: Current `runNew` always sets `date = now` and writes a minimal file. Add `--draft` (sets `status: draft`), `--date <iso>` (override pubdate), `--open` (open in `$EDITOR`), `--tags a,b`, `--author slug`. Also support `nectar new tag <slug>` and `nectar new author <slug>`. This is table-stakes vs Jekyll/Hugo's `new` command.

title: cli: `nectar serve` errors out instead of building on demand
body: `src/cli/commands/serve.ts` does `if (!existsSync(distDir)) { logger.error(...); return 1; }`. For first-time users this is hostile. Either auto-run `build` before serving, or add `--build` flag (default on for dev).

title: cli: `new` does not check existing file; overwrites silently
body: Race against itself if user runs twice. Add `--force` flag and otherwise refuse to overwrite.

title: cli: `serve` doesn't watch; users have to restart after edits
body: Wire up `fs.watch` over content/ and themes/, debounce, rebuild, send reload signal via Server-Sent Events to a tiny client snippet.

title: cli: argument parsing — env var fallback for every flag
body: Every flag should have an env var equivalent (NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG>). Example: --port falls back to NECTAR_SERVE_PORT. Useful in docker-compose. Document the mapping. Order: CLI flag > env var > config file > default.

title: cli: build should write to dist.tmp then rename atomically
body: Current emit writes directly into the final output_dir (pipeline.ts:26). Any failure mid-build leaves a broken site. Build into `<output>.tmp-<pid>` and rename on success. Add a `--no-atomic` escape hatch. Mention in --help.

title: cli: build: emit a build-manifest.json
body: On success, write `<output>/.nectar/build-manifest.json` containing routeCount, assetCount, generated_at, nectar version, config hash, theme name+version, list of output files with sizes/hashes. Useful for incremental deploys (rsync, S3 sync) and for `nectar deploy`.

title: cli: build: exit code 1 for every failure is too coarse
body: build.ts:18 returns 1 for any thrown error. Reserve codes: 0 ok, 1 generic, 2 usage/CLI error, 3 config error, 4 content error, 5 theme error, 6 render error, 7 emit/IO error, 130 SIGINT. Tag thrown errors with a `code` property and map at the boundary.

title: cli: build: no --base-url for absolute canonical/OG URLs
body: Distinct from --base-path. site.url today is config-only; preview deploys (Netlify/Vercel/Cloudflare PR URL) need `--base-url https://pr-42.example.com` so canonical, OG, RSS use the right host.

title: cli: build: no --concurrency / --jobs
body: Routes render serially (pipeline.ts:36-44 for-of). For large sites a parallel renderer with bounded concurrency (e.g. p-map with limit=os.cpus().length) is a big win. Add `--concurrency N` (default cpus). Test correctness — Handlebars helpers must be reentrant.

title: cli: build: no --dry-run flag
body: There is no way to preview what would be built without writing files. Add `nectar build --dry-run` that plans routes, loads templates, runs renders into memory, but never touches the filesystem. Print the same summary plus a per-route table when --verbose.

title: cli: build: no --include-drafts / --drafts
body: Content with `draft: true` is silently included or excluded depending on loader behaviour. Make policy explicit: by default drafts are excluded; `--include-drafts` includes them and warns 'Building with drafts'. Add `NECTAR_DRAFTS=1` env equivalent.

title: cli: build: no --watch flag
body: There is no built-in watch mode. The intended `nectar dev` would cover that, but a simple `nectar build --watch` (no server, just rebuild on change) is a common subset for SSG + external static host. Use fs.watch + debounce 100ms.

title: cli: check: doesn't validate links / external refs
body: A 'check' that doesn't verify cross-links and asset references is half a check. Add: (1) all internal `[text](./foo.md)` resolve to known content, (2) image refs in frontmatter (cover_image, logo) point to existing files in assets_dir, (3) navigation URLs resolve. Make slow checks opt-in via `--check-links` / `--check-external`.

title: cli: check: doesn't validate theme template existence vs route plan
body: check.ts only loads the theme. It does not run planRoutes and verify that every required template (index.hbs, post.hbs, page.hbs, tag.hbs, author.hbs) exists for the configured content. Add a 'theme-coverage' pass.

title: cli: check: no JSON output
body: Editor integrations need a parseable result. Add `--json` so check emits `{ config: { ok: true }, content: { posts: 5, warnings: [...] }, theme: { ok: true, missing_partials: [] } }`.

title: cli: config discovery — document precedence and --config behaviour
body: loadConfig honours `configPath` if given (build.ts:5-6) but discovery order otherwise is opaque to users. Document: --config flag > NECTAR_CONFIG env > cwd/nectar.config.{ts,js,toml,json} in that order. Print resolved path with --verbose.

title: cli: do not error on missing content dirs — auto-create with warning
body: First-run UX: a fresh `nectar init` may not have content/pages yet. loadContent should treat missing dirs as empty and warn, not throw. Test that `nectar build` succeeds on a brand-new project with only one post.

title: cli: dynamic import path uses .ts extension — verify cross-runtime
body: src/cli/index.ts:36-53 uses `import('./commands/build.ts')`. Bun resolves .ts but Node + ts-node would not. If the bin is ever shipped via `npm install`, this breaks. Strip .ts at build time or bundle the CLI into dist/cli.js.

title: cli: error messages should include docs links
body: Add `nectar.dev/errors/E0042` style stable error codes referenced from each diagnostic. Errors are objects with { code, message, hint, docsUrl }. Implementation: src/util/errors.ts.

title: cli: error path leaks stack trace in default mode (build.ts)
body: build.ts:16 prints `err.stack ?? err.message`. Stack is noisy by default. Print message + hint by default, full stack only with --verbose. Crash reports (when consented) still capture full stack.

title: cli: import-ghost should auto-detect file format
body: Ghost has changed export schemas over the years (v2, v3, v4, v5, lexical vs mobiledoc). The CLI should detect and dispatch, or refuse with 'Unsupported Ghost export version <x>. Try --legacy.' Pre-flight inspect the `db[0].meta.version` field.

title: cli: import-ghost: file argument not validated for existence/JSON
body: import-ghost.ts:5-9 only checks `if (!file)`. If the path doesn't exist, importGhostExport will throw deep inside. Pre-flight: existsSync, file size > 0, JSON.parse on first 4KB. Print 'File not found: <path>' with exit 2.

title: cli: import-ghost: no --output / --dry-run
body: src/cli/commands/import-ghost.ts:11-15 always writes into the configured content dirs. Add `--output <dir>` to write somewhere else (review-first workflow), and `--dry-run` to print what would be created without writing.

title: cli: logger has no structured fields (only string parts)
body: logger.info(`Built ${n} routes`) loses structure. Refactor to `logger.info('build.done', { routeCount: n, assetCount: m })`. Pretty-printed in TTY, JSON in --json mode. Foundation for NDJSON event log.

title: cli: missing --no-color / NO_COLOR / FORCE_COLOR detection
body: src/util/logger.ts has no color support and no TTY detection. When adding color (chalk/picocolors), the standard contract is: respect NO_COLOR (https://no-color.org/), FORCE_COLOR=1|2|3, and `--no-color`. Auto-disable when `!process.stdout.isTTY`. Centralise in src/util/color.ts.

title: cli: missing `nectar --json` global to flip output mode
body: Companion to --quiet/--verbose. When set at any position, all subcommands switch to NDJSON. Centralise the detection in src/cli/index.ts before dispatch.

title: cli: missing snapshot tests for --help output
body: Once we add per-subcommand help, snapshot it. Catches accidental --help drift (e.g. flag added without help text).

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar clean`
body: Remove dist/ and .nectar/cache safely. Prompt for confirmation unless `--yes`. Refuse if the target is outside cwd. `--keep dist/.well-known` lets users preserve special files. Useful before fresh builds in CI.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar completions <shell>`
body: Emit shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, fish, powershell. Cargo/rg style: `nectar completions zsh > ~/.zsh/_nectar`. Generate from the same CommandSpec used by --help.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar config get <key>`
body: Read a single key from the resolved config. Dotted paths: `nectar config get build.output_dir`. Useful for shell scripts: `OUT=$(nectar config get build.output_dir)`. Pair with --json.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar config path`
body: Print the absolute path of the config that would be loaded (with --config override applied). Useful for editor integrations, CI debugging, and `code $(nectar config path)`.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar content list`
body: `nectar content list [--kind posts|pages] [--draft] [--tag foo] [--author bar] [--json]`. Tabular listing with slug, title, date, status. Foundation for everything else (touch, rename, delete).

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar content rename <old-slug> <new-slug>`
body: Move the file, update slug in frontmatter, optionally add a redirect entry (e.g. write to config.build.redirects[]). Avoid breaking inbound links.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar deploy <target>`
body: Implement deployers for known targets: `cloudflare`, `netlify`, `vercel`, `github-pages`, `s3`, `r2`, `rsync`. Read credentials from env (NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN, ...). Pre-flight: ensure `nectar build` was run, manifest exists. Add `--build` to chain. Diff via the build-manifest for incremental.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar export <format>`
body: Inverse of import. `nectar export json` dumps all content as a single JSON file; `nectar export ghost-json` produces an importable Ghost backup; `nectar export rss` regenerates only the feed. Useful for backups and platform migrations.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar info` / `nectar env`
body: Print: nectar version, bun version, node version (if any), OS, arch, $EDITOR, $NECTAR_LOG_LEVEL, config path, theme name, route count estimate. Always include this in bug reports. Pair with --json.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar lint` (content lint, not theme lint)
body: Lint content: titles too long, missing alt text on images, broken local links, future-dated drafts, duplicate slugs, malformed frontmatter. Rule set is opt-in. Output table, `--json` for CI.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar migrate <source>`
body: Subsume import-ghost into a more general `nectar migrate ghost <file>` and add `nectar migrate wordpress <wxr.xml>`, `migrate hugo <dir>`, `migrate jekyll <dir>`, `migrate eleventy <dir>`. Shared options: --output, --dry-run, --on-conflict, --include-drafts. Deprecate `import-ghost` with an alias.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar tags list`
body: List all known tags (from frontmatter + content/tags/*.md), with post count. `--orphaned` shows tags referenced by no post; `--unused` shows tag pages with no posts. JSON output.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar tags rename <old> <new>`
body: Rewrite the tag across all post frontmatter and rename content/tags/<old>.md. Atomic. Print files modified count.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar theme new <name>`
body: Scaffold a theme directory under themes/<name>/ with package.json, default.hbs, index.hbs, post.hbs, page.hbs, partials/, assets/built-in/. Optionally `--from <existing-theme>` to copy from a starter (Casper, Source). Print next steps.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar theme zip`
body: Package the current theme into `<name>-<version>.zip` for upload to Ghost or distribution. Exclude node_modules, .git, source maps, *.test.*. Verify gscan-compatible structure before zipping (theme lint). Print resulting path.

title: cli: new: no --tags / --authors / --draft / --date
body: Frontmatter is fixed. Add `--tags news,foo`, `--authors alice`, `--draft`, `--date 2025-01-01`, `--template <name>`. They populate the YAML frontmatter on creation.

title: cli: no TTY detection for progress vs. plain logs
body: Logger always writes plain lines. In CI (non-TTY) we want unbuffered line-oriented output; in interactive shells we want spinners + redraws. Detect via `process.stdout.isTTY` and gate the spinner/progress UI accordingly.

title: cli: no `dev` subcommand combining build + watch + serve + reload
body: Standard expectation in modern SSGs. Add.

title: cli: no `nectar build --watch`
body: Standalone `--watch` flag on `build` is useful for power users who run their own preview server (e.g. caddy, nginx). Wire up Bun's `fs.watch` over `content/`, `themes/`, `nectar.toml` and re-run `build()` on change with debouncing.

title: cli: no env-var overrides for config keys
body: 12-factor deploys (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare) want `NECTAR_SITE_URL=https://prod.example.com` to override `[site].url` without committing changes. Implement an env-overlay layer in `src/config/loader.ts` that maps `NECTAR_<KEY_PATH>` to the schema.

title: cli: no progress bar / spinner during build
body: On a large blog (Ghost users routinely have 500+ posts) the current `build()` is a black box that prints one summary line at the end. Add a spinner showing current phase ("loading content", "planning routes", "rendering post 23/200") via `@clack/prompts` or a tiny in-house renderer that respects `--quiet` and TTY detection.

title: cli: no progress reporting during build
body: src/build/pipeline.ts iterates routes silently and prints one summary line. For sites with >100 routes the user has no feedback. Add a progress bar (e.g. `[ 42/120 ] posts/foo`), spinner during asset copy, and ETA. Use a tiny utility — avoid heavy deps. Disable when not TTY or --json.

title: cli: print Bun engine mismatch warning
body: package.json:38-40 declares `engines.bun >= 1.3.0`. If a user runs under bun 1.2, no warning. At CLI startup compare `Bun.version` against engines.bun and warn (or fail-fast with --strict).

title: cli: schema-export — `nectar schema config|frontmatter|theme`
body: Emit JSON Schema (from zod via zod-to-json-schema) so editors and external tools can validate without depending on the binary. Pipe to file: `nectar schema config > nectar.config.schema.json`. Publish at nectar.dev/schemas/ for `$schema` URLs.

title: cli: serve: does not auto-build before serving
body: serve.ts:13-15 checks for dist and exits 1 if missing. Better: offer `--build` (-b) to run a build first, and document it in the error message ('Run `nectar build` first, or pass --build').

title: cli: serve: does not honour site.url for printed link
body: Always prints `http://localhost:<port>` (serve.ts:40). When using a base_path != '/', the link should include it: `http://localhost:4321/blog/`. Otherwise the first click 404s.

title: cli: serve: invalid --port (NaN/negative) silently coerces
body: `Number(args[portFlag + 1])` (serve.ts:8) gives NaN for `--port abc`, which Bun.serve will then reject opaquely. Validate: must be integer 1..65535. Otherwise exit 2 with 'Invalid --port value: <given>'.

title: cli: serve: no --host flag
body: serve.ts:7-8 binds whatever Bun.serve defaults to (typically 0.0.0.0 or localhost). Add `--host` (default 'localhost'), with `--host 0.0.0.0` for LAN testing on mobile devices. Print the actual bound URL.

title: cli: serve: no graceful shutdown
body: serve.ts:18-38 calls Bun.serve and returns 0 immediately; the process stays alive because of the listener. There is no SIGINT handler that calls `server.stop()`. On Ctrl-C the connection is dropped abruptly. Capture `process.on('SIGINT', () => { server.stop(); process.exit(130); })`.

title: cli: signal-safe cleanup registry
body: Build `src/util/cleanup.ts`: an ordered list of cleanup callbacks (unlink temp dir, stop server, persist cache). Wired on SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP/exit. Each subcommand registers its own. Avoid ad-hoc try/finally scattered everywhere.

title: cli: support --no-foo negation for booleans
body: There is no way to disable a default-true boolean (e.g. `--no-color`, `--no-copy-content-assets`). parseArgs negation prefix `--no-` should be plumbed through. Provide a documented set: --no-color, --no-progress, --no-cache, --no-copy-content-assets, --no-watch.

title: cli: support multiple invocation styles for help inside subcommand
body: `nectar build help`, `nectar build --help`, `nectar build -h`. Currently only the parent `help` is honoured. Drop the subcommand-`help` form, but `--help` and `-h` should be intercepted in each runX function — or, better, before dispatch by the CommandSpec layer.

title: cli: support short flag aliases (-c, -p, -o, -v, -h)
body: Only `-h` and `-v` are partially handled at the top level. Subcommands take no shorts. Define a stable short-flag map: -c=--config, -p=--port, -o=--output, -w=--watch, -q=--quiet, -V=--verbose, -j=--json. Document in --help output.

title: cli: version constant duplicated and drifts from package.json
body: src/cli/index.ts:3 hardcodes `const VERSION = '0.0.1'`. package.json also has `"version": "0.0.1"`. They will drift. Read version from package.json at build time (import attribute or build-time replace), or via `Bun.file('package.json')`.

title: comments: `{{comments}}` helper renders an empty `<div data-nectar-comments>` with no provider wiring
body:
content.ts:112 emits `
`. Themes call `{{#if comments}}{{comments}}{{/if}}` (Source: post.hbs:60). Without a provider, the div is invisible. Fix: add `[components.comments] provider = "giscus|disqus|utterances|cusdis|hyvor|isso"` and have the helper emit the right script tag inline. Already partially filed (`comments: no Giscus / Disqus / Utterances wiring` P1) but specifically mention `data-nectar-comments` selector contract so users have a single place to override.

title: comments: no Giscus / Disqus / Utterances wiring
body: Implement `[components.comments] provider = "giscus|disqus|utterances"` and inject the right snippet under `{{comments}}`.

title: comments: post.comments boolean wired from frontmatter but never read by `{{comments}}` helper
body: loader.ts:349 sets `comments: true` unconditionally. Post type has the field. But the `comments` helper ignores it; it always emits the div. Honour the per-post flag: when `false`, render nothing.

title: community: no CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
body: Required to be taken seriously as an OSS project. Adopt Contributor Covenant 2.1. GitHub even has a one-click 'add COC' button.

title: community: no GitHub issue templates
body: Add `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug.yml`, `feature.yml`, `theme-compat.yml` (with a fields capture for theme name, Nectar version, Bun version, repro). Especially `theme-compat.yml` is critical because that's our differentiator.

title: config: no validation that [theme.custom] keys exist in theme package.json
body: Typos like `[theme.custom].titel_font` silently use defaults. Warn.

title: config: posts_per_page in nectar.toml is overridden by theme pkg.json
body: routes.ts uses `theme.pkg.posts_per_page || config.build.posts_per_page`. User intent likely the other way — user config overrides theme defaults. Flip.

title: config: schema does not validate theme custom-setting types against theme package.json
body: User can set `nectar.toml [theme.custom].title_font = 'Comic Sans'` and we'll accept it even though Source's enum only allows 3 values. Validate against pkg.custom definitions.

title: config: zod schema does not warn on unknown keys
body: Use `.strict()` to reject typos in nectar.toml — currently `[sites]` (typo) is silently ignored.

title: content: asDateISO falls back to epoch 1970 with no warning
body: src/content/frontmatter.ts:asDateISO returns `new Date(0).toISOString()` when a date string is unparseable. Posts with bad dates silently sort to the bottom of the feed instead of erroring. Surface a parse error mentioning the post path.

title: content: word_count counts whitespace-tokenised words, underestimates CJK by 90%+
body: markdown.ts:countWords splits on `/\s+/` — Japanese, Chinese, Thai etc. produce a count near 0 because they don't use ASCII whitespace between characters. Reading time for a 4000-character Japanese post would be ~1 minute. Use `Intl.Segmenter` with grapheme/word granularity for accurate CJK counts.

title: cross-theme: @setting context alias
body: Journal (and older themes) read `{{@setting.paid_members_enabled}}`. Add @setting as an alias of @site that surfaces site-wide config. Cheap fix: data.setting = data.site in buildRootData.

title: cross-theme: @site.accent_color CSS variable `--ghost-accent-color`
body: Liebling default.hbs:88 reads `--primary-subtle-color: var(--ghost-accent-color)` — Ghost auto-injects `--ghost-accent-color` into ghost_head. Nectar's ghost_head helper (src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts) does not emit a `<style>:root{--ghost-accent-color:#xxx;}</style>` block. Add it using site.accent_color (already in SiteData).

title: cross-theme: `kg-width-wide` / `kg-width-full` `kg-canvas` `gh-canvas` `gh-content` layout classes
body: Every modern theme expects post HTML to include `<figure class="kg-card kg-image-card kg-width-wide">` etc. on full-width images. Without these, theme CSS for wide/full images is dead. Nectar's markdown pipeline must emit kg-width-* on figure elements (likely via image-card detection or explicit `{:.kg-width-wide}` attributes).

title: cross-theme: card_assets package.json config option
body: Most modern themes declare `"card_assets": true` or `{exclude:[...]}` in package.json config. This tells Ghost to inject `koenig-lexical` card CSS/JS into the page automatically. Nectar's pkg.ts likely reads this but doesn't emit anything. Implement: when card_assets=true, include `cards.min.css/js` from @tryghost/shared-theme-assets (or vendor equivalent).

title: cross-theme: empty pagination object when only one page should still expose .page=1 .pages=1 .total=N
body: Many themes do `{{#match pagination.pages ">" 1}}`. If pagination is undefined the comparison short-circuits but accessing `.page` later may NaN. Always emit pagination = {page:1, pages:1, total:posts.length, prev_url:undefined, next_url:undefined}. Affects Headline, Wave, Bulletin, Journal etc.

title: cross-theme: ghost_head should emit Open Graph article:published_time / article:modified_time / article:tag for post routes
body: Casper-family themes don't render OG meta themselves — they rely entirely on ghost_head. Our ghost-head.ts JSON-LD has datePublished but no <meta property="article:published_time"> tag. Add for posts.

title: cross-theme: navigation helper expects `nav-{slug}` and `nav-current` classes
body: Our navigation helper (src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:5) emits `<li class="nav-{slugify(label)}">` only. Themes (Casper, Edition) inspect generated HTML expecting also `nav-current` on the current item. Add active-item detection by comparing item.url to route.url.

title: cross-theme: post.html should preserve embedded `<style>` and `<script>` from Ghost cards (HTML card, code injection)
body: Themes assume {{content}} can include arbitrary cards including `<script>` (HTML card embeds). Nectar's content helper (content.ts:5) wraps html in SafeString — good. But markdown.ts may sanitize and strip <script>. Verify HTML card content survives the markdown pipeline.

title: cross-theme: routes.yaml support absent
body: Wave (`blog.hbs`), Liebling (`page-newsletter`), Krabi (`members/*`), and many real-world themes rely on Ghost's routes.yaml for channel routing and custom slug→template mapping. Nectar currently has no routes.yaml parser (no references in src/build/routes.ts). Implement a parser that maps `routes:`, `collections:`, `taxonomies:` from routes.yaml into Nectar's route list. P1 for broad theme compat.

title: cross-theme: secondary_navigation absent when not configured → @site.secondary_navigation should be undefined, not []
body: Wave/Alto/London use `{{#unless @site.secondary_navigation}}` and `{{#if @site.secondary_navigation}}`. An empty array is truthy in Handlebars, breaking both conditionals. Either coerce empty arrays to undefined in SiteData or document the gotcha for theme authors.

title: cross-theme: visibility="tiers" Post.visibility union
body: Modern Ghost posts can have visibility='tiers' (gated by specific tier IDs). Our Post.visibility (model.ts:74) only accepts 'public'|'members'|'paid'. Casper post-card.hbs treats anything non-public as members/paid lock. Widen the union to include 'tiers' and 'filter'.

title: deploy: apache: no `.htaccess` emitter
body:
Many shared-hosting Ghost migrators end up on Apache (cPanel hosts). Nectar emits no `.htaccess` for mod_rewrite redirects, mod_headers cache rules, AddType, ErrorDocument.

**Fix**: `src/build/emitters/apache.ts` writes `dist/.htaccess`.

title: deploy: apache: no `ErrorDocument 404 /404.html` line
body:
Without it, Apache returns its default 404 page.

**Fix**: include in emitted `.htaccess`.

title: deploy: apache: no mod_rewrite trailing-slash rule
body:
Need `RewriteEngine On` plus `RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} -d` and `RewriteRule ^([^/]+)/?$ $1/index.html [L]` (or a clean-URLs variant).

**Fix**: include block in emitter.

title: deploy: caddy: no `Caddyfile` emitter
body:
Caddy is a popular alternative with auto-HTTPS. Nectar has no Caddyfile generator covering `file_server`, `try_files`, `header`, `redir`.

**Fix**: `src/build/emitters/caddy.ts` outputs `dist/.nectar/Caddyfile` from shared config.

title: deploy: caddy: no `try_files` example for clean URLs
body:
Caddy's `try_files {path} {path}/index.html` is needed; without docs users hand-roll regex.

**Fix**: include in emitted Caddyfile.

title: deploy: caddy: no header/redirect translation from shared YAML
body:
Caddy's `header` directive supports glob matchers; `redir` supports regex and status codes. Reuse shared schema.

**Fix**: caddy emitter writes `header /assets/built/* Cache-Control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"` etc.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: 404.html convention
body:
Cloudflare Pages serves `dist/404.html` automatically. Blocked by the missing-404 root issue. Once that's fixed, document the auto-serve behavior.

**Fix**: ensure 404.html emitter (cross-cutting) runs for this target; add note to docs.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/cloudflare-pages.md` covering: connecting Git, build command (`bun run build`), output directory (`dist`), env vars, custom domain DNS, `_headers`/`_redirects`/`_routes.json`, Image Resizing.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: no Wrangler config sample
body:
While Pages can build via Git directly, advanced users use `wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name=...`. There's no sample.

**Fix**: ship `examples/ci/cloudflare-pages.yml` (GitHub Actions w/ `cloudflare/wrangler-action`) and a `wrangler.toml` snippet.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: no `CF_PAGES_COMMIT_SHA` / `CF_PAGES_BRANCH` ingestion
body:
Cloudflare Pages exposes `CF_PAGES`, `CF_PAGES_COMMIT_SHA`, `CF_PAGES_BRANCH`, `CF_PAGES_URL`. We ignore them. Branch previews need `CF_PAGES_URL` injected as `site.url`.

**Fix**: read these in `loadConfig` and override `site.url` / expose under `@site.build`.

title: deploy: cloudflare-workers: `_headers`/`_redirects` not honored same as Pages
body:
Static Assets in Workers do not parse `_headers`/`_redirects` the way Pages does; instead, the user's Worker code must apply them. Nectar needs to either emit a Worker that does this, or a JSON manifest the Worker reads.

**Fix**: emit `dist/_routes-manifest.json` with redirects/headers; ship a reference Worker that consumes it.

title: deploy: cloudflare-workers: no `not_found_handling` config note
body:
Static Assets supports `not_found_handling = "single-page-application" | "404-page"`. For Nectar (multi-page), we want `404-page` which serves `dist/404.html`. Without docs, users default to single-page-application and break direct nav.

**Fix**: docs + sample `wrangler.toml` snippet.

title: deploy: cloudflare-workers: no `wrangler.toml` sample for Static Assets binding
body:
Cloudflare Workers Static Assets (the new model) requires `wrangler.toml` with `[assets] directory = "dist"` and optional `binding = "ASSETS"`. Nectar has no sample.

**Fix**: ship `examples/cloudflare-workers/wrangler.toml` and a no-op `index.ts` worker that defers to `ASSETS.fetch(request)`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: build output path not relativized — breaks subpath-hosted deploys (e.g. `/blog/`)
body:
Themes assume root-hosted URLs. When `build.base_path = '/blog/'` (e.g. GH Pages project site, or nginx subpath), `<a href="/foo/">` is wrong; needs to be `<a href="/blog/foo/">`.

**Fix**: verify all internal URLs are prefixed with `base_path` in `engine.ts` helpers; emit canonical with the same.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: docs/ has no `deployment/` directory
body:
`docs/` contains only DESIGN and GHOST_COMPATIBILITY. There is no `docs/deployment/<target>.md`. Ghost migrators routinely want one-page recipes per host.

**Fix**: scaffold `docs/deployment/index.md` plus one file per target (see per-target quickstart issues).

title: deploy: cross-cutting: emitters should share a `DeploymentArtifacts` interface
body:
Without an interface, each emitter will reimplement file writing, glob expansion, and conflict resolution.

**Fix**: define `interface DeployTarget { name: string; emit(ctx: { outputDir; redirects; headers; routes; }): Promise<void>; }` and a registry under `src/build/emitters/registry.ts`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: feeds (RSS/sitemap) absolute URLs not validated for branch previews
body:
`feeds.ts` uses `config.site.url` even when the build is a branch preview. RSS readers cache absolute URLs and now point to production from a preview build.

**Fix**: when preview env detected (NETLIFY/VERCEL/CF_PAGES env), inject `noindex` headers AND skip RSS/sitemap emission OR rewrite with `DEPLOY_PRIME_URL`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no `humans.txt` / `robots.txt` emitter
body:
Nectar emits `sitemap.xml` and `rss.xml` but no `robots.txt`. Every target needs one referencing the sitemap. Currently themes have to ship one as a static asset, which most don't.

**Fix**: add `components.robots.enabled` with a default `User-agent: *` block plus `Sitemap: <site.url>/sitemap.xml`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no `nectar deploy` subcommand
body:
`src/cli/index.ts` has no `deploy` verb. Users have to manually push `dist/` to each provider. A `nectar deploy <target>` (or `nectar emit <target>`) command that runs build + writes target-specific config (`_headers`, `_redirects`, `vercel.json`, `netlify.toml`, `.htaccess`, etc.) is needed.

**Fix**: add `src/cli/commands/deploy.ts` with a `--target` flag; emitters per target live under `src/build/emitters/<target>.ts`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no `noindex` / `X-Robots-Tag` for preview deploys
body:
Branch/preview deploys should not be indexed. Nectar doesn't inject `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` or emit `X-Robots-Tag: noindex` for non-prod environments.

**Fix**: when env signals preview (per cross-cutting branch URL detection), force `meta robots=noindex` and add `X-Robots-Tag` to headers emitter.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no asset cache-busting manifest for downstream CDN purging
body:
Theme assets are fingerprinted (see `emit.ts:emitAsset`) but no `manifest.json` mapping logical->fingerprinted paths is written. Cloudflare cache purge, CloudFront invalidation, and Fastly soft-purge all need a list of changed URLs. Without a manifest, full-site invalidations are required, blowing past free tiers.

**Fix**: emit `dist/.nectar/asset-manifest.json` keyed by logical path; expose `nectar manifest` command.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no integration tests for emitter outputs
body:
No tests cover `_headers`/`_redirects`/`vercel.json`/`.htaccess` shape. Regressions will ship silently.

**Fix**: add `tests/build/emitters/<target>.test.ts` per emitter using fixture configs.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no preview/branch-deploy base URL injection
body:
Branch deploys on Netlify/Cloudflare/Vercel produce URLs like `https://deploy-preview-42--site.netlify.app`. Nectar's `site.url` is fixed in config, so canonical tags, sitemap, RSS feed and absolute links all point to the production domain even on previews, creating SEO conflict and broken OG previews.

**Fix**: respect `NECTAR_SITE_URL`, `URL` (Netlify), `CF_PAGES_URL`, `VERCEL_URL` to override `config.site.url` at build time.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: trailing slash policy is hardcoded to dir/index.html only
body:
`src/build/routes.ts` emits `<slug>/index.html` for posts/pages/tags/authors. Some targets (S3 without CloudFront, GitHub Pages with custom domain edge cases) prefer `<slug>.html`. There is no `trailing_slash: 'always' | 'never' | 'preserve'` switch.

**Fix**: add `build.trailing_slash` to schema; have `routes.ts` produce either `<slug>/index.html` (always) or `<slug>.html` (never) and emit canonical/redirect rules in both directions.

title: deploy: docker: no `Dockerfile` for slim nginx-alpine image
body:
Repo has no `Dockerfile`. Migrants want `docker build -t mysite . && docker run -p 80:80 mysite`.

**Fix**: ship `examples/docker/Dockerfile` based on `nginx:1.27-alpine`, copy `dist/` and `nginx.conf`, expose 80.

title: deploy: docker: no multi-stage build (Bun build + nginx serve)
body:
Single-stage requires host build; multi-stage `FROM oven/bun AS build` then `FROM nginx:alpine` is cleaner.

**Fix**: ship multi-stage Dockerfile in `examples/docker/`.

title: deploy: fly: no `fly.toml` sample for static-only nginx machine
body:
Fly.io can host static sites via a small `nginx:alpine` Machine. Nectar has no `fly.toml` sample.

**Fix**: ship `examples/fly/fly.toml` + `Dockerfile` that copies `dist/` into nginx, plus a `nginx.conf` that does `try_files`.

title: deploy: fly: no headers/redirects in shared format
body:
Translating shared redirects.yaml + headers.yaml into nginx is a separate emitter; reuse the self-hosted-nginx emitter (see those issues) but applied at Docker-build time.

**Fix**: ensure nginx emitter outputs to a path the Dockerfile copies.

title: deploy: github-pages: 404.html path doesn't match Pages convention for project sites
body:
For user/org sites `404.html` at root is honored; for project sites under `https://user.github.io/repo/`, `404.html` must also be at the publishing-source root *plus* `build.base_path` needs to be `/repo/`. We don't validate `base_path` against the target.

**Fix**: in `nectar deploy --target=github-pages`, warn if `build.base_path === '/'` while a `CNAME` is absent.

title: deploy: github-pages: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/github-pages.md` covering: enabling Pages (Settings > Pages > GitHub Actions), `base_path` for project sites, `CNAME`, `.nojekyll`, meta-refresh redirect limitation, Enforce HTTPS toggle, env vars (`GITHUB_SHA` -> `NECTAR_COMMIT_SHA`).

title: deploy: github-pages: redirects.yaml has no GitHub Pages backend
body:
GitHub Pages does not support server-side redirects. The only option is HTML `<meta http-equiv="refresh">` pages. Nectar must emit one `<slug>/index.html` redirect stub per `redirects.yaml` entry on this target.

**Fix**: in github-pages emitter, materialize redirects as meta-refresh stubs with `<link rel="canonical">` to destination.

title: deploy: netlify: 404.html conventions
body: Netlify auto-serves `404.html` at publish-dir root. Blocked by missing 404 emitter; document once fixed.

title: deploy: netlify: branch deploy URL not injected (DEPLOY_PRIME_URL)
body:
Netlify exposes `URL`, `DEPLOY_URL`, `DEPLOY_PRIME_URL`. Branch previews need `DEPLOY_PRIME_URL` as `site.url`. We use the static value, so canonical tags and OG images point to production.

**Fix**: same as cross-cutting branch URL issue.

title: deploy: netlify: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/netlify.md` with: drag-and-drop UI deploy, `netlify.toml`, `_headers`/`_redirects`, env vars, branch deploys, Image CDN, deploy contexts (production/branch-deploy/deploy-preview).

title: deploy: netlify: no `netlify.toml` sample with build/publish/plugins
body:
`netlify.toml` is the source-of-truth for build config (build command, publish dir, redirect rules, edge functions, env). Nectar has none.

**Fix**: ship `examples/netlify/netlify.toml` with `command = "bun run build"`, `publish = "dist"`, optional `[[plugins]]`.

title: deploy: nginx: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/nginx.md`: server block, compression, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, TLS via certbot, log format, security headers, redirect map include.

title: deploy: nginx: no 404 fallback `error_page 404 /404.html;` sample
body:
Need `error_page 404 /404.html;` plus `location = /404.html { internal; }`.

**Fix**: include in emitter.

title: deploy: nginx: no ETag / Cache-Control directive sample
body:
nginx defaults: ETag enabled, no Cache-Control. For Nectar's fingerprinted assets we want `expires 1y; add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";` for `/assets/built/*` and `expires 0; add_header Cache-Control "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate";` for HTML.

**Fix**: include these blocks in the emitted nginx.conf.

title: deploy: nginx: no precompressed asset emitter (gzip/brotli)
body:
`gzip_static` and `brotli_static` directives need `.gz` and `.br` siblings next to assets. Nectar's `copyAssets` writes raw files only.

**Fix**: post-process step that gzip- and brotli-compresses HTML, CSS, JS, SVG, JSON, XML > 1KB into `<file>.gz` and `<file>.br`.

title: deploy: r2: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/cloudflare-r2.md`: bucket creation, S3 endpoint, public access tradeoffs, Worker frontend, custom domain via CF.

title: deploy: r2: no headers/redirects emitter (Worker-bound)
body:
If fronted by a Worker, headers/redirects come from Worker code reading a manifest. Reuses cross-cutting manifest issue.

**Fix**: emit `dist/_routes-manifest.json`; sample Worker consumes it.

title: deploy: r2: no index.html append handling (same as S3 root cause)
body:
Same `<slug>/` -> `<slug>/index.html` issue as S3; when fronted by a Worker, the Worker must append.

**Fix**: ship Worker sample that does append-index AND security-headers in one (`examples/r2/worker.ts`).

title: deploy: render: no `render.yaml` sample for static site
body:
Render's Blueprint format (`render.yaml`) provisions a static-site service. Nectar has no sample.

**Fix**: ship `examples/render/render.yaml` with `type: static`, `buildCommand: bun install && bun run build`, `publishPath: ./dist`.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: 404.html mapping via CloudFront custom error response
body:
S3 returns 403 for missing keys (with default bucket policy), which CloudFront must remap to 404 via `custom_error_response`. Without it, `<slug>` typos return 403.

**Fix**: docs + sample `custom_error_responses` entry mapping 403/404 to `/404.html` with `200` cache override left as-is.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/s3-cloudfront.md`: bucket creation, OAC, distribution config, custom domain via ACM, append-index function, headers policy, invalidation, cost watch.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no GitHub Actions workflow sample
body:
Users want a workflow that runs `bun run build`, `aws s3 sync dist/ s3://...`, then invalidates CloudFront. None ship.

**Fix**: `examples/ci/s3-cloudfront.yml` with OIDC role assumption (no static IAM keys).

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no OAC (Origin Access Control) Terraform/CloudFormation sample
body:
Current best practice is OAC over OAI for S3 origins. Nectar has no IaC sample. Migrators end up with public buckets or stale OAI configs.

**Fix**: ship `examples/s3-cloudfront/terraform/` (or CDK) creating bucket + OAC + distribution; document.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no headers emission via CloudFront response-headers-policy
body:
S3 itself can't set CSP/HSTS; CloudFront's response-headers-policy is the right place. Nectar has nothing.

**Fix**: emit a `cloudfront-response-headers-policy.json` from `headers.yaml`; document `aws cloudfront create-response-headers-policy` invocation.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no invalidation list output from build
body:
After upload, users must `aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths /* ...` or, smarter, just the changed paths. Nectar's pipeline doesn't emit a changed-files list.

**Fix**: emit `dist/.nectar/changed-paths.txt` by diffing against a previous `asset-manifest.json` (see cross-cutting manifest issue).

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no redirects backend (CloudFront Functions or bucket routing rules)
body:
S3 redirects via routing rules (XML in bucket properties) or per-object `x-amz-website-redirect-location`. Modern setup uses a CloudFront Function. Nectar emits neither.

**Fix**: ship CloudFront Function sample that consumes a built-in redirects map (inlined at deploy time from redirects.yaml).

title: deploy: vercel: 404.html behavior
body: Vercel routes 404s through `pages/404.tsx` typically; for static, it serves `404.html` if present. Blocked by missing-404 emitter; document.

title: deploy: vercel: `VERCEL_URL` / `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` not consumed
body:
Branch previews on Vercel set `VERCEL_URL` (the auto-generated deploy URL). Nectar's build doesn't pick it up, so previews emit production canonicals.

**Fix**: same as cross-cutting branch URL issue.

title: deploy: vercel: `cleanUrls` vs `trailingSlash` conflict not validated
body:
Vercel's `cleanUrls: true` strips `.html` and `index.html`; `trailingSlash: true` enforces trailing slash. These interact (and can produce redirect loops). Nectar must pick a coherent default based on config.

**Fix**: when emitting `vercel.json`, set `trailingSlash: true` + `cleanUrls: true` only when `build.trailing_slash === 'always'`; otherwise pick the safe combo and document.

title: deploy: vercel: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/vercel.md`: Git import, `vercel.json`, env vars, preview deploys, Image, custom domains, redirects.

title: docs: no CONTRIBUTING.md
body: Describe dev setup, branching, commit style, testing expectations.

title: docs: no auto-generated CLI reference
body: After we structure the CLI (separate issue), generate `docs/cli.md` from the command definitions so every flag is documented. Hugo and Astro both do this; users dramatically expect it.

title: docs: no auto-generated config reference from Zod schema
body: The Zod schema in `src/config/schema.ts` is the source of truth — emit `docs/config.md` from it with field names, types, defaults, and zod descriptions. Add `.describe()` chains to every field so docs are not empty.

title: docs: no published docs site
body: Use Nectar to build its own docs site (dogfood).

title: ghost-card: ALL: doc — `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` doesn't list any card support status
body:
The compat doc currently mentions only that mobiledoc/lexical cards aren't handled. There's no per-card status matrix. A user evaluating Nectar can't tell which cards survive migration.

**Fix**: add a card matrix to `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`:
| Card | Migrates | Renders | Notes |
| bookmark | ❌ | ❌ | – |
| gallery | ❌ | ❌ | – |
| ... | | | |
Keep it updated as cards land.

title: ghost-card: ALL: feed/rss — card HTML must be inlined or stripped for feeds
body: RSS feeds typically use the post HTML inline. Card markup with custom CSS classes doesn't carry inline styles, so RSS readers see unstyled blobs. Worse, `

title: ghost-card: ALL: render — `.gh-content` wrapper assumed by spacing rules but emitted by post.hbs
body:
Source theme expects post body inside `
…body…
` (Casper convention). The card spacing rules (`screen.css:2273-2289`) all start with `.gh-content`. If a theme partial wraps the body differently (e.g. `
` directly), every card spacing rule misses. **Fix**: document the `.gh-content` wrapper as a hard requirement in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`. Add a smoke test for partial templates that renders a card and asserts `.gh-content > .kg-card` exists.

title: ghost-card: ALL: render — `srcset` rebuilding doesn't account for `__GHOST_URL__` prefix
body: Even Turndown-survived `srcset` strings (e.g. on plain `` tags) contain `__GHOST_URL__/content/images/size/w600/…`. Without rewriting, every responsive variant 404s. **Fix**: pre-process `srcset` in HTML before Turndown OR build a markdown-to-html pass that rewrites image src/srcset/sizes against the site URL.

title: ghost-card: ALL: rendering — `gh_head` / `gh_foot` don't inject Koenig runtime scripts
body:
Ghost cards that need JS (audio, video, toggle, signup, twitter/instagram embeds) rely on Koenig's runtime scripts being injected via `{{ghost_head}}`/`{{ghost_foot}}`. Our `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts` likely emits only SEO+OG tags. Without the runtimes, half the cards are broken even with perfect markup.

**Fix**: extend `ghost-foot` helper to inject conditional script tags based on which card types appeared in the rendered body (collect a set in render context, emit only what's needed).

title: ghost-card: ALL: rendering — card spacing rules depend on `:not(.kg-card)` / `.kg-card` sibling selectors
ghost-card: ALL: rendering — no width modifier helper (`kg-width-regular|wide|full`)
body:
Every Ghost card supports three widths. The CSS at `screen.css:411,416` keys off `.kg-width-wide` and `.kg-width-full` directly. Authors need a way to set width per card.

**Fix**: every shortcode that wraps a `kg-card-*` element should accept a `width="regular|wide|full"` param and emit the matching class. Default `regular`. Document in docs.

title: ghost-card: ALL: tests — add per-card fixtures from a Ghost demo export
body:
There's no Ghost demo content checked into `tests/fixtures/`. Spinning up a real Ghost instance, creating one post per card type, and exporting JSON gives a canonical test corpus. Without this, every card-aware change is regression-prone.

**Fix**: add `tests/fixtures/ghost-export.json` with every card type present once. Add `tests/ghost/import.cards.test.ts` that imports the fixture and asserts the resulting markdown matches snapshots.

title: ghost-card: ALL: theme-dev — `kg-card-hascaption` modifier class missing from Source CSS
body:
Ghost adds `kg-card-hascaption` to any card that has a figcaption (rule at `screen.css:2109` references it for the width-full + width-full sibling exception). Without this class, posts with mixed captioned/uncaptioned media end up with inconsistent spacing.

**Fix**: every figure-emitting shortcode must conditionally add `kg-card-hascaption` when a caption is present.

title: ghost-card: bookmark: image-pipeline — thumbnail and favicon are remote URLs, no caching or responsive variants
body:
Bookmark cards reference a publisher favicon (16-32px) and an article thumbnail (often 600-1200px). Today we'd emit whatever URL Ghost stored, which usually points at `__GHOST_URL__/content/images/…` (a placeholder Ghost inserts and resolves at render time — see TryGhost/Ghost ImageHandler). Without a URL rewriter the placeholder leaks into the static output and the image 404s.
