**Fix**: add a `__GHOST_URL__` rewriting pass in `import.ts` (replace with `''` or the configured `site.url`), and let the image pipeline (when implemented) optionally fetch + relocate remote thumbnails into `content/images/bookmarks/` to avoid hot-linking and to enable `srcset`.

title: ghost-card: bookmark: rendering — no markdown syntax to author bookmarks natively
body:
Even if the migrator preserved metadata, there's no way to author a bookmark card in pure Markdown today. `src/content/markdown.ts` uses `marked` with GFM only — no directive/shortcode plugin. Authors who didn't come from Ghost have no path to a `kg-bookmark-card`.

**Fix**: add a `bookmark` shortcode handler (or markdown-it style directive) that takes a URL and either (a) calls the URL at build-time to fetch oEmbed/Open Graph metadata and caches it under `cache/bookmarks/<sha>.json`, or (b) requires the author to provide metadata inline. Emit the exact `kg-bookmark-card` markup the Source theme expects (see `example/themes/source/assets/css/screen.css:2381`).

title: ghost-card: bookmark: theme-dev — Source theme CSS targets `kg-bookmark-*` but no JS for tracking link clicks
body:
`example/themes/source/assets/css/screen.css` lines 2381-2417 style `.kg-bookmark-card`, `.kg-bookmark-container`, `.kg-bookmark-content`, `.kg-bookmark-title`, `.kg-bookmark-description`, `.kg-bookmark-metadata`, `.kg-bookmark-thumbnail`. Casper has identical structure. We must emit this exact DOM tree, with the outer element a `<figure>` and the link a `<a class="kg-bookmark-container">` (not a `div`) — the CSS uses `a.kg-bookmark-container` selectors at line 2389.

**Fix**: pin the exact HTML contract in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` and add a snapshot test under `tests/render` rendering a fixture with a bookmark card and asserting the emitted DOM matches Ghost's contract byte-for-byte (modulo whitespace).

title: ghost-card: button: migration — Turndown emits plain link, loses alignment and color variant
body:
Ghost button card: `
Get Started
` (alignment: `kg-align-left|center`, style: `kg-btn-accent` only currently). Turndown produces `[Get Started](url)` — alignment + button-ness lost. Author can no longer tell it apart from an inline link. **Fix**: Turndown rule matching `div.kg-button-card` → emit `{% button href="…" text="…" align="center" style="accent" %}`. Map to `kg-button-card` markup in render.

title: ghost-card: callout: rendering — Source CSS exists for callout but no markdown syntax authors it
body:
`screen.css:2334` styles `.kg-callout-card.kg-card` and `.kg-callout-card-accent a`. The CSS expects an outer `.kg-callout-card` element with optional color modifier and a `.kg-callout-emoji` + `.kg-callout-text` pair inside.

No markdown extension produces this. Pick either GFM alerts (`> [!NOTE]`) and remap to callout-card, or implement directive containers (`:::callout`).

**Fix**: add a marked extension/handler that maps a chosen syntax to the exact `kg-callout-card` DOM and exposes a `color` and `emoji` parameter. Document in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`.

title: ghost-card: callout: theme-dev — `kg-callout-card-<color>` modifier classes have no per-color CSS in Source
body:
The Source theme CSS only references `.kg-callout-card.kg-card` and `.kg-callout-card-accent a` — there is no per-color rule for `kg-callout-card-blue/green/yellow/red/pink/purple`. Ghost ships these in Koenig's stylesheet shipped with the editor; they don't reach static-site themes by default.

**Fix**: vendor the Koenig callout color palette CSS into `assets/css/screen.css` (or a dedicated `koenig.css`), or document that themes must include it. Without this, every callout looks identical regardless of color.

title: ghost-card: code: migration — language, caption, and line numbers are all lost
body:
Ghost code card: ``. Some posts use `kg-code-card-with-line-numbers` or store `linenums` in the lexical metadata. Turndown's `codeBlockStyle: 'fenced'` produces a fenced block — but **only when the `` element has a `class="language-X"`**. Some Ghost exports have the language on the `
` instead, or use `data-language`. The figcaption is dropped, breaking caption-aware themes.

**Fix**: pre-process to lift `data-language` / `language-*` onto the `` element if it's only on `
`. Turndown rule for `figure.kg-code-card` that emits a fenced block followed by `> Caption` (blockquote convention) or a real markdown directive.

title: ghost-card: code: rendering — marked emits `<pre><code class="language-X">` but no syntax highlighter is wired
body:
`src/content/markdown.ts` uses bare marked. The output for ```\`\`\`ts``` is `
…
` with no highlighter applied — code blocks render as plain monospace. Ghost themes typically ship Prism.js or highlight.js, hydrating client-side. Casper does this. Source… does not appear to. Verify. **Fix**: either: 1. Build-time highlighting via `marked-highlight` + Shiki (or `marked-shiki`) — emits styled spans, no runtime JS needed. 2. Vendor Prism + a theme stylesheet and hydrate client-side. Prefer Shiki at build time for performance.

title: ghost-card: embed: rendering — youtube/vimeo aspect ratio depends on `.kg-embed-card iframe` rule that doesn't exist
body:
Ghost's Koenig CSS sets `.kg-embed-card iframe { aspect-ratio: 16/9; width: 100%; }`. Source has no such rule. Result: YouTube iframes render at the default 300x150 unless the iframe itself has `width`/`height` attrs (which the embed sometimes does, sometimes doesn't, depending on provider).

**Fix**: add `.kg-embed-card iframe { width: 100%; aspect-ratio: 16/9; border: 0; }` to Source CSS or vendor Koenig's embed CSS.

title: ghost-card: file: migration — Turndown produces a bare anchor with no filesize, no description
body:
Ghost file card: `
Title
Description
x.pdf
1.2 MB
…svg…
`. Turndown emits `[Title Description x.pdf 1.2 MB](url)` — a single weird anchor. The `download` attribute, the filesize (which is computed by Ghost from the actual file), and the icon are all lost. **Fix**: Turndown rule matching `.kg-file-card` → emits `{% file href="…" title="…" description="…" name="…" size="…" %}` shortcode. Filesize: if the file is bundled with the Ghost export (Ghost includes files in the zip), recompute from disk; otherwise carry forward the human-readable string.

title: ghost-card: file: rendering — Source CSS targets kg-file-card-* but no shortcode emits the markup
body:
`screen.css:2442-2454` styles `.kg-file-card-container`, `.kg-file-card-contents`, `.kg-file-card-metadata`, `.kg-file-card-filesize::before`. The `::before` injects a separator dot between filename and filesize — so the markup MUST keep both in the same `.kg-file-card-metadata` parent in order, or the dot ends up in the wrong place. **Fix**: shortcode handler must emit DOM in this exact order: `
`. Add a snapshot test.

title: ghost-card: gallery: rendering — no gallery shortcode/extension in markdown pipeline
body:
`src/content/markdown.ts` has no extension point for galleries. Even if migration preserved a `{% gallery images=[…] %}` shortcode, nothing expands it. We need either a remark/marked extension or a pre-processing pass that takes the shortcode and emits the exact Ghost gallery DOM (rows of `kg-gallery-image` with `flex: <ratio>`).

**Fix**: implement a gallery shortcode handler that:
1. Groups images into rows of max 3 (Ghost's rule).
2. Computes `flex: <width>/<height>` per image so rows balance.
3. Emits `<figure class="kg-card kg-gallery-card kg-width-wide">` … matching the Source theme.
4. Supports an optional caption.

title: ghost-card: header-v1: migration — Turndown drops all of header (background, title, subtitle, CTA)
body:
Ghost header v1 (legacy): `

Title

Subtitle

Get Started
`. Turndown converts `

`/`

`/`` to bare `##`/`###`/`[link](url)` but **loses the background image, the style variant (`kg-style-dark|light|accent`) and the card-ness**. Renders as a normal heading sequence. **Fix**: Turndown rule for `div.kg-header-card` (no `-v2` suffix) → `{% header style="dark" background="…" title="…" subtitle="…" cta_text="…" cta_href="…" %}`.

title: ghost-card: header-v2: migration — alignment, button color, accent text not preserved
body:
Ghost header v2 (current) adds more knobs: `kg-align-center|left`, `kg-content-wide`, optional background image with `--bg-image-position` / `--bg-image-color` overlay, button style `kg-header-card-button-accent`, accent color via inline style. Markup root is `<div class="kg-card kg-header-card kg-v2 kg-width-full kg-align-center kg-style-image …">`.

None of v2's variants survive Turndown. Authors migrating from a v2 header end up with a flat heading block on the static site.

**Fix**: extend the header shortcode to accept `version="v2"`, `align`, `width`, `text_color`, `button_color`, `accent`. When emitting, branch on version.

title: ghost-card: header: image-pipeline — background image not relocated, lacks responsive srcset
body:
Headers carry a full-bleed background — often 2400px+. Today the `__GHOST_URL__/content/images/…` placeholder leaks into the `style="background-image: url(…)"` attribute (importer never touches it) and image is never copied to `content/images/`.

**Fix**: same placeholder-rewrite + asset-relocation pass as bookmark/file. For `background-image:` URLs, also emit a `<picture>` with `image-set()` srcset if asset pipeline supports it.

title: ghost-card: html: codeinjection_head/foot — preserved in frontmatter but never rendered
body:
`src/ghost/import.ts:152-153` carries `codeinjection_head` and `codeinjection_foot` into frontmatter. But the render side (`src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts`) likely doesn't inject post-level code injection into the `<head>`/`</body>` — confirm. If not, Ghost-imported posts lose per-post analytics snippets, custom CSS, etc.

**Fix**: extend `ghost_head` / `ghost_foot` helpers to concatenate site-level + post-level codeinjection.

title: ghost-card: image: image-pipeline — `loading="lazy"` not emitted on bare markdown images
body: Browsers support `` natively. Ghost always sets it. Our marked output does not. Above-the-fold images are a minor exception (you usually want `loading="eager"` and `fetchpriority="high"` for the first image / feature image), but everything else should lazy-load. **Fix**: in the figure-promotion pass, default to `loading="lazy"` for all in-body images; allow per-image override `{lazy=false}`.

title: ghost-card: markdown: migration — markdown card content is double-converted (markdown → html → markdown)
body:
Ghost markdown card stores raw Markdown inside `<!--kg-card-begin: markdown--> …rendered html… <!--kg-card-end: markdown-->`. The source-of-truth markdown is **only in mobiledoc/lexical**, not in HTML. The HTML export contains the *rendered* result.

When our importer Turndowns the rendered HTML, we get markdown back — but possibly different from the author's original (e.g. `\\` line breaks lost, footnote anchors flattened, GFM table alignment lost).

**Fix**: when both `lexical` and `html` are present and the lexical document contains a `markdown` card, extract that card's `.markdown` field directly into the body and skip Turndown for that region. Mark the region with `<!--kg-card-begin: markdown-->` sentinels on import to make the source visible.

title: ghost-card: paywall: excerpt — `custom_excerpt` is the only public-safe text
body:
If a post has `visibility:"members"` and a paywall, the only text we can safely use for SEO meta_description and OG description is `custom_excerpt` (author-provided). Falling back to `plaintext.slice(0, n)` could leak paid content.

**Fix**: in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:excerpt`, when `visibility !== 'public'`, ignore `plaintext` and use only `custom_excerpt`. Test with a fixture.

title: ghost-card: paywall: migration — `<!--members-only-->` comment is dropped by Turndown
body:
Ghost paywall card serialises as an HTML comment: `<!--members-only-->`. Everything after this comment in the HTML is paid content. Turndown by default discards HTML comments — the divider vanishes and the post becomes wholly public on import.

**Fix**: pre-process `post.html` before Turndown: replace `<!--members-only-->` with a unique sentinel (e.g. `

<!--nectar-paywall-->

`), then have Turndown emit it as a special line in the markdown (or write a custom rule that recognises the comment node). On render, split body at that marker.

title: ghost-card: paywall: rendering — no member gating runtime, but the divider needs to be honored
body:
Static sites cannot enforce member gates server-side — they'd need a real auth backend. But we can still:
1. Truncate paid content from the static HTML (treat the paywall as `<!--more-->`-style cut) and surface it only on the excerpt/list.
2. Or render the cut content but hide it behind a `members-only` CSS class for a JS-based gate (e.g. Outpost, Magic Pages member-bridge).

Today neither is implemented — the paywall is invisible on import.

**Fix**: add `nectar.config.ts → members.paywall = 'strip'|'gate-js'|'show'` and render accordingly. Document the security implication: `'gate-js'` is client-side and bypassable.

title: ghost-card: signup: migration — newsletter form HTML stripped to a heading and a button
body:
Ghost signup card: `

Subscribe

…

…

`. Turndown produces `## Subscribe`, a paragraph, and possibly a bare "Subscribe" word — the `
` and its `data-members-*` attributes are gone, breaking newsletter signup entirely. **Fix**: Turndown rule → `{% signup heading="…" subheading="…" button="Subscribe" disclaimer="…" background="…" labels="…" %}`. Preserve the `data-members-*` contract.

title: ghost-card: signup: rendering — Ghost themes rely on `data-members-*` JS contract we don't bundle
body:
Ghost's portal.js (member system) attaches behaviour to `data-members-form`, `data-members-email`, `data-members-submit`, `data-members-name`. Static sites have no member backend, so the form must be **either** wired to a third-party (Buttondown/ConvertKit/Mailchimp/Listmonk) **or** removed gracefully.

Today `src/render/helpers/content.ts:subscribe_form` emits `<form data-nectar-subscribe action="#" method="post">` — incompatible with Ghost's contract and with no real backend.

**Fix**: make the signup shortcode pluggable: `nectar.config.ts → members.provider = 'buttondown'|'convertkit'|'mailchimp'|'none'` with each provider emitting its own form action/method. Render `kg-signup-card` markup but with the configured form target.

title: ghost-card: toggle: migration — Turndown loses `<details>` semantics
body:
Ghost toggle card: `

Heading

Body

`. Turndown emits `#### Heading` plus a paragraph — no `
`/``, no toggle behaviour. Authors lose the collapse UI entirely. **Fix**: Turndown rule → either `
Heading\n\nBody\n\n
` (HTML in markdown) or a `{% toggle heading="…" %}…{% /toggle %}` shortcode. Map both to `kg-toggle-card` DOM at render.

title: ghost-compat: @config.posts_per_page used by theme but only theme.pkg drives our value
body: Source theme reads `@config.posts_per_page`. Expose it.

title: ghost-compat: @labs context undefined; themes that check it crash
body: Expose as empty object so `{{#if @labs.foo}}` is safe.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `codeinjection_head` and `codeinjection_foot`
body:
Ghost lets admins set site-wide head/foot code injection via Admin → Code injection. Themes read `@site.codeinjection_head`/`@site.codeinjection_foot` via `{{ghost_head}}`/`{{ghost_foot}}`. Our `SiteData` has no such fields and our `ghost_head` helper in `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts` only emits the post-level `codeinjection_head`.

Fix: add to config schema and inject in `{{ghost_head}}` / `{{ghost_foot}}`.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `members_enabled`, `paid_members_enabled`, `members_invite_only`
body:
Source's navigation, footer, header, cta, post-list partials all branch on `@site.members_enabled`, `@site.paid_members_enabled`, `@site.members_invite_only`. Our `SiteData` in `src/content/model.ts` and `buildSite` in `src/content/loader.ts` never set these. They evaluate as `undefined`/falsy, which is fine for sites that don't use members, but if a theme is built assuming "members_enabled=true" by default it will silently disable subscribe UI.

Fix: add an optional `[site.members]` config block and surface `members_enabled`, `paid_members_enabled`, `members_invite_only`, `comments_enabled` defaulting to false.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing meta fields (meta_title, meta_description, og_image, og_title, og_description, twitter_image, twitter_title, twitter_description)
body:
Ghost exposes these site-level SEO overrides via `@site.*`. Our `SiteData` doesn't model them, so themes generating fallback OG meta from `@site.og_image` etc. always get blank.

Fix: add to `SiteData`, accept in `[site]` config block, and have `{{ghost_head}}` consult them.

title: ghost-compat: @site.navigation items are plain {label,url}, missing `slug` and `current`
body:
Per Ghost docs, navigation items inside `partials/navigation.hbs` foreach expose `{{label}}`, `{{url}}`, `{{slug}}`, `{{current}}`. Our `NavigationItem` type only has `label` and `url`. Themes using `{{#if current}}` on nav items won't ever see the active state, and `{{slug}}` for nav-* CSS classes is missing.

Fix: enrich navigation items at render time with a per-route computed `slug` (slugified label) and `current` (compare against `route.url`), and expose them in the `{{navigation}}` helper's iteration context.

title: ghost-compat: Collections with custom `permalink` patterns not supported
body:
Ghost's collections accept `permalink: /{primary_tag}/{slug}/` to drive URL structure. Our `planRoutes` hard-codes `/{slug}/`. Themes (and existing imports) that expect `/blog/post-slug/` style routes break.

Fix: introduce a permalink template engine that supports `{slug}`, `{primary_tag}`, `{primary_author}`, `{year}`, `{month}`, `{day}`, `{id}`.

title: ghost-compat: Koenig editor card markup not emitted by Markdown pipeline
body:
Ghost emits semantic HTML for bookmark, callout, gallery, audio, video, button, signup, product, header, toggle, paywall cards (each with `kg-card`/`kg-*-card` classes). Theme CSS targets these. Our Markdown pipeline emits plain HTML — bookmark links are bare anchors, no `<figure class="kg-bookmark-card">`, no callout styling, no toggle card markup. Themes look broken on imported content.

Fix: add a custom Markdown extension or post-render transformer that converts conventional Markdown (e.g. blockquote-with-emoji → callout-card; horizontal rule + special syntax → bookmark-card; html `<details>` → toggle-card).

title: ghost-compat: Post `access` field absent — Ghost themes branch on `{{#if access}}`
body:
Ghost computes a runtime `access` boolean per post (does the current viewer have access). Source's post-card.hbs uses `{{#unless access}}{{^has visibility="public"}}<lock>{{/has}}{{/unless}}`. We never set `access`. Result: lock icon shows on every public post (because `access` is undefined → falsy → `unless` is truthy).

Fix: compute `access = visibility === 'public' || @member.tier matches`. Without members, always `true` for public posts; never `true` for members/paid.

title: ghost-compat: RSS guid should be the post URL with `isPermaLink="true"`
body:
Ghost emits `<guid isPermaLink="true">{{url absolute=true}}</guid>`. Without this, feed readers will not de-duplicate items correctly across cache windows.

Fix: include guid in standard form.

title: ghost-compat: RSS items must use `<![CDATA[...]]>` for HTML, not entity-escaped
body:
Ghost's RSS wraps content:encoded in CDATA so HTML renders in readers. If we naively XML-escape, readers (Feedly, NetNewsWire) show raw `

` tags. Fix: emit CDATA blocks for title (when special chars) and content:encoded.

title: ghost-compat: When RSS is implemented, it must emit Ghost-conformant channel/items
body:
Ghost's RSS includes: channel (title, description, link, generator, language, lastBuildDate, atom:link self-reference, image), items (title, description, link, guid permaLink=false, category for each tag, dc:creator, pubDate, media:content for feature_image, content:encoded with full HTML). Many readers depend on these.

Fix: emit a Ghost-shape RSS 2.0 feed with all standard extension namespaces (dc, atom, media, content).

title: ghost-compat: `404.html` fallback file not generated even when error.hbs absent
body:
Static hosts expect `404.html`. We don't emit anything. Even a minimal fallback (static "Page not found") would help.

Fix: always emit `404.html`; use error template if present, else a built-in default.

title: ghost-compat: `Author` model missing `url` consistency — currently set to `/author/<slug>/` but Ghost uses absolute URLs in some contexts
body:
Our `normalizeAuthor` (`src/content/loader.ts:275`) builds the URL via `joinUrl(config.site.url, …)` — that's already absolute. But the `Post.url` (line 311) is also absolute. Themes calling `{{url}}` on relative output and `{{url absolute=true}}` on absolute output may double-prefix. Verify by snapshot.

Fix: store URLs as paths (relative) and let `{{url}}` decide absolute vs relative.

title: ghost-compat: `Post.url`, `Page.url`, `Tag.url`, `Author.url` all baked as absolute strings — Ghost stores them as paths
body:
In Ghost, themes call `{{url}}` (relative) or `{{url absolute="true"}}` (absolute). Our model stores absolute URLs (`joinUrl(site.url, '/post/')`). When a theme calls `{{url}}` without `absolute`, our `url` helper returns the same absolute string anyway — no relative form exists. This breaks self-hosted sites with paths like `/blog/` and breaks `<a href="{{url}}">` patterns that expect a relative.

Fix: store the path internally; let `urlHelper` apply absolute prefix.

title: ghost-compat: `base_path` (subpath hosting) not applied to URLs in Post/Page/Tag/Author models
body:
`config.build.base_path` defaults to `/`. URLs in models are built from `site.url` (`joinUrl(site.url, …)`) without subpath consideration. Subpath sites generate broken links.

Fix: incorporate base_path into all URL builders.

title: ghost-compat: `meta_description` falls back to `excerpt` which doesn't exist on tag/author objects
body:
Same shape issue as meta_title. The chain `meta_description ?? excerpt ?? site.description` lands on site.description for tags. We lose `tag.description`/`author.bio`.

Fix: per-resource fallback chain.

title: ghost-compat: `meta_title` helper inline mode emits empty string instead of site.title for tag pages with no override
body:
On a tag route, `route.kind === 'tag'`. Our `metaTitleHelper` reaches `route.kind !== 'post' && !== 'page'`, then computes `baseTitle = explicit ?? site.title`. `explicit` is `ctx.meta_title` (undefined) or `ctx.title` (undefined for tag context — tag uses `name`). So `explicit = undefined`, `baseTitle = site.title`. But for tag pages we want `tag.name` first.

Fix: when `route.kind === 'tag'` and on the tag context, fall back through `tag.meta_title → tag.name → site.title`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{block}}` and `{{contentFor}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Documented utility helpers: "Used along with `{{contentFor}}` to pass data up and down the template hierarchy." Themes use them to inject head-block content from child templates into `default.hbs`. Absent.

Fix: register handlebars-layouts plugin or hand-roll block/contentFor.

title: ghost-compat: `{{content}}` helper does not gate by `access` (visibility/members)
body:
Ghost's `{{content}}` automatically renders the paywall (truncates content and inserts portal CTA) when a member doesn't have access. Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:5` renders the full HTML unconditionally. For `members`/`paid` posts on a public render, we leak full content — exact opposite of Ghost.

Fix: when `visibility !== 'public'` and no member is signed in, truncate at the paywall marker and insert a paywall snippet (or skip body entirely).

title: ghost-compat: `{{recommendations}}` helper not implemented (Source uses it!)
body:
`src/render/helpers/` has NO `recommendations` registration. Source's `partials/components/post-list.hbs:114` invokes `{{recommendations}}`. Currently the unregistered helper is rendered as `(empty)` by Handlebars — themes show a blank section under an `

Recommendations

` header. Fix: register `recommendations` as a no-op or read from an optional `[recommendations]` config table; gate by `@site.recommendations_enabled`.

title: ghost-compat: dynamic routing taxonomies
body: Tag/author taxonomy paths configurable in routes.yaml; we hard-code /tag/ and /author/.

title: ghost-compat: error.hbs / error-404.hbs templates never rendered
body:
Source ships error templates; Casper has `error.hbs` + `error-404.hbs`. Our `src/build/routes.ts` never emits a `/404.html` or `/error/` route. Most static hosts (Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, Nginx) need a `404.html` file.

Fix: when `error-404.hbs` exists, render and emit to `404.html` with `{ statusCode: 404, message: 'Page not found' }` context.

title: ghost-compat: image_sizes config defines width/height but our helper ignores both
body:
Even if `size="m"` is recognised, the actual width/height (used in Ghost's image API URL) is never extracted. See related img_url issues. Cluster gap; track separately.

Fix: implement Ghost's image-URL rewriting using width.

title: ghost-compat: import-ghost ignores Mobiledoc / Lexical post bodies — drops body silently
body:
`src/ghost/import.ts:203` warns and returns empty when no HTML is present. Most modern Ghost exports include Lexical only. Users without HTML in export get empty posts.

Fix: integrate `@tryghost/kg-lexical-html-renderer` (mentioned in DESIGN.md §3.8). Falling that, render a clear error and instruct user how to re-export with HTML.

title: ghost-compat: navigation items pointing to relative URLs (e.g. `/about/`) not absolutised by `{{navigation}}`
body:
Our impl emits `<a href="${item.url}">` literally. Ghost passes the URL through its internal URL service which prepends subpaths. With `base_path = '/blog'`, our nav items still point at `/about/` (broken).

Fix: in the nav helper, prepend `base_path` when item.url starts with `/`.

title: ghost-compat: paywall card and `{{content}}` paywall truncation not implemented
body:
Ghost splits content at the `<!--kg-card-begin: paywall-->` marker for `members`/`paid` posts; everything after is rendered only for matching members. We render full content always (see separate `{{content}}` gap).

Fix: detect a paywall marker (Markdown convention: `<!--paywall-->`) and gate the trailing content.

title: ghost-compat: posts with future `published_at` are included regardless of status
body:
If frontmatter sets a future date with status:published, the post appears immediately. Ghost waits until that date.

Fix: exclude posts where `new Date(published_at) > Date.now()` unless `[build].include_future_posts = true`.

title: ghost-compat: redirects.yaml support missing
body:
Ghost users migrate via `content/data/redirects.yaml`. We have no redirect mechanism — imports lose their redirect history.

Fix: load `redirects.yaml` (and legacy `redirects.json`); emit a `_redirects` file or per-host static HTML redirects.

title: ghost-compat: routes.yaml support missing entirely
body:
Ghost ships `content/settings/routes.yaml` that defines collections, channels, taxonomies, and custom routes. Themes ship `routes.yaml` examples that customise permalinks (e.g. `/blog/{slug}/`) and add channels (e.g. `/featured/`). Our `src/build/routes.ts` hard-codes the route shape with no configuration surface beyond `posts_per_page`. Themes that rely on a custom permalink pattern won't work.

Fix: add `routes.yaml` loader + planner that supports collections, channels, taxonomies, and `template`/`data` mappings.

title: ghost-compat: scheduled posts (status:scheduled) silently included in build as if published
body:
Our loader only excludes `status === 'draft'`. Posts with `status: scheduled` (Ghost concept for future publication) pass through to the rendered site even before their publish date.

Fix: filter out scheduled posts whose `published_at` is in the future, or treat them like drafts.

title: ghost-compat: visibility 'members' / 'paid' posts treated as 'public' in build
body: We have no auth so we render everything. Either: (a) skip non-public at build (closer to Ghost's free-tier behaviour with paywall preview) or (b) gate behind a toggle.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} block-param does not expose `pagination` as the second block param
body:
Ghost docs explicitly show `{{#get "posts" as |articles pages|}} {{pages.total}}`. Our `getHelper` does pass two block params (`[results, { resource }]`) but the second slot is a stub object with only `resource`, not a real pagination object with `page`, `pages`, `total`, `limit`, `prev`, `next`.

Fix: build a `PaginationInfo` for `{{#get}}` results based on `hash.limit`, `hash.page`, total items.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL does not support `null` checks (`featured:null`, `featured:-null`)
body:
Ghost's `{{recommendations filter="favicon:-null"}}` and post filters with `:null` are common. Our impl treats `null` as a literal string, so `featured:null` would match a post whose featured field is literally the string "null".

Fix: special-case `null` (and `true`/`false`) literals in `fieldMatches`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL does not support comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=)
body:
Ghost NQL allows e.g. `filter="published_at:>2024-01-01"` and `filter="count.posts:>5"`. Our `evaluateClause` in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` only matches exact equality and the negation prefix `-`. The `>`/`<`/`>=`/`<=` operators are not parsed.

Fix: detect the operator after the colon and dispatch to numeric/date comparison.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL fields restricted to a hard-coded switch
body:
`fieldMatches` in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` hard-codes `id`, `slug`, `featured`, `tag`, `tags`, `author`, `authors`, `visibility`, and otherwise does generic string equality. Important fields are missing: `primary_tag`, `primary_author`, `published_at`, `updated_at`, `status`, `type`, `page`, `feature_image`, `tier`. Themes filtering by `primary_tag:news` will silently miss matches because `item['primary_tag']` is an object, not a slug.

Fix: per-field accessors that drill into related objects (e.g. `primary_tag` → `item.primary_tag.slug`).

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL supports only `+` (AND), not `,` (OR)
body:
Ghost's NQL filter supports `,` for OR (e.g. `tag:[news,intro]+featured:true` mixes both). Our `evaluateFilterExpr` in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` splits ONLY on `+`. Top-level `,` outside brackets is treated as part of a value, so `tag:news,tag:intro` is parsed as a single key/value rather than two ORed clauses.

Fix: tokenise the filter respecting bracket nesting, then handle `+` (AND) and `,` (OR).

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} ignores `page` (pagination) attribute
body:
Per Ghost docs, `{{#get "posts" limit="5" page="4"}}` returns items 16-20. Our implementation in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` reads `limit` and `order` but never reads `hash.page`. Themes that page through results (e.g. an "older posts" widget) will always re-render the first page.

Fix: read `hash.page`, slice accordingly, and expose the resulting pagination object to the block-param form `as |posts pagination|`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#has}} missing `count`, `any`, `all`, `count:>N`, `count:<N`, nth pattern
body:
Ghost's `{{#has}}` supports a wide surface: `tag="count:>1"`, `tag="count:1"`, `any="twitter, facebook"`, `all="@labs.x,@labs.y"`, `number="nth:3"`, `index="0"`. Our `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` implementation only handles `tag`, `author`, `visibility`, `slug`, `number` (exact equality) and a catch-all string equality. Themes commonly use `{{#has tag="count:>1"}}` to display multiple-tag UIs, and Source uses the `index`/`number` variants inside foreach.

Fix: parse `count:` prefix, comma list as OR within an attribute, `any=` / `all=` aggregators, `nth:N` modulus and ranges.

title: ghost-compat: {{authors}} does NOT support `separator`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `limit`, `from`, `to`, `visibility`, `autolink`
body:
Per docs, `{{authors}}` mirrors the `{{tags}}` helper. Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:46` only joins by `", "` (hard-coded) and emits raw names. Source uses `{{authors autolink="false" separator=", "}}` in post-card.hbs — those hashes are silently ignored, autolinking is OFF in our impl regardless.

Fix: bring `{{authors}}` to feature parity with `{{tags}}` (which itself has gaps below).

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} uses dayjs but doesn't load locale data per @site.locale
body:
`src/render/helpers/date.ts` imports dayjs and `localizedFormat`, but never imports/loads non-English locale data. Sites set to `de`, `fr`, etc. will still output English month names. Themes calling `{{date format="MMMM"}}` will all say "January".

Fix: dynamically import `dayjs/locale/<locale>.js` based on `site.locale` and call `dayjs.locale(locale)`.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_foot}} does not emit the site-level codeinjection_foot, only the post-level
body:
`src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:54` reads `ctx.codeinjection_foot` (post/page field) but never appends the site's `@site.codeinjection_foot`. On non-post routes (home, tag, author) it emits nothing.

Fix: concatenate `site.codeinjection_foot` + `ctx.codeinjection_foot`.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `<meta property="article:published_time">`, `article:modified_time`, `article:tag`, `article:author`
body:
These are OG article extensions Ghost emits on post pages. Useful for Facebook, LinkedIn, news aggregators. Currently absent in `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts`.

Fix: when `ogType === 'article'`, emit article:* tags from post fields.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `accent_color` CSS variable inline
body:
Ghost themes get `<style>:root { --ghost-accent-color: #xxx; }</style>` injected by `ghost_head` from the site's accent_color setting. Source's `default.hbs` works around this by manually emitting `--background-color: {{@custom.site_background_color}}` but Casper relies on Ghost's auto-injected `--ghost-accent-color`.

Fix: emit `<style>:root{--ghost-accent-color:{{@site.accent_color}}}</style>` from ghost_head.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit a `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml">` for the site feed
body:
Ghost's ghost_head emits `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="…" href="…/rss/">`. Without it, RSS auto-discovery breaks (Reeder, browsers, podcatchers). Our `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:14` doesn't emit anything for RSS.

Fix: add the alternate link tag.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not inject Portal/Members/Search/Sodo-search JavaScript
body:
Ghost's real `ghost_head` injects `<script src="…/portal.min.js" data-i18n="…" data-ghost="…">` and the search SDK. Without these, `data-portal` buttons and `data-ghost-search` triggers in themes do nothing. Our docs note this is out of scope, but at minimum we should emit a comment / log so theme devs aren't confused.

Fix: opt-in `[components.portal]` and `[components.search]` that inject stub or real client scripts.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} does not strip Ghost CDN host before re-injecting size segment
body:
Imported Ghost content keeps absolute `https://CDN/content/images/2024/01/foo.jpg` paths. If a theme calls `{{img_url feature_image size="m"}}` on these, Ghost would rewrite the path to `/content/images/size/w600/2024/01/foo.jpg`. We bail at `if (… !candidate.startsWith('http'))`, returning the absolute URL unchanged. So srcsets containing CDN-hosted images won't be resized.

Fix: parse the URL, identify `/content/images/...` segment, and inject `size/wXXX` regardless of scheme.

title: ghost-compat: {{navigation}} helper does NOT delegate to `partials/navigation.hbs` when present
body:
Ghost docs: "Create `./partials/navigation.hbs` to customize markup." If a theme defines one, Ghost uses it; otherwise default `
    …
`. Our `src/render/helpers/navigation.ts` always emits hard-coded HTML — even when `partials/navigation.hbs` exists. This means Source's navigation customisation (the `is-secondary` styling, etc.) is broken even though the partial file is loaded. Wait — Source actually invokes the partial directly (`{{> "components/navigation"}}`) so it doesn't hit this. BUT casper / many themes rely on the helper delegating. Either way we should match Ghost. Fix: in `navigationHelper`, if `engine.theme.partials['navigation']` exists, render that partial with the navigation items as context.

title: ghost-compat: {{pagination}} helper does NOT delegate to `partials/pagination.hbs`
body:
Ghost docs: theme can override pagination by creating `partials/pagination.hbs`. Our impl in `src/render/helpers/navigation.ts` always emits hard-coded HTML. Source / Casper expect their own pagination partial to be honoured.

Fix: in `paginationHelper`, if `engine.theme.partials['pagination']` exists, render it with the pagination data as context.

title: ghost-compat: {{pagination}} helper missing the `{{page_url}}` helper for use inside pagination partial
body:
Ghost's pagination partial uses `{{page_url next}}`, `{{page_url prev}}`, `{{page_url N}}` to build page links. We never register a `page_url` helper. A custom `partials/pagination.hbs` that uses it will silently produce empty hrefs.

Fix: register a `page_url` helper that takes a number / 'prev' / 'next' and emits the corresponding URL based on `route.data.pagination.baseUrl`.

title: ghost-compat: {{tags}} does not filter internal `hash-` tags by default — Ghost hides them unless `visibility="all"`
body:
Ghost's default is `visibility="public"` which omits internal tags (slug starts with `hash-`). Our impl emits ALL tags. Themes building visible tag lists will leak internal admin tags.

Fix: default to public-only; require `visibility="all"` to include internal.

title: ghost-compat: {{tags}} does not support `prefix`, `suffix`, `limit`, `from`, `to`, `visibility`
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:60` reads only `separator` and `autolink`. Missing the rest. Themes that do `{{tags prefix="Tagged: " limit="3" visibility="public"}}` get no prefix and a full unfiltered list.

Fix: implement the full hash surface.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} doesn't honour theme-translations key fallback (en.json shared keys empty → fallback to literal key)
body:
Source's en.json has empty values (`" (Page %)": ""`). Our code in `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:12` does `active[key] ?? fallback[key] ?? key` — but an empty string is NOT nullish, so the empty value passes through and `{{t " (Page %)"}}` produces `""`. Ghost falls back to the key when the value is empty.

Fix: treat empty string as fallback trigger: `active[key] || fallback[key] || key`.

title: ghost-compat: {{url}} helper doesn't accept a positional argument — only reads `this.url`
body:
`src/render/helpers/urls.ts:17` reads `ctx.url`. Ghost's `{{url}}` accepts a parameter: `{{url '/tag/foo/'}}` and `{{url absolute="true"}}`. Themes that pass an arbitrary string to `{{url}}` will get empty.

Fix: accept first positional arg, fall back to `this.url`.

title: ghost-import: HTML→Markdown lossy on code blocks with language hints
body: turndown drops the language. Configure with `codeBlockStyle: 'fenced'` AND a custom rule for `<pre><code class="language-xxx">`.

title: ghost-import: turndown is not configured for Ghost-specific cards (bookmark, gallery, callout, signup)
body: Ghost cards have specific markup that turndown converts to gibberish or drops. Add per-card converters.

title: i18n: date formatting ignores locale
body: `src/render/helpers/date.ts` always uses default English month names (`'DD MMM YYYY'`). dayjs needs `dayjs/locale/<code>` imported and `.locale()` set per request. Currently a Japanese blog gets "05 May 2026" instead of "2026年5月5日".

title: i18n: only one locale per build — no multi-locale routing
body: `[site].locale` is a single string; helpers `{{t}}` and `{{lang}}` resolve it once. Multilingual blogs need per-post locale tags and `/en/`, `/ja/` URL prefixes with hreflang. Spec: frontmatter `locale: ja`, content tree under `content/<locale>/posts/...`, build emits per-locale route tree with `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="ja" href=...>`.

title: i18n: only one locale per build; no multi-locale routing
body: Many international blogs need `/en/foo/` and `/ja/foo/`. Define a content layout convention (per-locale subdirs) and route accordingly.

title: i18n: word_count and excerpt break on CJK text
body: `src/content/markdown.ts` does `text.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean)` for word counts and the excerpt is `plaintext.slice(0, 200)` characters. Japanese / Chinese / Korean text doesn't use spaces between words, so word_count is wildly off (often 1 for an entire essay) and reading_time follows. Use `Intl.Segmenter` with `granularity: 'word'` keyed off site locale.

title: image-pipeline: feature_image not validated to exist
body: If a post's `feature_image: /content/images/missing.jpg` references a missing file, the build still succeeds and the user gets a broken `` on production. `nectar check` should flag these; build should warn.

title: image-pipeline: no EXIF strip / SVG sanitisation on import
body: Privacy + XSS. Strip EXIF; sanitise SVGs via svgo or DOMPurify.

title: image-pipeline: no SVG sanitisation
body: User-supplied SVG (avatars, cover images) can carry inline `<script>` or `<foreignObject>` with JavaScript. Run all SVG content assets through DOMPurify (or `svgo --pretty --multipass` + a script stripper) on copy. Same for any SVG referenced from frontmatter.

title: image-pipeline: no WebP / AVIF transcoding
body: Ghost themes emit `<picture>` elements expecting WebP+JPEG/AVIF+JPEG fallback. We pass through whatever was uploaded, so a JPEG-heavy blog stays JPEG-heavy. Add per-format variants behind `[components.images]` config, keyed by mtime+hash for caching.

title: image-pipeline: no WebP/AVIF output
body: Modern formats save 30-50% bytes. Emit when source is jpg/png.

title: infra: bun.lock not pinned to integrity-checked SHA per dep
body: Confirm `bun install --frozen-lockfile` in CI.

title: infra: no CHANGELOG.md
body: Generate from conventional commits via release-please.

title: infra: no Renovate / Dependabot configuration
body: Add `renovate.json` or `.github/dependabot.yml`.

title: infra: no release automation
body: Use release-please / semantic-release. Tag → GitHub Release → npm publish + binary upload.

title: infra: no single-file binary build
body: Use `bun build --compile` to produce `nectar` binary per platform.

title: infra: package is `private: true` — cannot npm publish today
body: Flip when ready and add `files`, `bin`, `publishConfig`.

title: infra: tsconfig has no `~/` path alias declaration
body: Source code uses `~/config/schema.ts` etc. but `tsconfig.json` may rely on bun-only path resolution. Verify `paths` is declared so `tsc --noEmit` works and IDE tooling resolves correctly. If not, add `paths: { "~/*": ["./src/*"] }`. This also matters for downstream consumers of types.

title: members: context: `@member.paid` / `@member.comped` / `@member.tier` need typed stubs
body:
Several Ghost themes (including Source's `post-list.hbs:104`) do `{{#unless @member.paid}}`. With `@member = undefined`, that resolves truthy (since `undefined.paid` → undefined → falsy in `unless`) — accidentally correct. But `{{@member.subscriptions.length}}` returns NaN strings in some themes.

Fix: document explicitly in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` that:
- `@member` is always undefined in static builds
- Nested access returns undefined (Handlebars-safe)
- `subscriptions`, `tiers`, `email`, `name`, `uuid`, `firstname`, `paid`, `comped`, `status` are all undefined

Add a render-time warning when a template reads `@member.*` so authors know they're hitting dead context.

title: members: context: `@member` undefined is correct but inconsistent — should be a proxy that swallows any property access
members: context: `@site.paid_members_enabled` and Stripe-keyed defaults
body: Ghost themes branch on `@site.paid_members_enabled` (Source: `post-list.hbs:103`) to decide whether to show 'Upgrade' button. In Nectar this should default `false` unless the user opts into a paid-tier-capable provider (Buttondown paid, Beehiiv premium, Substack paid). Add to SiteData + derive from provider config.

title: members: context: `@site.recommendations_enabled` flag and recommendations source
body: Source theme's `post-list.hbs:111` gates the sidebar recommendations block on `@site.recommendations_enabled`. Without it set, no recommendations appear (this happens to be the safe default — but the corresponding `{{recommendations}}` helper is unimplemented and would crash if reached). Add `site.recommendations_enabled` flag + a `[[recommendations]]` array in `nectar.toml` (see separate recommendations issues below).

title: members: error reporting — silent no-op when portal config is malformed
body: If `[components.portal] provider = "buttondown"` is set but `publication` is missing, the build currently passes (because we don't validate). Make portal config a zod sub-schema with `superRefine` requiring provider-specific fields. Fail the build with a precise message.

title: members: example theme should keep portal markup working in default `nectar build`
body: Right now `example/themes/source` is shipped with portal markup but no portal provider config in `example/nectar.toml`. Running `nectar build` produces a site with dead `data-portal` buttons — a bad showcase. Add a sensible `[components.portal] provider = "none"` (so subscribe UI is stripped) OR `provider = "buttondown"` with a placeholder publication for the example.

title: members: portal: no Beehiiv adapter
body:
Beehiiv exposes `https://embeds.beehiiv.com/<pub-id>?slim=true` as an iframe and `https://api.beehiiv.com/v2/publications/<pub-id>/subscriptions` as POST endpoint. Add adapter that:
- Rewrites `data-members-form` to POST to a Beehiiv-compatible form-handler URL (CORS-safe via Beehiiv's hosted endpoint)
- Replaces `data-portal="signup"` button clicks with opening the Beehiiv embed in a modal (or new window)
- Documents that paid tiers, account management, and member-only content gating are unsupported on the static side

title: members: portal: no Buttondown adapter
body:
Buttondown is the most common Ghost alternative for indie newsletters. Document and implement a first-class adapter:
- `<form action="https://buttondown.email/api/emails/embed-subscribe/<user>">` for the inline form (`email-subscription.hbs` shape)
- Replace `data-members-form` with the Buttondown form action; convert `data-members-email` input name to `email`
- Optional: add hidden `tag` field from `@site.title` or post frontmatter

Keep the visual markup of `partials/email-subscription.hbs` intact — only the form's `action` and field names change at build time.

title: members: portal: no SSR-time removal mode (`provider = "none"`)
body:
When a user has no newsletter, they should be able to strip all signup UI from the build instead of leaving dead buttons. Add `[components.portal] provider = "none"` (or omit the section entirely) that strips elements matching `[data-portal]`, `[data-members-form]`, `[data-members-email]`, plus their wrapping `gh-footer-signup` / `gh-cta` ancestor sections.

Gate by a CSS-selector list configurable in `nectar.toml`.

title: members: portal: no `provider = "custom"` escape hatch with raw form action / field map
body:
Power users will want full control:
```toml
[components.portal]
provider = "custom"
form_action = "https://example.com/subscribe"
email_field_name = "EMAIL"
hidden_fields = { source = "nectar", list_id = "abc123" }
success_text = "Check your inbox to confirm"
```
Impl: the build-time transform reads these and rewrites `<form data-members-form>` with `method="POST"` and the relevant fields. Output success_text near `data-members-error`.

title: members: tiers: no `{{price}}` helper
migration: no URL rewriting from Ghost paths
body: After downloading images, internal links inside post HTML (e.g. `<a href="/my-ghost-site.com/old-slug">`) still point at the original domain. Add a rewrite pass keyed off the importer's `--source-url=https://oldblog.com` flag.

title: migration: no WordPress importer
body: Beyond Ghost, the second-largest migration source is WordPress (WXR XML export). A `nectar import-wordpress export.xml` would 10x our addressable user base. Vendor `xml2js` + a converter that produces the same content layout.

title: migration: no preview/dry-run for importer
body: Add `nectar import-ghost --dry-run` that prints a summary table (posts found, drafts skipped, lexical bodies skipped) without writing files. Critical for users with thousand-post exports — they want to know what will land before it does.

title: migration: no redirect map output
body: Even with same slug structure, Ghost users have redirect chains (custom redirects from the admin UI). Read `/content/data/redirects.json` if present in the export bundle and emit `_redirects` (Netlify), `vercel.json`, and `nginx.conf` snippets. Also map any post-slug changes.

title: migration: no tests against realistic Ghost export fixtures
body: There are no fixtures for `import.ts` (e.g. a captured anonymised export from a real 100-post blog). Add `tests/fixtures/ghost-exports/{small,medium,large}.json` + a golden snapshot of the produced markdown tree.

title: newsletter: no concept of email-only post vs publish+email post
body: Ghost posts have an `email_only` flag and an `email` object (subject, segment, replyTo). When `email_only=true` the post does not appear on the web — only goes out via email. We have no flag. Add `email_only: boolean` to Post frontmatter; when true, *do not* emit a public route. Emit instead a stub `/email-only/<slug>` only when `[build].emit_email_only_stub = true`.

title: observability: build output is not deterministic / reproducible
body: `loadMarkdownDir` uses `Bun.Glob.scan` whose order is filesystem-dependent. `theme.assets` is a Map keyed by insertion order. This makes the byte-identical reproducible-build claim impossible. Sort all glob results and Map iterations explicitly. Verify by running `bun run build` twice and `diff -r` the output.

title: observability: no per-route build-time reporting
body: `build()` returns `{ outputDir, routeCount, assetCount }` — no timing info. Track wall-clock per phase (load, plan, render, asset copy, feed emit) and per-route render time. Surface a `--profile` flag that writes `dist/.nectar-build-stats.json`. Indispensable when troubleshooting why a 1000-post blog takes 90s.

title: packaging: binaries not code-signed (macOS gatekeeper, Windows SmartScreen)
body: An unsigned `nectar` binary on macOS Sonoma will be quarantined and Windows will throw SmartScreen warnings. Add Apple Developer ID + notarytool steps for darwin builds and an Authenticode signing step for windows builds. Without this, the first-run UX is broken for the exact demographic (non-developer bloggers) we are trying to win from Ghost.

title: packaging: no SemVer policy documented
body: We are at 0.0.1 with breaking changes likely. Publish a `VERSIONING.md` clarifying what counts as breaking (CLI flags, config keys, helper output, plugin API), and pin theme-compatibility guarantees to a major. Migrators need to know whether to pin `nectar@0` vs `nectar@^1`.

title: packaging: no `exports` field — programmatic API unreachable
body: We have a `build()` function in `src/build/pipeline.ts` but no `exports` map, so downstream code that wants to embed Nectar (e.g. a Cloudflare Pages plugin, a Vite integration) cannot import `nectar/build` cleanly. Define `exports` for the CLI entry, the programmatic build entry, and `nectar/types` so plugin authors can type their hooks.

title: packaging: no checksum/sigsum file alongside binary releases
body: Release binaries need a `SHASUMS256.txt` (and ideally `.minisig` or cosign signature) so package managers (brew, scoop, nix) can verify what they downloaded. Add to release.yml.

title: packaging: no npm provenance / SLSA attestation
body: Add `npm publish --provenance` from a GitHub Actions OIDC token to give downstream supply-chain consumers cryptographic proof of the build. Pair with `actions/attest-build-provenance` for the binary artifacts. This is now the default expectation for new packages.

title: packaging: no published Docker image
body: A Ghost user who currently runs `ghost/ghost:latest` will look for `nectar/nectar:latest`. Ship a multi-arch Dockerfile (alpine + oven/bun base) that exposes `nectar build` as ENTRYPOINT and add a release job that pushes to ghcr.io and Docker Hub on tag.

title: perf: assets: no on-disk hash cache — rehash every build
body:
loadThemeAssets hashes every asset on every build, even when nothing changed. Hashing a 100MB asset tree is multiple seconds of pure CPU + I/O that recurs on every `nectar build`.

Fix: persist a `.nectar-cache/asset-hashes.json` keyed by (mtimeMs, size). If the (path, mtime, size) tuple matches the cache, reuse the hash. Invalidate on theme version bump. Expected near-instant asset stage on incremental rebuilds.

title: perf: assets: serial readFile + hash per asset
body:
src/theme/assets.ts loadThemeAssets awaits readFile and sha1Short per file in a sequential `for await` loop. With 500+ theme assets (a typical Ghost theme has images, fonts, CSS, JS, sourcemaps), this is hundreds of serial round-trips.

Fix: collect paths via glob, then Promise.all(paths.map(processAsset)) with a concurrency limit. 5-10x speedup on asset stage.

title: perf: assets: sha1 hash reads entire file into RAM
body:
src/theme/assets.ts sha1Short does `await readFile(file)` returning a full Buffer before hashing. For a 40MB image this materialises 40MB in heap per file, repeated for every asset. Memory spikes and OOMs on themes with many large assets.

Fix: stream the file through Bun.CryptoHasher with `Bun.file(file).stream()` piped through the hasher (`hash.update` on each chunk), or use `Bun.hash(Bun.file(file))` if available. Constant memory regardless of file size.

title: perf: build: RSS embeds full post.html — feed bloat
body:
src/build/feeds.ts:36 emits `<content:encoded><![CDATA[${post.html}]]></content:encoded>` for every item. With 10k items × avg 30KB html = 300MB single feed. Ghost paginates RSS by 25 items for exactly this reason.

Fix: cap items at config.components.rss.items (default 20 already exists — good), and add a `full_content` flag defaulting to false so the feed contains excerpt only. Optionally paginate into rss-1.xml, rss-2.xml via `<atom:link rel=next>`.

title: perf: build: rm output dir is destructive and slow on large dist
body:
pipeline.ts:26 `await rm(outputDir, { recursive: true, force: true })` deletes the entire dist before every build. For a 10k-route dist with ~50MB of assets this is multiple seconds of unlink syscalls. And it churns SSD write cycles.

Fix: don't rm; instead, write new files (idempotent), and at the end of the build delete only files in dist that are not in the planned-route + asset manifest (set difference). Tied to incremental-build issue.

title: perf: build: sitemap not split at 50k URL limit
body:
src/build/feeds.ts emitSitemap writes a single sitemap.xml with all URLs. Google's spec caps sitemaps at 50,000 URLs / 50MB uncompressed; sitemaps beyond that are silently truncated or rejected.

Fix: when urls.length > 50000, emit sitemap-1.xml, sitemap-2.xml, ... and a sitemap-index.xml pointing to them. Also enable gzipped variants. Required for any blog with 50k+ posts/tags/authors.

title: perf: emit/copyContentAssets: serial copyFile, no skip-unchanged
body:
src/build/emit.ts copyContentAssets (lines 35-52) awaits copyFile in series for every content image. A photo blog with 5000 images × 10ms = 50s. And it overwrites every file every build, defeating any downstream CDN's Last-Modified-based caching.

Fix: (1) Promise.all with concurrency limiter (32-64). (2) Skip copy when destination exists with matching size+mtime. (3) Use Bun.file().write() which is faster than fs.copyFile in Bun.

title: perf: emit: copies fingerprinted AND logical asset (2× disk write)
body:
src/build/emit.ts emitAsset (lines 28-32) copies the same source twice — once to the fingerprinted path and once to the logical path — to support templates that reference either form. This doubles disk writes, doubles output size, and confuses caching (logical URL is served without long cache-control headers in practice).

Fix: emit only the fingerprinted file. For logical references, either (a) generate an HTTP redirect file / `_redirects`, (b) symlink, or (c) resolve all logical references at build time so the logical copy is never needed. Cuts asset-stage time and dist size by ~50%.

title: perf: ghost-import: serial writeFile per post
body:
import.ts loop awaits writeFile + ensureDir per post (lines 156-157). For 50k posts × ~3ms = 150s wall time.

Fix: Promise.all in batches of 256. Pre-create the two destination dirs once outside the loop.

title: perf: ghost-import: turndown runs serially in-thread
body:
Each post's html is converted to Markdown via TurndownService synchronously. For 50k posts with avg 5KB html, turndown takes ~5-10ms each → 5-8 minutes total.

Fix: shard via worker_threads. turndown is dependency-isolated, can run cleanly per worker.

title: perf: loader: full content graph held in RAM
body:
ContentGraph holds posts[], pages[], tags[], authors[], plus bySlug maps — and each Post has full .html and .plaintext strings. For 10k posts × avg 50KB html = 500MB RSS minimum. With paginated index pages that hold references to posts[], GC can't reclaim until build ends.

Fix: (a) lazy-load post.html — only when route.template === 'post'. Index/tag/author pages need only the card-relevant fields (title, slug, excerpt, feature_image, dates, primary_tag, authors). (b) For scale, stream posts through the route planner instead of buffering all in memory.

title: perf: no Bun.Glob concurrency / async iteration is single-flight
body:
Every loader uses `for await (const rel of glob.scan(...))` which yields paths one at a time and the body awaits per iteration. The glob walk itself isn't parallelised against the per-file work.

Fix: collect all paths into an array first (`const paths = await Array.fromAsync(glob.scan(...))`), then process with Promise.all + concurrency limit. Decouples directory walk from file processing.

title: perf: output: <script src=source.js> blocks rendering — no defer/async/module
body:
example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html:363 emits a plain `<script src=...></script>` with no `defer`, `async`, or `type=module`. Even though it's near the end of body, it's still parser-blocking from the moment the parser encounters it. JS payload is ~52KB.

Fix: theme should output `<script src=... defer></script>`. Better still: emit `type=module` for an ESM bundle so it's deferred by spec. Cheap INP/TBT win.

title: perf: output: CSS not critical-path inlined
body:
Generated HTML (example/dist/index.html line 24) loads `<link rel=stylesheet href=/assets/built/screen.css>` as a render-blocking external request. screen.css is 54KB. LCP element renders after this round trip. Modern best practice: inline the above-the-fold CSS (~14KB compressed) and async-load the rest.

Fix: integrate critical-css extraction (lightningcss / critters / postcss-extract-critical) into copyAssets. Emit per-template critical CSS as `<style>` in <head> and `<link rel=stylesheet media=print onload='this.media=...' >` for the rest.

title: perf: output: JS preloaded AND <script src> — redundant fetch hint
body:
Generated HTML emits `<link rel=preload as=script href=source.js>` (line 10) AND later `<script src=source.js>` (line 363). Modern browsers handle the <script> tag with the same priority; the preload is at best redundant, at worst it causes a double-fetch on some browsers when the URLs differ slightly (e.g. trailing slash) or breaks integrity if SRI is added later.

Fix: drop the `<link rel=preload as=script>` for the deferred main bundle, and add `defer` to the <script> tag at the bottom. The script is below </body>-adjacent so its execution is already late; defer just guarantees it.

title: perf: output: inline color-contrast script runs synchronously in <head>
body:
default.hbs lines 27-44 inject an inline script in <head> that calls getComputedStyle + writes documentElement.className. This blocks the parser before stylesheet has applied. The work is also unnecessary at runtime — it should be done at build time when the background color is known.

Fix: compute `has-dark-text` / `has-light-text` at build time from config.site.accent_color / @custom.site_background_color and emit it directly on <body class>. Removes a ~3KB inline script + the FOUC risk.

title: perf: output: no <link rel=preconnect> to image CDN / cross-origin sources
body:
When post.feature_image points to an external host (e.g. Ghost Images CDN, Cloudinary), there's no preconnect hint emitted. Cold DNS + TLS handshake adds 100-300ms before the image fetch even starts.

Fix: at build time detect external image hosts (origin != site.url) and emit `<link rel=preconnect crossorigin href=...>` per unique origin in the page (cap at 3).

title: perf: output: no AVIF/WebP fallback chain via <picture>
body: img_url helper accepts `format=webp` (used in post-card.hbs) but no transform actually emits a .webp file. Even if it did, modern best practice is ``. AVIF saves 30-50% vs WebP at same quality. Fix: pipeline emits avif+webp+jpeg variants per image_sizes entry; theme uses . Tied to image-resize implementation.

title: perf: output: no Cache-Control / immutable headers on fingerprinted assets
body:
Fingerprinted assets are content-addressed but the generator emits no hosting hint that they should be served with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`. Most hosts default to short TTLs.

Fix: emit a `_headers` (Netlify/CF Pages) and `nginx.conf` snippet and `vercel.json` example documenting the immutable strategy for /assets/*-<hash>.* paths and short TTLs for /index.html.

title: perf: output: no precompressed .br/.gz emitted
body:
Static HTML/CSS/JS can be pre-Brotli'd at build time so the host serves the .br file directly (most CDNs support this). Currently nothing.

Fix: in copyAssets/writeHtml, also emit `.br` (Brotli quality 11) and `.gz` companions for *.html/*.css/*.js/*.svg/*.xml. Gated on config.build.precompress (default true).

title: perf: output: srcset emits same URL 5-6 times for unresized images
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html shows srcset='/content/images/welcome-cover.svg 320w, ... 2000w' — same URL six times. Browser picks 2000w and downloads the full file regardless of viewport. This is wasted bytes for every on every page. Fix: actually generate the resized variants (see img_url issue). If the source is SVG (vector), skip the srcset entirely and emit a single `` since SVG scales losslessly.

title: perf: pipeline: writeHtml awaited per route — serial fsync
body:
pipeline.ts:39 awaits writeHtml inline. mkdir is recursive but called per route via ensureDir (emit.ts:8) — for routes in same directory this is wasted syscalls.

Fix: batch writes by directory, mkdir once per directory, then Promise.all the writeFile within. Or pre-create the directory tree once after planRoutes (we know all outputPaths upfront).

title: perf: scale: chunked rendering required for >1k routes
body:
Holding all rendered HTML strings in memory while the loop runs is fine for small sites but at 10k × 50KB = 500MB extra peak. With the proposed Promise.all batching, we must cap concurrency or memory explodes.

Fix: pipeline must consume routes in batches of e.g. 500 — render batch, write batch, release html strings, GC, then next batch. Or stream HTML straight to disk without ever holding it.

title: perf: scale: sitemap.xml >50k URLs requires split index
body:
Companion to the feeds split issue. At 10k posts + 100k tags + paginated subpages, total URLs easily exceed 50k. Current emit creates one file.

Fix: see feeds split issue. Also gzip the sitemaps and reference .xml.gz from sitemap-index.xml — Google supports it natively.

title: performance: Markdown rendering is sequential
body: Parallelise content load via Promise.all over file globs.

title: performance: no content cache between builds
body: Markdown rendering is re-done each build even when the .md file hasn't changed. Cache parsed `{ html, plaintext, word_count, reading_time }` keyed by file mtime + content sha. Storage: `.nectar/cache/<hash>.json` (add to `.gitignore`).

title: performance: no incremental builds
body: `build()` deletes `dist/` and rewrites everything every time. For a 500-post blog this is 5-10 seconds even on M2. Track per-post mtime + theme-fingerprint and only re-render affected routes. Pre-condition for a usable `nectar dev`.

title: performance: rendering is sequential, not parallel
body: `build()` loops `for (const route of routes) engine.render(route)` synchronously. Each render is CPU-bound (Handlebars). For a 1000-post blog this is the wall-clock bottleneck. Either run a Bun worker pool (one per CPU) sharing the compiled templates, or batch routes with `Promise.all` (helpers are pure once content is loaded).

title: render: `get` helper filter DSL is a tiny subset of NQL
body: Ghost uses NQL (https://ghost.org/docs/content-api/#filtering). We support `key:value`, `key:-value`, `key:[a,b]` and `+` AND. Missing: `,` OR, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, `~`, `~^`, `~$`, quoted strings, escapes, parens, `null`. Implement NQL grammar properly.

title: render: `ghost_head` JSON-LD `Article` schema missing required fields
body: Google validator requires `mainEntityOfPage`, `publisher.logo.width/height`, `image` as object with width/height. Our payload is missing those. Use https://search.google.com/test/rich-results to verify and fix.

title: render: `ghost_head` does not emit RSS feed discovery link
body: `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" href="/rss.xml">` is missing. Browser RSS detection and feed-reader autodiscovery require it.

title: render: `ghost_head` does not emit pagination rel=prev/rel=next
body: On a paginated index, `<link rel="prev" href="...">` and `<link rel="next" href="...">` improve SEO. Emit them from ghost_head when route.data.pagination exists.

title: render: `has` helper supports only first matched hash key; multi-key should AND not bail
body: helpers/blocks.ts:hasHelper loops keys and `break`s on first match. Ghost's behaviour for `{{#has tag="x" author="y"}}` is an OR of provided constraints (default). With `any:` / `all:` prefixes it changes. Implement Ghost's semantics: parse `key:value` pairs from a single `match=` argument and honour `count:>N` and `slug:[a,b]`.

title: render: `is` helper does not recognise multi-target with space (Ghost accepts both ',' and space)
body: Ghost themes write `{{#is "post page"}}` and `{{#is "post, page"}}`. We split on `,` only. Split on `[,\s]+`.

title: render: `match` helper does not coerce string "true"/"false" booleans
body: `{{#match @custom.background_image "true"}}` — @custom comes through as boolean from package.json, but theme writes the string. Implement implicit string<->bool comparison.

title: render: `navigation` helper renders bespoke HTML even if a `navigation.hbs` partial exists
body: Source theme provides `partials/components/navigation.hbs`. When the theme has a navigation partial, prefer rendering that partial; only fall back to our minimal output when missing.

title: render: `pagination` helper renders bespoke HTML; should defer to partials/pagination.hbs when present
body: Same as navigation — Source's theme provides one. Use it when available.

title: render: missing helpers — `cancel_link`, `comment_count`, `member_count`, `signup`, `tiers`, `price`
body: Themes that use members features call these. We don't implement them. Either stub safely (return empty) so themes don't crash, or implement.

title: render: template-as-partial registration collides with same-named partial
body: engine.ts:registerPartials registers every template (index, post, page, ...) under its bare name as a partial. A theme with a partial named `index` (legit Ghost theme pattern) would silently overwrite or be overwritten by the template registration. Namespace partials under a separate prefix and resolve `{{> name}}` accordingly.

title: search: Pagefind integration not wired
body: DESIGN.md lists `[components.search] type = "pagefind"` but there is no implementation. Add a post-build step that shells out to `npx pagefind --source dist` and copies the resulting `pagefind/` bundle. Provide a `{{> search}}` partial.

title: search: `data-ghost-search` button has no runtime
body:
Source theme's `search-toggle.hbs` and `header-content.hbs:75` use `data-ghost-search`. Ghost ships sodo-search (Ember-based modal). Nectar has no search runtime — clicks do nothing. Already filed as `search: no Pagefind integration` (P1) but doesn't address the `data-ghost-search` selector specifically.

Fix: when `[components.search]` is configured, automatically inject a tiny JS shim that:
1. Locates `[data-ghost-search]` triggers
2. Opens a modal with Pagefind/Algolia/Meilisearch UI
3. Wires keyboard shortcut (cmd+K / ctrl+K)

title: search: index excludes `visibility != public` posts by default
body: Member/paid posts should not leak into client-side search indexes (Pagefind indexes everything in dist/). Add `<meta name="pagefind-skip">` automatically on routes whose source post has non-public visibility; document Algolia/Meili equivalents.

title: search: no Pagefind integration
body: Wire as an optional component that runs after emit and writes `dist/pagefind/`.

title: security: Bun.Glob scans for content/assets follow symlinks by default
body: Same root cause as the theme issue: src/content/loader.ts:157 (content), src/build/emit.ts:38 (content/images), and src/theme/assets.ts:10 use `new Bun.Glob('**/*').scan(...)` with no symlink filtering. A malicious contributor PR adding `content/posts/oops.md -> /home/runner/.npmrc` causes the build to read and (in the case of assets) ship secrets into the published `dist/`. Mitigation: explicitly opt out of symlinks via Bun.Glob options or by `lstatSync`+`isSymbolicLink()` filter; document that content directories must contain only regular files.

title: security: Ghost import does no size or shape validation before JSON.parse (DoS / OOM)
body: src/ghost/import.ts:91-92 reads the entire export file into a string and JSON.parses it with no streaming and no size cap. A user pointed at a multi-GB Ghost export (legit large blogs or maliciously inflated JSON via repeated keys / deeply nested arrays) blows process memory and may crash the host. JSON.parse also exhibits worst-case quadratic behaviour on certain inputs. Mitigation: stat the file first and refuse > some configurable cap (e.g. 256 MB default); consider a streaming JSON parser (stream-json) for very large exports; document max size.

title: security: `feature_image_caption` is rendered raw via Handlebars triple-stash in typical Ghost themes
body: Ghost stores feature_image_caption as HTML. The example theme and most Ghost-derived themes render it with `{{{feature_image_caption}}}`. src/content/loader.ts:204 reads it as a string from frontmatter with no sanitisation. Threat: contributor PR adds `feature_image_caption: "<script>…</script>"` and ships persistent XSS. Mitigation: sanitise this field at load time, or document that themes must escape it.

title: security: example theme inserts user-controlled `@custom.site_background_color` into a `<style>` block (CSS injection / breakout)
body: example/themes/source/default.hbs:23 contains `--background-color: {{@custom.site_background_color}}`. Inside a `<style>` block, Handlebars' HTML-escape does not protect against CSS-context attacks like `red; } body { display: none } @import url('//evil.tld/x.css'); /*`. A user can land that string in their nectar.toml `[theme.custom]` table, or it can come from a malicious theme's defaults (see separate finding). Mitigation: in the helper resolving `@custom.color`, validate against `^#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}$` or a `color()` whitelist; never inline arbitrary strings into `<style>`.

title: security: import-ghost preserves `codeinjection_head/foot` verbatim — no review prompt
body: src/ghost/import.ts:152-153 round-trips `codeinjection_head`/`codeinjection_foot` from the Ghost export straight into frontmatter. Users importing exports from blogs they no longer control (sold, taken over) silently inherit attacker scripts. Mitigation: by default *omit* code injection fields and emit a summary like `Skipped code injection in 7 posts. Re-run with --keep-code-injection to import them.`

title: security: import-ghost trusts post.html for turndown but inlines user `feature_image` / `*_image` URLs into frontmatter without scheme check
body: src/ghost/import.ts:136-152 forwards `feature_image`, `og_image`, `twitter_image`, `profile_image`, `cover_image` strings into `JSON.stringify`-quoted frontmatter. A compromised export can set these to `javascript:alert(1)` or `data:text/html,`, which later flow through the templates into `` / ``. Mitigation: validate that the URL is http(s):// (or a relative path) before writing it to frontmatter; refuse `javascript:`/`data:`/`file:` and log a warning.

title: security: locale JSON files are loaded with no schema validation; values used in HTML via triple-stash
body: src/theme/loader.ts:80-99 globs `locales/*.json`, JSON.parses each, and stores the string map. The `t` helper (src/render/helpers/i18n.ts) returns the raw string, and themes frequently use `{{{t "..."}}}` (e.g. example theme's footer.hbs:16 contains a triple-stash `t` with literal `<a>` markup, and post-card.hbs:39 uses `{{{t "By {authors}"}}}`). A malicious or compromised locale file can inject `<script>` into every page. Mitigation: by default escape `t` output, expose a `t-safe` variant for trusted markup; validate locale JSON shape (string keys -> string values, length cap) and warn on suspicious tokens.

title: security: marked GFM tables / autolink regexes have a history of ReDoS — pin marked and track CVEs
body: package.json pins `marked: ^14.1.4`. Marked has had multiple ReDoS CVEs (e.g. CVE-2022-21680, CVE-2022-21681 in older majors; v14 is current but new ones land regularly). With no Renovate / Dependabot config in repo, transitive patch versions only update at human pace. Mitigation: enable Dependabot or Renovate, run `bun pm scan` (after configuring a scanner) in CI, and add a SECURITY.md describing the disclosure policy.

title: security: navigation labels and URLs are inserted into HTML with only HTML-text escaping (no URL-scheme validation)
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:14-16 builds `…` from config-supplied `navigation[*].url`. `escapeAttr` HTML-encodes quotes but does *not* reject `javascript:` URLs. A config that contains `url = "javascript:alert(document.cookie)"` produces a clickable XSS link on every page. Same pattern in pagination links, `link` helper. Mitigation: validate that `url` parses as a relative path or has scheme http(s) / mailto; refuse `javascript:` / `data:` / `vbscript:`.

title: security: no CI at all — no automated lint/test/audit on PRs
body: Confirmed via `ls .github/` (file missing). The Biome `check`, `tsc --noEmit`, and `bun test` scripts in package.json are never run automatically. A malicious PR can land with broken types or smuggled dependencies if a maintainer merges quickly. Mitigation: add `.github/workflows/ci.yml` running typecheck + biome + tests + `bun pm scan` on PR.

title: security: no Content-Security-Policy guidance for hosted sites
body: README.md does not document recommended HTTP response headers for hosting. Given that the engine emits raw HTML from Markdown plus `codeinjection_*` plus theme-controlled inline scripts, a strict CSP is essential. Without guidance, users deploy to Netlify/Vercel/GitHub Pages with no CSP, no Referrer-Policy, no Permissions-Policy. Mitigation: ship a `docs/security/hosting.md` with `_headers` / `netlify.toml` / `vercel.json` snippets enabling: `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' …`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000`, `Permissions-Policy: interest-cohort=()`, etc.

title: security: no Dependabot / Renovate / scheduled `bun pm scan` configured
body: There is no .github/dependabot.yml, no renovate.json, and `bun pm scan` requires a configured scanner (`[install.security] scanner = ...` in bunfig.toml) which is also absent. Dependencies will silently age; new CVEs in handlebars / marked / gray-matter / turndown will go unnoticed. Mitigation: add Dependabot for the bun/npm ecosystem, configure a scanner package (e.g. `@socketsecurity/scanner` or similar) in bunfig.toml, and run `bun pm scan` in CI on pushes to main and on PRs.

title: security: no SECURITY.md / vulnerability disclosure policy
body: Repo lacks a SECURITY.md, a `security@` contact, and any private disclosure channel. External researchers have nowhere to report issues responsibly. Mitigation: add SECURITY.md per https://github.com/github/SECURITY.md template; enable GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting; list supported versions and triage SLAs.

title: security: no documentation of trust boundaries (who can supply Markdown / themes / config)
body: README.md focuses on features. There is no documented threat model. A blog operator accepting outside-contributor PRs needs to know which Markdown frontmatter fields are dangerous (`codeinjection_*`, `feature_image_caption`, `slug`), which config fields are dangerous (`site.url`, `theme.custom.*`), and that themes are essentially code. Mitigation: add `docs/security/threat-model.md` describing each surface, the trust level it expects, and the mitigations applied (or recommendations like 'review PR diffs for `codeinjection_*` and raw `<script>`'). Cross-link from README.

title: security: scheduled posts are not filtered, so future-dated content is published silently
body: src/content/loader.ts:30 skips only `status === 'draft'`. A post with `status: scheduled` (the type union allows it on line 110) is treated as published and rendered. Threat: an author who intends to schedule a post for a later date discovers it is already public. This is also an information-disclosure path (embargoed announcements leak via RSS / sitemap). Mitigation: skip scheduled until `published_at <= now`, and document the date semantics.

title: security: theme `package.json` is JSON.parsed with no schema validation; custom defaults flow into HTML
body: src/theme/pkg.ts:27 does `JSON.parse(raw) as RawPkg` with no zod schema. Whatever the theme author put under `config.custom.<key>.default` is propagated through `engine.theme.pkg.customDefaults` into `buildCustom()` (src/render/engine.ts:141-146) and reachable from templates as `{{@custom.foo}}`. Many themes (including the bundled `source` theme) interpolate `@custom.*` into `<style>` blocks and inline scripts (see default.hbs:23 `--background-color: {{@custom.site_background_color}}`). If the theme ships a default like `"site_background_color": "#fff; } body { display: none; } /*"` or, worse, `"</style><script>alert(1)</script>"`, the user's `<style>` block is broken out of.
