Mitigation: validate theme package.json with zod (whitelist types text|color|select|boolean|image; coerce defaults to safe values), and when the theme inserts `@custom.*` into HTML contexts the engine should HTML-encode by default (recommend triple-stash only for explicitly-safe values).

title: security: theme partials path traversal via crafted `partials/` filenames is unbounded
body: src/theme/loader.ts:37-48 globs `**/*.hbs` under the theme dir and registers everything under `partials/` as a partial whose name is the relative path. There is no follow-symlink check on `Bun.Glob.scan`. An untrusted theme could ship a symlink at `partials/foo.hbs -> /etc/passwd` (or any sensitive file on the build host) which `readFile` would then slurp and register; depending on how it is referenced, the contents land in the output HTML. Same hazard applies to the templates scan and to `loadThemeAssets` (src/theme/assets.ts:11). Mitigation: pass `followSymlinks: false` (Bun.Glob default is currently true per docs — verify), or stat each entry with `lstatSync` and reject symlinks, or refuse paths whose resolved real path leaves the theme root.

title: seo: JSON-LD Article schema violations
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html line 64 JSON-LD: (a) `image` is a relative URL — schema.org/ImageObject requires an absolute URL; (b) `author[*].url` and `publisher.logo.url` are okay, but `image` should be an ImageObject with width/height/url; (c) `dateModified` equals `datePublished` even though the source has no updated_at — Google may treat the article as never-updated; (d) no `mainEntityOfPage`. Verify at https://validator.schema.org/. Fix: in ghost-head.ts buildJsonLd, run image through absoluteUrl, expand to ImageObject, only emit dateModified when post.updated_at differs from published_at, and add mainEntityOfPage = canonical.

title: seo: No <link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml"> for feed discovery
body: None of the pages emit `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" href="/rss.xml" title="Nectar Example">` in <head>. Browsers and feed readers won't auto-discover the feed. Fix: in ghost-head.ts (or a new helper), append the alternate link when config.components.rss.enabled is true. Also add an Atom alternate if/when Atom is added.

title: seo: No BreadcrumbList JSON-LD emitted on any page
body: Posts on Nectar live under a tag and an author. Google strongly recommends BreadcrumbList structured data so result snippets render the path. None is present in any audited file. Verify with https://search.google.com/test/rich-results on the deployed URL. Fix: in buildJsonLd, for kind=post emit a BreadcrumbList with Home > Tag > Post, and for kind=tag/author emit Home > Tag (or Home > Author).

title: seo: og:image dimensions / type / alt are not emitted
body: Open Graph spec recommends emitting og:image:width, og:image:height, og:image:type, and og:image:alt for proper preview cards on FB/LinkedIn/Slack. None of these are present in example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html. Also `twitter:image:alt` is missing. Verify with https://www.opengraph.xyz/ on the deployed URL. Fix: enrich ghost-head.ts to read image metadata at build time and emit the supplementary tags.

title: seo: og:image is an SVG — most platforms reject SVG previews
body: hello-nectar uses og:image=/content/images/welcome-cover.svg. Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack and X all require raster images (JPG/PNG) for OG previews — SVG is ignored or shows a broken image. This is a Nectar-level concern because the build does not generate fallback raster OG images. Fix: add a build step that rasterises SVG feature images to PNG (via @resvg/resvg-js or sharp) when no explicit og_image is provided. Track as the 'OpenGraph image generation' bullet from the audit prompt.

title: seo: rss content:encoded contains relative URLs
body: The Hello, Nectar post body in example/dist/rss.xml line 8 (``) contains {{img_url}} textual fragments — that's fine — but for the longer posts, image URLs and internal links are relative (e.g. /content/images/... in real posts). RSS readers fetch the feed from rss.xml's URL but the post URL is the canonical one, so relative URLs misresolve. RSS 2.0 best practice is fully-qualified URLs in content:encoded. Fix: in feeds.ts, post-process post.html through a small DOM rewriter (cheerio / linkedom) that rewrites `src="/…"` and `href="/…"` to absolute URLs based on site.url.

title: seo: rss.xml has no <atom:link rel="self">
body: RSS 2.0 best practice (and the W3C feed validator) requires `<atom:link href="https://site/rss.xml" rel="self" type="application/rss+xml"/>` in <channel>. example/dist/rss.xml is missing it. Source: src/build/feeds.ts line 41 does not declare the atom namespace either. Verify at https://validator.w3.org/feed/ on the deployed feed. Fix: add `xmlns:atom="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"` and emit the atom:link self.

title: seo: sitemap.xml has no <lastmod>, <changefreq>, or <priority>
body: example/dist/sitemap.xml emits only `<loc>` per URL. While these tags are optional per sitemaps.org/protocol.html, `<lastmod>` is the one signal Google still respects. Source: src/build/feeds.ts lines 12-15 emits only loc. Fix: change `urls` to `{ url, lastmod, changefreq? }[]`; in routes.ts populate lastmod from post.updated_at ?? post.published_at, and for tag/author archives use the most recent post.updated_at in that collection.

title: subscribe-forms: `{{input_email}}` helper emits `data-members-email` but no `name=`
body: content.ts:122 outputs `<input data-members-email type="email" required placeholder="…">`. Missing `name="email"`. Without it, a fallback browser submit posts no payload. Add `name="email"` by default; let the provider adapter override.

title: subscribe-forms: `{{subscribe_form}}` helper renders a useless placeholder
body: content.ts:116 renders `<form data-nectar-subscribe action="#" method="post"><input type="email" name="email" /></form>`. Action is `#`. Real themes use this helper from custom layouts. Wire it to the same provider-rewrite pipeline as `data-members-form`.

title: test: asset: falls back to `assets/<path>` when only logical key is registered
body:
Input: `{{asset "built/source.js"}}` where theme only registered the key `assets/built/source.js`.
Expected: helper still resolves via the second candidate (`assets/built/source.js`) and emits its fingerprinted URL.
Why: themes commonly reference assets without the `assets/` prefix; both candidate keys must be tried.

title: test: asset: resolves fingerprinted path for known asset key
body:
Input: `{{asset "css/screen.css"}}` where the theme registers `assets/css/screen.css` with fingerprintedPath `assets/css/screen.abc123.css`.
Expected: `assets/css/screen.abc123.css` (prefixed with build.base_path).
Why this matters: Ghost docs state `asset` is the canonical way to produce cache-busted URLs for theme assets; if the fingerprinted variant is not picked up, cache busting silently regresses to the source path.

title: test: authors: block iterates each author
body:
Input: `{{#authors}}{{name}};{{/authors}}`.
Expected: `Jane;John;`.
Why: documented block form.

title: test: authors: inline returns comma-separated names
body:
Input: `{{authors}}` with two authors.
Expected: `Jane, John`.
Why: documented inline form.

title: test: body_class: emits route kind token
body:
Input: `{{body_class}}` on tag route.
Expected: contains `nectar-route-tag` (or Ghost-style `tag-template`).
Why: pin route token convention.

title: test: concat: joins multiple positional args without separator
body:
Input: `{{concat "a" "b" "c"}}`.
Expected: `abc`.
Why: documented default.

title: test: concat: respects separator hash
body:
Input: `{{concat "a" "b" "c" separator="-"}}`.
Expected: `a-b-c`.
Why: documented separator option.

title: test: content: returns html as SafeString
body:
Input: `{{content}}` with html containing `

hi

`. Expected: SafeString of the html unchanged. Why: documented baseline.

title: test: content: words=N strips HTML and truncates
body:
Input: `{{content words=3}}` with html `

one two three four

`. Expected: `one two three`. Why: documented truncation strips tags.

title: test: date: formats published_at with default DD MMM YYYY
body:
Input: `{{date}}` with `this.published_at='2024-03-15T00:00:00Z'`.
Expected: `15 Mar 2024`.
Why: documented default format.

title: test: date: respects custom format hash
body:
Input: `{{date format="YYYY-MM-DD"}}` with same date.
Expected: `2024-03-15`.
Why: documented format option.

title: test: date: timeago=true returns human-friendly relative string
body:
Input: `{{date timeago=true}}` with date 2 hours in the past (fake-timed).
Expected: `2 hours ago`.
Why: documented relative formatter.

title: test: date: timezone setting shifts output
body:
Input: same UTC timestamp rendered under `site.timezone='Asia/Tokyo'`.
Expected: time shifted by +9.
Why: ensures tz plugin wired correctly.

title: test: encode: URL-encodes input
body:
Input: `{{encode "hello world & friends"}}`.
Expected: `hello%20world%20%26%20friends`.
Why: documented URL encoder.

title: test: excerpt: characters=50 truncates by characters
body:
Input: `{{excerpt characters=50}}`.
Expected: 50-character slice.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: excerpt: falls back to ctx.excerpt then plaintext
body:
Input: ctx with no custom_excerpt but `excerpt='auto'`.
Expected: `auto`.
Why: documented fallback chain.

title: test: excerpt: returns custom_excerpt when set
body:
Input: `{{excerpt}}` with `this.custom_excerpt='hand-written'`.
Expected: `hand-written`.
Why: documented precedence.

title: test: excerpt: words=10 truncates to 10 words
body:
Input: `{{excerpt words=10}}` with 30-word body.
Expected: exactly 10 whitespace-separated words.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: foreach: @index is zero-based
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{@index}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b','c']`.
Expected: `012`.
Why: Ghost docs explicitly state index starts at 0.

title: test: foreach: @key is exposed for object iteration
body:
Input: `{{#foreach obj}}{{@key}}:{{this}};{{/foreach}}` where `obj = { a:1, b:2 }`.
Expected: contains both `a:1;` and `b:2;`.
Why: Ghost docs require @key when iterating maps/objects. Current implementation iterates via Object.values losing keys — this test pins the gap.

title: test: foreach: @number is one-based
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{@number}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b','c']`.
Expected: `123`.
Why: documented one-based counterpart to @index; commonly used in numbered lists.

title: test: foreach: exposes @first on first iteration only
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{#if @first}}*{{/if}}{{this}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b']`.
Expected: `*ab`.
Why: documented data variable; themes use it to inject separators or first-item markup.

title: test: foreach: exposes @last on final iteration only
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{this}}{{#if @last}}!{{/if}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b']`.
Expected: `ab!`.
Why: themes rely on @last to suppress trailing separators.

title: test: foreach: from skips initial items (1-indexed)
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items from=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d]`.
Expected: `bcd` (Ghost from is 1-based).
Why: pinned to catch off-by-one regressions.

title: test: foreach: inverse block runs when iterable is empty
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}x{{else}}none{{/foreach}}` with `items = []`.
Expected: `none`.
Why: Handlebars-style inverse documented for foreach.

title: test: foreach: iterates array and renders block for each item
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{this}}{{/foreach}}` where `items = ['a','b','c']`.
Expected: `abc`.
Why: baseline iteration; everything else depends on this.

title: test: foreach: limit truncates the visible list
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items limit=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d]`.
Expected: `ab`.
Why: documented hash for slicing lists.

title: test: foreach: to limits the end index inclusively
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items to=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d]`.
Expected: `ab`.
Why: documented hash; pairs with from.

title: test: foreach: visibility="public" excludes members-only items
body:
Input: `{{#foreach posts visibility="public"}}{{slug}};{{/foreach}}` where posts include a `visibility: 'members'` item.
Expected: members-only item is filtered out.
Why: documented filter; required for correct list rendering on public listings.

title: test: get: default order is `published_at desc`
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts"}}...{{/get}}` with no order hash.
Expected: descending by published_at.
Why: pin default.

title: test: get: empty result triggers inverse block
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="slug:does-not-exist"}}A{{else}}B{{/get}}`.
Expected: `B`.
Why: documented empty handling.

title: test: get: filter interpolates `{{post.id}}` against current context
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="id:-{{post.id}}"}}...{{/get}}` (used for 'related posts' patterns).
Expected: current post excluded.
Why: documented pattern; pin interpolation contract.

title: test: get: filter="tag:foo" returns matching posts
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="tag:foo"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: only posts whose tags include `foo`.
Why: documented NQL filter.

title: test: get: limit truncates result
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" limit=2}}...{{/get}}` with 5 posts.
Expected: only the first two (after default ordering).
Why: documented limit hash.

title: test: get: order="published_at asc" sorts ascending
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" order="published_at asc"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: oldest first.
Why: order direction documented.

title: test: get: order="published_at desc" sorts descending
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" order="published_at desc"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: newest first.
Why: default direction; pin behaviour.

title: test: get: resource="posts" returns engine.content.posts
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts"}}{{#foreach this}}{{slug}};{{/foreach}}{{/get}}`.
Expected: every post slug joined by `;`.
Why: baseline coverage.

title: test: ghost_foot: appends codeinjection_foot when present
body:
Input: ctx.codeinjection_foot=`<script>boot()</script>`.
Expected: snippet emitted as SafeString.
Why: documented passthrough.

title: test: ghost_head: JSON-LD Article on post
body:
Input: post with id, title, publish/update dates.
Expected: `<script type="application/ld+json">` includes @type Article with headline, dates, author array.
Why: documented JSON-LD support.

title: test: ghost_head: appends codeinjection_head when present
body:
Input: ctx.codeinjection_head = `<script>x()</script>`.
Expected: snippet appended verbatim.
Why: documented passthrough.

title: test: ghost_head: emits OG title/description/site_name
body:
Input: `{{ghost_head}}` on a post.
Expected: og:title, og:description, og:site_name meta tags.
Why: documented OG suite.

title: test: ghost_head: emits canonical link
body:
Input: `{{ghost_head}}` on home route with site.url `https://x.com/`.
Expected: contains `<link rel="canonical" href="https://x.com/">`.
Why: SEO essential.

title: test: ghost_head: emits og:image and twitter:image when feature_image present
body:
Input: post.feature_image set.
Expected: both meta image tags emitted.
Why: documented image fields.

title: test: ghost_head: emits twitter:card summary_large_image
body:
Input: any route.
Expected: tag present.
Why: documented Twitter card.

title: test: ghost_head: escapes attribute values
body:
Input: ctx.title containing quotes/angle brackets.
Expected: properly escaped in og:title and twitter:title.
Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: ghost_head: og:type='article' on post route
body:
Input: route.kind='post' with route.data.post set.
Expected: `<meta property="og:type" content="article">`.
Why: documented branch.

title: test: has: author="<slug>" matches
body:
Input: `{{#has author="jane"}}yes{{/has}}` with `authors=[{slug:'jane'}]`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented author check.

title: test: has: no key matches → inverse runs
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="x"}}A{{else}}B{{/has}}` with mismatched data.
Expected: `B`.
Why: inverse semantics documented.

title: test: has: tag list (`a, b`) matches if any present
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="news, sport"}}yes{{/has}}` with only `sport` tag present.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented comma-list OR semantics.

title: test: has: tag mismatch fires inverse
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="news"}}yes{{else}}no{{/has}}` with empty tags array.
Expected: `no`.
Why: covers inverse path.

title: test: has: tag="<slug>" matches by slug
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="news"}}yes{{/has}}` with `tags=[{slug:'news',name:'News'}]`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented tag slug match.

title: test: has: visibility="members" matches
body:
Input: `{{#has visibility="members"}}gated{{/has}}` with `this.visibility='members'`.
Expected: `gated`.
Why: gating logic depends on this.

title: test: img_url: extracts `feature_image` from object argument
body:
Input: `{{img_url this}}` where `this = { feature_image: '/content/images/hero.jpg' }`.
Expected: `/content/images/hero.jpg`.
Why: Ghost's `img_url` accepts both a string path and a content object; covers the `extractImage` branch.

title: test: img_url: returns absolute URL when `absolute=true`
body:
Input: `{{img_url "/content/images/foo.jpg" absolute=true}}` with site.url `https://example.com/`.
Expected: `https://example.com/content/images/foo.jpg`.
Why: Ghost docs explicitly require `absolute=true` to produce a fully-qualified URL for OG/feed contexts.

title: test: img_url: returns the path unchanged when no hash given
body:
Input: `{{img_url "/content/images/foo.jpg"}}`.
Expected: `/content/images/foo.jpg` (string, not SafeString).
Why: relative path is the default output per Ghost docs.

title: test: is: `author` matches author route
body:
Input: `{{#is "author"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='author'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: covers author context branch.

title: test: is: `home` is true when kind is home
body:
Input: `{{#is "home"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='home'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: home is the alias bundle covering home+index.

title: test: is: `paged` false on page 1 (default)
body:
Input: same as above with `pagination.page = 1` or missing.
Expected: `no`.
Why: pin off-by-one safeguard.

title: test: is: `paged` true when pagination.page > 1
body:
Input: `{{#is "paged"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}` with `route.data.pagination.page = 2`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: themes hide hero blocks on paged URLs via this.

title: test: is: `tag` matches tag route
body:
Input: `{{#is "tag"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='tag'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: covers tag context branch.

title: test: is: comma-separated targets OR together
body:
Input: `{{#is "home, tag"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind = 'tag'`.
Expected: `yes` (any target matches).
Why: Ghost docs explicitly allow `"home, tag"` for OR semantics.

title: test: is: matches `page` when route kind is page
body:
Input: `{{#is "page"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind = 'page'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: cover the page kind branch.

title: test: is: matches `post` when route kind is post
body:
Input: `{{#is "post"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}` with `route.kind = 'post'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: baseline route detection used by every theme.

title: test: is: no match fires inverse block
body:
Input: `{{#is "author"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}` with `kind='post'`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: covers inverse path.

title: test: link: renders anchor with href
body: Input: `{{#link href="/about/"}}About{{/link}}`. Expected: `About`. Why: documented link helper.

title: test: link_class: returns activeClass when route.url matches `for`
body:
Input: `{{link_class for="/about/"}}` with route.url `/about/`.
Expected: `nav-current`.
Why: documented active link detection.

title: test: link_class: trailing-slash differences normalise to a match
body:
Input: route.url `/about` vs `for="/about/"`.
Expected: still `nav-current` after normalisation.
Why: pin documented loose match.

title: test: match: 1-arg falsy renders inverse
body:
Input: `{{#match foo}}yes{{else}}no{{/match}}` with `foo = ''`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: pin falsiness semantics.

title: test: match: 1-arg truthy renders fn block
body:
Input: `{{#match foo}}yes{{else}}no{{/match}}` with `foo = 'bar'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented one-arg signature checks truthiness.

title: test: match: 2-arg equality compares with ===
body:
Input: `{{#match a b}}yes{{/match}}` where `a==='x'`, `b==='x'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented two-arg variant equates with strict equality.

title: test: match: 3-arg `=` operator
body:
Input: `{{#match a "=" b}}yes{{/match}}` with both `'foo'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented operator string.

title: test: meta_description: ctx.meta_description wins
body:
Input: ctx with `meta_description`, `excerpt`, and site.description.
Expected: ctx.meta_description.
Why: documented chain top.

title: test: meta_description: falls back to excerpt
body:
Input: no meta_description, but excerpt set.
Expected: excerpt.
Why: documented chain step.

title: test: meta_title: explicit meta_title overrides ctx.title
body:
Input: ctx has both `meta_title` and `title`.
Expected: meta_title wins.
Why: documented precedence.

title: test: meta_title: home route uses site.title
body:
Input: route.kind='home', no explicit title.
Expected: site.title.
Why: documented home behaviour.

title: test: meta_title: post route uses post.title
body:
Input: route.kind='post', this.title='Hello'.
Expected: `Hello`.
Why: documented chain on post.

title: test: navigation: escapes label and url
body: Input: item `{label:'x', url:'javascript:alert(1)'}`. Expected: escaped output, no raw HTML/JS injected. Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: navigation: renders primary navigation list
body:
Input: `{{navigation}}` with two primary items. Expected: `
  • Home
    • ...
`. Why: baseline navigation.

title: test: navigation: type="secondary" uses secondary_navigation array
body:
Input: `{{navigation type="secondary"}}`.
Expected: only secondary items rendered.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: no CLI integration tests
body: Spawn `bun src/cli/index.ts <command>` and assert exit codes / stdout/stderr.

title: test: no Lighthouse / a11y CI gate
body: Run `@axe-core/cli` over example/dist/ and fail on serious issues.

title: test: no coverage reporting
body: Bun supports `bun test --coverage`. Wire into CI and threshold at e.g. 70% lines.

title: test: no fixture-based test for ghost-import
body: Add a minimal Ghost JSON fixture (5 posts, 2 pages, 3 tags, 2 users with cross-references) and assert importer outputs.

title: test: pagination: omits next anchor on last page
body:
Input: pagination with `page===pages`.
Expected: no `.older-posts` link.
Why: same as above.

title: test: pagination: omits prev anchor on first page
body:
Input: pagination on page 1 of N.
Expected: no `.newer-posts` link.
Why: prevents broken/cycle nav.

title: test: pagination: renders newer/older anchors when URLs present
body:
Input: pagination `{page:2, pages:5, prev_url:'/page/1/', next_url:'/page/3/'}`.
Expected: contains both `class="newer-posts"` and `class="older-posts"` anchors.
Why: baseline pagination markup.

title: test: pagination: returns empty string when pages <= 1
body:
Input: `{{pagination}}` with `pagination.pages=1`.
Expected: `''`.
Why: documented no-op behaviour.

title: test: plural: empty case uses `empty` hash
body:
Input: `{{plural 0 empty="none" singular="% item" plural="% items"}}`.
Expected: `none`.
Why: documented empty branch.

title: test: plural: plural case (n>1) substitutes % with n
body:
Input: same hash with count=5.
Expected: `5 items`.
Why: documented plural with %.

title: test: plural: singular case (n=1)
body:
Input: same hash with count=1.
Expected: `1 item`.
Why: documented singular.

title: test: post block: exposes post from route data
body:
Input: `{{#post}}{{slug}}{{/post}}` when route.data.post = { slug: 'hello' }.
Expected: `hello`.
Why: context block helper.

title: test: post_class: emits 'post' baseline token
body:
Input: `{{post_class}}` on a post.
Expected: contains `post`.
Why: documented baseline.

title: test: post_class: featured token when post.featured
body:
Input: post.featured=true.
Expected: contains `featured`.
Why: documented token.

title: test: post_class: includes tag-<slug> per tag
body:
Input: post.tags=[{slug:'news'},{slug:'sport'}].
Expected: contains `tag-news tag-sport`.
Why: documented per-tag tokens.

title: test: reading_time: 1-minute case uses singular template
body:
Input: `{{reading_time}}` with `this.reading_time = 1`.
Expected: `1 min read`.
Why: documented singular branch.

title: test: reading_time: n-minute case uses plural template
body:
Input: `{{reading_time}}` with `this.reading_time = 7`.
Expected: `7 min read`.
Why: documented plural branch.

title: test: render engine smoke tests do not assert helper output byte-for-byte
body: Add specific helper tests with exact-string assertions.

title: test: social_url: mastodon `@user@host` produces full URL
body:
Input: `this.mastodon='@jane@mastodon.social'`.
Expected: `https://mastodon.social/@jane`.
Why: handle-with-host parsing.

title: test: social_url: twitter handle (with and without @)
body:
Input: `{{social_url type="twitter"}}` with `this.twitter='@jane'`.
Expected: `https://twitter.com/jane`. Repeat without `@`.
Why: documented stripAt behaviour.

title: test: t: falls back to en when active locale missing key
body:
Input: `{{t "goodbye"}}` with `ja` missing key, `en.goodbye='Bye'`.
Expected: `Bye`.
Why: documented fallback chain.

title: test: t: named `{key}` placeholder substitutes hash arg
body:
Input: `{{t "hi.user" name="Jane"}}` with template `Hi {name}`.
Expected: `Hi Jane`.
Why: documented placeholder syntax.

title: test: t: returns the translation for active locale
body:
Input: `{{t "hello"}}` with locale ja mapping `hello → こんにちは`.
Expected: `こんにちは`.
Why: baseline translation.

title: test: tags: autolink=false renders names only
body:
Input: `{{tags autolink=false}}`.
Expected: plain comma-joined names, no anchors.
Why: documented opt-out.

title: test: tags: autolink=true renders anchors
body: Input: `{{tags}}` (default autolink). Expected: each tag wrapped in `name`. Why: documented default.

title: test: tags: escapes tag name and url in anchors
body:
Input: tag `{name:'<x>', url:'"bad'}`.
Expected: attributes/text escaped.
Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: tags: inline returns comma-separated names
body:
Input: `{{tags}}` with tags.
Expected: tag names joined by `, `.
Why: baseline.

title: test: url: absolute=true resolves against site.url
body:
Input: `{{url absolute=true}}` with site `https://example.com/blog/` and ctx.url `/welcome/`.
Expected: `https://example.com/welcome/` (or `/blog/welcome/` depending on resolution semantics).
Why: pin documented absolute behaviour.

title: test: url: returns ctx.url when not absolute
body:
Input: `{{url}}` with `this.url='/welcome/'`.
Expected: `/welcome/`.
Why: documented default mode.

title: testing: no CLI integration tests
body: There are no tests that spawn the CLI and assert stdout/stderr/exit-code. Add `tests/cli/{build,new,check,serve,import-ghost}.test.ts` using `Bun.spawn`. Catches arg-parsing regressions that unit tests miss.

title: testing: no XSS / sanitisation tests
body: Frontmatter and `codeinjection_*` accept raw strings that get emitted into HTML attributes (`<meta content="...">`). Add tests for: `<script>` in title, double-quote in description, NUL bytes in slug. Today some paths use a custom `escapeAttr`/`escapeHtml` per file (duplicated, drift risk) — consolidate and test.

title: theme-dev: no HMR / live-reload in the browser
body: Inject a tiny `/__nectar/livereload.js` snippet (only in dev) that opens a WebSocket and reloads on rebuild. For CSS-only changes, hot-swap stylesheets without full reload. Without this, theme iteration is back to Ctrl-R hell.

title: theme-dev: no `nectar theme:new` scaffolder
body: A theme author who wants to start fresh has to copy Source manually. Add `nectar theme:new <name>` that scaffolds `themes/<name>/{default.hbs,index.hbs,post.hbs,page.hbs,partials/,assets/{css,js}/,package.json,locales/en.json}` matching Ghost's expected layout.

title: theme-dev: no documentation of available helpers / context
body: Theme authors guess from reading Ghost's docs. Generate `docs/theme-reference.md` automatically from helper registrations (`registerXyzHelpers`) and the Post/Page/Tag/Author TypeScript types in `src/content/model.ts`. Add JSDoc to each helper, then a tiny `bun run docs:gen` script.

title: theme-dev: no source-map for Handlebars stack traces
body: Handlebars throws raw "Parse error on line 23" with no file context. Wrap `hb.compile` to remember `{ file, source }` and rethrow with `'theme/partials/post-card.hbs':23:4 — Expected closing tag '{{/foreach}}'`.

title: theme-dev: no watch mode for theme files
body: Even when we add `nectar dev`, we need theme `.hbs`/`.css`/`.js` to invalidate the partial registry and re-render only affected routes. Plan: hash each `.hbs`, on change rebuild the engine and re-render only routes whose template depends on that file (track partial dependencies during compile).

title: theme/alto: @site.secondary_navigation truthiness for non-empty array
body: default.hbs:78,83 uses `{{#unless @site.secondary_navigation}} no-menu{{/unless}}`. SiteData.secondary_navigation is an array; an empty array is truthy in Handlebars, so {{#unless}} renders unexpectedly. Document the gotcha or override unless/if to treat empty arrays as falsy (Ghost's behavior). Affects Wave/Dawn/Alto/London identically.

title: theme/bulletin: `pagination.total` and `pagination.page` numeric fields used
body: index.hbs:14 reads `pagination.total`. Our PaginationLike type (src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:64) exposes page/pages/prev_url/next_url but not `total`. Add `total: number` to pagination output.

title: theme/bulletin: {{#get "posts" fields="id" limit="10" page="2"}} fields + page hash params
body: partials/article.hbs:49 uses `fields="id"` (column projection) and `page="2"` (pagination). Our get helper ignores both. Add hash-param parsing for `page=` (offset = (page-1)*limit) and `fields=` (project subset of fields, even if just to honor docs).

title: theme/casper: error.hbs (404) template not wired up
body: Casper ships error.hbs and error-404.hbs (visible in repo tree). Nectar's build/routes.ts does not appear to emit /404.html using error.hbs. Add a synthetic 404 route that compiles error.hbs/error-404.hbs into out/404.html. Verify build pipeline emits this file when present in the theme.

title: theme/casper: {{comment_count}} helper not implemented
body: partials/post-card.hbs lines 71-73 and post.hbs use {{comment_count}} with hash params (class=, autowrap=, empty=, singular=, plural=). Nectar src/render/helpers/content.ts does not register comment_count; the helper resolves to '' so the count badge in the post feed is missing. Implement a no-op or static stub that outputs the wrapper span/div Casper's CSS targets (`.post-card-meta-length`) plus accept hash params, even if the count itself is always 0 for static builds.

title: theme/dawn: custom-narrow-feature-image.hbs / custom-no-feature-image.hbs alternate post layouts
body: Dawn ships three custom-*.hbs alternate post layouts. Nectar must select the right template based on the post's `custom_template` field. See Headline issue. Without selector, all posts use default post.hbs.

title: theme/digest: @site.recommendations_enabled flag
body: digest/index.hbs probably (and journal/index.hbs:128) read `{{#if @site.recommendations_enabled}}`. Not in SiteData. Add the boolean (default false) and document that Nectar does not implement recommendations server-side.

title: theme/digest: home.hbs as separate template (no {{!< default}} header)
body: digest/home.hbs is just `{{!< default}}` — uses Ghost's convention where 'home.hbs' overrides 'index.hbs' on the home route specifically. Nectar's routes/template-resolution (src/build/routes.ts) must prefer home.hbs over index.hbs when route.kind === 'home'. Verify and add test using Digest.

title: theme/digest: {{#get "posts" fields="id" page="2"}} archive detection idiom
body: digest/partials/content.hbs:46 uses the same fields/page idiom as Bulletin. Same fix required: support `page=` hash param in get helper to enable second-page existence check.

title: theme/digest: {{recommendations}} helper unimplemented
body:
digest/home.hbs and journal/index.hbs:133 use `{{recommendations}}`. This is a Ghost 5.x helper that emits cross-site recommendation cards from `/recommendations.json`. Nectar should at minimum register a no-op helper that emits an empty `
` to avoid SafeString-undefined errors.

title: theme/edition: visibility="filter" branch in {{#has}} unhandled
body: partials/content-cta.hbs:10 uses `{{#has visibility="filter"}}` for tier-gated posts. Our has helper (src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:67) only checks `ctx.visibility === 'filter'`. Ghost's actual semantics: visibility is one of 'public','members','paid','tiers','filter'. Verify Nectar's Post.visibility union accepts these values (currently restricted to 'public'|'members'|'paid' in src/content/model.ts:74).

title: theme/edition: {{tiers}} variable for tier-gated posts not provided
body: partials/content-cta.hbs:11 reads `tiers=tiers`. Ghost binds a `tiers` array on the post context with the tiers gating the visibility. Nectar's Post model has no `tiers` field. Add tiers: Tier[] to Post and a Tier type ({name, slug, monthly_price, yearly_price}). Without it, the CTA falls back to empty string.

title: theme/editorial: {{#foreach navigation}} expects `current` field on items
body: editorial/partials/navigation.hbs:7 uses `{{#if current}}active{{/if}}`. NavigationItem in src/config/schema.ts does not include a `current` boolean. The navigation helper in src/render/helpers/navigation.ts also doesn't compute current state per item. Add current=true on the matching item based on route.url.

title: theme/editorial: {{#get "posts" slug="contact" as |contact|}} get-by-slug
body: editorial/partials/sidebar.hbs:30 calls `{{#get "posts" slug="contact"}}`. Our get helper does not parse `slug=` hash as a filter; it only accepts `filter=` and `order=`/`limit=`. Either translate `slug=` into a `filter='slug:X'` or add explicit hash handling.

title: theme/editorial: {{twitter_url @site.twitter}} / {{facebook_url @site.facebook}} helpers unimplemented
body: editorial/default.hbs:36,39 uses positional helpers `{{twitter_url X}}` and `{{facebook_url X}}`. These are Ghost-standard helpers Nectar does not register (only `social_url type=` is registered). Implement twitter_url and facebook_url that take a positional handle/URL argument.

title: theme/headline: @config.posts_per_page context missing
body: index.hbs:13 reads `limit=@config.posts_per_page`. buildRootData() exposes `config: engine.config` but Ghost themes expect `@config.posts_per_page` derived from theme package.json `config.posts_per_page`, not Nectar's own config schema. Wire `@config` to the parsed theme pkg.config (or merge it) so this expression returns 7 (Headline's default).

title: theme/headline: custom-full-feature-image.hbs / custom-wide-feature-image.hbs alternate templates
body: Headline ships empty custom-*.hbs files used by Ghost's admin template picker. Nectar must treat any file starting with `custom-` as a selectable template variant and surface it in post/page schema so that show_title_and_feature_image / template selection works. Add discovery + per-post `custom_template` field.

title: theme/headline: {{#get "posts" filter="tag:{{slug}}" limit="7"}} interpolation inside filter strings
body: partials/topic-grid.hbs:7 uses `filter="tag:{{slug}}"` — interpolation inside a filter value. Our get helper does run a regex replacement (`{{post.id}}` style), but only for `{{path.like.this}}`. With a bare `{{slug}}` (no dot path) ensure ctx.slug from the foreach'd tag substitutes correctly. Add a test using Headline's topic grid loop.

title: theme/headline: {{#get "tags" include="count.posts" order="count.posts desc"}} not supported
body: home.hbs:34-40 and index.hbs:13 use `{{#get "tags" include="count.posts" order="count.posts desc" limit="3"}}`. Our get helper in src/render/helpers/blocks.ts ignores the `include` hash, and `applyOrder` calls .localeCompare on nested keys like 'count.posts' (returns 0). Implement include='count.posts' to project the existing tag.count.posts onto sortable top-level, or teach applyOrder to follow dotted paths.

title: theme/journal: pagination.total used to gate 'A collection of N issues' string
body: Similar to Bulletin: requires pagination.total numeric field. Add `total` to pagination payload (src/build/pipeline.ts or wherever pagination is computed).

title: theme/journal: {{#if @setting.paid_members_enabled}} uses @setting (not @site)
body: journal/index.hbs:87 uses `{{@setting.paid_members_enabled}}`. Ghost exposes @setting as a parallel context to @site. Nectar provides only @site/@blog/@config. Add @setting as an alias for @site (or with explicit settings subset) so legacy themes work.

title: theme/krabi: @member.subscriptions / plan / default_payment_card_last4 context
body: krabi/members/account.hbs heavily reads `@member.subscriptions[].cancel_at_period_end`, `.current_period_end`, `plan.currency_symbol`, `plan.interval`, `default_payment_card_last4`. Nectar exposes member: undefined statically. Provide TS interfaces for Member/Subscription/Plan so themes don't crash on undefined; or build a stub that resolves all subscription paths to empty values.

title: theme/krabi: default-wide.hbs alternative layout via `{{!< ../default-wide}}`
body: krabi/members/account.hbs:1 uses `{{!< ../default-wide}}`. Nectar's layout splitter (src/render/layouts.ts splitLayout) must resolve `../default-wide` (relative path navigating up out of members/ subdir). Add resolver for relative `..` paths in `{{!<}}` directive.

title: theme/krabi: {{cancel_link class="..." errorClass="..."}} helper
body: krabi/members/account.hbs:58-66 uses `{{cancel_link}}` — Ghost helper that outputs an HTML anchor that calls /members/api/cancel-subscription. Nectar does not register it. Emit a placeholder `Cancel subscription` even if non-functional in static builds.

title: theme/krabi: {{price plan.amount}} positional price helper
body: krabi/members/account.hbs:27 uses `{{ price plan.amount }}`. Ghost's price helper formats an amount divided by 100 with the currency. Nectar has no price helper. Register a price helper that formats Number(amount)/100 with currency from sibling plan.currency.

title: theme/liebling: `{{{block "herobackground"}}}` head-style injection from partials/hero.hbs
body: default.hbs:80 emits `{{{block 'herobackground'}}}` — this is Liebling's pattern of contentFor-ing a `<style>` block from the hero partial into <head>. Without contentFor, the responsive hero CSS doesn't load. Track as a sub-issue of block/contentFor implementation.

title: theme/liebling: page-authors.hbs and page-newsletter.hbs custom routing
body: Liebling ships page-authors.hbs and page-newsletter.hbs — these are routed via routes.yaml on Ghost. Nectar needs either routes.yaml support or filename-based convention (`page-{slug}.hbs` for matching page slug). Same shape as Casper's custom-{name}.hbs. Confirm template resolution order: custom-template → page-{slug}.hbs → page.hbs.

title: theme/liebling: {{#has author="count:>1"}} count-comparison predicate
body: post.hbs:127 uses `{{#has author="count:>1"}}`. Our has helper (blocks.ts:67) only matches exact slug/name, not the `count:>1` modifier. Implement `count:OPN`  where OP is >, <, =, >=, <= and N is integer; evaluates against ctx.authors.length (or tags.length for tag key).

title: theme/liebling: {{> header background=feature_image}} hash-param passed to partial as scope variable
body: post.hbs:22 calls `{{> header background=feature_image}}`. Inside header.hbs, `{{#if background}}` must resolve. Handlebars partial-include with hash creates a new context layer — verify this works in Nectar (no custom partial resolver).

title: theme/liebling: {{authors separator=", " prefix=(t "Among with...") from="2"}} extra authors helper hash params
body: post.hbs:129 uses `prefix=` and `from=` on the authors helper. Our authors helper (content.ts:46) only joins by ', '. Add `separator`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `from`, `limit` hash params (Ghost's signature).

title: theme/london: {{#has number="nth:3"}} nth-index predicate
body: london/partials/post-card.hbs:8 uses `{{#has number="nth:3"}}post-card-large{{/has}}`. Our has helper (blocks.ts:98) only supports exact number matches against pagination.page — not the `nth:N` modulo predicate used here on @number from foreach. Implement `nth:N` to test (index+1) % N === 0.

title: theme/ruby: primary_tag.accent_color in CSS variables
body: ruby/post.hbs:10 uses `style="--tag-color: {{primary_tag.accent_color}};"`. Same Tag model fix as Solo. Add accent_color to Tag.

title: theme/solo: comment_count helper with empty=/singular=/plural= hash params
body: post.hbs:55 calls `{{comment_count empty="" singular=(t "comment") plural=(t "comments")}}`. Nectar must implement comment_count to accept these formatting hash params even when always rendering 0 (e.g. emit `0 comments` using the plural form).

title: theme/solo: primary_tag.accent_color not on Tag model
body: post.hbs:47 uses `style="--tag-color: {{primary_tag.accent_color}}"`. Tag in src/content/model.ts:43-54 has no accent_color field. Add accent_color: string|undefined to Tag and ensure import from Ghost preserves it. Without it, the CSS variable is set to empty string and the styled tag chip loses its color.

title: theme/wave: `blog.hbs` empty template + custom routing required
body: Wave ships an empty `blog.hbs` (0 bytes) and an `rss.hbs`. These rely on Ghost's routes.yaml/channel routing where `/blog/` is a custom channel. Nectar has no channel/routes.yaml support; the page is never emitted. Decide on routes.yaml support and/or fallback to index.hbs.

title: theme/wave: comment_count helper invocations like `{{comment_count class="gh-comments-count"}}`
body: post.hbs:43 and others. comment_count not implemented (see casper P1). Wave displays the count next to share-button. Implement comment_count to emit a ``.

title: theme: no theme:lint analog of Ghost's gscan
body: Validate that referenced helpers exist, partials resolve, custom settings are well-formed.

title: web-standards: No favicon link or icon emission
body: No `<link rel="icon">`, `<link rel="apple-touch-icon">`, or favicon.ico exists in example/dist. Browsers will fall back to /favicon.ico — which returns 404. Each page load logs a network error. Fix: copy theme.assets favicon files into the dist root and emit the appropriate `<link rel="icon" type="image/png" sizes="32x32" href="/favicon-32x32.png">` etc. Document the supported file conventions.

title: web-standards: No image optimization or responsive srcset variants
body: All `srcset` declarations in example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html (lines 128-132 etc.) declare 320w through 2000w but point to the same SVG file. img_url with size= is documented as 'best-effort' (per ghost-theme-compatibility post). This means card and hero images do not actually pick the right size, wasting bandwidth on mobile. Fix: implement the `components.images` component that uses sharp to resize/encode to AVIF/WebP at build time and rewrite img_url tags accordingly.

title: web-standards: html-validate finds multiple `<h4>` issues and other structural smells
body: Run `npx html-validate example/dist/**/*.html`. Expected findings (manually verified): (a) `<h4>` after `<h1>` without intermediate h2/h3 (hello-nectar line 114); (b) multiple `<main>` per page on tag/author archives; (c) empty `aria-label`. Fix: integrate html-validate into the build / CI step (`bun run lint:html`) and fail the build on errors.

title: a11y: Header logo link uses absolute origin and lacks aria-label/site name affordance
body: example/dist/index.html line 73: ``. The href is the absolute origin (causing a full reload from same-origin), and the link's accessible name comes only from the . If a user disables images, the logo link becomes a blank focus stop with no accessible text. Fix: change the href to `/` to enable client-side caching/replay, and add a visually-hidden span with the site name (or aria-label="Nectar Example — Home") for image-off scenarios.

title: a11y: alt text uses post titles verbatim for decorative cards
body: All card images on every page reuse the post title as the alt text (e.g. 'Optional Components', 'Ghost Theme Compatibility' on example/dist/tag/news/index.html lines 184, 167). The full card is wrapped in a single `<a class="gh-card-link">` that already exposes the title via its text — so the image alt duplicates the link text. WCAG/axe rule 'image-redundant-alt'. Fix: when an image lives inside a link whose accessible name already includes the post title, emit alt="" so the image is treated as decorative.

title: analytics: no built-in member analytics dashboard
body: Ghost ships a `/ghost/#/dashboard` with member growth, MRR, open rates. Static sites can't surface live data. Document that users should rely on their ESP's own analytics (Buttondown / Beehiiv / Substack dashboards) — link from `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`.

title: api: 404 envelope — emit Ghost-shaped error JSON for missing slug requests
body:
Ghost 404s return:
```json
{ "errors": [{ "message": "Resource not found error, cannot read post.", "context": "...", "type": "NotFoundError", "details": null, "property": null, "help": null, "code": null, "id": "..." }] }
```

Static host returns its own 404 HTML. SDK will fail to JSON-parse.

Proposal: emit a `dist/content/404.json` with Ghost's error envelope. Hosts that support custom 404 status (Netlify `_redirects`, Cloudflare Pages) can route `/content/*` 404s to this file with the right status code. Document the hosting config.

title: api: ?absolute_urls=true — Ghost option to rewrite relative URLs in html to absolute
body: Ghost Content API supports `?absolute_urls=true` which rewrites relative `` to absolute. Useful for RSS readers / external consumers. Proposal: emit `html` with absolute URLs by default in the Content API shadow (cheap, one-time rewrite at build). Document that the `?absolute_urls` query param is ignored because output is always absolute. Follow-up: keep relative URLs in the on-page HTML for portability; only rewrite in the JSON shadow.

title: api: Admin API — provide a one-way Admin→Markdown import (already exists?) and document it as the migration path
body:
We have `src/ghost/import.ts`. Verify it covers: posts, pages, tags, authors, settings, images. If anything is missing, file separate issues.

Proposal for this issue: docs page `docs/MIGRATION.md` explaining the import flow and listing what's lost (members, custom integrations, snippets, custom fields not in frontmatter).

title: api: Admin API — skip entirely; document that Nectar is read-only
body:
Ghost Admin API (`/ghost/api/admin/*`) is write-capable: posts CRUD, tags CRUD, members, integrations, etc.

Proposal: do not emit. Document loudly in README that `@tryghost/admin-api` is *not* compatible with Nectar — users author content via Markdown files + git, not via the Admin API. Link to the `nectar import` command for the migration path.

title: api: Author model lacks count.posts (blocker for /content/authors/ shadow)
body:
`src/content/model.ts:Author` has no `count` field. Ghost Content API exposes `count.posts` per-author when `?include=count.posts` is requested. Theme `{{#get "authors"}}` blocks would render without post counts, and the static JSON shadow can't be added without first extending the model.

Fix: add `count: { posts: number }` to `Author`, populate during content graph build in `src/content/loader.ts` after posts are linked.

title: api: CORS — Apache .htaccess + nginx snippet alternatives
body:
Not every host supports Netlify/Cloudflare's `_headers`. Self-hosters on nginx/Apache need CORS too.

Proposal: ship docs section + sample snippets at `docs/deploy/cors-{nginx,apache,caddy}.md` showing how to set the same headers. Optional flag `[components.content_api] emit_htaccess = true` writes a `.htaccess` to `dist/content/`.

title: api: Content API key (?key=) — accept and ignore; document stub for SDK init compatibility
body:
Ghost Content API requires `?key=<26 hex chars>`. `@tryghost/content-api` requires a key at SDK init or it throws.

Nectar gap: we don't auth; static files are world-readable. But SDK consumers still need a key value to pass.

Proposal: document that the SDK can be initialized with any 26-char hex placeholder (e.g. `nectarnectarnectarnectar00`) and Nectar will ignore it. Ship an example snippet in `docs/SDK.md`. Optionally add a build-time sanity check that emits a dummy key file like `dist/content/.key` for tooling that scrapes it.

title: api: GET /content/authors/: emit dist/content/authors.json with count.posts
body:
Ghost behavior: returns all authors with `count.posts` when `?include=count.posts`.

Nectar gap: no authors JSON; `count.posts` not even computed on `Author` (model lacks the field — see separate issue).

Proposal: add `count.posts` to `Author` derived from `posts.filter(p => p.authors.some(a => a.id === author.id))`, then emit `dist/content/authors.json` and `dist/content/authors/slug/<slug>.json`. Drop email/internal-only fields per Content API contract.

title: api: GET /content/pages/: emit dist/content/pages.json + per-page shards
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/pages/` returns the static-page collection with same envelope as posts. Distinct from posts (filtered server-side, `page=true`).

Nectar gap: pages are loaded into `ContentGraph.pages` but never serialized.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/pages.json` (full list, paginated if >15) and `dist/content/pages/slug/<slug>.json`. Same field projection as Post minus post-only fields (`featured`, `prev`/`next`).

title: api: GET /content/posts/: emit paginated shards dist/content/posts/page/N.json matching meta.pagination shape
body:
Ghost behavior: paginated with `?page=N&limit=L`; response carries `meta.pagination = { page, limit, pages, total, next, prev }`. Default `limit=15`, max `limit=15` for posts (and `limit=all` for full dump).

Nectar gap: no pagination shards.

Proposal: for each `limit` in `[15, 'all']` (configurable list), emit:
- `dist/content/posts.json` (page 1, default limit)
- `dist/content/posts/page/2.json`, `.../3.json`, ...
- `dist/content/posts/all.json` (limit=all)

Each payload includes `meta.pagination` with `page`/`limit`/`pages`/`total`/`next`/`prev` (next/prev are page numbers, not URLs, per Ghost). Document that arbitrary `?limit=N&page=M` query combos won't be honored — only the canonical shards.

title: api: GET /content/posts/:id/ and /content/posts/slug/:slug/: emit per-post JSON shadows
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/posts/:id/` and `/content/posts/slug/:slug/` return `{ posts: [Post] }` (single-element array).

Nectar gap: no per-post JSON; we only have the HTML at `/:slug/`.

Proposal: alongside `dist/<slug>/index.html`, emit `dist/content/posts/slug/<slug>.json` and `dist/content/posts/<id>.json`. Same Post projection as the index shard. Allows `@tryghost/content-api`'s `posts.read({slug})` to work read-only against a static host.

title: api: GET /content/tags/: emit dist/content/tags.json with count.posts populated
body:
Ghost behavior: returns all public tags with `count.posts` populated when `?include=count.posts` is set. Default order `?order=name asc`.

Nectar gap: `Tag.count.posts` is already on the model but never written out.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/tags.json` with the full public tag list (drop `visibility: internal`), each tag carrying `count.posts`. Also `dist/content/tags/slug/<slug>.json` for individual lookup.

title: api: author.url — same audit as tag.url
body: Mirror of the tag.url issue. Authors' URLs in the Content API are absolute `https://site.com/author/:slug/`. Verify.

title: api: cache-control headers per resource — short TTL for posts, longer for tags/authors
body:
Ghost API typically returns `Cache-Control: no-cache` or short TTLs. For a static deploy we want longer TTLs to leverage CDN.

Proposal: in `dist/_headers`:
- `/content/posts/*` → `max-age=300` (5 min)
- `/content/tags/*`, `/content/authors/*`, `/content/settings/*` → `max-age=3600` (1 hour)
- `/content/tiers/*`, `/content/newsletters/*` → `max-age=86400` (1 day, basically empty)

Configurable via `[components.content_api.cache]`.

title: api: example consumer — ship docs/EXAMPLE_SPA.md showing @tryghost/content-api against a Nectar build
body:
Reference implementation. A 50-line React/Vue/Svelte snippet that fetches `posts.browse()` against a Nectar deployment, demonstrating the path setup and key stub. Acts as living documentation and a regression target.

Proposal: write the doc, link from README. Bonus: add to `example/` as a runnable subdir.

title: api: filter (NQL) — emit pre-baked tag/author filtered shards instead of supporting arbitrary NQL
body:
Ghost behavior: `?filter=tag:foo+published_at:>'2024-01-01'` uses NQL — a non-trivial query language (`tag:`, `tags:[a,b]`, `featured:true`, `visibility:public`, date comparators, AND `+` / OR `,`).

Nectar gap: static files can't run NQL.

Proposal: don't implement NQL. Instead emit **pre-baked shards** for the common filters that themes and SDKs hit:
- `dist/content/posts/tag/<slug>.json` — `filter=tag:<slug>` equivalent
- `dist/content/posts/author/<slug>.json` — `filter=author:<slug>` (deprecated Ghost name) and `authors:<slug>`
- `dist/content/posts/featured.json` — `filter=featured:true`

Document that arbitrary `?filter=` will not work; recommend client-side filtering of the full `posts.json`. Issue a follow-up to add a tiny NQL→predicate shim in a future `nectar serve` if needed.

title: api: filter NQL — document explicit non-support and 404 strategy
body:
Consumers will inevitably pass `?filter=...` to a static host. They'll get the unfiltered full dump (query string stripped) silently — which is *worse* than a 404 because results look correct but contain too many items.

Proposal: ship a `_headers` / `_redirects` snippet (Netlify/Cloudflare format) that 404s any request to `/content/*.json?filter=*` or similar. Better yet, document a `nectar.config.ts` flag `api.strict_query_rejection = true` that, when running behind a worker, returns 404 on unknown query params.

Minimum for this issue: README/`GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` section listing which query params are honored vs ignored.

title: api: members-only fields on Post — strip from public JSON (paywall_html, members content)
body:
Ghost posts can carry `<!--members-only-->` markers; Content API returns truncated content for non-members. Without members, we either emit full content or strip the marker.

Proposal: parse `<!--members-only-->` in Markdown, drop everything after it from `html` and `plaintext` for `visibility: members`/`paid` posts, OR (simpler) reject those visibilities at build with a clear error since Nectar doesn't support paywalls.

title: api: meta.pagination.next/prev as numbers, not strings
body:
Ghost returns `next` and `prev` as numbers (or null), not strings. SDK parses them as numbers. Easy to get wrong with JSON serializers.

Proposal: ensure the pagination helper emits numbers. Add a test that does `typeof body.meta.pagination.next === 'number' || body.meta.pagination.next === null`.

title: api: no convention for npm plugin discovery
body: Document `nectar-plugin-<name>` as the npm-search prefix (mirroring `eslint-plugin-*`). Auto-load any installed package matching that prefix unless explicitly disabled in config. Publish a tiny scaffold (`nectar plugin:new`) too.

title: api: no plugin discovery convention (`nectar-plugin-*` packages)
body: Pick one — naming convention + import contract — and document.

title: api: no semver-stable contract on Post/Page shape
body: `src/content/model.ts` currently lives privately. Once plugins consume it, every field add/rename becomes breaking. Mark certain fields `@experimental` and document the stable subset in VERSIONING.md.

title: api: post.access / post.visibility — emit visibility but document access is always 'public'
body:
Ghost: `post.access` boolean (true if current member can read), `post.visibility` (`public`/`members`/`paid`/`tiers`). Without a session, Content API returns posts based on the key's tier permissions.

Nectar: no members, no session, no paywall.

Proposal: always emit `access: true`, preserve `visibility` from frontmatter for completeness, but filter out `visibility: members`/`paid` posts from the public JSON by default (configurable). Document.

title: api: post.excerpt vs post.custom_excerpt vs post.plaintext — verify Ghost's exact rules
body:
Ghost's Content API rules:
- `excerpt` = `custom_excerpt` if set, else first ~50 words of `plaintext`
- `custom_excerpt` = the field set in the editor, may be null
- `plaintext` = HTML stripped

Nectar: `Post` model has all three. Need to verify `loader.ts` populates them with Ghost's exact precedence and word count (~50 words, not chars).

Proposal: audit `src/content/loader.ts:normalizePost` against Ghost's source. File a fix if mismatched.

title: api: post.html field — verify it matches Ghost's serialization (no Ghost-specific card markup leaking)
body:
Ghost's `post.html` includes Ghost-specific markup: `<figure class="kg-card kg-image-card">`, `<!--kg-card-begin: html-->` comments, member-only paywall splits, etc.

Nectar emits Markdown→HTML plain. Consumers reading `post.html` from the static API may rely on Ghost card classes (e.g. to apply CSS for kg-image-card layouts).

Proposal: document the divergence in `GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`. Optionally add `[components.markdown] emit_kg_classes = true` that wraps images in `<figure class="kg-card kg-image-card">` and similar. Phase 2: a markdown plugin that renders inline `{% card %}` directives into kg-card HTML.

title: api: post.id format — verify it's a 24-char hex ObjectId or document divergence
body:
Ghost IDs are MongoDB ObjectIds (24 hex). Some SDK consumers regex-validate.

Nectar: check `src/content/loader.ts` — if we're using slugs or random IDs, document divergence or generate deterministic 24-hex from a hash of slug+published_at.

Proposal: deterministic hex(sha1(slug+published_at)).slice(0,24).

title: api: post.reading_time — verify Ghost's algorithm (275 wpm + image adjustment)
body:
Ghost's `reading_time` = `ceil(word_count / 275) + image_seconds/60`, where each image adds 12s decreasing to 3s. See `@tryghost/helpers/lib/utils/reading-time.js`.

Nectar: `Post.reading_time` exists but algorithm not verified.

Proposal: port Ghost's exact formula; add a test fixture comparing against `@tryghost/helpers`. Note: existing self-audit already flags word_count CJK bug — fix that first.

title: api: post.url — verify absolute, trailing-slash, respects custom permalink config
body: Ghost has configurable permalink structures via routes.yaml. We don't support routes.yaml (separate issue elsewhere). For the API shadow, ensure `post.url` is absolute with trailing slash. If the user configures a permalink prefix later, the JSON shadow must update.

title: api: post.uuid — emit a stable UUID per post (separate from id)
body:
Ghost has both `id` (24-hex ObjectId) and `uuid` (RFC4122) on posts. Some consumers key off uuid.

Nectar: only `id` (we may be generating ObjectId-shaped or slug-shaped). Need to verify and add `uuid` (deterministically derived from content path + a project namespace, so it's stable across builds).

Proposal: add `uuid` to `Post`/`Page` derived via `uuid v5` with namespace = site URL, name = slug.

title: api: posts include excerpt of first paragraph if custom_excerpt unset — verify Ghost's word count (50 words default)
body:
Ghost: auto-excerpt is *50 words* of plaintext, not characters, not full paragraph. Easy to get wrong.

Proposal: audit `src/content/loader.ts` excerpt generation; ensure it's `plaintext.split(/\s+/).slice(0, 50).join(' ')` (or `Intl.Segmenter`-based for CJK). Add test.

title: api: search — sodo-search drop-in: emit posts list at /content/posts.json (already covered) is sufficient for sodo
body:
`sodo-search` (Ghost's official search widget) fetches `/ghost/api/content/posts/?limit=all&fields=id,slug,title,excerpt,url,updated_at,visibility&order=updated_at%20desc`. If we already emit `posts/all.json` with the right fields + path, sodo-search works without code change.

Proposal: verify by dropping sodo-search into the example theme and pointing it at a built site. File follow-up issues for any field/shape mismatch found.

title: api: tag.url — verify it matches Ghost's /tag/:slug/ convention and config.url is prefixed
body:
Ghost emits absolute URLs in API responses (`https://site.com/tag/foo/`). Theme helpers expect this.

Nectar: `Tag.url` exists; verify it's absolute (with `[site].url` prefix) and ends with trailing slash.

Proposal: spot-check in `loader.ts`; add a test.

title: build-pipeline: No HSTS / no documented host-headers file
body: Nectar is a static SSG so security headers belong at the edge, but the project ships no `_headers` (Cloudflare/Netlify) or `nginx.conf` example. Newcomers will deploy with no HSTS, no X-Content-Type-Options, no Permissions-Policy. Fix: add `docs/HOSTING.md` with copy-paste `_headers` / `netlify.toml` / `cloudflare.json` / `nginx.conf` snippets recommending HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.

title: build-pipeline: No HTML minification or whitespace normalization
body: src/build/emit.ts `writeHtml()` simply writes the engine output, which preserves Handlebars indentation. Inspecting any dist file shows large runs of leading whitespace and HTML comments. Production builds should run output through @swc/html-minifier or html-minifier-terser. Fix: add a pipeline step `minifyHtml()` toggled by `build.minify_html` config flag (default true in production).

title: build-pipeline: copyContentAssets() silently swallows errors
body: src/build/emit.ts lines 48-50: the `try {} catch {}` block on copyContentAssets swallows every error ("content/images is optional"). If a permission error or partial read occurs the build silently emits missing images. Fix: distinguish ENOENT (silent) from other errors (re-throw with logger.warn).

title: build-pipeline: ghost-head.ts emits raw `codeinjection_head` without sanitization
body: ghost-head.ts line 49: `if (typeof head === 'string' && head) parts.push(head)` injects arbitrary HTML from post/page front-matter into the document head. This is a stored-XSS sink if any markdown front-matter source is user-contributed (multi-author Git repo, PR-based content). Fix: document the security model clearly (`codeinjection_head/foot is trusted, must only be set by repository owners`), and gate it behind a config flag (`security.allow_codeinjection = false` by default).

title: build-pipeline: ghost-head.ts uses ctx.published_at directly in JSON-LD — no ISO 8601 validation
body: ghost-head.ts line 113: `datePublished: ctx.published_at` is emitted verbatim. If a markdown file has `date: 2026-05-19` (no time), the generated JSON-LD will contain just `"2026-05-19"` which is valid ISO 8601 date but Google Article guidelines prefer full timestamps with timezone. Verify by parsing example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html JSON-LD with `JSON.parse(...)`. Fix: normalize to RFC3339 (`new Date(ctx.published_at).toISOString()`) in buildJsonLd, falling back gracefully.

title: build-pipeline: link_class helper only highlights exact URL match — parent routes are never marked current
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts lines 50-61: link_class returns the active class only when route.url === target (or normalised equal). A user reading /tag/news/page/2/ with nav-tag pointing to /tag/news/ will see no current-page state. Fix: also mark active when `target` is a prefix of `route.url` and target ends with '/'.

title: build-pipeline: pagination helper hardcodes English '— Newer Posts' / 'Older Posts'
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts lines 30, 34: ←/→ arrows and English labels are hardcoded into the pagination output. For non-English sites this leaks 'Newer Posts' / 'Older Posts' regardless of i18n config. Fix: route through the {{t}} helper (engine.i18n) with default fallback to English.

title: build-pipeline: pipeline.ts feeds urls=routes.map() includes outputs that aren't user-facing
body: src/build/pipeline.ts line 56: `urls: routes.map((r) => r.url)`. This includes paginated archive pages, error pages (when added), and any future internal routes. Sitemap will leak them. Fix: filter to only routes where `route.indexable !== false`, and add an `indexable` field to RouteContext.

title: build: RSS includes full content:encoded; some prefer summary-only
body: Add `[components.rss].full_content = true|false` flag.

title: build: asset hashing reads whole file into memory
body: theme/assets.ts reads each file via readFile(). For big images/videos that's RAM-heavy. Stream via Bun.file().stream() into the hasher.

title: build: emit.ts copies both fingerprinted and logical asset paths (2x disk)
body: Production builds should only emit fingerprinted versions. Logical ones are useful for development and for compatibility with hand-written URLs in content; gate behind `[build].emit_logical_asset_aliases` flag (default false in production).

title: build: home and index treat as the same template but URLs collide
body: If `home.hbs` exists we route `/` to it; pagination uses index. If only `index.hbs` exists we route `/` to it. Confirm: when both exist, page 2 should go through index, not home. Test.

title: build: no per-tag or per-author RSS feed
body: Ghost emits `/tag/foo/rss/`. Add.

title: build: no redirects.yaml support
body: Ghost emits `_redirects` (Netlify) or nginx config from `redirects.yaml`. Implement.

title: build: pagination URLs hard-code '/page/N/' — Ghost allows custom pagination prefix
body: Routes.ts hard-codes `/page/N/`. Ghost lets themes override via routes.yaml. Make configurable.

title: ci: no Bun caching in CI
body: First-time CI runs will re-download all deps every time. Use `oven-sh/setup-bun@v2` with the built-in lockfile-keyed cache, plus a separate cache for `node_modules` and `bun.lock`. Saves about 30s per job at our current size.

title: ci: no PR preview deploys
body: Migrators evaluating Nectar will want to see what an example build looks like. Add a Cloudflare Pages / Netlify preview job: on PR build the `example/`, upload `example/dist`, post a sticky comment with the preview URL. Massive DX win for any PR that touches rendering.

title: ci: no coverage reporting
body: `bun test --coverage` is available but never run. Add a coverage job that uploads lcov to Codecov (or coveralls), and gate the README with a coverage badge. Helps gate PRs that drop helper coverage below threshold.

title: cli: `--config` accepts only a single path; no merge layering
body: Users who want `nectar.toml` + `nectar.local.toml` (env-specific overrides) can't. Allow `--config` to be repeatable or accept comma-separated paths and deep-merge in order. Also support env-var driven config selection (`NECTAR_ENV=production`).

title: cli: `nectar help <subcommand>` form
body: Support both `nectar build --help` and `nectar help build` (git/cargo style). Also support `nectar <sub> -h`. Currently only top-level `nectar help` works (index.ts:25-28).

title: cli: `new` always lowercases slugs via strict slugify — losing CJK info
body: `slugify('日本語')` returns empty. Use `slug` package with transliterate or accept the original as-is when ASCII percent-encode is acceptable.

title: cli: `version --check` for upstream release check
body: Add `nectar version --check` that fetches the latest release from npm or GitHub (with 1d cache in ~/.cache/nectar/release.json) and prints whether an upgrade is available. Respect NECTAR_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1. Reference: npm, deno, bun all do this.

title: cli: argument parsing — boolean default-true flags need --no-* siblings auto
body: Schema-driven: any boolean flag with default true automatically gets a `--no-<name>` parser entry. Avoid manual duplication.

title: cli: argument parsing — disallow positional after flags policy
body: Currently `nectar new --slug foo post "Hello"` would not parse correctly with indexOf. After moving to parseArgs, decide: do we allow `--flag` interleaved with positionals? Recommended yes (parseArgs default) with `allowPositionals: true, strict: true`. Document.

title: cli: argument parsing — repeated flag policy
body: Decide whether `--tag a --tag b` accumulates (preferred for list-ish flags) or last-wins. parseArgs `multiple: true` supports this. Apply consistently and call it out in --help.

title: cli: build summary line is hard to grep / parse
body: build.ts:11-13 emits `Built 42 routes (10 assets) → dist`. Add stable, parseable variants: in --json mode emit `{ event: 'build.done', ... }`; in default mode keep human text but also write the manifest. Avoid encouraging users to grep this line.

title: cli: build: stderr vs stdout semantics undocumented
body: logger.info → stdout, warn/error → stderr (util/logger.ts:9-10). Document this contract in --help so users can `nectar build > build.log` and still see errors. Add a test asserting where each level lands.

title: cli: build: support multiple --config files for layering
body: Allow `--config base.config.ts --config prod.overrides.ts` and deep-merge in order. Lets users keep a base config + environment overlays without bash gymnastics.

title: cli: discoverability — `nectar` with no args should not exit 0 silently
body: Currently `nectar` (no args) prints usage and exits 0 (index.ts:24-29). That conflates 'help requested' with 'no command'. POSIX convention is exit 1 (or 2) when invoked without a command; exit 0 only when user explicitly asks for help. Differentiate.

title: cli: editor integration — VSCode extension
body: Ship `vscode-nectar`: syntax highlighting for nectar.config.toml, schema-aware autocompletion (JSON schema published from zod), task definitions (`nectar build`, `dev`, `check`), problem matcher for build errors, snippets for new post frontmatter. Repo: `nectar/editors/vscode`.

title: cli: ensure consistent line endings on Windows
body: new.ts:33 writes `frontmatter.join('\n')`. On Windows users may end up with mixed CRLF/LF in the same repo. Decide policy (LF everywhere, matching git's autocrlf=input) and document. Add a test using a temp dir on win32 CI.

title: cli: error stack traces dumped to stderr without source maps
body: Production-builds map back to TypeScript via Bun's built-in support. Confirm `--source-map=external` or inline works; add to CI smoke.

title: cli: i18n of CLI messages — single source, en only today
body: All strings are hard-coded English (logger calls in build/new/serve/check/import-ghost). Externalise into src/cli/i18n/<locale>.json keyed by message id, with a tiny `t('build.done', { count })` helper. Detect locale via $LANG / $LC_MESSAGES. Required before community localisation PRs are even feasible.

title: cli: import-ghost name is inconsistent — should be `migrate ghost`
body: Discoverability suffers from a kebab-case top-level command unique to one source. Move under `nectar migrate ghost <file>` and keep `nectar import-ghost` as a deprecated alias for one major version, printing a warning.

title: cli: import-ghost: --keep-html option to preserve original mobiledoc/lexical-rendered HTML
body: Conversion to Markdown via turndown loses fidelity for complex Ghost cards (galleries, embeds). Provide `--keep-html` to write the rendered HTML alongside (e.g. `foo.md.html`) or to embed as a raw HTML block in the Markdown.

title: cli: import-ghost: no --include / --exclude filters
body: Add `--include-drafts`, `--include-pages`, `--only-tags news,blog`, `--since 2024-01-01`. Lets users do partial migrations safely.

title: cli: import-ghost: no streaming / progress for large exports
body: Ghost JSON exports can be 10MB+. Print a progress line per N=50 posts processed. Currently the user sees nothing until the final summary (import-ghost.ts:13-15).

title: cli: logger does not include timestamps in non-TTY mode
body: util/logger.ts:7-12 emits raw lines. CI logs benefit from leading ISO timestamps. Add when !isTTY or when NECTAR_LOG_TIMESTAMPS=1. Format: `[2025-05-19T10:00:00.000Z] info build.done`.

title: cli: missing --version --json for machine-readable version output
body: `nectar version` only prints a string. Expose `--json` form: `{ "name": "nectar", "version": "0.0.1", "bun": "...", "node": "...", "commit": "..." }`. Useful for CI matrix workflows.

title: cli: missing `nectar --print-config` to dump the resolved config
body: After loading + merging defaults, dump the final config to stdout. Use cases: debugging schema defaults, generating fixtures, pipelining to other tools. Implement as `nectar config print` (TOML/JSON via --format).

title: cli: missing `nectar fmt` for content frontmatter normalization
body: Normalize frontmatter: sort keys, lowercase tags, ISO dates with Z, ensure trailing newline. Reuse gray-matter. Idempotent. `nectar fmt --check` for CI (exit 1 if anything would change).

title: cli: missing `nectar open <slug>` to open content in editor
body: `nectar open my-first-post` resolves the slug to the file and spawns $EDITOR on it. Tiny helper that meaningfully smooths the loop of edit-build-preview.

title: cli: missing diagnostics report bundling
body: Add `nectar diagnostics bundle` that produces a tarball of: resolved config, list of content files (no bodies), theme manifest, last build manifest, last N log lines, env (redacted). For users to attach to bug reports.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar authors list`
body: List all authors with post counts. `--orphaned` to surface authors referenced by no post.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar config set <key> <value>`
body: Write a value back to nectar.config.{ts,js,toml,json}. Detect file format and preserve comments where possible (use @iarna/toml writer for TOML, AST edits for TS). For TS configs, prefer writing a sibling .nectar.local.toml override.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar config validate`
body: Like `nectar check` but config-only. Faster. Returns 0/1 with zod error pretty-printed. Replace check's config phase with a call into this.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar content delete <slug>`
body: Move (don't rm) to `.nectar/trash/<timestamp>/<slug>.md`. Always reversible. `--purge` to permanently delete after 30d.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar content show <slug>`
body: Print frontmatter + first N lines of body for a piece of content. `--frontmatter` shows only YAML. Useful for scripting and debugging.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar content touch <slug>`
body: Update the `updated_at` field (and optionally `published_at`) to now. Equivalent of `touch` for content, but frontmatter-aware. Common operation when re-publishing.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar tags merge <from> [from...] <into>`
body: Merge multiple tags into a single canonical tag. Idempotent. Common after a content audit.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar theme list`
body: Discover available themes by scanning config.theme.dir. Print: name, version (from theme package.json), path, default? (matches config.theme.name). Useful when site has multiple themes for A/B.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar theme serve`
body: Like `nectar dev` but focused on theme dev: re-renders only when theme files change, uses a small fixture set so it's fast (skip loading 1000 posts). Helpful for theme authors not building a real site.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar upgrade`
body: Self-update the binary/script. Detect install method (bun install -g, npm install -g, bunx, brew) and run the right upgrade. Or print the right command if non-self-updatable. Respect NECTAR_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1.

title: cli: new: empty title from quoted empty string crashes with bad UX
body: `nectar new post ""` prints 'A title is required' and exits 2 (new.ts:15-18). Good. But `nectar new post '   '` (only whitespace) also needs the same treatment — verify .trim() handles it (it does). Add a positive test and a friendly message: 'Title cannot be empty. Example: nectar new post "My First Post"'.

title: cli: new: no --open to launch $EDITOR after scaffold
body: After scaffolding, users almost always want to edit. Add `--open` to spawn $EDITOR (or $VISUAL) on the created file. Respect EDITOR env var. Fall back to logging the path.

title: cli: new: no --slug override
body: slug is always derived from title via slugify (new.ts:20). Add `--slug <value>` for cases where title has unicode or custom canonical slug is needed. Validate slug matches /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/.

title: cli: new: rejects only kinds 'post' and 'page' — no extensibility
body: src/cli/commands/new.ts:11 hardcodes 'post' | 'page'. Themes may want to scaffold 'author', 'tag', custom collections. Drive the list from the loaded config + theme manifest. Allow `nectar new <kind> <title>` for any known kind.

title: cli: no `--no-clean` flag and no clean-room mode
body: `build()` unconditionally `rm -rf`s the output dir. For deploy targets that hash filenames (e.g. fingerprinted assets shared with a prior build) we want `--no-clean`. Also add `nectar clean` as an explicit op.

title: cli: no `--quiet` / `--verbose` flags
body: Logger reads `NECTAR_LOG_LEVEL` env var but there's no CLI flag pair. Add `--quiet` (errors only), `--verbose` (debug), `--log-format=json|pretty`. Composable with CI pipelines.

title: cli: no exit-on-warn / warnings-as-errors flag
body: Closely related to --strict. Add a global `--warnings-as-errors` that flips logger.warn to logger.error + tracks an exit-1 condition. Useful when strict has subcommand-specific meaning.

title: cli: no progress reporter for builds
body: For sites with many posts, the user has no feedback. Add a simple spinner+counter (`Rendering 12/150...`).

title: cli: no shell completion (zsh / fish / bash)
body: Add `nectar completion zsh|bash|fish` that prints a completion script. Subcommand and flag names are stable enough to autogen. Major polish item that Ghost CLI doesn't even have.

title: cli: no spinner during expensive theme load
body: loadTheme + loadContent in pipeline.ts run in parallel but can take seconds. Show a spinner with status: 'Loading theme…', 'Compiling templates…', 'Indexing content…'. Important first-impression UX.

title: cli: no stdin handling for piped input
body: Several commands could accept stdin: `cat post.md | nectar new post --stdin`, `nectar import-ghost - < export.json`. Today no command reads stdin. Audit each subcommand and document.

title: cli: no support for project-local .nectarrc
body: Many CLIs honour a dotfile (.nectarrc.json) for per-project flag defaults. Add discovery; flags from .nectarrc are overridden by env vars and CLI flags. Mention in `nectar config path` output.

title: cli: serve does not emit access logs
body: No request logging. Add a single-line access log at `--verbose` (method, path, status, ms) to stderr.

title: cli: serve: MIME type detection is implicit
body: `new Response(Bun.file(filePath))` relies on Bun's MIME guess. For .webmanifest, .xml, .rss, this is usually fine but unverified. Add a test for the common types and an explicit Content-Type override for known SSG outputs (sitemap.xml application/xml, rss.xml application/rss+xml).
