title: cli: serve: directory traversal guard works but undocumented
body: serve.ts:25-27 checks `filePath.startsWith(distDir)`. Good. Add a test that `/..%2f..%2fetc%2fpasswd` is rejected and document this guarantee. Use `path.relative` and check it doesn't start with '..' for additional defence.

title: cli: serve: no --open flag to launch browser
body: All modern SSGs (vite, astro, eleventy --serve) open the browser to the bound URL on first start. Add `--open` flag. Use `Bun.spawn(['open', url])` on macOS, `xdg-open` on linux, `start` on win.

title: cli: serve: no caching headers in dev
body: Static server sets no Cache-Control. For local dev, set `Cache-Control: no-store` so refreshes don't show stale assets. For prod static hosts this isn't your problem, but a dev-mode default helps.

title: cli: shell completion files install location
body: Provide a `nectar completions install [--shell auto]` helper that writes to the right location: bash → /usr/local/etc/bash_completion.d, zsh → $fpath, fish → ~/.config/fish/completions, pwsh → $PROFILE. Don't require sudo by default; prefer user paths.

title: cli: support `--` end-of-options separator
body: Currently no way to pass a literal `--config` to a downstream consumer or a filename starting with `--`. Adopt parseArgs which exposes `positionals` after `--` automatically. Document in help.

title: cli: telemetry — opt-in anonymous usage stats
body: Add an opt-in telemetry pipeline (à la Next.js / Astro): subcommand invoked, duration, success/failure, nectar+bun versions, OS, anon machine id. Off by default; `nectar telemetry enable|disable|status`. Send to a configurable endpoint. Document the exact payload in TELEMETRY.md.

title: cli: telemetry — opt-in crash reports
body: On uncaught exception, prompt 'Send anonymous crash report? (y/N/never)'. Include: error class+message+sanitised stack, command line (with arg values redacted), versions. Stash never-answer in ~/.config/nectar/telemetry.json. Disabled in non-TTY by default.

title: cli: unknown subcommand should suggest closest match
body: index.ts:55-58 prints `Unknown command: X` with full usage. Use Levenshtein distance against the known command list and print `did you mean 'build'?`. Cargo, npm, and gh all do this. Exit code 2 is correct.

title: community: no GitHub Discussions / forum
body: Enable Discussions and seed a few categories: Q&A, Show & Tell (themes), Ideas, Announcements. Cheap to set up, raises perceived activity, deflects support load from issues.

title: community: no PR template
body: Add `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` with sections: summary, linked issue, theme regression check, test plan, screenshots if visual.

title: community: no release-notes template
body: Define `.github/release-notes-template.md` or a release-please section ordering: breaking, features, perf, bugfixes, deps, internal. So every release reads the same.

title: config: no support for environment-variable overrides
body: E.g. NECTAR_SITE_URL useful for staging vs prod builds.

title: config: relative paths in config are not resolved against the config file location
body: If a user passes `--config path/to/nectar.toml`, our content/theme dirs resolve from cwd not from the config dir. Document or change.

title: config: site.url must end without trailing slash; we don't normalise
body: URL joins use `endsWith('/')` to decide. A user supplying `https://example.com//` breaks. Normalise on load.

title: config: theme.dir is relative to cwd; cannot reference an installed npm theme
body: Allow `theme = "nectar-theme-foo"` to resolve from node_modules.

title: content: drafts have no preview path; no way for authors to preview unpublished work
body: We skip `status: draft` at build. To preview, an author would have to flip status and rebuild. Add a `nectar build --include-drafts` flag emitting to `dist/drafts/<slug>/index.html` with `noindex` and a banner.

title: content: empty title falls back to slug as title
body: loader.ts:normalizePost — if `data.title` is missing, we use slug as title. That produces titles like `hello-nectar` instead of erroring. Either error out (preferred) or title-case it. Currently it's silently weird.

title: content: scheduled posts not supported
body: Ghost has `status: scheduled` posts that publish at a future timestamp. We accept the status value but treat it like published. Either filter them out at build (default) or build with a `publishAt` time check + a `--rebuild-at <ISO>` hint.

title: content: slug derivation strips leading underscore filenames
body: src/content/loader.ts:slugFromPath uses `basename(filePath)`, then runs `slugify` which removes underscores. A file like `_index.md` collapses to empty string slug, then the post becomes inaccessible. Reject empty slugs at load time with a clear error pointing to the file.

title: content: tag/author auto-creation does not warn
body: loader.ts resolves unknown tag/author slugs into stub objects silently. A typo in `tags: [neews]` creates a phantom `neews` tag with a single post and no description. Emit a warning when this happens (per slug, deduped).

title: cross-theme: Tag.accent_color, Tag.og_image, Tag.twitter_image, Tag.og_title, Tag.twitter_title, Tag.codeinjection_head, Tag.codeinjection_foot fields
body: Modern themes (Solo/Ruby/Headline) read primary_tag.accent_color. Ghost also supports tag-level og_*/twitter_*/codeinjection_*. Add these to Tag in src/content/model.ts:43-54 so themes have full parity.

title: cross-theme: `body_class` per-route includes `home-template`, `paged`, `tag-template`, `author-template`, `archive-template`, `post-template`, `page-template`
body: Our computeBodyClass (engine.ts:148) emits these — but Casper CSS expects also `tag-<slug>` and `author-<slug>` on tag/author archives (we set them ✓) AND `error-template` on 404, `private-template` on password page, `home-template` only on /, not /page/2. Verify all variants.

title: cross-theme: `data-ghost-search` button → search modal JS
body: Casper/Headline/Edition/etc. all sprinkle `<button data-ghost-search>` for the search modal. Ghost auto-loads sodo-search.min.js (from shared-theme-assets) and wires this. Nectar doesn't ship sodo-search. Document and consider vendoring a static search index (Lunr/MiniSearch) wired to `data-ghost-search`.

title: cross-theme: `data-portal` / `#/portal/*` Members Portal links
body: Every Casper-family theme links to `#/portal/signin`, `#/portal/signup`, `#/portal/account`, `#/portal/account/plans` with `data-portal="signin"` etc. Ghost loads portal.min.js. Static Nectar has no portal. Document that these links go nowhere (or rewrite to /signin /signup paths and provide stubs).

title: cross-theme: `feature_image_caption` is raw HTML, not plain text
body: Every theme renders `{{feature_image_caption}}` (typically `<figcaption>{{feature_image_caption}}</figcaption>`). Ghost stores HTML here (e.g. inline links). Nectar's Post.feature_image_caption is `string|undefined` — verify markdown-loader preserves HTML and that {{feature_image_caption}} is *not* escaped. May require `{{{feature_image_caption}}}` triple-stash; survey themes to confirm.

title: cross-theme: `{{#is "home"}}` vs `{{#is "index"}}` semantic difference
body: Some themes use `{{#is "home"}}` (Casper, Edition) while others use `{{#is "index"}}`. Our is helper (blocks.ts:50) aliases home↔index — good. But verify the `home` alias is set on the first page of index pagination (route.kind=='home') and NOT on /page/2 (which should be 'index'/'paged' but not 'home'). Test.

title: cross-theme: ghost_head should emit JSON-LD with publisher.logo URL absolute, including 'sameAs' for social
body: Our ghost_head (ghost-head.ts:99) emits basic Article/WebSite JSON-LD but omits Ghost's standard fields: mainEntityOfPage, sameAs (social links from site/author), wordCount, articleBody snippet, publisher.logo width/height. Themes don't read this directly but SEO tooling does. Align JSON-LD shape with Ghost's reference output.

title: cross-theme: ghost_head should emit `<meta name="twitter:site" content="@handle">` when @site.twitter set
body: Our ghost_head misses twitter:site / twitter:creator meta tags. Ghost emits these from site.twitter and post.primary_author.twitter respectively. Add.

title: cross-theme: meta_title `page=(t " (Page %)")` hash param + locale
body: London/Alto/Wave use `{{meta_title page=(t " (Page %)")}}`. The locale-resolved string becomes the `page` hash; Nectar replaces `%` with pagination.page in content.ts:94. Verify this works end-to-end with non-English locales.

title: cross-theme: pagination helper emits HTML but themes mostly hand-roll their own
body: Alto, Edition, Wave have `partials/pagination.hbs`; some themes invoke `{{pagination}}`. The helper emits hard-coded English copy (`Newer Posts`, `Older Posts`). Switch to using {{t}} or accept hash params (newer=, older=) for i18n.

title: cross-theme: post_class needs `featured`, `no-image`, `tag-<slug>`, `image-cover`, `feature-image`
body: Our computePostClass (engine.ts:165) emits post + tag-<slug> + featured. Themes also check `no-image` (Casper/Solo). Add `no-image` when feature_image is falsy.

title: deploy: apache: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/apache.md`: `.htaccess` placement, AllowOverride, enabling mod_rewrite/mod_headers/mod_deflate, common shared-hosting gotchas.

title: deploy: apache: no Cache-Control / ETag directives
body:
Need `<FilesMatch "\.[0-9a-f]{8}\.(css|js|woff2)$">Header set Cache-Control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"</FilesMatch>` and HTML overrides.

**Fix**: include in emitter, derived from shared cache rules.

title: deploy: azure-static-web-apps: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/azure-swa.md`: SKU choice (Free/Standard), config schema, custom domain, auth providers.

title: deploy: azure-static-web-apps: no GitHub Actions workflow sample
body:
Azure SWA is typically deployed via `Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1`. No sample.

**Fix**: `examples/ci/azure-swa.yml`.

title: deploy: azure-static-web-apps: no `staticwebapp.config.json` emitter
body:
Azure Static Web Apps uses `staticwebapp.config.json` for routes, response headers, navigationFallback (=404). Nectar emits nothing.

**Fix**: `src/build/emitters/azure-swa.ts` emits `staticwebapp.config.json` with `routes`, `responseOverrides`, `globalHeaders`, `navigationFallback: { rewrite: "/404.html" }`.

title: deploy: caddy: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/caddy.md`: Caddyfile, automatic HTTPS, storage volume, reverse-proxy upgrade path.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: build output exceeds 25k-file limit not surfaced
body:
Cloudflare Pages caps free plans at 20k files (Pro at 25k). Large content-asset directories silently fail.

**Fix**: emit file-count warning in `build` summary; hard-error when target=cloudflare-pages and count > limit.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: no R2-as-image-origin recipe
body:
Cloudflare Image Resizing can pull from an R2 bucket. Nectar's `copy_content_assets: true` copies images into dist, but for large media archives, users want R2 origin + URL rewrite to `/cdn-cgi/image/<opts>/https://<bucket>.r2.dev/<key>`.

**Fix**: add `image_cdn.provider: cloudflare` adapter (see cross-cutting issue); document R2 origin path.

title: deploy: cloudflare-pages: no `_routes.json` for Functions exclusion
body:
If a user adds Cloudflare Pages Functions (e.g. `/api/*`) later, they need `_routes.json` to opt out of static asset interception. Nectar's build offers no scaffold even when Functions are absent (recommended to include `{"version":1,"include":["/*"],"exclude":[]}` for clarity).

**Fix**: emit a minimal `_routes.json` and document customization.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: HSTS preload-list eligibility not validated
body:
Sites wanting `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload` need apex + every subdomain to be HTTPS. Nectar's headers emitter could include the directive blindly, locking users out.

**Fix**: gate `preload` flag behind explicit `security_headers.hsts.preload: true` + warning.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: `nectar deploy` should detect target from env vars
body:
When invoked inside Netlify/CF Pages/Vercel/Render, `nectar deploy` should infer `--target` automatically from env (`NETLIFY=true`, `CF_PAGES=1`, `VERCEL=1`, `RENDER=true`).

**Fix**: implement detection in `deploy` command.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: `serve.ts` doesn't simulate target redirect/header behavior
body:
`src/cli/commands/serve.ts` is a vanilla static server; it doesn't read `_headers`/`_redirects` so previewing 'what production will do' is impossible.

**Fix**: add `--simulate=<target>` flag that loads the emitted artifact and applies redirect/header rules.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: emitters need conflict-detection for files Nectar would overwrite
body:
If user keeps a hand-written `_headers` in `static/`, our emitter overwrites it. Need merge or conflict-detection.

**Fix**: when target emitter writes `_headers`/`_redirects`/`vercel.json`, fail if hand-written exists unless `--force` or `merge: true`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: feeds/sitemap urls assume trailing slash; no fallback when site.url has trailing slash
body:
`src/build/feeds.ts:emitSitemap/emitRss` strips trailing slash with `replace(/\/$/, '')` but never re-validates `route.url` shape. If a user opts into `trailing_slash: 'never'`, generated sitemap entries will 301 on every crawl, killing SEO budget on cheap edge providers.

**Fix**: derive URLs from the same canonical helper used by route emitter.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no BUILD_ID / COMMIT_SHA injection into templates or build metadata
body:
Every modern target (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages, Render, Fly) exposes commit metadata via env vars. Nectar doesn't read or expose them, so themes can't render a build badge, and feeds can't include a `lastBuildDate` from CI metadata.

**Fix**: read `NECTAR_BUILD_ID`, `NECTAR_COMMIT_SHA`, `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA`, `CF_PAGES_COMMIT_SHA`, `COMMIT_REF` (Netlify) etc.; expose under `{{@site.build}}` in render context.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no Cache-Control varies between Workers/Pages/CDN edge — needs target-aware emitter
body:
Cloudflare Workers Static Assets honors `Cache-Control` from the file metadata only via specific opt-in; Pages applies `_headers`. The emitter must know the target to choose the right delivery channel.

**Fix**: emitter registry supplies `applyHeaders(file, headers)` per target.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no `.well-known/` passthrough for ACME / mta-sts / security.txt
body:
There's no convention to copy a user-supplied `static/.well-known/` directory into `dist/`. Custom-domain users (GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages w/ external CA, Caddy with non-default storage) routinely need this.

**Fix**: support a top-level `static/` (or `public/`) directory copied verbatim, same as content assets.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no `sri:` / `csp:` interaction documentation for inline scripts
body:
Themes that inline scripts (`<script>...</script>`) need `script-src 'unsafe-inline'` OR a per-script nonce/hash. Without docs and a CSP emitter that computes hashes, every theme will need 'unsafe-inline'.

**Fix**: post-process step to extract inline script bodies, compute `sha256-<b64>`, emit them in CSP.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no built-in image CDN integration (Cloudflare/Netlify/Vercel/Cloudinary/imgproxy)
body: Images are emitted as raw files from `content/images/`. There's no rewriter to push `` through Cloudflare Image Resizing (`/cdn-cgi/image/...`), Netlify Image CDN (`/.netlify/images?url=`), Vercel Image (`/_next/image`-style), Cloudinary fetch, or imgproxy. **Fix**: introduce `image_cdn:` config with adapters per target; rewrite during HTML emission (post-process step in `pipeline.ts`).

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no compression-level / minification toggle for HTML output
body:
All targets benefit from minified HTML (whitespace removal, optional attribute quote stripping). `writeHtml` writes raw rendered output.

**Fix**: optional post-process minifier (configurable on/off) in `pipeline.ts` before writing.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no documentation of per-target redirect-syntax matrix
body:
`_redirects` vs `vercel.json.redirects` vs `firebase.json.redirects` vs `.htaccess` vs nginx `return 301`. Migrators need a matrix.

**Fix**: add `docs/deployment/redirect-syntax-matrix.md`.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: no internal helper to canonicalize 'public URL' across emitters
body:
Many emitters need the absolute URL of a route. Today, `feeds.ts` recomputes it. Lifting a shared helper keeps emitters consistent.

**Fix**: add `src/build/url.ts` with `absoluteUrl(route, config)` used by sitemap, RSS, redirects, headers emitters.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: per-target dry-run / diff command missing
body:
`nectar deploy --target=<x> --dry-run` should print the files that would be emitted (and a diff against last build). Nothing exists.

**Fix**: implement as part of the new deploy command.

title: deploy: cross-cutting: redirects.yaml has no validation against existing routes
body:
If a user adds `from: /foo, to: /bar` but `/foo/index.html` also exists in `dist/`, the file wins on most providers; user is confused. Need a `nectar check` warning.

**Fix**: extend `src/cli/commands/check.ts` to flag redirect-shadows-route conflicts.

title: deploy: docker: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/docker.md`: image structure, env vars (RUNTIME_SITE_URL?), reverse-proxy patterns, healthcheck, compose.

title: deploy: docker: no HEALTHCHECK directive
body:
Container orchestrators rely on `HEALTHCHECK CMD wget -q -O /dev/null http://localhost/healthz`. Nectar's nginx conf has no `/healthz` location.

**Fix**: include in emitted conf + Dockerfile sample.

title: deploy: docker: no `.dockerignore`
body:
Without `.dockerignore`, `node_modules/`, `.git/`, `dist/` (when using multi-stage) bloat the build context.

**Fix**: ship `examples/docker/.dockerignore`.

title: deploy: docker: no docker-compose.yml sample
body:
Self-hosters with reverse proxies (Traefik, Caddy) want a compose snippet.

**Fix**: ship `examples/docker/docker-compose.yml`.

title: deploy: firebase-hosting: cleanUrls / trailingSlash conflict
body:
Same combinatorial issue as Vercel; Firebase has both flags too.

**Fix**: same validation as Vercel emitter.

title: deploy: firebase-hosting: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/firebase.md`: project creation, `firebase init hosting`, custom domain, preview channels.

title: deploy: firebase-hosting: no `firebase deploy --only hosting` workflow sample
body:
Users want a GitHub Actions workflow using `FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy`. No sample.

**Fix**: `examples/ci/firebase.yml`.

title: deploy: firebase-hosting: no `firebase.json` emitter
body:
Firebase Hosting reads `firebase.json` with `hosting.headers`, `hosting.redirects`, `hosting.rewrites`, `hosting.cleanUrls`, `hosting.trailingSlash`. Nectar emits nothing.

**Fix**: `src/build/emitters/firebase.ts` writes `firebase.json` from shared config.

title: deploy: fly: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/fly.md`: `flyctl launch`, region selection, Dockerfile, scaling, custom domain, certs.

title: deploy: fly: no healthcheck endpoint config
body:
Fly Machines need `[[services.http_checks]]` or `[[checks]]`. Nectar's emitted nginx must serve `/healthz`. No sample.

**Fix**: nginx.conf snippet exposing `/healthz` returning `200 ok`.

title: deploy: github-pages: no headers emission (impossible on GH Pages, must document)
body:
GitHub Pages does not allow setting arbitrary response headers. CSP/HSTS must be set as `<meta http-equiv>` in HTML, with the limitations that implies (no HSTS preload, no frame-ancestors). Nectar should document this hard limitation and offer meta-tag fallback.

**Fix**: docs note; meta-tag CSP emitter activated when target=github-pages.

title: deploy: github-pages: project-site `base_path` not auto-derived from repo name
body:
For `https://user.github.io/repo/` the build needs `base_path = '/repo/'`. CI runs blind unless user manually sets it. The repo name is available via `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`.

**Fix**: in `loadConfig`, if `GITHUB_PAGES=true` and no custom domain configured, derive `base_path` from `${GITHUB_REPOSITORY##*/}`.

title: deploy: netlify: no GitHub Actions sample (for non-Git deploy)
body:
Some teams run Netlify via `netlify deploy --prod --dir=dist` from GitHub Actions instead of Git integration. No sample exists.

**Fix**: `examples/ci/netlify.yml` using `netlify/actions/cli@master`.

title: deploy: netlify: no Image CDN URL rewriter
body: Netlify Image CDN serves `/.netlify/images?url=&w=&q=`. Nectar doesn't rewrite `` tags. Migrators with thousands of Ghost-uploaded images suffer slow LCP. **Fix**: see cross-cutting image CDN issue; ship Netlify adapter that rewrites local `/content/images/...` URLs.

title: deploy: render: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/render.md`: Blueprint, custom domain, pull-request previews, redirects/headers reuse.

title: deploy: render: no `_redirects`/`_headers` format support docs
body:
Render supports Netlify-style `_redirects`/`_headers` files in the publish dir. Document that the Netlify emitter is reused.

**Fix**: docs note in `docs/deployment/render.md`.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no ACL / public-access-block validation
body:
Modern S3 enforces Block Public Access by default; misconfigurations either expose the bucket directly or block CloudFront. Nectar has no `nectar deploy --target=s3 --preflight` check.

**Fix**: deploy command should `aws s3api get-bucket-policy-status` and warn if public.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no Brotli encoding for upload (S3-side)
body:
S3 doesn't transparently brotli; CloudFront's automatic compression only applies to gzip for some content types and not all (no brotli without origin pre-compression). Pre-compress and upload `.br` siblings, set `Content-Encoding: br` on those keys.

**Fix**: precompress pipeline emits `.br`/`.gz`; deploy command sets per-object content-encoding.

title: deploy: s3-cloudfront: no lifecycle rule guidance for log buckets / old fingerprinted assets
body:
Old hashed assets accumulate forever; access logs accumulate forever. Users blow past free tier without lifecycle rules.

**Fix**: docs in `docs/deployment/s3-cloudfront.md` covering lifecycle: expire `assets/built/` non-current versions after 30 days; transition access logs to Glacier.

title: deploy: vercel: Vercel Image not integrated
body:
Vercel Image (`/_vercel/image?url=&w=&q=`) is automatically available for static deploys. No rewriter exists in Nectar.

**Fix**: see cross-cutting image CDN; add `image_cdn.provider: vercel` adapter.

title: deploy: vercel: `output: 'export'`-style guidance not present (Vercel adapter limitations)
body:
Vercel auto-detects static output but unique to Nectar's CLI, the detection may fail without `vercel.json`'s `outputDirectory: 'dist'`. Document explicitly.

**Fix**: docs + sample `vercel.json` minimal config.

title: deploy: vercel: no GitHub Actions / CLI sample
body:
Users wanting non-Git deploys via `vercel --prod` lack a sample workflow.

**Fix**: `examples/ci/vercel.yml` using `amondnet/vercel-action`.

title: docs: no CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
body: Adopt Contributor Covenant 2.1.

title: docs: no PR template
body: Add `.github/pull_request_template.md` with summary / test plan / breaking change checkboxes.

title: docs: no `examples/` directory beyond `example/`
body: We have one `example/` for tests. Real users want: minimal-blog, photo-blog, multi-author, multi-language, members-style-with-stripe-redirect, newsletter+rss-only. Each as a runnable folder. Forms the basis of deploy templates (e.g. `cloudflare-pages-template`).

title: docs: no `nectar` CLI reference page
body: Auto-emit usage docs from each command's help output.

title: docs: no config reference auto-generated from Zod schema
body: Use `zod-to-json-schema` + a script to emit `docs/config-reference.md` and keep it in sync via CI.

title: docs: no issue templates
body: Add `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,ghost-compat}.yml`.

title: dx: TypeScript path alias `~/*` works in tsc but not always at runtime in Bun without explicit config
body: Bun honours `tsconfig.compilerOptions.paths` natively. Verify by running on a clean machine and add to CI.

title: dx: no `nectar deploy` quickstart
body: Add thin wrappers: `nectar deploy cloudflare`, `nectar deploy netlify`, `nectar deploy vercel`, `nectar deploy gh-pages`. Each one prints/applies the canonical config snippet. The aim isn't to replace those tools, it's to drop the "how do I deploy this" friction to one command on day one.

title: dx: no `nectar doctor` self-diagnostic
body: Like `brew doctor` / `flutter doctor`. Print Bun version, Nectar version, detected `nectar.toml`, detected theme, content counts, missing required directories, environment variables, suspicious config (e.g. relative `site.url`). First thing maintainers ask in issues anyway.

title: ghost-card: ALL: a11y — alt text required for image/gallery/product/header background
body: Ghost permits empty alt; many imported posts have it. Static-site generators should warn at build time on missing alts. **Fix**: build-time linter pass that walks rendered HTML, flags `` without alt (or alt="" on non-decorative images). Fail build with `--strict` or just warn.

title: ghost-card: ALL: contributor docs — no guide for adding a new card
body:
Adding a new card today requires touching: `import.ts` (Turndown rule), `markdown.ts` (shortcode/directive), shortcode handler, theme CSS, fixture, snapshot test. There's no documented checklist.

**Fix**: add `docs/contrib/ADDING_A_CARD.md` with the 6-step checklist. Reference an exemplar (the first card we fully implement).

title: ghost-card: ALL: error recovery — malformed shortcode shouldn't crash the build
body:
If a shortcode is malformed (unclosed `{% toggle %}` without `{% /toggle %}`), the build should report a clear error and continue (or skip the post), not crash with a marked stack trace.

**Fix**: wrap shortcode parsing in try/catch; emit a structured build error with post path + line number; continue building other posts.

title: ghost-card: ALL: migration — duplicate `<h1>` injection (post.title + first content h1)
body:
Ghost stores the post title separately from the body; the body should never start with `<h1>`. But some imports (especially from RSS or Substack mirrors) embed an h1 in the body too. After Turndown, we end up with two h1s on the page (template-emitted + body-leading). Bad for SEO and a11y.

**Fix**: in `renderPostBody`, detect a leading `<h1>` whose text matches `post.title` and strip it.

title: ghost-card: ALL: render — `loading="lazy"` on first feature_image hurts LCP
body:
Browsers prioritise images marked `loading="eager"` (default) for LCP. If every image is forced to `loading="lazy"` for size savings, the feature image / first hero image becomes slower. Tradeoff: above-the-fold images should be eager, below should be lazy.

**Fix**: figure-promotion pass marks only the first in-body image (when no `feature_image`) or the `feature_image` itself as `loading="eager" fetchpriority="high"`. All others lazy.

title: ghost-card: ALL: render — `marked-gfm-heading-id` adds `[id]` to headings, breaks card-spacing :not([id]) selector
body:
Source CSS uses `:not([id])` in `.gh-content :not(.kg-card):not(table):not([id]) + :is(.kg-card, table)` to avoid the spacing rule when an anchor heading is followed by a card. Our `marked-gfm-heading-id` plugin auto-adds an `id` to every heading — which is correct behavior for permalinks, but a `<h2 id="foo">` followed by a `<figure class="kg-card">` no longer matches the rule (`[id]` excluded).

Result: spacing between a heading and a card is wrong by ~1.5rem.

**Fix**: confirm the Source CSS actually wants `[id]` excluded (Ghost's intent: keep spacing tight when an inline anchor sits between content). Either remove the `:not([id])` from Source CSS, or restrict heading-id assignment to skip headings followed by cards.

title: ghost-card: ALL: round-trip — exported markdown should re-export to identical Ghost HTML
body:
A useful invariant for migration confidence: take Ghost HTML → Turndown to markdown → marked back to HTML → diff against original (modulo whitespace and irrelevant attrs). If the diff is nonempty we've lost something. This is the property test for migration fidelity.

**Fix**: add `tests/ghost/round-trip.test.ts` that iterates the card-corpus fixture and computes a normalised diff per card. Acceptance: zero diff for currently-supported cards; documented expected-loss for currently-unsupported.

title: ghost-card: ALL: validation — no schema validation of shortcodes / directives at build time
body:
Once we add shortcodes, authors will make typos (`{% bookark %}`, missing required params). With no validator, the build silently emits literal `{% bookark %}` text in HTML.

**Fix**: every shortcode handler validates required params; unknown shortcode names fail the build with a file:line:col pointer.

title: ghost-card: bookmark: oEmbed fetch — no cache, no rate-limit, no offline mode
body:
If we add build-time oEmbed resolution for bookmark/embed cards, we need a persistent cache (`cache/embeds/<sha>.json`), TTL, and a `--offline` mode that uses cached data only (CI runs, dev rebuilds).

**Fix**: design a cache layer in `src/build/cache.ts` shared between bookmark, embed, and (future) image probing.

title: ghost-card: button: rendering — Source CSS styles `.kg-card.kg-button-card .kg-btn` but no alignment fallback
body:
`screen.css:2357` styles `.kg-card.kg-button-card .kg-btn`. The button card requires the parent `.kg-card.kg-button-card` to be `display: flex; justify-content: <left|center>`. That rule is missing from Source — buttons will collapse to baseline left regardless of `kg-align-center`.

**Fix**: add `.kg-button-card { display: flex; } .kg-button-card.kg-align-left { justify-content: flex-start; } .kg-button-card.kg-align-center { justify-content: center; }` to Source CSS, or vendor Koenig's button CSS.

title: ghost-card: button: rendering — no markdown shortcut to author a button
body:
There is no syntax in our marked pipeline to write a button. Authors stuck with `[text](url)` inline links cannot promote to a CTA.

**Fix**: introduce a `{% button %}` shortcode or directive (`:::button`). Document in markdown reference.

title: ghost-card: callout: emoji-icon — emoji vs SVG icon distinction lost
body:
Callouts can have either an emoji (`
💡
`) or no icon (`kg-callout-card-without-emoji` modifier). Turndown loses both. A callout without an emoji ends up with the emoji slot empty in our rendered output, breaking layout. **Fix**: shortcode handles three cases: emoji, custom emoji, no-icon. Renderer applies the `kg-callout-card-without-emoji` class when no icon.

title: ghost-card: callout: nested formatting — bold/links inside callout-text lost
body:
Callout body supports inline markdown (bold, italic, links). Turndown handles inline OK, but the wrapping `kg-callout-text` div is lost — so the inline content lands directly in our shortcode body. If our shortcode is `:::callout\n**bold** text\n:::` it parses fine; if it's `{% callout body="…" %}` the body would need HTML-escaping.

**Fix**: choose directive syntax (`:::callout`) over inline-arg shortcode for cards with rich body content.

title: ghost-card: code: caption — figcaption on `kg-code-card` lost
body:
Ghost code cards can have a figcaption that themes render as a caption below the code. Turndown drops it.

**Fix**: when emitting a fenced block from a `kg-code-card`, append the caption as a special trailing line (e.g. `\n^ Caption text` parsed by a marked extension) or use a shortcode `{% code lang=… caption="…" %}…{% /code %}`.

title: ghost-card: code: lexical preservation — language stored on lexical card not always reflected in `<code class>`
body:
When Ghost renders to HTML, the lexical code card writes `<pre><code class="language-X">`. But some old posts have `language` on the `<pre>` instead. Some have it nowhere (the editor stored it in the lexical node but the HTML renderer dropped it). Going through Turndown without language inference yields fenced code blocks with no language.

**Fix**: when parsing lexical (the proper migration), read `node.language` directly from the lexical JSON. When falling back to Turndown HTML, look for `data-language` / `class="language-*"` on both `<pre>` and `<code>`.

title: ghost-card: code: theme-dev — no `.kg-code-card` selectors in Source CSS
body:
Source theme has no `kg-code-card` rules; code blocks render in whatever generic `pre, code` styling exists. No figcaption, no line numbers, no copy-button.

**Fix**: add `.kg-code-card { … } .kg-code-card pre { … } .kg-code-card figcaption { … }` to Source CSS, or vendor Koenig's code-card CSS. Document line-number contract (`.kg-code-card-with-line-numbers pre` adds counters via CSS).

title: ghost-card: email: rendering — no concept of "email-only content" in Nectar
body:
Nectar generates static HTML only. There is no newsletter rendering path. So `email` and `email-cta` cards have no positive render target — they should always be stripped from the body.

However, the **plaintext/excerpt** generators (`src/content/markdown.ts:htmlToPlaintext`) may still see content if migration leaked it through. Audit excerpt/feed generation to confirm.

**Fix**: confirm the migration strip is the single source of truth; add doc note in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` that email-only cards are dropped on import. If users want newsletter generation, file a separate epic.

title: ghost-card: embed: codepen/gist/figma/spotify/soundcloud — provider-specific data lost
body:
Ghost supports a long tail of oEmbed providers: CodePen, GitHub Gist, Figma, Spotify, SoundCloud, Loom, Bandcamp, Apple Music, Pinterest, Reddit, Slideshare, etc. Each has its own embed HTML shape — iframe, blockquote+script, or pure script.

Our Turndown pass treats them all uniformly (badly). The migrator must keep the source URL at minimum so the renderer can pick the right provider strategy.

**Fix**: list supported providers in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` with their migration target shape. For unsupported providers, fall back to a bookmark-style link with the URL.

title: ghost-card: embed: instagram — `<script async src="//www.instagram.com/embed.js">` only loaded once per page
body:
If a post has 3 Instagram embeds, we want to inject the embed.js once, not three times. Same for Twitter and TikTok.

**Fix**: in `ghost-foot` helper, collect a `Set<EmbedProvider>` during render and emit each provider's script tag at most once at the end of body.

title: ghost-card: embed: lazy-load — embeds never lazy-load by default
body:
Heavy embeds (YouTube ~1MB, Twitter widgets ~250KB) load eagerly today. Browsers support `<iframe loading="lazy">` natively; we should always emit it. For Twitter/Instagram scripts, only inject the script tag when at least one card of that provider exists on the page.

**Fix**:
1. Embed renderer always emits `loading="lazy"` on iframes.
2. Per-page collect a set of embed providers used; inject provider scripts conditionally in `{{ghost_foot}}`.
3. Optionally support `<lite-youtube>` / `lite-vimeo-embed` for click-to-play.

title: ghost-card: embed: twitter — Twitter blockquote needs `?dnt=1` for privacy
body:
Ghost emits `
…
` for privacy. Turndown converts the blockquote to `> Tweet text`, losing the `twitter-tweet` class and `data-dnt`. The embed hydration script never fires. **Fix**: Turndown rule for `blockquote.twitter-tweet` preserves it as raw HTML (via `keep`) or converts to a `{% twitter url="…" %}` shortcode.

title: ghost-card: gallery: caption — gallery figcaption lost on migration
body:
Galleries can have a figcaption attached to the outer figure. Turndown drops it (figure → transparent, figcaption → likely emitted as a stray italic line). Authors lose gallery captions on import.

**Fix**: gallery shortcode accepts `caption="…"`. Turndown rule preserves it.

title: ghost-card: gallery: image-pipeline — no srcset/sizes for in-gallery images
body:
Ghost generates 600w/1000w/1600w/2400w responsive variants for gallery images and emits `srcset` + `sizes` matching the grid breakpoint. Our import strips all of this (`turndown` produces a bare `![alt](src)`), and the markdown pipeline never re-adds it.

**Fix**: when image pipeline lands, the gallery shortcode must request multi-resolution variants from the asset pipeline and emit srcset matching Ghost's contract: `sizes="(min-width: 720px) 720px, 100vw"`.

title: ghost-card: gif: migration — Giphy `` source set collapses to first `` only
body: Ghost GIF card (from `/giphy` slash command or Tenor) renders as: ``. Some exports use `` with multiple `` (MP4 vs GIF for bandwidth) — Turndown drops `` and ``, keeping only the inner ``. Result: animated MP4 fallback (which is ~10× smaller) is lost; users always download the full GIF. **Fix**: Turndown rule for `picture` (or for image cards whose `src` contains `giphy.com`/`tenor.com`) → emit a shortcode with both MP4 and GIF sources. Same rendering target as image but ``-wrapped.

title: ghost-card: html: theme-dev — no `.kg-card.kg-html-card` selector means custom HTML doesn't inherit card width modifiers
body:
Ghost wraps html cards as `
…
`. The Source CSS for `kg-width-wide`/`kg-width-full` (`screen.css:411,416`) keys off `.kg-width-*` directly, so widths work. But there's no `.kg-html-card` rule for spacing; the card-spacing rules at lines 2273-2289 (`:not(.kg-card) + .kg-card`) match it correctly only if the outer element has `kg-card` class. **Fix**: ensure Turndown converter keeps the `
` wrapper when emitting back. If converting to a `{% raw %}` shortcode, the renderer must re-wrap with the same classes.

title: ghost-card: image: anchor wrap — `<a>` around image lost or kept randomly
body: Linked images (``) are common. Turndown's default produces `[![alt](src)](href)`. Marked converts this back to a paragraph with an anchor wrapping the img — usually correctly. But the surrounding `figure` wrapper is gone, so theme styling and figcaption don't apply. **Fix**: the figure-promotion pass must detect `` and emit ``.

title: ghost-card: image: rendering — caption Markdown not parsed (Ghost renders inline Markdown in figcaption)
body:
Ghost allows `**bold**` and `[links](url)` inside figcaptions. They're stored as HTML in the lexical document, rendered as HTML by Ghost. Our migration drops the figcaption to bare text and our renderer (no figcaption pipeline) wouldn't re-parse markdown in it either.

**Fix**: when caption arrives at the image shortcode, run it through a *small* inline-only marked pass (no block-level, no nested figures).

title: ghost-card: paywall: theme-dev — Ghost themes expect `{{#has visibility="public"}}` to gate snippets
body:
Ghost themes such as Casper render different UI when a post is `visibility:"members"|"paid"`. Source has scattered `{{#has visibility=…}}` checks. We expose `post.visibility` in frontmatter, but the `{{#has}}` helper may not branch on visibility correctly today.

**Fix**: audit `src/render/helpers/flow.ts` for the `has` helper's `visibility=` predicate, ensure parity with Ghost's contract (https://ghost.org/docs/themes/helpers/has/). Add tests for `{{#has visibility="public,members,paid"}}` matching.

title: ghost-card: product: migration — Turndown flattens product card into prose paragraphs
body:
Ghost product card: `

Title

×N

…

Buy
`. Turndown produces: an `` link, then `#### Title`, then star glyphs (or nothing — empty spans), then description prose, then `[Buy](url)`. Star rating is lost (encoded as span count), button-ness is lost. **Fix**: Turndown rule → `{% product image="…" title="…" rating="4" description="…" button="Buy" href="…" %}`.

title: ghost-card: product: rendering — Source CSS expects exact image+title+button structure
body:
`screen.css:2419` styles `.kg-product-card.kg-card .kg-product-card-image`, `:2423` styles `a.kg-product-card-button`, `:2428` styles `.kg-product-card-title`, `:2436` styles `.kg-product-card-description :is(p, ul, ol)`. The DOM tree must be exactly: image → title → rating → description → button (or `:is(p, ul, ol)` selector for description breaks).

**Fix**: when shortcode handler is implemented, lock DOM order and add a snapshot test.

title: ghost-card: signup: form-action — no built-in plumbing to a backend, default `action="#"` is silent failure
body:
Today `src/render/helpers/content.ts:subscribe_form` emits `<form action="#" method="post">`. Clicking submit reloads the page with no submission. There's no warning, no validation, nothing logged.

**Fix**: when `members.provider = 'none'`, log a build warning that signup forms are no-ops; emit a clear `data-nectar-noop` attribute and a console.warn at runtime.

title: ghost-card: signup: provider integration — Buttondown/ConvertKit/Mailchimp adaptors not implemented
body:
A static site needs a third-party newsletter backend. Each provider has a different form action URL and required hidden inputs. No adaptors exist today.

**Fix**: implement `src/render/helpers/members/*.ts` with one file per provider (buttondown, convertkit, mailchimp, listmonk, customformaction). Provider selection via `nectar.config.ts`. Document required env vars / API keys.

title: ghost-card: signup: theme-dev — no Source CSS for kg-signup-card
body:
Source theme has no `kg-signup-*` rules. The card's `kg-signup-card-image-bottom` / `-left` / `-top` layout variants, the disclaimer styling, and the input/button skin all depend on Koenig-injected styles.

**Fix**: vendor signup-card CSS or build a minimal stand-in that respects the layout variants Ghost authors use. Document the contract in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`.

title: ghost-card: toggle: nested formatting — toggle content can have lists/code/images
body:
Toggle bodies can contain any block-level content (lists, code, images, even nested cards). An inline-arg shortcode like `{% toggle title="…" body="…" %}` can't carry nested blocks. Need a paired-block shortcode `{% toggle title="…" %}…body markdown…{% /toggle %}` or directive `:::toggle{title="…"} … :::`.

**Fix**: align on directive syntax. Confirm marked extension supports nested block parsing inside the directive body.

title: ghost-card: toggle: rendering — no markdown directive for toggles
body:
Even if `
` is the chosen output, marked's default HTML pass-through accepts `
` only when the author hand-writes it. We need a directive (`:::toggle` or `> [!TOGGLE]`) so authors don't have to write HTML. **Fix**: add a marked extension mapping a directive to `

…

…
`.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `comments_access`, `comments_enabled`
body:
Casper post.hbs guards subscribe CTA with `{{#unless @site.comments_enabled}}`. Themes also branch on `@site.comments_access` to decide what footer/comments UI to show. Our `SiteData` lacks both.

Fix: add to schema and config; default `comments_enabled = false`.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `recommendations_enabled`
body:
Source's post-list partial guards the recommendations sidebar with `{{#if @site.recommendations_enabled}}`. We never set this field on `SiteData`. As a result the recommendations block is always hidden — even if a user wants to render a static list.

Fix: add `recommendations_enabled` to `SiteData`, default false, and let users opt in via config.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing portal fields (portal_button, portal_button_icon, portal_button_signup_text, portal_button_style, portal_name, portal_plans, portal_signup_checkbox_required, portal_signup_terms_html, signup_url)
body:
Ghost themes that render their own portal triggers read these `@site.*` fields to honour admin settings. We don't expose any of them. Themes coded to gate on `@site.portal_button` will silently always hide the subscribe button.

Fix: add a `[site.portal]` config sub-table that surfaces all `portal_*` keys.

title: ghost-compat: Channels (multi-tag aggregation routes) not supported
body:
Ghost's `controller: channel` lets users define routes like `/apple-news/` with `filter: tag:[iphone,ipad,mac]`. We have no concept of channels — themes won't render them.

Fix: in `planRoutes`, after loading routes.yaml, emit channel routes with the appropriate `template` and filtered post list.

title: ghost-compat: Custom static routes (`/features/: features`) not supported
body:
Ghost lets you map any URL to any template. We have no mechanism to add additional static routes. Themes shipping with companion routes.yaml (e.g. a `/podcast/` overview page) need this.

Fix: support route entries `path: template` and `path: { template, data, content_type }`.

title: ghost-compat: Per-collection RSS not supported (`/blog/rss/` for collection `/blog/`)
body:
Ghost auto-emits RSS at `<collection-path>/rss/`. Currently we emit only a single global RSS (in `components.rss`). Themes with multiple collections lose feeds.

Fix: once collections land, emit RSS per collection.

title: ghost-compat: Post `uuid` field absent — themes use it for RSS guid and analytics
body:
Ghost posts have a `uuid` distinct from `id`. We don't model `uuid`. Themes that emit `<guid>{{uuid}}</guid>` get nothing.

Fix: add `uuid` to Post (synthesize via `crypto.randomUUID` and persist if needed for stable RSS).

title: ghost-compat: Sitemap `lastmod` should reflect updated_at, not just published_at
body:
Ghost uses the latest of updated_at / published_at. We should follow suit so search engines refresh on edits.

Fix: use Math.max(updated, published) timestamp.

title: ghost-compat: Sitemap items must include `<image:image>` with feature images
body:
Ghost includes Schema.org image extension in sitemaps (image:loc, image:caption). Search engines use this for image indexing.

Fix: when emitting sitemap-posts.xml, add `<image:image><image:loc>…</image:loc></image:image>` for each post with a feature_image.

title: ghost-compat: Taxonomies are hard-coded to `/tag/` and `/author/` paths
body:
Ghost's routes.yaml lets you remap or disable taxonomies (e.g. `tag: /topics/{slug}/`). We hard-code `/tag/<slug>/` and `/author/<slug>/` in `src/build/routes.ts:65` and `:90`. Cannot customise.

Fix: derive these paths from routes.yaml (or `[taxonomies]` config block).

title: ghost-compat: `<base>` tag for `base_path` deployment not emitted, but theme image URLs assume site root
body:
Sites hosted at subpaths need either a `<base href>` or rewritten URLs. We have neither. Combined with the navigation/asset URL gaps, subpath hosting is broken.

Fix: rewrite all URLs at emit time; optionally emit `<base href>` for fully relative HTML.

title: ghost-compat: `@custom` evaluation order — defaults from package.json should be overridden by user config
body:
Our `buildCustom` in `src/render/engine.ts:141` spreads `pkg.customDefaults` first, then `config.theme.custom`. Correct precedence. But for `select`-type defaults, we set the first option (`pkg.ts:36`) when no default is declared — Ghost leaves the value unset (null) so themes branch differently. Our `{{#match @custom.foo "Bar"}}` will incorrectly match the first option even when admin hadn't picked.

Fix: don't synthesize a default; let `@custom.foo` be undefined unless explicitly set.

title: ghost-compat: `@member.paid` access path always undefined — themes branch on it everywhere
body:
Source footer/header use `{{#unless @member.paid}}` to upsell. Since `@member` is undefined, `@member.paid` is a property-access on undefined which Handlebars renders as empty. The branch behaves correctly (falsy) but if a theme uses `{{@member.paid}}` directly to render text, it gets empty string.

Fix: replace `member: undefined` with a Proxy that returns undefined for any path — same falsy semantics but no property-of-undefined warnings in strict mode.

title: ghost-compat: `@member` is `undefined`, but Ghost themes use `{{#unless @member}}` extensively — verify our handling
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:138` sets `member: undefined`. Handlebars treats `{{#unless undefined}}` as truthy → the inverse runs, which is what themes want for "not signed in". But the docs `{{#if @member}}` should be falsy → fall-through. Verify both paths with tests.

Fix: test coverage.

title: ghost-compat: `@site.url` should never include trailing slash when accessed by themes — verify normalisation
body:
Ghost normalises `@site.url` to drop the trailing slash. Our config allows arbitrary input. If a user sets `url = "https://example.com/"`, themes that do `{{@site.url}}/about/` produce `//about/`.

Fix: strip trailing slash from `site.url` at config load.

title: ghost-compat: `Author` model missing `og_image`, `twitter_image`, `codeinjection_*`, `accent_color`
body:
Same as tags — per-author social/SEO overrides are missing. Themes that show per-author social images on archive pages won't get them.

Fix: extend `Author` model in `src/content/model.ts` and parsing in `normalizeAuthor`.

title: ghost-compat: `Tag` model missing `og_image`, `og_title`, `og_description`, `twitter_image`, `twitter_title`, `twitter_description`, `codeinjection_head`, `codeinjection_foot`, `canonical_url`
body:
Ghost tags have per-tag SEO and OG overrides. Our model only has meta_title/meta_description. The `tag.hbs` ghost_head won't pick up these fields for tag-archive OG sharing.

Fix: add to `Tag` model and `normalizeTag` in `src/content/loader.ts`.

title: ghost-compat: `body_class` missing `post-template-<slug>`, `page-template-<slug>`, `author-template-<slug>`, `tag-template-<slug>` per-resource modifiers
body:
Ghost emits `post-template-<slug>` etc. so themes can target a specific post in CSS. Our `computeBodyClass` only emits the generic `post-template`. Themes with per-post CSS rules need the per-slug class.

Fix: append `post-template-${post.slug}` (and equivalent for page/tag/author).

title: ghost-compat: `data-ghost-search` triggers do nothing without Sodo-search SDK
body:
Source's `search-toggle.hbs` and header search emit `<button data-ghost-search>`. Ghost loads Sodo-search via ghost_head; we don't. Static-site search needs an alternative wiring (e.g. Pagefind hooked into the same data attribute).

Fix: when `[components.search]` is configured (e.g. with pagefind), emit the necessary scripts and rewire the attribute.

title: ghost-compat: `data-portal` attribute is preserved in raw HTML but never wired up
body:
Source's navigation/footer/cta emit `<button data-portal="signup">`. Without Ghost's Portal script (`portal.min.js`), these buttons do nothing in the rendered site. Theme appears broken to end users.

Fix: document the limitation; provide an optional `[components.portal]` that injects a polyfill or no-op handler.

title: ghost-compat: `data: post.slug` / `data: page.slug` linking on custom routes not supported
body:
Ghost can attach a post/page object to a custom route so a static template can render it. We have no equivalent.

Fix: implement after routes.yaml; allow `data` field to inject a single resource into the route's render context.

title: ghost-compat: `excerpt` field default truncation length differs from Ghost (Ghost uses 50 words; we use 200 chars)
body:
`src/content/loader.ts:200` does `rendered.plaintext.slice(0, 200)`. Ghost's default excerpt is 50 words of plaintext (no trailing whitespace handling). The visible result differs from Ghost rendering, breaking visual parity in card views that don't pass `words=` / `characters=`.

Fix: change to first 50 words (matching Ghost).

title: ghost-compat: `post_class` missing `no-image`, `featured`, `access` modifiers
body:
Ghost emits `no-image` when post has no feature image, and visibility-based access classes. Our `computePostClass` in `src/render/engine.ts:165` only handles `tag-<slug>` + `featured`.

Fix: append `no-image` when `!post.feature_image`, append `access` / `members-only` / `paid-only` based on visibility.

title: ghost-compat: `readable_url` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's `{{readable_url}}` outputs a human-friendly URL (strips protocol, www, trailing slash). Used in bookmark cards. Not registered in `src/render/helpers/`.

Fix: register `readable_url` helper.

title: ghost-compat: `social_accounts` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's `{{#social_accounts}}` block iterates connected social handles on the site or author. Themes use it to render social icons dynamically. Not in our helper list.

Fix: register a block helper that iterates `[twitter, facebook, instagram, ...]` present on the active context.

title: ghost-compat: `{{!< layout}}` directive regex requires the directive to be on the first line
body:
`src/render/layouts.ts:1` regex is anchored with `^\s*\{\{!<\s*…\}\}\s*`. If a theme has a comment or BOM before the directive, layout extension breaks silently and the template renders without the wrapper. Ghost is robust to whitespace and comments.

Fix: strip leading comments `{{!-- … --}}` and BOMs before applying the regex.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#match}}` does not support `or` chains (Ghost supports `{{else match}}`) — verify behaviour
body:
Source heavily uses `{{#match … }} … {{else match …}} … {{/match}}`. Handlebars treats `{{else match …}}` as `{{else}}{{#match …}}…{{/match}}` (a chained helper). Our `matchHelper` doesn't track the chained-else state but Handlebars handles it natively. Verify with a test.

Fix: add explicit test for the `else match` chain.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#match}}` truthy-only mode swallows the inverse return on inline (non-block) invocation
body:
When `options.fn` is undefined (inline `{{match …}}`), our `matchHelper` returns the raw `result` boolean. Ghost returns the empty string for false and the matched value for true. Themes inline'ing `{{match val "x"}}` to print a class may get the literal string "false".

Fix: when inline and `!result`, return empty string. When inline and `result` and only one arg, return the truthy operand.

title: ghost-compat: `{{> "icons/avatar"}}` partial doesn't fall back gracefully when missing
body:
Source's post.hbs references `{{> "icons/avatar"}}`. Handlebars throws if the partial is missing — but we register many. A user-supplied theme missing an icon partial will crash the build, not skip the icon.

Fix: register a fallback `missing-partial` that renders an empty string and warns.

title: ghost-compat: `{{> partial}}` paths use POSIX separators internally; Windows users on raw paths fail
body: We normalise to `/` already, but spot-check tests on Windows.

title: ghost-compat: `{{comment_count}}` helper not implemented
body:
Themes display "3 comments" via `{{comment_count empty="" singular="comment" plural="comments"}}`. Our helpers do not register this. Templates using it render `(empty string)`.

Fix: register `{{comment_count}}` returning 0 with proper singular/plural handling.

title:
ghost-compat: `{{comments}}` outputs `
` — Ghost emits a full Comments embed
body: Our stub in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:112` emits a div with `data-nectar-comments`. Ghost emits `

title: ghost-compat: `{{concat}}` does not accept SafeStrings (loses HTML safety)
body:
Our `concatHelper` in `src/render/helpers/strings.ts:5` calls `args.slice(0, -1).map(String)` which on a SafeString instance returns `[object Object]`. Themes that concat a partial output with a literal will get garbage.

Fix: detect SafeString and call `.toString()` properly; consider returning SafeString when any input is SafeString.

title: ghost-compat: `{{content}}` `words=` option truncates HTML by stripping tags, breaking semantic markup
body:
Our `truncateWords` in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:147` regex-strips all HTML and joins by space — the result is plaintext, not HTML, so the SafeString wrapper outputs broken excerpts in places themes expect HTML.

Fix: write a real HTML-aware truncator that closes open tags (e.g. truncate-html or a hand-rolled one).

title: ghost-compat: `{{contrast_text_color}}` and `{{color_to_rgba}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost utility helpers: convert colors. Used by themes to derive a readable text color from a user-chosen background (Source's inline JS replicates this manually, but Casper uses the helper). Absent in our helpers.

Fix: register both: `color_to_rgba("#fff", alpha=0.5)` returns `rgba(…)`; `contrast_text_color("#fff")` returns `"dark"` or `"light"`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{link_class}}` only matches exact URL, not subpaths
body:
`src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:50` compares with `normaliseUrl` (strips trailing slash). Ghost matches on subpath: e.g. `/tag/news/` should make the `/tag/` nav item active. Our impl returns empty.

Fix: when the `for` URL is a prefix of the current URL, treat as active.

title: ghost-compat: `{{link}}` helper does not auto-add `nav-current` / active class based on URL match
body:
Ghost's `{{#link}}` automatically adds an active class when its `href` matches the current route. Useful for navigation. Our impl does not. Themes calling `{{#link href="/about/" activeClass="current"}}` get no auto-class.

Fix: integrate the `link_class` logic into `link` so the active class is auto-injected.

title: ghost-compat: `{{link}}` helper does not honour `data-*` attributes (e.g. data-portal)
body:
Ghost's `{{#link}}…{{/link}}` accepts arbitrary `data-*` hash attributes. Our `src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:40` only honours `href`, `class`, `target`. Themes that do `{{#link href="…" data-portal="signin"}}` will lose the `data-portal` attribute.

Fix: pass through any `data-*` hash attr verbatim.

title: ghost-compat: `{{meta_description}}` falls back to `excerpt` instead of `site.description` when on tag/author routes
body:
On tag pages, our `metaDescriptionHelper` returns `ctx.meta_description ?? ctx.excerpt ?? site.description`. But on a tag context, `excerpt` doesn't exist (the Tag model has no excerpt). Same for authors. Tag pages will fall back to `site.description`, but for authors, `bio` is the analogue and we don't check it.

Fix: per-context fallback chain (tag → description, author → bio).

title: ghost-compat: `{{meta_title}}` does not append `" (Page %)"` translation correctly on multi-page archives
body:
Source's default.hbs uses `{{meta_title page=(t " (Page %)")}}`. Our `metaTitleHelper` in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:79` reads `hash.page` and replaces `%` with `pagination.page`. The translated string is `" (Page %)"` (with leading space). Issue: we only emit the suffix when `pagination.page > 1`. The leading space stays, so output becomes `"Site - Page 2"` — wait actually format is `${baseTitle}${pageSuffix.replace('%', String(pagination.page))}` → `"Site (Page 2)"` — looks correct on archive pages. But on tag/author pages we don't pass a `pageSuffix`. Ghost emits `"Tag - Sitename (Page 2)"` style. Verify and align.

Fix: also append page suffix on tag/author paginated views (when `route.kind === 'tag'|'author'`).

title: ghost-compat: `{{meta_title}}` does not include site title in tag/author pages
body:
Ghost emits `{{tag.name}} - {{site.title}}` style on tag pages by default; we just emit `tag.name`. SEO/preview impact.

Fix: combine resource name with site title for non-post/page routes.

title: ghost-compat: `{{prev_post}}` and `{{next_post}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost's docs list `{{#prev_post}}…{{/prev_post}}` / `{{#next_post}}…{{/next_post}}` block helpers that scope to the previous/next post on `post.hbs`. Our `Post` model has `prev` and `next` fields (good) but no helpers expose them as Ghost-style blocks. Themes coded against the helper get nothing.

Fix: register `prev_post` and `next_post` as block helpers that read `this.prev` / `this.next`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{subscribe_form}}` stub doesn't post anywhere — but Ghost expects `<form data-members-form action="…">`
body:
Our stub emits `<form data-nectar-subscribe action="#" method="post">`. Themes targeting Ghost's `data-members-form` attribute won't get progressive enhancement.

Fix: change attribute to `data-members-form` and include the documented hidden inputs Ghost emits.

title: ghost-compat: custom-*.hbs (admin-selectable) per-page templates not supported
body:
Ghost lets admins pick a `custom-{template-name}.hbs` per page from a dropdown. Our pages always render via `page.hbs`. Pages whose frontmatter declares a custom template won't honour it.

Fix: read `template` from frontmatter; resolve via `theme.templates['custom-' + template]`.

title: ghost-compat: error templates expect `{{statusCode}}` and `{{message}}` context — not supplied
body:
Casper's `error.hbs` references `{{statusCode}}` and `{{message}}`. Even if we render the template, our `buildContext` doesn't populate them.

Fix: in `planRoutes`, set `route.data.error = { statusCode, message }` for error routes and extend `buildContext` to expose them.

title: ghost-compat: featured posts highlight on home requires `featured` filter in {{#get}}
body: Verified working in our example, but only because our content has featured posts. Test the empty case.

title: ghost-compat: gscan-style theme linting not provided (no nectar check theme)
body:
Ghost ships `gscan` to lint themes. Our `nectar check` exists (CLI commands enumerated) but it's unclear whether it warns on Ghost-incompatible patterns.

Fix: add a `gscan-lite` mode to `nectar check` that scans `.hbs` files for helpers/contexts we don't implement and reports.

title: ghost-compat: import-ghost doesn't copy `content/images/` from the Ghost export
body:
Ghost exports include uploaded images. Our importer doesn't extract them. Users get post bodies with absolute CDN URLs that may go away when the Ghost site is shut down.

Fix: when importing, also scan the Ghost content dir for `images/` and copy.

title: ghost-compat: import-ghost doesn't migrate routes.yaml or redirects.yaml
body:
A Ghost export often ships these as separate files; users must copy them manually. We don't even prompt.

Fix: when a routes.yaml is present in the Ghost content dir, copy it to the Nectar config dir.

title: ghost-compat: kg-bookmark-card markup not emitted
body:
Specific to bookmarks: Ghost auto-generates a card from any URL pasted on its own line. We have no such logic. Imported posts with bookmark cards become bare links.

Fix: in the importer (`src/ghost/import.ts`) preserve the HTML rather than converting to Markdown; or post-process Markdown to re-render bookmark blocks.

title: ghost-compat: kg-image-card markup with `kg-width-wide` / `kg-width-full` not emitted
body: Ghost emits `

title: ghost-compat: per-tag and per-author RSS feeds (`/tag/<slug>/rss/`) not emitted
body:
Ghost emits an RSS feed for every tag and author archive. Power users subscribe to specific tags.

Fix: in the RSS emitter, also emit per-tag and per-author feeds.

title: ghost-compat: post-slug.hbs / page-slug.hbs / tag-slug.hbs / author-slug.hbs custom per-resource templates not supported
body:
Ghost picks `post-{slug}.hbs` over `post.hbs` when present (similarly for page/tag/author). Our `renderRoute` always uses the generic `route.template`. Themes shipping `post-welcome.hbs` for a special welcome post won't have it picked up.

Fix: in `planRoutes`, when emitting a post route, prefer `theme.templates[\`post-${slug}\`]` over `post`.

title: ghost-compat: prev/next navigation links in HTML head (`<link rel="prev">` / `next`) not emitted
body:
Ghost adds prev/next link tags to paginated archives. Good for SEO. Our ghost_head doesn't.

Fix: when on a paged route, emit `<link rel="prev" href="…">` / `<link rel="next" href="…">`.

title: ghost-compat: relative image paths in posts not rewritten to public URLs
body:
Markdown posts can reference `./images/foo.jpg`. Our pipeline doesn't normalize these to `/content/images/foo.jpg` or whatever the public path is.

Fix: in `renderMarkdown` (or a post-process step), rewrite relative image URLs to absolute public paths.

title: ghost-compat: robots.txt support — Ghost emits a default unless theme provides one
body:
Ghost serves a default `/robots.txt` (disallows admin paths, advertises sitemap). Theme may override. We don't ship a default; if a theme ships `robots.txt` we don't propagate it.

Fix: copy `robots.txt` from theme root if present; emit a default sitemap-referencing one otherwise.

title: ghost-compat: tag visibility:internal computed only from `hash-` slug prefix, not from frontmatter
body:
`src/content/loader.ts:291` infers internal status from slug. Ghost honours `visibility: internal` directly on the tag record. Frontmatter `visibility:` field is ignored.

Fix: read `data.visibility` from tag frontmatter; fall back to slug-prefix heuristic.

title: ghost-compat: theme `gulpfile.js` and built/built sources — Nectar doesn't run theme build steps
body:
Source ships a gulpfile that compiles CSS/JS into `assets/built/`. Themes that haven't run their own build will have an empty `built/` dir, and our asset map will miss `built/screen.css`. The `{{asset "built/screen.css"}}` helper will fall back to a non-existent path.

Fix: warn on `nectar build` when referenced assets resolve to missing files; document the requirement to run the theme's own build first.

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} `as |alias|` block-param syntax not honoured
body:
Source/Casper use `{{#get "posts" as |alias|}}{{#foreach alias}}…{{/foreach}}{{/get}}` which works because Handlebars itself binds the block param. But Ghost also allows `{{#foreach posts as |post|}}` to alias the iterated item. Our `foreachHelper` calls `options.fn(item, …)` without block-param plumbing, so `as |post|` is silently ignored — the inner template sees the item as `this`, which is normally fine, but breaks templates using `{{post.title}}` rather than `{{title}}`.

Fix: when `options.fn.blockParams > 0`, pass `[item, { /* foreach state */ }]` as the second arg.

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} `from`/`to` semantics are 1-indexed inclusive — but our `to` defaults to `min(items.length, from+limit-1)` which conflicts with Ghost's documented behaviour
body:
Ghost docs: `from` and `to` are 1-indexed inclusive bounds. Without `to`, all remaining items are emitted (limited by `limit`). Our `parseNum(options.hash.to) ?? Math.min(items.length, from + limit - 1)` interacts badly when `to` is omitted AND `limit` is omitted (limit defaults to items.length → to = items.length+from-1 which is past end; slice handles it but mismatches Ghost).

Fix: separate the three: `from = hash.from ?? 1`, `to = hash.to ?? items.length`, `limit = hash.limit ?? Infinity`. Apply slice(from-1, to) then take .slice(0, limit).

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} doesn't support `columns` and the `@rowStart`/`@rowEnd` flags
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:21` sets `data.rowStart = false; data.rowEnd = false;` unconditionally — never honouring the `columns=` hash option. Themes that do `{{#foreach posts columns=3}}` for grid layouts won't get `@rowStart`/`@rowEnd`, breaking row-aware CSS.

Fix: when `hash.columns` is set, compute `i % columns === 0` (rowStart) and `(i+1) % columns === 0 || i === last` (rowEnd).

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL interpolation `{{post.id}}` swallows {{}} braces inside filter string
body:
Source's post.hbs uses `filter="id:-{{post.id}}"` which Handlebars itself evaluates BEFORE the helper sees it. Casper uses `filter="id:-{{id}}"`. Our impl ALSO re-interpolates `{{...}}` inside the filter string against `this`, which is a duplicate of Handlebars's own substitution. If Handlebars already substituted, the second pass does nothing. But if `hash.filter` was passed as a sub-expression, the second pass may corrupt the value.

Fix: remove the manual `{{...}}` re-interpolation in `evaluateClause`. Trust Handlebars to interpolate before the helper runs.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} resource `tiers` not supported
body:
Ghost's `{{#get}}` accepts five resources: `posts`, `pages`, `tags`, `authors`, `tiers`. Our `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` handles only the first four. Themes that use `{{#get "tiers"}}` to render pricing tables will get an empty result with no warning.

Fix: at minimum, return an empty iterable for `tiers` and log a warning that membership tiers aren't supported in Nectar.

title: ghost-compat: {{#has}} `tag="a,b"` is OR within the attr, but multiple attrs should also OR (we currently OR but stop on first match for all attrs)
body:
Per Ghost docs, multiple hash attrs on `{{#has}}` are OR'd together (`{{#has tag="x" author="y"}}` → true if either). Our loop does break on first match, which is OR-correct, but we never test nested has (AND-via-nesting) and we never short-circuit `^has`. Need explicit test coverage and document that the negated form (`{{^has}}`) inverts the OR.

Fix: add tests for OR-of-attrs, nested-AND, and `{{^has}}` semantics.

title: ghost-compat: {{#has}} ignores number range patterns (e.g. number="3,6,9" inside foreach)
body:
Ghost lets you do `{{#has number="3, 6, 9"}}` inside a `{{#foreach}}` to apply a special class on those rows. Our implementation in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` does a single `Number(value)` equality check against `route.data.pagination.page`, completely missing the foreach loop variable case. This means Source's home `{{#match @number 2}}` chains that rely on similar logic do work (because they use match), but any theme that uses `{{#has number="..."}}` will break.

Fix: distinguish between the route-pagination case and the foreach-context case by reading `@number`/`@index` from `options.data`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} accepts only a comma-separated string, not multiple positional args
body:
Ghost accepts both forms: `{{#is "post, page"}}` and `{{#is "post" "page"}}`. Our impl in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` flat-maps args and then splits on commas — multiple bare positional strings work, but the doc-blessed multi-arg form was never tested. Worse, if a theme passes a hash like `{{#is context="post"}}` we ignore it (Ghost doesn't support that either, so this part is OK), but the lack of tests means regressions are easy.

Fix: add tests; ensure positional + comma-split both behave.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} does not support "error" context
body:
Ghost's `{{#is}}` helper accepts `error` to detect that the current route is an error page (`error.hbs`, `error-404.hbs`). Our `RouteKind` type even includes `error` but the alias table in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` (`aliases` object) has no entry for it, so `{{#is "error"}}` will always fall through to the inverse branch.

Fix: add `error: ['error']` to the aliases map, and make sure error routes set `route.kind = 'error'`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} does not support "private" context
body:
Ghost's `{{#is}}` helper supports a `private` context that matches when a publication is password-protected. Our implementation in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` only maps `home`, `index`, `post`, `page`, `tag`, `author`, `paged`. Themes that have a `private.hbs` template or guard with `{{#is "private"}}` will silently render the wrong branch.

Verify: grep Casper / Source for `{{#is "private"}}`. Build a theme that uses it and confirm Nectar never matches.

Fix: add `private` to the alias map in `registerBlockHelpers` (it always returns false unless we add a `private` flag to the route/config) and document the limitation.

title: ghost-compat: {{asset}} `hasMinFile` option is parsed but unused
body:
Ghost's `{{asset}}` accepts `hasMinFile="true"` so themes can ship both `screen.css` and `screen.min.css` and have the helper choose the latter in production. Our handler in `src/render/helpers/assets.ts:21` reads `options.hash.hasMinFile` but the ternary appends `''` either way — it's a dead read.

Fix: when truthy, look up the `.min.<ext>` variant first via the asset map.

title: ghost-compat: {{asset}} does not output the file's fingerprinted name when the file isn't .css/.js (e.g. images)
body:
`src/theme/assets.ts:33` only fingerprints `.css`, `.js`, `.mjs`. Ghost fingerprints (via `v=hash` query param) every asset reference. Theme images (logos, icons) won't bust cache. Themes may reference e.g. `{{asset "images/icon.svg"}}` — works but no cache busting.

Fix: add `?v=<hash>` query string to non-fingerprintable assets so caches still bust.

title: ghost-compat: {{asset}} helper double-escapes the result via escapeHtml even though it's used as an href
body:
`src/render/helpers/assets.ts:21` does `return new engine.hb.SafeString(escapeHtml(url + …))`. Wrapping in SafeString tells Handlebars not to escape — but we've already escaped manually, so an URL containing `&` becomes `&` and stays that way even in href/src context where Ghost emits raw `&`. The Ghost helper emits a SafeString of the raw URL.

Fix: drop the manual `escapeHtml` call on the URL; SafeString is sufficient.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} default format is hard-coded "DD MMM YYYY", Ghost default is `ll` (locale-aware short)
body:
Per docs, when called without options Ghost uses Moment's `ll` format (e.g. "Jan 15, 2026"). Our `src/render/helpers/date.ts:31` defaults to `"DD MMM YYYY"`. Themes that omit format will produce a different visual result than on Ghost.

Fix: change default to `'ll'` and ensure dayjs/localizedFormat handles it.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} does not honour `locale=` hash override
body:
Ghost docs: `{{date format="MMMM Do, YYYY" locale="fr-fr"}}`. Our impl never reads `options.hash.locale`. Multi-locale themes can't override per-call.

Fix: read `hash.locale`, call `dayjs.locale(locale)` for that invocation.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} JSON-LD missing `mainEntityOfPage`, `keywords` (tags), `wordCount`, `commentCount`
body:
Ghost's structured-data JSON-LD includes additional Schema.org fields. Our `buildJsonLd` is minimal. For SEO parity, themes that rely on rich snippets won't match Ghost.

Fix: extend the JSON-LD with `mainEntityOfPage`, `keywords` (joined tag names), `wordCount`, and (when known) `commentCount`.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} JSON-LD only emits Article on post pages, never Person/WebSite for author/tag pages
body:
Ghost emits `@type: Person` for author pages, `@type: Series` for tag pages (or similar). Our `buildJsonLd` returns the generic `WebSite` for anything that's not a post.

Fix: branch on `route.kind` and emit Person / Series / WebPage as appropriate.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `<meta name="twitter:site">` from @site.twitter or @site.x
body:
Ghost emits both `twitter:site` (publication handle) and `twitter:creator` (per-post author handle). Our impl only emits `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`. Missing handles reduce Twitter Card validation.

Fix: include twitter:site (from `@site.twitter`) and twitter:creator (from `primary_author.twitter`).

title: ghost-compat: {{navigation}} helper does not pass `isSecondary` to partial
body:
Ghost docs explicitly say: inside `partials/navigation.hbs` you may branch on `{{#if isSecondary}}` to detect which call (primary vs `type="secondary"`) is rendering. Our helper has no such flag because it doesn't delegate to a partial at all.

Fix: once delegation is implemented, pass `isSecondary: type === 'secondary'` to the partial's context.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} `%` positional placeholder substitutes the FIRST hash value indiscriminately
body:
`interpolate` in `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:30` replaces every `%` with `Object.values(hash)[0]`. Source's `{{reading_time minute=(t "1 min read") minutes=(t "% min read")}}` works because the `reading_time` helper passes `count` separately. But any template that hash-passes other named values (e.g. `numberOfPosts=12`) AND has a `%` somewhere would substitute the wrong value because hash key order is non-deterministic.

Fix: prefer a numbered positional `%1`, `%2` or document that `%` only substitutes the singular numeric value.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} translation interpolation uses `{name}` braces but Ghost docs `{name}` AND positional `%` — verify ICU MessageFormat fragments
body:
`src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:22` does `out.replaceAll('{key}', value)`. Source's footer uses `{{t "Powered by {ghostlink}" ghostlink="…"}}` — works. But Ghost supports plural-form ICU patterns (`{count, plural, one {…} other {…}}`); we don't.

Fix: at least document the limitation; long-term, integrate a real ICU lib (e.g. `@formatjs/intl-messageformat`).

title: ghost-compat: {{url}} helper does not accept `data` block params from `{{#get}}`
body:
Ghost's `{{url tag}}` inside a foreach-over-get accepts the iterated object directly. Our impl ignores any positional arg.

Fix: accept any positional `{ url }` object and prefer its `.url`.

title: ghost-import: does not import settings (site title, description, navigation) from Ghost
body: Ghost's export includes `settings: {...}`. Map to `nectar.toml` on import (with a confirmation).

title: ghost-import: missing JSON Schema validation of the export file
body: Right now we trust the shape and access nested properties. Validate top-level structure and report clear errors for partial/corrupt exports.

title: ghost-import: no detection of duplicate slugs between posts/pages
body: If a Ghost site has a post and page with same slug, we overwrite. Detect and report.

title: ghost-import: no dry-run mode
body: Add `--dry-run` printing the plan (N posts, M pages, ...). Useful before nuking content/.

title: good-first-issue: emit a build manifest (dist/.nectar/manifest.json) listing every file we wrote
body: Powers safe re-emit (vs rm -rf).

title: i18n: date format string is en-rooted (`DD MMM YYYY`)
body: Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with options derived from locale.

title: i18n: no number / currency formatting
body: No helper for `{{number value}}` or `{{currency value cur="USD"}}` driven by `[site].locale` via `Intl.NumberFormat`. Themes that show price/stat blocks fall back to raw numbers.

title: image-pipeline: asset hashing reads whole file into memory
body: `loadThemeAssets` does `await readFile(file)` then hashes the whole buffer. For themes with bundled fonts or background videos this blows up. Stream-hash via `Bun.CryptoHasher` reading chunks.

title: image-pipeline: image_dimensions not embedded as width/height attributes
body: Improves CLS. Compute at build via probe-image-size.

title: image-pipeline: no EXIF strip
body: Migrators uploading photos via Ghost had EXIF preserved (camera, GPS coords on iPhone shots). Add EXIF stripping (configurable; default-on for privacy) when we touch images. Same component that does resizing.

title: image-pipeline: no LQIP / blur-up support
body: Modern Ghost themes use a tiny base64 placeholder for above-the-fold images. Generate a 16px-wide JPEG and inline as `data:` on the `` `style="background:url(...)"`. Without it, our LCP scores will look worse than the same blog on Ghost.

title: image-pipeline: no LQIP/blurhash for above-the-fold images
body: Improves CLS and perceived perf.

title: image-pipeline: no content-addressed image storage
body: Today `copyContentAssets` mirrors `content/images/` to `dist/content/images/`. Two posts referencing the same image cost double bandwidth on CDN-without-dedup. Move to `dist/_images/<sha256-prefix>/<filename>` and rewrite URLs in HTML + frontmatter. Makes incremental builds + CDN caching far cheaper.

title: infra: no Docker image
body: Provide an Alpine-based image baking in bun + nectar.

title: infra: no GitHub Actions caching for bun install
body: Use actions/cache or oven-sh/setup-bun's built-in cache.

title: infra: no Homebrew tap
