body: Add a tap (`oven-sh/bun` style) for `brew install nectar`.

title: infra: no pre-commit hook (lint-staged / lefthook / husky)
body: Catch Biome and typecheck regressions before push so CI isn't the first signal. Use `lefthook` (Go, fast, language-agnostic) configured to run `bun run check` on staged files. Optional opt-out via env.

title: members: `cancel_link` / `signup_url` helpers missing
body: Some Ghost themes use `{{cancel_link}}` (for paid members) and `{{signup_url}}`. Already partly noted in self-audit's missing-helpers issue but worth its own stub: `cancel_link` → undefined / empty, `signup_url` → portal provider's signup URL when configured.

title: members: `member_count` / `comment_count` helpers absent
body: `{{member_count}}` is used in social-proof phrasing ("Join 1,234 readers"). Static builds can't know live counts. Stub returns empty string; allow override via `[components.portal] member_count = 1234` (manual). Document.

title: members: `{{#contentFor}}` block helper missing — used by many member-facing themes to swap default partials
body: Ghost themes can declare `{{#contentFor "some-block"}}…{{/contentFor}}` in a child template and have it injected into a `{{{block "some-block"}}}` slot in the layout. Not used by Source but common in member-portal themes. Implement to unblock more migrations.

title: members: context: `@site.members_invite_only` for invite-only publications
body: Source theme's `navigation.hbs:37` branches on `@site.members_invite_only` to hide the Subscribe button (only show Sign In). For Nectar, expose as `[components.portal] invite_only = true`. When set, the build-time DOM transform removes Subscribe buttons but keeps Sign In links.

title: members: i18n: portal strings ("Subscribe", "Sign in", "Account", "Email sent") all hard-coded in theme
body: Source theme wraps every portal-related string in `{{t "Subscribe"}}` etc. Verify our `t` helper resolves them from theme `locales/<lang>.json`. Add to the locale test fixture: ensure `"Subscribe"`, `"Sign in"`, `"Account"`, `"Email sent"`, `"jamie@example.com"`, `"Recommendations"`, `"See all"`, `"Upgrade"`, `"Search this site"`, `"Search posts, tags and authors"`, `"This post is for members only"` all translate.

title: members: portal: no ConvertKit / Kit.com adapter
body: ConvertKit (now Kit.com) exposes `https://api.convertkit.com/v3/forms/<form-id>/subscribe` and a hosted form URL `https://<user>.ck.page/<form-id>`. Adapter rewrites `data-members-form` action and adds the `api_key` hidden field (or uses the hosted endpoint to avoid leaking the key). Document that magic-link signin is not supported.

title: members: portal: no MailerLite / Mailchimp / EmailOctopus adapters
body: Many migrators come from these providers. Provide a shared `[components.portal] provider = "mailerlite|mailchimp|emailoctopus"` with the relevant form action and field name remapping. Keep adapters in `src/components/portal/<provider>.ts` to make adding more straightforward.

title: members: portal: no Substack adapter
body: Substack publications expose `https://<pub>.substack.com/subscribe?just_signed_up=true` as a public signup form. Add adapter that rewrites `data-portal="signup"` to open this URL in a popup, and rewrites the embedded `<form data-members-form>` to POST to Substack's `https://substack.com/api/v1/free?nojs=true` (or use the official `subscribe-widget` iframe). Document the lack of per-domain branding control.

title: members: theme-package.json contract — themes may declare `members: required` in their package.json
body: Ghost themes' `package.json` has `"config": { "members": "required" }` (some themes refuse to install if members feature is disabled). Decide policy: warn at theme-load time if a theme declares `members: required` but no portal provider is configured.

title: members: tiers: no `tier` context on member-visibility-keyed posts
body: Ghost posts can have `visibility = "tiers"` plus a `tiers: [premium]` list. Our Post type only knows `'public' | 'members' | 'paid'`. Extend to `visibility: 'public' | 'members' | 'paid' | 'tiers'` and add `tiers: string[]`. Visibility filter in `blocks.ts` already mostly handles 'paid'; extend.

title: migration: importer doesn't preserve internal IDs/UUIDs
body: `importGhostExport` synthesises `id: 'post-' + slug` and drops `post.uuid`. If a user later re-imports an updated export, we can't reconcile because we lost the original keys. Preserve `id`, `uuid`, `created_at` in frontmatter so re-imports are idempotent.

title: migration: no Hugo / Jekyll importer
body: Many small blogs are Hugo or Jekyll today. Their content is already Markdown — main work is re-mapping frontmatter (e.g. `categories` → `tags`, `aliases` → `redirects`). Add `nectar import-hugo` / `nectar import-jekyll`.

title: newsletter: no separate email template (`email.hbs` / `email-template.hbs`)
body:
Ghost themes ship a parallel `email.hbs` for rendering newsletters (inlined CSS, table-based layout). Source theme doesn't have one but many do. Nectar should:
1. Detect `email.hbs` in the theme dir and skip rendering it as a web route
2. Optionally expose `nectar build:email --post=<slug>` that renders just the email template against a post and outputs MJML/inlined HTML to `dist/email/<slug>.html`
3. Document the workflow: pipe to Postmark/Resend/SES.

title: observability: logs aren't structured / no JSON mode
body: `src/util/logger.ts` writes plain text only. CI pipelines and self-hosted log aggregators want JSON. Add `NECTAR_LOG_FORMAT=json` that switches `emit()` to emit `{ ts, level, msg, ...fields }`. Also let helpers attach trace context (route URL, file).

title: observability: no JSON log mode for CI / log shippers
body: Add NECTAR_LOG_FORMAT=json mode in logger.ts.

title: observability: no memory profiling hooks
body: Large Ghost exports (image-heavy, lots of posts) will balloon memory because we hold all rendered HTML in memory pre-emit. Add `--inspect` to surface peak RSS, and document `BUN_INSPECT=1` for V8 heap snapshots. Pair with the streaming-emit fix.

title: observability: no per-route timing report
body: Build summary should include slowest routes, helper hotspots.

title: observability: no source-map handling for stack traces
body: Once we ship compiled JS, errors will point at minified line numbers. Bundle with `--sourcemap=external` and resolve via `Error.prepareStackTrace` so `nectar build` errors still point at original .ts lines.

title: packaging: no Homebrew tap
body: Ghost users on macOS expect `brew install nectar`. Create a `homebrew-tap` repo with a formula that downloads the signed darwin binary from each GitHub Release. Automate via the `Homebrew/actions/setup-homebrew` workflow plus a tap-bump step in release.yml. Document the tap in the README install section.

title: packaging: no Scoop bucket for Windows
body: For Windows-first writers we need `scoop install nectar`. Create `scoop-nectar` repo with a manifest pointing at the windows-x64 binary, and bump on release. Without this, our Windows story is `bun install -g` plus PATH munging.

title: perf: assets: synchronous statSync inside async glob loop
body:
src/theme/assets.ts:13 uses `statSync(file)` inside an `await glob.scan()` loop. statSync blocks the event loop on every asset, preventing the concurrent readFile from kicking off. For large asset trees this serialises everything.

Fix: replace with `await stat(file)` from node:fs/promises, or call `Bun.file(file).size` which is async and lazy.

title: perf: build: no telemetry / per-phase timing reported
body:
pipeline.ts logs only final route+asset counts. With no per-phase breakdown (load: Xms, plan: Yms, render: Zms, assets: Wms, feeds: Vms), users and devs cannot identify regressions.

Fix: wrap each phase in `performance.now()` and log a one-line summary on completion. Optional --verbose flag dumps per-route render times.

title: perf: build: sitemap/RSS rebuilt from scratch every build
body:
src/build/feeds.ts emitSitemap and emitRss regenerate the entire feed every build, even when no posts changed. For a 100k-URL sitemap this is a multi-MB string concatenation in memory.

Fix: cache the serialized feed by content hash of (post list + mtimes). If the hash is unchanged, skip writing. Use a streaming writer (write XML to a stream rather than building one giant string) for large feeds.

title: perf: docs: no perf section / no published benchmarks
body:
README.md and docs/ contain no benchmarks, no scaling guidance, no recommended host config. Users have no way to know if their build is slow.

Fix: add docs/PERFORMANCE.md with: target metrics (build 1k posts in <3s, render <0.5ms/route), suggested hosting headers, image guidance (max 5MB, prefer webp/avif), and a worked example of incremental builds. Add tests/bench/*.bench.ts that prints results.

title: perf: emit/copyContentAssets: reads full file into memory via copyFile semantics
body:
node:fs/promises copyFile may buffer in user space depending on platform. For multi-GB asset trees, doing many in flight can spike memory.

Fix: prefer `Bun.file(src).stream().pipeTo(Bun.file(dst).writer())` for streaming copy, or copyFile with COPYFILE_FICLONE flag on supported filesystems (APFS, BTRFS) to zero-copy via reflinks.

title: perf: ghost-import: ensureDir called per post — repeated mkdir
body:
Same loop calls `ensureDir(join(opts.cwd, dir))` per post — same dir each time. Wasted syscall.

Fix: mkdir each of content/posts and content/pages once, before the loop.

title: perf: helpers/blocks: `get` slice copies entire array
body:
blocks.ts:128 does `results = engine.content.posts.slice()` to defend against mutation by sort. For a 10k-post array this is a 10k pointer copy per invocation.

Fix: keep the sorted/indexed list immutable and return the slice without copying. Or use `results = applyOrder([...content.posts], order)` only when order differs from canonical.

title: perf: helpers/date: dayjs().tz(...).format() per date helper call
body:
src/render/helpers/date.ts dateHelper instantiates a dayjs object and applies a timezone conversion every time `{{date}}` is rendered. Each post card has ≥1 date call; with paginated lists you get ~12-50 dayjs allocations per page × 10k pages = 500k dayjs objects.

Fix: precompute formatted date strings on the Post during loader.ts (cheap_date, iso_date, etc) and let the helper just look them up by format key. Or cache by `${value}|${format}|${tz}`.

title: perf: helpers: navigation helper builds entire ul on every page
body:
navigation.ts navigationHelper joins escapeHtml + escapeAttr + slugify per nav item per page. Result is identical across the entire site (nav doesn't change between pages).

Fix: cache navigation html per (type, locale) at engine boot. Reuse via SafeString.

title: perf: loader: post.plaintext stored alongside html — doubles memory
body:
loader.ts:197 stores both html and plaintext. plaintext is derived from html and used only for excerpt fallback + word_count. After loader runs, plaintext can be discarded.

Fix: drop `plaintext` from Post after computing excerpt + word_count. Saves ~50% of content RAM.

title: perf: markdown: marked.parse is async but body of renderMarkdown is serial
body:
renderMarkdown awaits marked.parse then derives plaintext+word_count synchronously. Since renderMarkdown is called from the (currently serial) loader, the await suspends but no parallelism is enabled.

Fix: companion to loader parallelism — Promise.all the renderMarkdown calls across posts.

title: perf: output: HTML not minified
body:
Generated HTML is whitespace-heavy from Handlebars + handlebars partials. example/dist/index.html is 8.8KB; minified would be ~5.5KB. Multiplied across pages and gzipped, savings are modest but consistent.

Fix: optional html-minifier-terser pass post-render (gated on config.build.minify_html=true). Defer to ship-it-fast for now but expose the knob.

title: perf: output: SVG images run through srcset machinery — redundant
body:
Companion to the previous. SVGs are vectors — width descriptors mean nothing to them. The current template emits a 6-entry srcset on SVG covers, adding ~500B/img to every page and inviting the picker to download '2000w' which is just the original.

Fix: in post-card.hbs and feature-image.hbs, branch on `endsWith('.svg')` (or expose `is_vector` on the image) and omit srcset.

title: perf: output: font-display: optional may hide text indefinitely
body:
default's typography/sans partial sets `font-display: optional`. 'optional' instructs the browser to use the fallback if the font isn't available within ~100ms — text never swaps later. On slow connections users see the fallback font for the entire session. Great for CLS but poor for brand consistency.

Fix: expose font-display as a theme custom setting (default 'swap' for most blogs, 'optional' as an opt-in). Document the tradeoff.

title: perf: output: no HTTP/2 Server-Push or 103 Early Hints support documented
body:
SSG output is just files — fine. But for hosts that support 103 Early Hints (Cloudflare, Fastly, Vercel), the generator can emit a `_headers` (Netlify/CF) or `_redirects` file listing critical preload hints. Currently nothing is emitted.

Fix: optional `early-hints.json` artifact per route, plus a `_headers` file for Netlify/Cloudflare Pages with `Link: </assets/built/screen.css>; rel=preload; as=style` etc. Documented in docs/.

title: perf: output: rss.xml grows unbounded — no streaming write
body:
feeds.ts builds the whole RSS string in memory before writing. For a 10k-item feed (~300MB) this OOMs. Even at 100 items it's wasteful for a streaming-capable runtime like Bun.

Fix: open `Bun.file(rss.xml).writer()`, write header, write each item with `writer.write(...)` inside the loop, then close. Constant memory.

title: perf: output: theme bundle source.js is 52KB unminified-ish — verify gzip size
body:
example/dist/assets/built/source.js is 52613 bytes. Without measuring Brotli/gzip we don't know the over-the-wire cost. For a static site, this is the main INP contributor.

Fix: add a Lighthouse / size-limit check to CI on example/dist; alert if bundle grows beyond 60KB Brotli'd.

title: perf: pipeline: copyAssets runs AFTER all routes rendered — serializes phases
body:
pipeline.ts emits routes first, then assets, then content assets, then sitemap, then RSS — all sequentially. These are mostly independent.

Fix: kick off `copyAssets` and `copyContentAssets` as Promises while routes are still rendering. Sitemap can start once routes are planned (it only needs URLs, not html). RSS needs post.html so it remains last. Net: ~30% faster total wall time on small sites where assets dominate.

title: perf: pipeline: ensureDir / mkdir recursive called per write
body:
emit.ts:8 calls `ensureDir(dirname(dest))` for every single writeHtml. mkdir recursive is cheap if the path exists (one stat syscall) but it still hits the filesystem. For 10k routes that's 10k stat calls.

Fix: precompute the set of unique directories from planned routes, mkdir each once at the start of the emit phase.

title: perf: render: buildContext mutates ctx then Object.assigns post — duplicate refs
body:
engine.ts buildContext does `Object.assign(ctx, data.post)` AND `ctx.post = data.post`. This copies every key of the post (≈30 fields) AND keeps a reference. For 10k posts that is 300k unnecessary assignments and a bloated context object passed to Handlebars (slower hash lookups in compiled templates).

Fix: pass `data.post` as the context directly when route.kind === 'post'/'page'. Keep `ctx.post` only when the inner template references both `{{title}}` and `{{post.title}}` (it shouldn't have to). Saves allocations and improves Handlebars lookup speed.

title: perf: render: ghost_head rebuilds JSON-LD per render — cache per post id
body:
src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts buildJsonLd executes for every route render (including paginated index pages where the same site-level JSON-LD is rebuilt 100s of times). Computing the author array, publisher block, absoluteUrl etc allocates a fresh object every call.

Fix: memoize by route.template + (post?.id ?? 'site'). Store the serialized JSON string on the engine in a WeakMap<Post, string>. Eliminates ~30-50µs/render plus a few KB of garbage per render.

title: perf: scale: RSS >10k items needs pagination (Ghost paginates by 25)
body:
Even with the config.components.rss.items cap, users may set it to a high number. No safeguard.

Fix: hard-clamp items to a max (e.g. 250) and emit paginated rss-2.xml … with <atom:link rel=next>. Aligns with Ghost behavior.

title: perf: theme: serial readFile of every .hbs in loadTheme
body:
src/theme/loader.ts loadTheme:38-51 does `await readFile(file)` per .hbs in series. A medium Ghost theme has ~30-80 hbs files. Each ~1ms × 80 = 80ms serial vs ~5ms parallel.

Fix: collect paths, Promise.all the reads, then assign into templates/partials maps.

title: performance: emit phase blocks on per-file fs writes
body: `writeHtml` does `await ensureDir; await writeFile` per route, serially in the render loop. Decouple: collect `{ outputPath, html }` in memory, then concurrently write with bounded concurrency (e.g. `pLimit(32)`). Same for `copyAssets` and `copyContentAssets`.

title: performance: no build-profile output (--profile)
body: Pair with the observability issues. `nectar build --profile` should write `dist/.nectar/profile.json` with `{ phase, duration_ms, route, bytes_emitted }` so users can diagnose their own slowness without filing an issue.

title: performance: pipeline.ts deletes dist with `recursive: true, force: true` — race-prone under watch
body: When `nectar dev` lands, multiple parallel saves could trigger overlapping `rm` + `writeFile`s. Plan: write to a temp directory, swap atomically. Today this only bites under load but it bites hard ("why is dist/index.html missing for 200ms").

title: performance: route planner walks posts repeatedly (O(N*M) tag/author filters)
body: `routes.ts` does `content.posts.filter(...)` inside the tag and author loops — O(tags × posts) and O(authors × posts). On a blog with 100 tags × 1000 posts that's 100k pass-through. Build inverse maps `Map<tagSlug, Post[]>` and `Map<authorSlug, Post[]>` during content load.

title: performance: theme assets duplicated on disk (fingerprinted + logical copy)
body: `copyAssets` writes both `dist/assets/built/screen.css` and `dist/assets/built/screen.<hash>.css`. Double the disk + double the upload. Either emit only the fingerprinted copy (and update helpers to reference it) or symlink. Themes with megabytes of fonts pay 2x.

title: performance: theme assets re-read for fingerprinting on every build
body: Cache by mtime + size in `.nectar/asset-cache.json`.

title: recommendations: no `data-portal="recommendations"` modal stub
body: Source's `post-list.hbs:115` has a 'See all' button with `data-portal="recommendations"`. Ghost opens a full-list modal with all recommendations. Nectar's portal shim should bind clicks on this selector to scroll to a `<section id="all-recommendations">` block that we auto-emit on a `/recommendations/` route when `[[recommendations]]` is configured.

title: render: HTML output not minified
body: Add optional minifier (html-minifier-terser) post-render.

title: render: `authors` inline rendering does not honour `separator=`/`autolink=`/`limit=`/`from=` like Ghost
body: helpers/content.ts:authorsHelper inline returns `list.map(a=>a.name).join(', ')`. Ghost supports `separator`, `lastSeparator`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `autolink`, `limit`, `from`, `to`. Implement these.

title: render: `body_class` does not include `tag-hash-x` markers for internal tags
body: Ghost's body_class includes tag tokens. We add `tag-<slug>` only on tag routes. Add per-post tag tokens too, and special-case internal tags (prefixed with `#`).

title: render: `get` does not return Pagination metadata to the block
body: Ghost's `{{#get}}` exposes a `pagination` object to the block. We expose only the array. Themes that paginate inside `{{#get}}` cannot.

title: render: `get` filter interpolation only expands {{post.id}} when ctx is post; misses nested
body: blocks.ts:evaluateClause interpolates `{{post.id}}` from `this` (the surrounding context). On a non-post page, post is undefined and the interpolated value is empty, returning all posts. Either pass the route context or error.

title: render: `get` helper ignores `include=` parameter (authors, tags, count)
body: Our Post model already has full authors/tags hydrated, so `include="authors"` is a no-op. That's fine for now, but `include="count.posts"` (Ghost's tag/author counts) is silently dropped. Resolve counts on the way out.

title: render: `ghost_foot` outputs unescaped codeinjection_foot — intentional, but document risk
body: Raw HTML by design. Document this as the one explicit XSS exit (only authors can edit content). Recommend CSP nonces.

title: render: `has` helper does not support count comparators (count:>0)
body: Real Ghost themes write `{{#has count:"tags">0}}`. Not implemented.

title: render: `match` 3-arg numeric operators force Number(left) > Number(right) on strings
body: Comparing strings like `{{#match posts.length ">=" 4}}` works because posts.length is a number. But `{{#match foo ">" bar}}` with both strings gets `NaN > NaN === false`. Document this and add a `compareAs` hash option, or detect string vs number.

title: render: `meta_description` returns empty string when missing; Ghost returns first sentence of content
body: Improve fallback chain: meta_description → custom_excerpt → og_description → excerpt → site.description → first sentence of plaintext.

title: render: `post_class` does not include `image` / `no-image`, `featured`, `no-content`, page-specific tokens
body: Ghost's post_class is richer. Add tokens: `image` when feature_image exists, `no-image` otherwise, `no-content` when content empty.

title: render: `reading_time` uses 275 wpm regardless of locale
body: Ghost's algorithm: 275 wpm + 12 sec per image. We don't count images. With CJK we should switch units (chars / chars-per-min). Implement a locale-aware reading time.

title: render: `social_url` does not support `discord`, `mastodon` profile URLs as-is
body: If author.mastodon is already a URL, our builder wraps it as `https://mastodon.social/@<URL>` — broken. Detect existing URLs.

title: render: `t` helper interpolates {key} once but doesn't escape; injecting HTML risk via i18n
body: Translation strings often contain `%`. We pass them through `new SafeString`? No — we return a raw string. If a theme uses `{{t}}` inline without triple-braces, the HTML is escaped. With triple, XSS surface from locale files. Document.

title: render: `tags` inline renders <a> in HTML escape mode; `autolink=false` works but no `prefix`/`suffix`/`limit`
body: Same gap as `authors`. Implement Ghost's full `tags` helper signature.

title: render: foreach `from=`/`to=` evaluate on visible-then-sliced data (Ghost slices first)
body: Our foreach does `visibilityFilter(items).slice(from-1, to)`. Ghost's behaviour: it slices the input first, then filters. Worth verifying against Ghost's reference impl; can produce off-by-one when public+members posts are interleaved.

title: render: foreach `visibility` filter does not apply when filtering posts
body: helpers/blocks.ts:foreachHelper applies visibilityFilter only on items with a `visibility` field. Tags and authors have no visibility; the filter passes them through. Document this and add explicit support for `visibility="public"` filtering tags by `tag.visibility==='public'`.

title: render: foreach data frame missing `rowStart`/`rowEnd` for grid layouts
body: Several Ghost themes use `@rowStart` / `@rowEnd` for masonry/grid wrapping. We set them to `false` unconditionally. Compute them from a `columns=` hash option (default 1) on the foreach call.

title: render: layout inheritance recompiles inner+layout per render
body:
src/render/engine.ts:renderRoute recompiles both inner and layout Handlebars templates on every route. With N routes × M layouts that's wasted CPU at build time and a hot path during dev rebuilds.

**Fix**: cache compiled templates and layout pairs on engine init; key by template name.

title: render: missing helper — `image_dimensions`
body: Used by themes that emit width/height attributes. Requires reading image dimensions at build (`probe-image-size` or `sharp.metadata`).

title: render: missing helper — `next_post` / `prev_post`
body: Some themes use them (in addition to `post.prev`/`post.next`). Stub.

title: render: no nonce support for inline `<script>` and `<style>` in ghost_head
body: For CSP. Accept `[build].csp_nonce` and emit `nonce="..."` on our inline tags.

title: render: post-as-partial does not exist for {{> post}} (custom layouts)
body: Some Ghost themes use `{{> post}}` from a custom layout to render the post body. We register templates as partials, so this works in some cases but uses the raw template source not the body-after-layout-split, leading to double layout rendering or compile errors.

title: render: {{img_url absolute=true}} broken for external URLs
body: imgUrlHelper passes `candidate` to `new URL(url, siteUrl)` only when relative. But the early `if (size && ...)` path always normalises to a leading slash, breaking absolute external URLs. Detect external before normalising.

title: search: Lunr integration not wired
body: Lunr is the lightweight client-only alternative for sites under a few hundred posts. Implement a build step that emits `dist/search-index.json` and ships a tiny vanilla-JS search widget. Useful where Pagefind's WASM overhead is too much.

title: search: no Algolia / Meilisearch adapter scaffolds
body:
Beyond Pagefind, document two more common providers:
- Algolia: emit a `dist/.nectar/algolia-records.json` that users push via `algoliasearch` CLI; provide DocSearch-compatible CSS classes.
- Meilisearch: emit the same JSON shape; provide a `nectar push:meilisearch` thin wrapper around `meilisearch-js`.
Keep emit-only logic in nectar; pushing is user's responsibility.

title: search: no search UI partial provided
body: Even if Pagefind is wired, themes need an HTML widget. Ship a default `partials/search.hbs` and a small CSS module that themes can opt into via `{{> search}}`. Allow theme override.

title: security: Markdown render is unbounded — single 1 GB post blocks the build
body: src/content/markdown.ts:14 calls `marked.parse(body)` on whatever fits in a `string`. There is no per-post size cap. A contributor PR with a 500 MB Markdown file (or a small file with deeply nested blockquotes / lists) can OOM the build runner. Bun strings cap at 2 GB; even close to that bound, marked's tokenizer is quadratic in some pathological inputs. Mitigation: stat each .md file at load, refuse > N MB (configurable, default e.g. 5 MB), and consider an explicit `marked` token-count guard.

title: security: `asset` helper emits the URL inside a SafeString without context-aware encoding
body: src/render/helpers/assets.ts:21 returns `new SafeString(escapeHtml(url + …))`. The `escapeHtml` only encodes `&<>`, missing `"` and `'`. If the URL ends up inside `href="…"` (the usual case) Handlebars' own attribute escape would normally save us, but a SafeString *skips* further escaping. So an asset filename like `a"><script>x</script>.css` (file names with `"` are legal on POSIX) ends up unencoded in the attribute. Mitigation: encode for attribute context (or simply not wrap in SafeString and let Handlebars escape) — or reject filenames containing `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`, backticks at load time.

title: security: `import-ghost` does not check for path collisions before overwriting existing content
body: src/ghost/import.ts:155 writes `content/posts/${slug}.md` unconditionally. Re-running the importer (or running it against an export that has two posts sharing a slug, which is invalid Ghost but possible in tampered exports) silently overwrites prior files. The user may have made local edits between imports. Mitigation: refuse to overwrite without `--force`; emit a report listing files that would change.

title: security: `link_class` and `link` helpers take `for=`/`href=`/`target=` from theme without origin checking
body: Themes can pass any string. While these helpers HTML-escape attribute values, they do not validate URL schemes — `{{#link href="javascript:alert(1)"}}Click{{/link}}` renders a working XSS link. Mitigation: refuse non-http(s)/mailto/relative href in `link`, document the constraint, and add a test.

title: security: `link` helper accepts arbitrary `target` and lacks `rel=noopener` by default
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:40-48 emits `<a … target="${escapeAttr(target)}">` with no `rel="noopener noreferrer"` injection. Themes using `{{#link target="_blank"}}` to off-site URLs leak `window.opener` to the target page, enabling tabnabbing. Mitigation: when `target=_blank` is present, automatically append `rel="noopener noreferrer"` (or refuse without it).

title: security: `nectar new` overwrites existing files without prompt
body: src/cli/commands/new.ts:33 unconditionally `writeFile(dest, …)`. Running `nectar new post "Existing Post"` on a title that slugifies to an existing slug clobbers the previous post — no `--force` flag required, no collision check. Same issue in `nectar import-ghost` (overwrites content/posts/<slug>.md on every run). Mitigation: refuse to overwrite unless `--force` is passed, or write to `<slug>-<n>.md` when collisions exist.

title: security: `social_url` helper for Mastodon trusts user-supplied host in handle
body: src/render/helpers/urls.ts:48-53: when the Mastodon handle contains an `@`, the helper splits on it and emits `https://${host}/@${user}` with no validation of `host`. A handle of `me@evil.example/?" onmouseover=alert(1) x="` produces a URL containing a quote that breaks out of any unescaped HTML attribute context. While Handlebars triple-stash escapes by default, helpers like `<a href="{{social_url type=\"mastodon\"}}">` rely on Handlebars' attribute escaping, which does not validate URL syntax. Mitigation: validate the host against a hostname regex, refuse malformed inputs, return empty string.

title: security: `tags` helper builds links with handcrafted `escapeAttr` that misses unicode tricks
body: src/render/helpers/content.ts:73 produces `${escapeHtml(tag.name)}`. `tag.url` is computed from `joinUrl(site.url, '/tag/${slug}/')`. If a contributor sets `slug: \">

title: security: bun.lock present but no integrity-only install check in CI
body: `bun.lock` exists, but there is no CI workflow at all in .github/ (none exists in repo). On `bun install` Bun verifies tarball hashes against the lockfile, but human-run installs on contributor machines can silently miss tampering. Mitigation: add a CI workflow that runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile` and `bun pm scan`, and document `--frozen-lockfile` for contributors.

title: security: config schema does not validate URLs / colors / locales
body: src/config/schema.ts: every `z.string()` accepts arbitrary content. `site.url` is not URL-checked. `site.accent_color` is a free-form string — themes that drop it into CSS open CSS-injection (already covered by separate finding). `site.locale` is unconstrained and used as `<html lang=\"…\">`. Mitigation: tighten schema with `z.string().url()`, regex for accent_color, BCP-47 regex for locale.

title: security: example theme ships gulpfile.js / yarn.lock — running `npm install` inside the theme directory executes theme-author code
body: example/themes/source/ contains a real Ghost theme with its own package.json + gulpfile + yarn.lock. If a user runs `npm install` inside the theme dir (Ghost themes typically do for asset builds), arbitrary preinstall scripts run as the user. Not an issue for Nectar itself, but document that users should treat third-party themes as untrusted code and pin `--ignore-scripts` when installing theme dev deps.

title: security: feeds emit `<![CDATA[…]]>` of post.html without escaping `]]>` sequence
body: src/build/feeds.ts:36 wraps `${post.html}` in a CDATA section. If `post.html` contains the literal sequence `]]>` (legal in HTML, common in code-block examples), the CDATA ends prematurely and the rest of the RSS document is corrupted (or worse, attacker-controlled markup is interpreted as RSS). Mitigation: split `]]>` into `]]]]><![CDATA[>` (the standard RSS-safe replacement) before insertion.

title: security: ghost_head canonical, og:url derived from `site.url + route.url` without validating site.url
body: src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:85 constructs canonical via `new URL(path, base)`. If `site.url` in config is `javascript:` (yes, `new URL('/', 'javascript:foo')` throws — but `new URL('/', 'http://x\"')` does not throw and produces nonsense). A misconfigured `site.url` can poison every canonical / og:url / RSS link. Mitigation: zod-validate `site.url` as a strict http(s) URL at config load.

title: security: handlebars partial recursion is unbounded — infinite-loop DoS via theme
body: src/render/engine.ts:60-72 registers every template/partial under both `name` and `partials/name`. There is no detection of cyclic partials (`a.hbs` includes `{{> b}}`, `b.hbs` includes `{{> a}}`). Handlebars itself does not stop infinite recursion; the build will hang or stack-overflow. Threat: malicious theme. Mitigation: track a render-depth counter in a private data frame (`@depth`) and throw if it exceeds e.g. 64, or do a static cycle check at theme-load time.

title: security: helper `t` interpolation uses `replaceAll` with no escaping — values may contain markup
body: src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:21-34: `out.replaceAll("{key}", String(value))` inserts hash-arg values into a string then returns it; in themes this is often used inside `{{{t "…"}}}` (triple-stash), so any `<` in the value renders as HTML. With locale files acting as semi-trusted input (theme-shipped), an attacker who controls a locale file or a `t` call site can XSS. Mitigation: HTML-escape value before interpolation when the helper is used in default (double-stash) context; document that triple-stash with `t` requires the substituted values to be trusted.

title: security: no SBOM and no provenance / signed releases
body: Repo does not generate an SBOM (CycloneDX / SPDX), the project is not published yet (`private: true`) but once published there is no plan for `--provenance` (npm) or sigstore signing. Downstream consumers cannot verify the published tarball was built from the claimed commit. Mitigation: when releasing, publish with `npm publish --provenance` from GitHub Actions, and add a CI job that emits a CycloneDX SBOM as a release asset.

title: security: no Subresource Integrity (SRI) for fingerprinted assets
body: Assets are fingerprinted by filename but no `integrity="sha256-…"` attribute is emitted on `<link>` / `<script>` tags. If a user serves CSS/JS from a CDN that gets compromised, browsers cannot reject tampered files. Mitigation: extend the `asset` helper to optionally emit SRI hashes when invoked from `<link>` / `<script>` contexts (`{{asset 'x.js' integrity=true}}` or a dedicated `sri` helper).

title: security: per-route render context spreads `post` properties onto `this` (prototype-conflict risk)
body: src/render/engine.ts:101-103 does `Object.assign(ctx, data.post)`. A post with frontmatter keys named `__proto__` / `constructor` / `prototype` would be problematic. zod schema does not currently parse posts (loader returns raw objects). Mitigation: filter / reject reserved keys in frontmatter; create `ctx` from `Object.create(null)`.

title: security: serve command binds to all interfaces and has no auth / rate limit
body: Bun.serve in src/cli/commands/serve.ts has no `hostname` option, so it binds 0.0.0.0 on most platforms. On a developer laptop on coffee-shop Wi-Fi this exposes the unbuilt dist contents (which may include draft excerpts, source maps, etc.) to anyone on the LAN. No rate limit, no concurrency cap, no request log. Mitigation: default `hostname: '127.0.0.1'`, add `--host` flag for explicit binds, document that `nectar serve` is not production.

title: security: serve command path normalisation may allow traversal on Windows / decoded URLs
body: src/cli/commands/serve.ts:21-27 builds `filePath = normalize(join(distDir, url.pathname))` and rejects if `!filePath.startsWith(distDir)`. Issues: (1) `distDir` lacks a trailing separator, so `dist` matches `distOther` (low impact since outputDir is normally not adjacent to lookalikes, but the check is incorrect in principle); (2) `url.pathname` is URL-decoded by `new URL`, so `%2e%2e/secret` becomes `../secret` before the join — relying on `normalize` to collapse `..` is OK on POSIX but `path.normalize` does *not* resolve `..` past the root when the path is relative, which can lead to surprising results on Windows where `\` is also a separator; (3) no defence against backslash in URL path on Windows. The serve command is documented as 'local only' but operators sometimes proxy it; document loudly that it is not production-grade. Mitigation: use `path.resolve` + `realpathSync` and compare `filePath.startsWith(distDir + path.sep)`.

title: security: theme assets directory walk reads every file fully into memory for hashing
body: src/theme/assets.ts:13-15 reads each asset into a Buffer to compute SHA-1. A theme containing a 4 GB binary asset (legal — themes can ship arbitrary files) OOMs the build. Mitigation: stream-hash files larger than e.g. 16 MB using `Bun.file(...).stream()` piped into the hasher.

title: security: turndown processes Ghost HTML with no size/timeout — ReDoS / memory blowup risk
body: src/ghost/import.ts:8 instantiates `new TurndownService(...)` once, then calls `turndown.turndown(post.html)` per post (line 205) with no length limit. Turndown internally builds a DOM via @mixmark-io/domino and runs many regexes; a maliciously authored Ghost post body (e.g. millions of nested empty tags or a pathological string for one of turndown's collapsed-whitespace regexes) can pin a CPU core or exhaust memory. Mitigation: cap per-post HTML length (e.g. 10 MB), wrap turndown in a try/catch and skip with a warning if it throws, and consider running large imports in a child worker so OOM does not kill the CLI.

title: seo: Home page <title> is just 'Nectar Example' with no descriptor
body: example/dist/index.html line 5: `<title>Nectar Example</title>`. There is no tagline or description in the title, so search engines and tab bars carry only the site name. Best practice: `<title>{{site.title}} — {{site.tagline_or_description}}</title>` on the home page only. Fix: thread `site.description` (already configured) into the home-route meta.title.

title: seo: JSON-LD on home/tag/author archives is a stub WebSite — should be CollectionPage with hasPart
body: On example/dist/index.html, /tag/news/, /author/casper/ JSON-LD is `{ "@type": "WebSite", name, url }`. For tag and author archives a `CollectionPage` (with `mainEntity` of ItemList referencing each post) is more semantically correct. Home is acceptable as WebSite but should include `potentialAction` for SearchAction. Fix: in ghost-head.ts buildJsonLd, switch on route.kind to emit WebSite (home) with SearchAction, CollectionPage (tag/author/index), and Article (post).

title: seo: No per-post OG image generation — only echoes feature_image
body: ghost-head.ts (lines 81-84) selects og_image / twitter_image / feature_image. There is no code path that generates a per-post OG image from the title and author. Result: posts without a feature_image (e.g. if a user omits one) emit no og:image at all, and existing posts share the same generic cover art. Fix: introduce an optional component `components.og_images = { enabled = true, template = "…" }` that renders an SVG-to-PNG OG image per post at build time and rewrites the meta tag.

title: seo: No rel="prev" / rel="next" on paginated archives
body: Tag/author/home archives can paginate via /tag/news/page/2/ etc., but no <link rel="prev"> or <link rel="next"> is emitted in <head>. Although Google deprecated rel=prev/next, Bing and feed crawlers still use them. Verify by inspecting <head> on a paginated tag page (when more than `posts_per_page` posts exist for a tag). Fix: in ghost-head.ts, read route.data.pagination.prev_url / next_url and emit alternate-pagination links.

title: seo: defaultMeta() canonical is always site root, not route URL
body: src/build/routes.ts `defaultMeta()` (lines 149-161) sets `canonical: config.site.url` for every route. The ghost-head helper happens to recompute canonical from `route.url` (ghost-head.ts line 85) so the rendered HTML ends up correct, but the meta object on the route is misleading and any future feature relying on it (sitemap lastmod? AMP? linkpreview?) will pick up the wrong value. Fix: in defaultMeta, build `canonical: absoluteUrl(config.site.url, route.url)` and remove the duplicate logic from ghost-head.ts.

title: seo: rss.xml is missing <lastBuildDate>, <generator>, <docs>, <ttl>
body: src/build/feeds.ts (lines 40-50) channel skeleton lacks: <lastBuildDate>, <generator>Nectar</generator>, <docs>https://www.rssboard.org/rss-specification</docs>, optional <ttl>, and a channel-level <image>. Verify at https://validator.w3.org/feed/. Fix: add these channel-level elements with values pulled from build time and site config.

title: seo: rss.xml items lack <author>, <category>, <enclosure> for feature_image, and <dc:creator>
body: Each <item> in example/dist/rss.xml emits only title/link/guid/pubDate/description/content:encoded. There is no `<dc:creator>` for the post author, no `<category>` for each tag, no `<enclosure>` for the feature_image. Many feed readers (NetNewsWire, Feedly, Inoreader) render author and inline images poorly without these. Fix: add dc namespace, emit `<dc:creator>` per post.authors[0].name; emit one <category> per tag; emit <enclosure> if feature_image exists.

title: seo: sitemap.xml emits paginated archive URLs but not the page URLs themselves
body: Looking at example/dist/sitemap.xml, /tag/news/ is listed but /tag/news/page/2/ is not (because planRoutes() only contains pagination index 0 in this small example). However, if more posts exist, the planRoutes() emits page/2/, page/3/, … and they get pushed into urls[] (pipeline.ts line 56 `urls: routes.map(r => r.url)`). The result: paginated tag archives bloat the sitemap with low-value URLs. Best practice: omit paginated archive pages (page/2+) from the sitemap. Fix: in pipeline.ts filter routes whose url matches /page/\d+/$/ before sending to emitSitemap.

title: subscribe-forms: no client-side success/error UX without provider JS
body:
Source's form has `

` and a `Email sent` for success. Ghost's portal JS toggles these. With our shim, after the form POSTs to provider, the user is taken to the provider's success page — `data-members-error` and the success span are dead. Fix: include a tiny ~1KB inline JS that does fetch(form.action, …) and toggles success/error classes locally. Optional; off by default; opt-in via `[components.portal] inline_submit = true`.

title: subscribe-forms: no honeypot / spam protection in default form rewrite
body: When we rewrite `data-members-form` to a provider URL, also inject a hidden honeypot field (`<input name="website" tabindex="-1" autocomplete="off" style="display:none">`). Buttondown / Beehiiv ignore it; bots fill it; we can reject server-side. Document for `provider = "custom"` users.

title: test: asset: base_path with trailing slash does not double up
body:
Input: base_path `/blog/`, asset `app.js`.
Expected: `/blog/assets/app.js` (single slash).
Why: pin joinPath behaviour.

title: test: asset: leading slash in input is stripped before lookup
body:
Input: `{{asset "/built/app.js"}}` should look up `built/app.js` and `assets/built/app.js`, not `/built/app.js`.
Expected: same fingerprinted output as the unslashed variant.
Why: many Ghost themes write `/assets/...` paths; the helper must not double-prefix or miss the registry.

title: test: asset: returned URL is a SafeString with HTML-escaped output
body:
Input: a registered asset whose fingerprinted path contains `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`.
Expected: SafeString instance with those characters escaped to `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`.
Why: prevents accidental XSS when asset names contain special characters; SafeString avoids double-escaping by Handlebars.

title: test: asset: returns base_path-joined raw `assets/<path>` for unknown asset
body:

Expected: `/blog/assets/missing.png`.
Why: covers the unresolved fallthrough branch; ensures missing files do not throw and that base_path is prepended.

title: testing: no coverage threshold gates
body: Once Codecov is wired (separate issue), set a floor (e.g. 70% lines, 80% on `src/render/helpers/*`) and block PRs that drop below. Without a gate, coverage trends down.

title: testing: no fuzz / property tests
body: `marked` + `gray-matter` + user content is a rich attack surface. Add property tests around frontmatter parsing (random YAML), markdown rendering (random unicode), slug derivation (random titles). `fast-check` works under Bun.

title: testing: no large-content soak test
body: Add a generated fixture of 5000 posts + 100 tags + 10 authors and assert build completes under N seconds and under M MB peak RSS. Pins us to a published perf SLA.

title: testing: no perf benchmark suite
body: Add `tests/bench/` using `mitata` or Bun's built-in bench: render-1000-posts, paginate-tags-with-100-tags, import-ghost-5000-posts. Track regressions over time (publish JSON to a gh-pages branch).

title: theme-dev: no `nectar theme:zip` distribution helper
body: Ghost themes are uploaded as `.zip`. Add `nectar theme:zip <path>` that produces a tidy archive (excluding `node_modules`, `*.map`, `.DS_Store`) ready to share or upload back to a Ghost instance. Also useful for our future theme marketplace.

title: theme-dev: no custom-settings UI / form generation
body: `@custom` exists in DESIGN.md but theme authors who declare `package.json` `config.custom.*` get no UI for end users to flip those switches. Either: (a) emit a `nectar.toml` snippet showing defaults, or (b) ship a tiny `nectar admin` web UI (out of scope?) that writes the toml. (a) is the minimum.

title: theme-loader: locale parse failures are silently swallowed
body: src/theme/loader.ts:loadLocales has `try { JSON.parse } catch {}` that ignores broken locale files. Print a warning.

title: theme/alto: comment_count class hash-param + reading_time hash params already use t-helper subexpression
body: partials/loop.hbs:34 calls `{{reading_time minute=(t "1 min read") minutes=(t "% min read")}}` mixing helper-subexpression with hash params. Nectar's reading_time helper (content.ts:34) accepts `minute=` and `minutes=` — confirm subexpression args resolve before being passed. Add test.

title: theme/alto: white_logo_for_dark_mode image custom + img_url chain
body: default.hbs:24 uses `{{img_url @custom.white_logo_for_dark_mode}}`. img_url helper (src/render/helpers/assets.ts) expects a string or object — passing a custom-config value works only if buildCustom stores the URL string. Verify image-type customs hit the img_url string branch correctly with absolute=true also.

title: theme/alto: {{#get "posts" filter="featured:true" limit="10" include="tags" as |featured|}} block-param syntax
body: partials/featured.hbs:1 uses `as |featured|` block params. Our get helper supports block params (src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:124) but the test coverage is thin; add a regression test for Alto's featured carousel including the inverse branch ({{#if featured}}).

title: theme/biron: t-helper with positional+hash combo `{{t "Page {page} of {pages}" page=page pages=pages}}`
body: biron/partials/pagination.hbs:7. Our t helper interpolates `{page}` / `{pages}` placeholders. Verify two named placeholders both substitute correctly (not just first).

title: theme/biron: {{#foreach navigation}} expects `slug` field (slugified label)
body: biron uses similar nav pattern to Editorial. Same need for `slug` on NavigationItem.

title: theme/biron: {{action}} / {{hidden}} / {{script}} / {{input_email autofocus=...}} helpers
body: biron/partials/subscribe_form.hbs uses old form helpers and additional hash params like `class=` `placeholder=` `value=` `autofocus=` on input_email. Our input_email (content.ts:122) only honors `placeholder`. Add full hash-param support: class, value, autofocus, id.

title: theme/bulletin: home.hbs branching on @member vs @site.members_enabled with three-state fallback
body: bulletin/home.hbs has nested {{#if @site.members_enabled}}{{#unless @member}}...else...{{/unless}}{{else}}...{{/if}}. Make sure both flags are reliably present in @site/@member context (see casper P0). Without them, all three branches collapse to the bottom 'foreach posts limit="1"' branch, which is actually a safe degradation but visually wrong.

title: theme/bulletin: t-helper interpolation with HTML inside concat subexpr
body: partials/article.hbs:8 uses `{{{t "in {primaryTag}" primaryTag=(concat "" primary_tag.name "")}}}`. Verify our t helper preserves HTML content from named arguments (does not double-escape). Our interpolate() function does `replaceAll(`{${key}}`, String(value))` — should be safe with triple-stash, but add test.

title: theme/casper: @member / @member.paid context unavailable
body: default.hbs:59-68, post.hbs:103-118 use `{{#unless @member}}` / `{{else}}` and access gating. buildRootData() in src/render/engine.ts currently hardcodes `member: undefined`, which is correct for unauthenticated SSG but Casper expects the conditional branches to render the signed-out marketing UI; verify the `{{else}}` branch (subscribe CTA) actually renders by adding tests for unauth path, and document a way to inject `member` for preview.

title: theme/casper: `{{has visibility="public"}}` negated form `{{^has visibility="public"}}` works only by accident
body: partials/post-card.hbs:23,52 use `{{^has visibility="public"}}` followed by `{{#has visibility="members"}}`. Our `has` helper (src/render/helpers/blocks.ts) accepts visibility, but it breaks on `{{else}}{{#has visibility="members"}}` chains when ctx.visibility is missing. Verify ctx.visibility is always non-empty on post objects (default 'public') and add tests for the inverted form.

title: theme/casper: custom config `image` type values would need URL resolution (it has none, but Headline/Dawn/Alto/Liebling do)
body: Casper itself only uses select/boolean/text in package.json custom. However the broader fleet (Headline, Dawn, Alto, Liebling) declares `type: image` settings (white_publication_logo_for_transparent_header, white_logo_for_dark_mode, dark_mode_logo). buildCustom() in src/render/engine.ts just merges plain values; ensure `@custom.<name>` for image fields returns a proper resolvable URL string. Add doc + test that image-type customs work with img_url and a direct {{@custom.x}}.

title: theme/dawn: feed-calendar uses `{{date published_at format="DD"}}` (positional date argument)
body: partials/loop.hbs:6 passes `published_at` as positional arg to date helper, separate from format=. Our date helper (src/render/helpers/date.ts:19) accepts a positional arg first — verify and add test specifically for `{{date published_at format="MMM"}}`.

title: theme/ease: very small theme — verify default.hbs members nav UI degrades when @member/@site.members_enabled flags missing
body: ease/default.hbs:34-57 mirrors Casper's members UI but with the same dependency on @site.members_enabled etc. Confirm the falsy-fallback branch produces a usable layout.

title: theme/edition: {{> "content" width="wide"}} hash params not propagated to partial
body: post.hbs:6 passes `width="wide"`. content.hbs:39 reads `{{kg-width-{{width}}}}`. Verify hash-params from {{> partial}} reach the partial body. In Nectar's registerPartials() (engine.ts:60-72) partials are raw strings compiled by Handlebars; the param should arrive but a unit test should confirm.

title: theme/editorial: @labs.subscribers legacy flag
body: editorial/post.hbs:31 uses `{{#if @labs.subscribers}}`. buildRootData() sets `labs: {}` (engine.ts:136). Subscribers section never renders. Either expose labs.subscribers=true by default (Editorial is legacy) or document that @labs.* is always empty so old themes silently lose features.

title: theme/editorial: subscribe_form helper expects placeholder hash param
body: editorial/post.hbs:35 calls `{{subscribe_form placeholder="youremail@example.com"}}`. Our subscribe_form helper (content.ts:116) emits a static form ignoring the hash. Accept `placeholder` and inject into the input element.

title: theme/editorial: {{#foreach navigation}}{{slug}}{{/foreach}} needs `slug` field on nav items
body: Same partial line 7 uses `nav-{{slug}}` class. NavigationItem in schema (label + url) lacks slug. Either derive slug from label (slugify) or expose explicit slug field.

title: theme/headline: custom field `type: image` requires URL output, not raw value
body: package.json declares `white_publication_logo_for_transparent_header` as type=image. Templates expect `{{@custom.white_publication_logo_for_transparent_header}}` to yield a usable image URL. Nectar passes whatever string is configured. Document and validate that image-type customs are stored as resolvable URLs and that {{img_url @custom.x}} produces correct output (currently img_url just returns the path).

title: theme/headline: partial-include hash params (`{{> "loop-grid" has_large_post=false}}`) leak into wrong scope
body: index.hbs:20 and partials/related-posts.hbs:12 pass hash params to `{{>}}` partials. Vanilla Handlebars merges them into the *context*, but our engine.ts hands the partial a forEach'd post object that is not a plain object. Verify that hash-params on partial-include are reachable as `has_large_post` inside loop-grid.hbs and don't shadow `post.has_large_post`. Add regression test.

title: theme/headline: {{#match pagination.pages ">" 1}} numeric comparison
body: home.hbs:7 uses `{{#match pagination.pages ">" 1}}`. Our match helper (src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:152) coerces via Number() — works — but pagination.pages may be undefined on routes where pagination isn't built (e.g. home with single page). Ensure that pagination is always populated (at least `{page:1, pages:1, total:N}`) so the comparison evaluates false instead of NaN > 1.

title: theme/journal: ^is "paged" inverse-route check
body: journal/index.hbs:5,37 use `{{^is "paged"}}` to render the 'latest issue' card only on page 1. Our `is` helper supports `paged` (returns true if pagination.page > 1) — confirm the inverse also works correctly via the helper's `inverse` branch.

title: theme/journal: {{#foreach posts from="2"}} skip-first-post idiom
body: journal/index.hbs:38 uses `{{#foreach posts from="2"}}`. Our foreach (blocks.ts:13) supports `from=` and `to=` — confirm that from=2 + no limit yields posts[1..end]. The implementation slices `from - 1` to `to` where to defaults to `from + limit - 1`. Bug: when limit is omitted, limit = items.length, so to = 2 + items.length - 1 — OK. Add explicit test for journal's idiom.

title: theme/krabi: @labs.members flag (deprecated)
body: krabi/post.hbs:28 uses `{{#if @labs.members}}`. labs.members was a Ghost 2.x flag. buildRootData() returns `labs: {}`. Set labs.members=true when @site.members_enabled is true so old themes degrade gracefully.

title: theme/krabi: legacy {{action}}/{{hidden}}/{{script}} helpers in subscribe_form
body: biron/partials/subscribe_form.hbs (and Krabi members templates) use `<form action="{{action}}">{{hidden}}{{script}}</form>`. These are Ghost 1.x-2.x helpers that the subscribe_form helper auto-populated as form action+csrf+JS. Nectar should either register no-op `action`/`hidden`/`script` helpers that emit safe defaults (action='/members/api/send-magic-link', hidden=hidden inputs, script='') or refuse to compile and document legacy unsupported.

title: theme/krabi: members/signin.hbs members/signup.hbs members/subscribe.hbs members/account.hbs as templates
body: Krabi ships members/{signin,signup,subscribe,account}.hbs. These are routed via Ghost's hidden /members/* routes. Nectar should either emit static copies at /members/account/index.html etc. or document that members/* templates are not routed in static builds.

title: theme/liebling: `data-members-form="subscribe"` newsletter form requires Ghost Members JS
body: partials/newsletter-form.hbs uses `data-members-form="subscribe"` — Ghost Members' Portal script wires this up. Static Nectar builds don't include Portal. Either ship a minimal subscribe form handler in assets or document that subscribe forms are inert without Portal JS.

title: theme/liebling: enable_native_search custom + ghostSearchApiKey JS injection
body: default.hbs:99-109 conditionally injects `<script>const nativeSearchEnabled = true; const ghostSearchApiKey = '...'</script>`. The `@custom.search_api_key` is an API key (sensitive). Make sure Nectar's config layer doesn't accidentally leak secret keys in committed configs; document expected handling.

title: theme/london: meta_title with page= positional hash (`page=(t " (Page %)")`)
body: london/default.hbs:10 uses `{{meta_title page=(t " (Page %)")}}` — t-subexpression returns a string with `%`. Our meta_title (content.ts:79) accepts page=, replaces % with pagination.page. Verify the chain t→meta_title preserves the `%` placeholder.

title: theme/ruby: t-helper with sub-expression `(authors)` inline
body: ruby/post.hbs:11 uses `{{{t "By {authors}" authors=(authors) }}}`. The subexpression `(authors)` calls our authors helper with no fn → returns names joined ', '. Verify the helper returns a plain string (currently it does when options.fn is absent — content.ts:54).

title: theme/solo: package.json custom field `type: color` for background_color
body: Solo is the only theme using `type: color`. Templates expect `{{@custom.background_color}}` to be a CSS-valid color (`#xxxxxx`). Nectar's buildCustom() returns whatever was set — make sure config schema validates color format and doesn't strip the leading #. Add explicit color-type handling and a unit test.

title: theme/wave: og_description used as audio podcast src URL
body: partials/player.hbs:3 uses `<audio src="{{og_description}}">`. Wave abuses og_description as a podcast audio URL. Nectar exposes og_description (model.ts:85) — verify it round-trips raw (not HTML-escaped or replaced with meta_description). Ensure {{og_description}} preserves the original URL and tests cover this.

title: theme/wave: {{#unless @site.secondary_navigation}} requires navigation array
body: default.hbs:78,83 uses `{{#unless @site.secondary_navigation}}`. Our SiteData has secondary_navigation (model.ts:14) as NavigationItem[]. Empty array is *truthy* in Handlebars unless the registerHelper override returns falsy for empty arrays. Verify unless behaves correctly with empty arrays — likely need to coerce empty arrays to falsy somewhere or update Wave-style usage. Document edge case.

title: theme: no `nectar theme:new` scaffolder
body: Add a CLI that creates a minimal theme skeleton.

title: theme: no `nectar theme:zip` to package a theme for distribution
body: Source theme has `gulp zip`. Add equivalent.

title: web-standards: <script> and <link> tags miss type="text/css" on some, lack type=module on JS
body: example/dist/index.html line 179: `<script src="/assets/built/source.a573c212aa.js"></script>` — no type="module", no defer/async. Modern themes should ship ES modules so the renderer can output `<script type="module" src=…>` and avoid blocking parsing. Fix: detect ESM-compatible bundles at theme load and emit type="module"; otherwise emit defer.

title: web-standards: Font preload references a font file that is also referenced inside an inline @font-face
body: example/dist/index.html lines 12, 19: the preload `<link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" href="/assets/fonts/inter-roman.woff2" crossorigin="anonymous">` and the inline @font-face src match — good. But font-display is `optional` (line 18), which Lighthouse flags as 'reducing text visibility' since the font often won't be applied if not cached. Fix: change font-display to `swap` (recommended for blog text content) — `optional` is only sensible when the build has good control of cache-control, which a static SSG doesn't.

title: web-standards: HTML output contains many empty / whitespace-only blocks
body:
example/dist/index.html lines 121-126, 138-141, etc. contain ` ...` and empty `

` / `
` blocks. While valid HTML, this bloats payload (~10-15% in this example), and the empty author spans push assistive-tech to announce nothing where author should appear. Fix: guard partial output with `{{#if}}` blocks rather than rendering empty wrappers, and run output through an html-minifier pass for production builds.

title: web-standards: No <picture> with AVIF/WebP fallback
body: Card and article images emit only with the source format. No wrapper with AVIF/WebP sources. This costs ~25-40% of image bytes vs modern formats. Fix: in the image helper, render with and when those variants exist on disk.

title: web-standards: No Content-Security-Policy guidance, no CSP <meta>
body: Pages run inline scripts (the contrast-calc script in <head>) and inline styles, which would be blocked by any reasonable CSP. Nectar doesn't emit a CSP meta or document the recommended headers for the host. Fix: (a) compute a nonce for the inline contrast script (or remove it — see issue on FOUC); (b) ship a recommended `_headers` file (Cloudflare Pages) and `netlify.toml` snippet in docs; (c) optionally emit `<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" …>` via a component.

title: web-standards: No cache-busting on /content/images/ — only on /assets/built/
body: example/dist/index.html line 24 references `/assets/built/screen.9d5b34cbe9.css` (fingerprinted) but content/images/welcome-cover.svg (line 111) is referenced by its raw path. If a user replaces the image without renaming, CDN caches will serve the stale file. Fix: add an option to fingerprint /content/images/* during build and rewrite references in HTML/RSS.

title: web-standards: No critical CSS / above-the-fold inlining
body: screen.css is fetched render-blocking on every page. For TTFB-bound first paint this is a major LCP cost. Other SSGs (Hugo, Astro) extract critical CSS at build. Fix: integrate a critical-css component (using `critters` or `penthouse`) that inlines first-paint rules and async-loads the rest.

title: web-standards: No theme-color meta
body: No `<meta name="theme-color" content="…">` in any page. Mobile Safari and Chrome use this to colourise the URL bar. Fix: emit `<meta name="theme-color" content="{theme.config.background_color}">` plus a `media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)"` variant.

title: web-standards: No web app manifest emitted
body: No /site.webmanifest or /manifest.json exists in example/dist, and pages emit no `<link rel="manifest">`. PWA installability, theme-color, and Android tab colouring are unavailable. Fix: when config.site provides a logo and a theme color, emit a minimal manifest with name, short_name, start_url, theme_color, background_color, icons and link to it.

title: web-standards: Preload directives target both as="style" and as="script" but the JS preload is pointless
body: example/dist/index.html line 10: `<link rel="preload" as="script" href="/assets/built/source.a573c212aa.js">`. The same file is loaded at the bottom of <body> via a parser-blocking <script> tag. Preload of the same URL just before the bottom-of-body script wastes a connection slot and provides no perf win unless the script becomes async/defer. Fix: either drop the preload or move the actual <script> to <head> with defer.

title: a11y: No focus-visible / focus-management CSS hints documented
body: Inspecting the buttons and links shows no focus outline class is emitted (relies on UA default). The CSS file (`screen.css`) is theme-controlled, but Nectar should document a minimum-contrast focus-visible requirement for themes and warn at load-time if `:focus-visible` is missing. Verify by tabbing through file://.../example/dist/index.html. Fix: add a contrib doc to docs/THEME_GUIDE.md requiring themes to ship :focus-visible styles meeting WCAG 2.4.7.

title: a11y: PhotoSwipe markup is duplicated on post and page templates but missing on listing pages
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html and example/dist/about/index.html emit the entire `.pswp` overlay markup (lines 322-362 / 159-199). The same markup is omitted from index, tag, author. Inconsistency aside, this is a ~2KB payload added to every post page even when no images are clickable (the SVG covers aren't lightboxable). Fix: only emit the PhotoSwipe markup when at least one image in the page content has a lightbox attribute, or move it behind the components system.

title: activitypub: WebFinger / .well-known endpoints from Ghost not emitted
body: Ghost emits `/.well-known/webfinger` for ActivityPub. We don't. Add optional emit of static WebFinger response from `[components.activitypub] handle = "@me@example.com"` config — purely static (no incoming request handling). Lets users get found by their fediverse handle.

title: activitypub: experimental Ghost Fediverse plugin not modelled
body: Ghost 5.x added experimental ActivityPub support (one-account-per-publication). For Nectar, recommend external tools (Bridgy Fed, fed.brid.gy) since static sites can't actually respond to AP signed requests. Document as out-of-scope with link to user guidance.

title: analytics: no membership-tier-level segmentation source
body: Member-segmented analytics (free vs paid open rates) live in Ghost's email send logs. Document gap: refer users to their ESP's analytics; in Nectar, no per-tier data crosses the static boundary.

title: api: ?fields= projection — document non-support; emit full records only
body:
Ghost behavior: `?fields=title,slug` returns a projected subset.

Nectar gap: static JSON always returns the full record.

Proposal: don't implement field projection. Document that the static API always returns full objects; clients should project in-memory. Cite `@tryghost/content-api`'s behavior — it doesn't strictly require server-side projection.

title: api: ?formats=html,plaintext — both always emitted; ?formats= ignored
body:
Ghost: `?formats=html,plaintext,mobiledoc` toggles which content representations come back. Default is `html` only; `plaintext` and `mobiledoc` are opt-in.

Nectar gap: `Post` model already has `html` + `plaintext`. Mobiledoc/Lexical not supported (per GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md).

Proposal: always emit both `html` and `plaintext` (cheap, already computed). Omit `mobiledoc`/`lexical` (would need source-of-truth content representation we don't keep). Document that `?formats=mobiledoc` returns no field — clients should fall back to `html`.

title: api: ?include=authors,tags — always embed by default (no opt-out cost)
body:
Ghost behavior: `?include=tags,authors` decides whether each post carries embedded tags/authors arrays vs id-only refs.

Nectar gap: model already has full `tags[]` / `authors[]` arrays.

Proposal: always emit posts with `tags`, `authors`, `primary_tag`, `primary_author` embedded. Adds ~hundreds of bytes per post but avoids needing client-side joins. Document as a deliberate divergence: `?include=` is ignored, includes are always on.

title: api: ?include=count.posts — pre-populate on tags/authors shadows unconditionally
body:
Ghost requires explicit `?include=count.posts` on tags/authors endpoints to get post counts.

Proposal: in the static JSON, always include `count.posts`. Saves a request param consumers tend to forget, and the data is free at build time.

title: api: ?key= validation — emit a key registry file dist/.well-known/ghost-content-keys.json (optional)
body:
Some Ghost integrations sniff allowed keys. For full SDK compatibility we don't need this, but a `.well-known` file documenting that any key is accepted may help integrations.

Proposal: low priority; mention in docs. Don't implement until a real consumer reports breakage.

title: api: ?order= — emit canonical orderings only (posts: published_at DESC; tags: name ASC; authors: name ASC)
body:
Ghost supports arbitrary `?order=field+direction`.

Proposal: static shards use Ghost's defaults — posts `published_at desc`, tags `name asc`, authors `name asc`. Document non-support of arbitrary `?order=`. Clients can re-sort in memory.

Verify defaults match Ghost's actual defaults before shipping.

title: api: ?v=5.0 version query — strip and document; emit a single representation
body:
Ghost SDK appends `?v=v5.0` (or `?v=v3.0` for legacy). Static hosts ignore query strings.

Proposal: only emit the latest (v5) representation. Document that older `?v=` values won't get a different file. Future-proof by versioning the *path* (e.g. `/ghost/api/content/v5/posts.json`) if we ever ship a v6 schema differential.

title: api: AMP — Ghost emits /post-slug/amp/; decide and either emit or 410
body:
Ghost themes ship an `amp.hbs` template; Ghost auto-generates `/post-slug/amp/`. Google has deprecated AMP ranking signals; many Ghost sites disable it.

Proposal: opt-in `[components.amp] enabled = false` default. When enabled, emit `dist/<slug>/amp/index.html` rendering through theme's `amp.hbs` (or a built-in fallback). When disabled, document that consumers should not link to `/amp/` paths.

title: api: GET /content/newsletters/: emit empty dist/content/newsletters.json stub
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/newsletters/` returns newsletter definitions; tied to members/email features.

Nectar gap: out of scope, but absence causes SDK 404s.

Proposal: emit empty-array shadow identical in shape to /content/tiers/ stub. Cheap, removes a class of consumer breakage.

title: api: GET /content/tiers/: emit empty dist/content/tiers.json stub for SDK compatibility
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/tiers/` returns membership tiers. Nectar is SSG-only with no membership.

Nectar gap: SDK consumers calling `api.tiers.browse()` get 404 / network error today.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/tiers.json = { tiers: [], meta: { pagination: { page:1, limit:15, pages:0, total:0, next:null, prev:null } } }` so the SDK call resolves to an empty array cleanly. Document in `GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` that members are out of scope.

title: api: HEAD/OPTIONS support — document via _headers preflight
body:
Browsers fire OPTIONS preflight on cross-origin requests with auth headers. Many static hosts auto-handle OPTIONS, but not all.

Proposal: in the CORS `_headers` block, ensure preflight returns `Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400` to cache the preflight. Document host-specific quirks (S3 needs explicit OPTIONS in bucket policy).

title: api: TypeScript types — publish @nectar/content-api-types matching the emitted JSON shape
body:
Consumers writing TypeScript want types for the JSON they fetch. `@tryghost/content-api` ships its own types but they include fields we omit (members, mobiledoc).

Proposal: publish `@nectar/content-api-types` exporting `NectarPost`, `NectarPage`, `NectarTag`, `NectarAuthor`, `NectarSettings`, `NectarPagination`. Re-export from `src/content/model.ts` with API-only fields stripped/added. Lowest priority — wait for first external consumer to ask.

title: api: document non-API endpoints we DO emit (sitemap.xml, rss.xml, robots.txt)
body: Cross-link from `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` API section: which JSON we emit, which we don't. List existing emissions (sitemap, rss) and proposed (content/*.json). Saves consumers from wondering.

title: api: emit dist/.well-known/ghost.json with site metadata (api version, generator) for service discovery
body:
Optional service-discovery file consumers could probe to detect a Nectar-served Ghost-compatible site vs a real Ghost.

```json
{ "generator": "nectar", "version": "0.x", "ghost_api_version": "v5.0", "endpoints": { "content": "/ghost/api/content/" } }
```

Consumers can use this to opt into Nectar-aware behavior (e.g. skip admin calls).

title: api: image URLs — Ghost's /content/images/size/wWIDTH/path responsive variants — document non-support
body:
Ghost's Image API resizes on-the-fly: `/content/images/size/w600/2024/01/foo.jpg`. Themes use this via `{{img_url ... size="w600"}}`.

Nectar gap: covered separately as a helper. For the API shadow: image URLs in `post.html` / `post.feature_image` won't have responsive variants unless the user pre-generates them.

Proposal: document. Optional follow-up: `[components.images] sizes = [600, 1200, 2000]` pre-generates resized assets at build via `sharp`. Out of scope for this audit; file separately if not already.

title: api: oembed — explicitly skip and document
body:
Ghost has `GET /oembed/?url=...` that proxies to oembed providers (YouTube, Twitter, etc.) and is used by the editor to embed cards. Build-time SSG has no editor, no need for the endpoint.

Proposal: do not emit. Add a one-line note in `GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` under "things we don't handle".

title: api: per-tag RSS at /tag/:slug/rss/ — already filed under feeds; cross-reference
body: Per task instructions, RSS variants (per-tag, per-author) are already filed separately. Cross-link from this API audit: any consumer expecting `/tag/foo/rss/` is hitting an RSS endpoint, not the Content API, but `@tryghost/content-api` consumers may also want `/content/posts/?filter=tag:foo` to reflect the same set — covered by the tag-filtered shard issue above. No new work in this issue; ensure label hygiene only.

title: api: post.email_only / post.email — set false/null on all posts
body:
Ghost has email-only posts (`email_only: true`, only sent to subscribers, never on the public site). Plus `post.email` object with stats.

Nectar: no email, no members.

Proposal: emit `email_only: false`, `email: null` on every post for shape compatibility with the SDK's TypeScript types.

title: api: post.featured boolean — verify it's filterable via the featured shard
body: Crosscheck: tag/author shards proposed above. `featured.json` should contain `posts.filter(p => p.featured)`. Trivial; just confirm it's wired.

title: api: post.send_email_when_published — emit false constant
body: Internal Ghost field. SDK types include it. Static API should emit `false` for shape parity.

title: api: webhooks — explicitly skip; document for tooling consumers
body:
Ghost Admin API has webhooks. Static SSG has nothing to webhook from.

Proposal: document non-support. If a CI/CD integration needs build-completion notifications, point users at GitHub Actions / their host's deploy hooks.

title: build-pipeline: <html> emits no `data-build` / build hash attribute for cache invalidation diagnostics
body: Useful for operators is a build hash on <html data-build="$git_sha"> for traceability when a CDN serves stale content. Not present. Fix: thread build metadata into the root partial via a `<meta name="nectar:build" content="$git_sha">`.

title: build-pipeline: JSON-LD emits `dateModified: undefined` when updated_at missing — silently dropped by JSON.stringify
body: ghost-head.ts line 114 — `dateModified: ctx.updated_at` — when updated_at is missing the field is omitted by JSON.stringify (good) but the inverse (when both fields exist but equal) means search engines see a 'never updated' signal. Fix: only emit dateModified when it differs from datePublished by more than a few seconds.

title: build-pipeline: emitAsset() copies fingerprinted + logical paths but doesn't dedupe across multiple themes
body: src/build/emit.ts lines 24-33 copies each asset twice (fingerprinted + logical). For large themes this doubles I/O. Fix: write the file once and create a hardlink / symlink for the logical path on POSIX, or emit only one copy and add a build-time redirect file.

title: build-pipeline: feeds.ts escapeXml replaces apostrophes with ' — invalid in HTML, fine in XML
body: src/build/feeds.ts line 59 — `'` is valid in XML 1.0 but **not** in HTML5 named entities. Since rss.xml is XML this is fine, but the same `escapeAttr` in ghost-head.ts replaces ' with ' (line 155). The two escapers are inconsistent. example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html line 53: `content="First post! A quick tour of what's working..."` while RSS uses `'`. Verify: grep `'` in /dist HTML — should be zero. Fix: consolidate into one escape utility shared by feeds.ts and ghost-head.ts.

title: build-pipeline: ghost-head.ts emits og:type="website" for pages (about) but article structure is more appropriate for content pages
body: ghost-head.ts line 87-88: only `route.data?.post` triggers og:type="article". The about page (a content-heavy /about/) gets og:type=website and the JSON-LD becomes WebSite. Better SEO is og:type=article (or 'profile' for author) with appropriate JSON-LD. Fix: extend computeMeta to map route.kind=page → og:type=article and emit Article JSON-LD with publisher set to the site.

title: build-pipeline: navigation slugify ignores unicode — 'カテゴリ' becomes 'nav-' with hyphens stripped
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts lines 71-76: `slugify` uses `/[^\w]+/g` which only matches ASCII. A nav item labeled 'カテゴリ' produces `<li class="nav-">`, breaking class-based targeting. Fix: import or implement a unicode-aware slugifier (e.g. github-slugger or @sindresorhus/slugify) — already a likely dependency for content slugs.

title: build-pipeline: writeHtml() doesn't compress, no gzip/brotli precompression option
body: Hosts like GitHub Pages serve pre-compressed files only if they exist alongside (`.gz`/`.br`). Nectar emits only raw HTML/CSS/JS. Fix: add an optional `[build.precompress]` step using Bun.gunzipSync / zstd / brotli to emit `.br` and `.gz` next to each text asset.

title: build: asset fingerprint hash is sha1 truncated to 10 hex chars
body: sha1 is broken cryptographically but fine for cache-busting. The 10-char prefix has ~40 bits — birthday collision odds at 10k files = nontrivial. Switch to base64url of sha256 first 12 chars.

title: build: emitter does not write atomically
body: Concurrent reads of dist/ during build can serve partial files. Write to a temp file then rename.

title: build: feeds.ts emitSitemap signature takes `content` but doesn't use it
body: Clean up signature.

title: build: no `_redirects` / Netlify config emit
body: From redirects.yaml when implemented.

title: ci: no auto-merge workflow for trusted PRs
body: After Renovate is wired, configure auto-merge for green minor/patch dep PRs via `peter-evans/enable-pull-request-automerge` + branch protection. Keeps the dep maintenance load near zero.

title: ci: no markdown link checker
body: `docs/DESIGN.md` already references files and external links (e.g. bun.sh). Add `lychee` or `markdown-link-check` on PRs that touch *.md to catch broken anchors and 404s.

title: ci: no spell-check workflow
body: Add `cspell` (or `typos`) over `docs/`, `README.md`, `src/**/*.ts` strings. Ghost's docs are infamously well-edited; sloppy spelling in our docs will undercut perceived quality.

title: cli: `import-ghost` accepts only a single export file
body: Ghost's CLI export can split DBs into multiple files. Accept a directory.

title: cli: `serve` does not select an open port if 4321 is taken
body: Bun.serve throws. Try 4321, then 4322, ... up to 4400; log the chosen port.

title: cli: build does not surface deprecation warnings from dependencies
body: marked, handlebars and turndown can emit deprecation messages. Capture and forward through logger.warn with `[deps]` tag so we can act on them.

title: cli: check doesn't print a final 'All checks passed' line
