body: check.ts:13-23 prints per-phase 'OK' lines but no terminal summary. Add a final `All checks passed` (green when colours land). When `--strict`/errors, end with `1 error, 2 warnings`.

title: cli: command alias map (cargo-style)
body: Allow short aliases: `b` for build, `s` for serve, `n` for new, `c` for check. Plus user-defined aliases in ~/.config/nectar/aliases.json. Cargo's `[alias]` is the gold standard.

title: cli: editor integration — Helix/Neovim LSP for frontmatter
body: Provide a tiny LSP (or contribute to existing markdown-oxide / harper) for nectar frontmatter: validate against the content schema, autocomplete tag/author slugs from existing content. Could be a JSON Schema + standard YAML LSP for low effort.

title: cli: editor integration — Obsidian publish plugin
body: Obsidian users are a huge cohort for SSGs. Plugin that publishes selected vault notes into a Nectar project's content/posts dir, mapping frontmatter (tags, aliases, cssclass→none, date), copying linked images into assets_dir, and writing wikilinks as markdown links. Calls `nectar new` underneath.

title: cli: missing 'nectar plugins list' as future extension surface
body: Reserve the namespace. If/when plugins ship (e.g. remark/rehype plugins, image transforms), users will need `nectar plugins list|add|remove|update`. Stub it now to return 'no plugins installed' and keep the name.

title: cli: missing `--locale` global for date / number formatting in output
body: When humans read build summaries with `Intl.NumberFormat`, locale matters. Default to system, but allow `--locale en-US` override for CI consistency.

title: cli: missing `nectar test` placeholder
body: Themes/plugins eventually want a unit test runner. Reserve the name; for now `nectar test` could forward to `bun test` and warn it's a passthrough.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar authors rename <old> <new>`
body: Rewrite author slug across content. Mirror of tags rename. Critical when an author changes their handle.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar cache`
body: `nectar cache path|clear|stats`. Once render caches and image transforms exist, users need to manage them. Defer until caching is implemented but reserve the name.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar redirects`
body: List/add/check redirects. Needed when renaming slugs (see content rename issue). Pair with config.build.redirects[] schema extension.

title: cli: missing subcommand — `nectar serve --proxy <api-base>`
body: Many sites need to call an external API during dev. Add proxy support: requests to /api/* are forwarded to the configured base URL. Bun.serve fetch can do this trivially.

title: cli: new: date defaults to UTC ISO but timezone-aware blogs want local
body: new.ts:27 uses `new Date().toISOString()` (always Z). Per global rule 'UTC統一' this is fine, but document it and allow `--date` override. Also include the site timezone in the frontmatter `published_at` if config.site.timezone is set, to make Ghost-compat seamless.

title: cli: new: should print next steps after success
body: After 'Created path', show 'Next: nectar build && nectar serve' or 'nectar dev'. Keep it short; suppress with --quiet.

title: cli: new: usage line is wrong for missing kind
body: When kind is omitted, new.ts:11-14 prints the message but technically the user might run `nectar new`. The error 'Usage: nectar new <post|page> "Title"' is helpful, but should distinguish 'missing kind' from 'invalid kind' and include exit codes & link to docs. Also: log to stderr (currently logger.error already does).

title: cli: no `--json` output mode for machine integration
body: CI/CD pipelines want structured output. Add `--format=json` to all subcommands.

title: cli: no `nectar upgrade` / version check on run
body: Add a once-per-day stale-version check (network-skipped under CI/`NECTAR_NO_UPDATE_CHECK`) that prints "A new version of Nectar is available: 0.4.0 → 0.5.0 (run `nectar upgrade`)". For a CLI users may install once and forget for months, this matters.

title: cli: no `version --json` and no version stability promise
body: Document SemVer policy and stability of public surface.

title: cli: no opt-in usage telemetry
body: To prioritise feature work we need to know which helpers are exercised, which themes are used, build time distribution. Add an opt-in `nectar telemetry on|off|status` that POSTs anonymised events to a Plausible/Umami endpoint. First run must prompt with clear privacy copy and default to off. See Next.js / Astro for reference.

title: cli: no shell completions (zsh/fish/bash)
body: Use `commander`/`yargs` or hand-roll. Generate via `nectar completions <shell>`.

title: cli: no support for ~/.config/nectar/config.json user-global flags
body: Companion to per-project .nectarrc. User-global defaults for things like NO_COLOR, telemetry preferences, default deploy target. Standard XDG path.

title: cli: serve does not gzip / br responses
body: Bun.serve does not automatically compress. For local dev it doesn't matter, but if anyone uses `nectar serve` as a minimal prod fallback they'll be sad. Add `--compression auto|gzip|br|none` (default none in dev).

title: cli: serve should print a copy-pasteable curl example for tricky URLs
body: When `--base-path /blog` is set, the printed URL must include it. Beyond that, on --verbose dump a couple of representative URLs (homepage, latest post, sitemap, rss) so the user can verify quickly.

title: cli: serve: support HTTPS via --tls-cert/--tls-key
body: Some browser features (service worker, secure cookies) require HTTPS. Bun.serve supports `tls: { cert, key }`. Add `--tls-cert <pem>` and `--tls-key <pem>` flags. Bonus: `--tls` with mkcert auto-generated certs in ~/.cache/nectar/certs.

title: cli: standardise help width / wrap
body: Wrap at min(process.stdout.columns, 100). Currently the usage block is a fixed array of strings (index.ts:6-18). Inconsistent wrapping on narrow terminals (<80 cols) is annoying — make help renderer width-aware.

title: cli: tab characters in help text break alignment
body: Cosmetic but worth fixing once the help renderer exists. Render flag tables with padStart/padEnd, not literal spaces. Add a snapshot test against `nectar build --help`.

title: comments: no webmention/IndieWeb provider
body: Some Ghost users on the indieweb stack pipe through webmention.io. Add it as an option alongside giscus/disqus/utterances.

title: community: no bug-bounty / responsible-disclosure incentive
body: Once funded, even a $100 bounty per critical vuln (huntr.dev or directly) puts us on the radar of supply-chain researchers. Document criteria in SECURITY.md.

title: community: no funding info (.github/FUNDING.yml)
body: Add GitHub Sponsors / Open Collective / Polar / Ko-fi link. Even if we don't expect revenue early, signalling sustainability matters to evaluators who burned out on Ghost's pricing.

title: config: navigation entries lack icon/external-link/target metadata
body: Real Ghost themes can read additional nav metadata. Extend schema.

title: cross-theme: `feature_image_alt` falls back to title across many themes (`{{#if feature_image_alt}}{{feature_image_alt}}{{else}}{{title}}{{/if}}`)
body: Just a usage pattern audit: verify ctx.feature_image_alt is string|undefined (model.ts:65 ✓) and that empty string is treated as falsy in {{#if}} (Handlebars default).

title: cross-theme: `img_url` with `format=` hash for webp/avif unsupported
body: Some themes pass `format="webp"`. Document or implement format negotiation. Currently the hash is silently ignored.

title: cross-theme: `lang` helper should respect post-level locale override
body: Our lang helper (i18n.ts:16) always returns the site locale. Ghost allows posts to override (locale on post). Check and add post.locale support if used.

title: cross-theme: `published_at` filter operator like `published_at:<'2024-01-01'`
body: Themes sometimes use date filters in {{#get filter="published_at:<'2024-01-01'"}}. Our filter parser (blocks.ts:255) doesn't recognize date comparisons or quotes. Add operators >, <, >=, <= on date strings.

title: cross-theme: `{{asset}}` with `hasMinFile="true"` selects .min variant
body: Our asset helper (assets.ts:8) accepts `hasMinFile` but discards it. Ghost's behavior: in production prefer `<name>.min.<ext>` if present. Implement: if hasMinFile && theme.assets has `<base>.min.<ext>`, use it.

title: cross-theme: `{{date format="YYYY"}}` with no positional arg uses current date inside footers
body: Most default.hbs use `© {{date format="YYYY"}}` in footer. Our date helper (date.ts:30) falls back to new Date().toISOString() — works. Add explicit test that the no-positional form returns the current year.

title: cross-theme: `{{has tag="..."}}` inside non-post context returns nothing
body: Some themes use {{has tag=...}} in get-helper foreach context. Our has helper (blocks.ts:67) reads this.tags. Verify within foreach the iterated post's tags are accessible.

title: cross-theme: pagination.next_url / prev_url present, but pagination.next / prev block helpers also expected
body: Some themes (Dawn old) use `{{#prev}}...{{/prev}}` and `{{#next}}...{{/next}}` block helpers from Ghost's pagination context. Verify Nectar exposes the relevant fields.

title: deploy: bunny: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/bunny.md`: Storage Zone, Pull Zone, custom domain, Edge Rules.

title: deploy: bunny: no Bunny CDN pull-zone / storage-zone docs
body:
Bunny CDN can either pull from origin or serve a Storage Zone uploaded via S3-compatible API or `bunnycdn-storage-cli`. No docs in Nectar.

**Fix**: docs in `docs/deployment/bunny.md`.

title: deploy: digitalocean-app-platform: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/digitalocean-app-platform.md`: App Spec, custom domain, deploy-on-push, env vars.

title: deploy: digitalocean-app-platform: no `app.yaml` (App Spec) sample
body:
DO App Platform uses App Spec (YAML) for static-site components. No sample.

**Fix**: ship `examples/do-app-platform/app.yaml` with `type: static`, `build_command`, `output_dir`, `routes`.

title: deploy: fastly: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/fastly.md`: service setup, origin (S3/R2/GCS), VCL/Compute@Edge, purging.

title: deploy: surge: deploy quickstart docs
body: Write `docs/deployment/surge.md`: `surge dist/ my-site.surge.sh`, custom domain, CNAME.

title: docs: no architecture diagram in DESIGN.md beyond ASCII art
body: Add SVG diagrams via Mermaid.

title: dx: build summary doesn't show output size
body: Final log line says `Built X routes (Y assets)` but no total bytes, largest pages, largest assets. Add a per-build summary that lists top-5 largest outputs and total size. Helps users notice when a stray 50MB GIF made it into `dist/`.

title: ghost-card: ALL: AMP — none of the card markup is AMP-compatible
body:
Ghost optionally renders an AMP version (`amp.hbs`). AMP forbids `<iframe>` (use `<amp-iframe>`), `<video>` (use `<amp-video>`), arbitrary CSS, inline scripts. Our card markup is broadly AMP-hostile.

**Fix**: if AMP is in-scope (it may not be — Ghost is deprecating AMP), build an AMP-specific render path with `<amp-*>` substitutes. Otherwise document that AMP is unsupported.

title: ghost-card: ALL: a11y — `<figure>` should have role + figcaption association
body:
Figures with captions should associate `<figcaption>` with `<figure>` via implicit ARIA. Our shortcode-emitted figures should respect this, including `aria-labelledby` on the figure pointing to the figcaption when used as a card title.

**Fix**: render-pass adds `aria-labelledby` where appropriate; lint pass verifies.

title: ghost-card: ALL: i18n — card-emitted strings ("Subscribe", "Loading…", "Play") not translated
body:
Audio/video/signup/button cards include user-facing English strings. Themes need to translate them via `t` helper.

**Fix**: every card-emitting helper that includes a string passes it through the `t` helper (Source has `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts`). Default English; allow theme overrides.

title: ghost-card: ALL: migration — emoji shortcodes (`:smile:`) and Unicode emoji handling
body:
Ghost stores emoji as Unicode characters directly (UTF-8). Turndown and marked both pass them through. No issue here — but worth confirming with a CJK/emoji-heavy fixture so we don't silently lose them due to file-encoding glitches.

**Fix**: add a fixture with mixed Japanese + emoji + RTL Arabic in callout/toggle bodies and ensure round-trip preserves them.

title: ghost-card: ALL: migration — multi-image responsive (`<picture><source>…</picture>`) flattened
body: Ghost rarely uses `` but some plugins emit it for AVIF/WebP serving. Turndown only keeps the inner ``. AVIF/WebP variants are lost. **Fix**: Turndown rule for `` that preserves all `` variants in a shortcode. Renderer emits the full picture element.

title: ghost-card: ALL: source theme partial — `loop.hbs` strips card markup when used in lists
body:
Post list partials (`loop.hbs`, `post-card.hbs`) render an excerpt that often comes from `{{excerpt}}` (plaintext) — fine. But some themes call `{{content words=20}}` (HTML truncation), which would leave open `<figure class="kg-card …">` tags with no children. Our `truncateWords` in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:147` strips tags entirely — safe but loses any leading inline formatting.

**Fix**: `truncateWords` should sanitise to plain text (it already does), so cards in list previews aren't an issue. Confirm with a fixture where post body starts with a card.

title: ghost-card: ALL: theme-dev — Source has both `.kg-content-wide` and `.kg-width-wide` rules; clarify which wins
body:
`screen.css:411-412` ties `.kg-width-wide` and `.kg-content-wide > div` together. `:393` has `.kg-width-full.kg-content-wide`. The two classes serve different scopes (`kg-width-*` on the card, `kg-content-wide` on the post wrapper). Migration shortcodes need to set only `kg-width-*` and rely on the theme to declare `.gh-content` (or `.kg-content-wide`).

**Fix**: document the contract in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` so theme authors and shortcode authors don't collide.

title: ghost-card: audio: cover-art generation — Ghost generates default waveform thumbnail
body:
When an audio file has no cover art, Ghost generates a waveform SVG and uses it as the thumbnail. This SVG is in the HTML export as a base64 data URI or inline `<svg>`. Turndown drops SVG. We lose the visual placeholder.

**Fix**: detect the inline SVG and preserve it inside the audio shortcode as base64, or generate a fresh waveform at build (requires audio file on disk + a waveform lib).

title: ghost-card: blockquote-alt: migration — `<blockquote class="kg-blockquote-alt">` flattened to `>` blockquote
body:
Ghost has two blockquote types: default and `kg-blockquote-alt` (a larger pull-quote variant). Turndown emits both as `> Quote text` — the alt variant is indistinguishable on round-trip.

**Fix**: Turndown rule for `blockquote.kg-blockquote-alt` → emit `> [!ALT]\n> Quote text` (GFM-alert style) or a custom directive. On render, restore the `kg-blockquote-alt` class.

title: ghost-card: blockquote-alt: rendering — Source CSS styles `.kg-blockquote-alt` but nothing emits it
body:
`screen.css:2349` styles `blockquote.kg-blockquote-alt`. Without a directive in markdown there's no way to produce the class.

**Fix**: pair with the migration directive above. Update markdown docs.

title: ghost-card: bookmark: caption — bookmarks can have figcaptions, currently lost
body: Ghost bookmark cards accept an optional figcaption (``). Turndown drops the figcaption. **Fix**: bookmark shortcode accepts `caption="…"`. Renderer applies `kg-card-hascaption` when present.

title: ghost-card: callout: backwards compat — pre-v5 callouts used different class names
body:
Older Ghost (v4) callouts may have used `kg-card-callout` instead of `kg-callout-card`, or different color class names. Imports from very old Ghost installs may not match newer Turndown rules.

**Fix**: a fixture of Ghost v4 HTML; Turndown rules must match both old and new selectors.

title: ghost-card: callout: nesting — callouts inside toggles inside callouts
body:
Ghost permits arbitrary card nesting. If our directive parser is line-based and doesn't handle balanced fences (`:::callout` inside `:::toggle`), nested cards break.

**Fix**: use a parser that tracks fence depth (`remark-directive` does this; ad-hoc regex usually doesn't). Add a torture-test fixture.

title: ghost-card: code: migration — inline `<code>` with backticks: Turndown handles correctly, no action needed
body:
Inline code (`foo` outside a `
`) is handled by Turndown's default rule → backtick-wrapped. No card type involved. Confirm no regression with code containing backticks (escape with double-backticks). Add a fixture.

**Fix**: add a smoke test for `code with \`backticks\` inside`.

title: ghost-card: code: prismjs language map — Ghost stores language names that don't always match Prism
body:
Ghost lets users type anything in the language field (`Typescript`, `ts`, `tsx`, `TS`). Prism (and Shiki) expects canonical names (`typescript`). Migration should normalise via a small map.

**Fix**: in the code Turndown rule, normalise language strings: `ts → typescript`, `js → javascript`, `sh|bash → bash`, `yml → yaml`. Reject unknown languages (or pass through as the fence info string).

title: ghost-card: code: rendering — no copy-to-clipboard button (Casper has it)
body:
Modern Ghost themes (Casper) attach a copy button to every code block via JS at hydration. Source does not. Authors used to Ghost will notice it's missing.

**Fix**: small JS in Source's `assets/js/` that finds `pre code` and prepends a `<button class="kg-code-copy">`. ~20 lines. Optional.

title: ghost-card: email-cta: migration — segment targeting (`free`/`paid`) is lost
body:
`email-cta` carries a `segment` attribute (`status:free`, `status:-free`, `status:paid`) controlling who sees it in the email. Ghost serialises this in the HTML comment fence as `<!--kg-card-begin: email-cta-->`. Inner HTML has no marker.

On web we should strip these unconditionally; on a hypothetical newsletter exporter we'd need the segment. Today both data points (the card body AND the segment) are lost.

**Fix**: same strip-on-import as `email`. If/when newsletter export is built, preserve segment metadata via a frontmatter array `email_ctas: [{ segment, html }, …]` rather than inline body content.

title: ghost-card: embed: codepen — `data-prefill` and theme attrs lost
body:
CodePen embeds carry `data-default-tab`, `data-theme-id`, `data-pen-title`, `data-user`, `data-slug-hash` on a `

`. Turndown reduces to a `[link](url)`. Embed dies. **Fix**: provider rule for `.codepen` paragraphs; preserve all data attrs in shortcode.

title: ghost-card: embed: github gist — `<script src="https://gist.github.com/USER/HASH.js">` dropped
body:
Gist embeds are pure `<script>` tags. Turndown drops `<script>`. Gist embeds vanish entirely.

**Fix**: preprocess HTML to detect Gist scripts and convert to `{% gist url="…" %}`. Renderer re-emits the script tag (or uses Gist's noscript HTML fallback).

title: ghost-card: embed: reddit — `<blockquote class="reddit-card">` and oEmbed handling
body:
Reddit embeds use a blockquote+script pattern similar to Twitter. Lost on Turndown. Lower priority since fewer authors use them.

**Fix**: provider-specific rule for Reddit blockquotes.

title: ghost-card: embed: spotify — height/width preservation
body:
Spotify embeds vary in height (152px for track, 232px for playlist, 380px for full). Turndown drops both. A fixed height in the embed renderer would render wrongly for some types.

**Fix**: embed shortcode preserves `height` param; default per provider+type when missing.

title: ghost-card: embed: tiktok — `<blockquote class="tiktok-embed" cite="https://…">` cite attr lost
body:
TikTok embeds carry the canonical URL in a `cite` attr on the blockquote. Turndown's default blockquote rule drops attrs.

**Fix**: embed shortcode preserves canonical URL; renderer emits the full blockquote contract.

title: ghost-card: embed: vimeo — `<iframe>` with `dnt=1` lost on import
body:
Vimeo embeds with privacy flag (`dnt=1` in src) become plain iframe URLs after Turndown drops the iframe.

**Fix**: embed shortcode preserves the full source URL including query params.

title: ghost-card: embed: youtube privacy — `youtube-nocookie.com` domain not used
body:
Privacy-focused themes use `youtube-nocookie.com` instead of `youtube.com` (no cookies set until play). Ghost lets authors opt in. We should expose this as a config flag.

**Fix**: `nectar.config.ts → embeds.youtube_privacy = true` swaps domain at render. Document.

title: ghost-card: gif: rendering — no special GIF handling, no autoplay-on-scroll
body:
Performance-minded sites convert GIFs to `<video autoplay muted loop playsinline>` MP4s on render. Ghost does this when the export retains the Giphy `mp4_url`. We have no path to do the same — we'd render the GIF directly.

**Fix**: add an `images.gif_strategy = 'gif'|'mp4'|'auto'` config. `'mp4'` requires the asset pipeline to convert at build time (ffmpeg or pre-converted by Giphy).

title: ghost-card: gif: theme-dev — gif cards inherit `.kg-image-card` styling which is acceptable as fallback
body:
Since Ghost emits GIFs as image cards, the `.kg-image-card`/`.kg-image` CSS in Source applies. No additional CSS needed unless we promote to `<video>`.

**Fix**: if we add MP4-conversion, ensure the rendered `<video>` element is sized identically (`.kg-image` class on the video element, or sibling rule).

title: ghost-card: header: button color — `--kg-header-button-color` CSS var lost
body:
Header v2 supports a per-card accent color stored as an inline `--kg-header-button-color: #abc;` custom property on the root element. Turndown drops inline styles. The header button defaults to the theme's accent on render.

**Fix**: shortcode accepts `button_color="#abc"`. Renderer emits `style="--kg-header-button-color: #abc"` on the wrapper.

title: ghost-card: header: subscribe form variant — `kg-header-card-button-subscribe` opens portal modal
body:
When a header CTA is set to "subscribe" mode (member-only sites), Ghost emits a button with `data-portal="signup"` that opens the portal modal. We don't have portal. The button does nothing.

**Fix**: when migrating, detect `data-portal="signup"` and either (a) convert to a link to a `/subscribe` page, or (b) wire to the configured `members.provider`. Document.

title: ghost-card: hr / divider: migration — Turndown emits `* * *` which marked converts back to `<hr>` fine
body:
Ghost divider card: `<hr class="kg-divider-card"/>` (or just `<hr>`). Turndown's default rule emits `* * *` which marked renders as `<hr>`. **The `kg-divider-card` class is lost** but nothing in the Source theme styles it specifically — only generic `hr` rules apply.

**Fix**: low priority. If we ever want to style dividers specially, add a Turndown rule preserving `kg-divider-card` via a comment or HTML passthrough.

title: ghost-card: hr: rendering — no `.kg-divider-card` styling; default `hr` is fine
body:
Source CSS has no `kg-divider` selector. Default `<hr>` styling applies. The `kg-card.kg-width-*` spacing rules at `screen.css:2273-2289` don't match a bare `<hr>` (no `.kg-card` class) so the spacing-around-card rules miss it.

**Fix**: optionally emit `<hr class="kg-card kg-divider-card">` to inherit card spacing. Trivial.

title: ghost-card: image: alignment — `align-left|center|right` modifiers used by older posts dropped
body:
Older Ghost mobiledoc posts used `kg-align-left|center|right` on image cards (now superseded by `kg-width-*`). Turndown drops the class. Posts that depend on right-aligned floating images render as full-width images.

**Fix**: Turndown rule for `kg-align-*` preserves alignment in shortcode `align` param. Renderer maps to `kg-align-*` modifier classes. Add minimal CSS to Source for left/right float.

title: ghost-card: image: animated PNG/WebP — `loading="lazy"` interaction
body:
Animated WebPs and APNGs behave like GIFs but ride through Ghost as image cards. They auto-play on scroll into view. `loading="lazy"` is fine; no special handling beyond size-budgeting.

**Fix**: add a fixture with an animated WebP to confirm round-trip preserves the format and dimensions.

title: ghost-card: image: lightbox — Ghost provides no built-in lightbox; many themes attach one
body:
Casper's `assets/js/casper.js` includes a lightbox that attaches to `.kg-image` on click (PhotoSwipe-based in newer versions). Source CSS does not include lightbox styles either. If we want feature parity, we need a small lightbox JS that hydrates `.kg-image` and `.kg-gallery-image`.

**Fix**: optional `nectar.config.ts → images.lightbox = 'casper'|'photoswipe'|'none'`. Document the bundle-size cost.

title: ghost-card: markdown: rendering — markdown cards are just markdown so no special handling needed
body:
On the render side, a markdown card's content **is** markdown. No special selector exists. The Ghost wrapper `<!--kg-card-begin: markdown-->` is informational only.

No fix needed beyond confirming `src/content/markdown.ts` ignores `<!--kg-card-* -->` comments (it does: marked passes them through as HTML comments which browsers ignore).

title: ghost-card: product: image-pipeline — product image has no srcset, hot-links to Ghost source
body:
Product card images use the same `__GHOST_URL__/content/images/…` placeholder, with no responsive variants. The card is typically 720px wide on desktop but the source image is often 2000px+ — wasted bandwidth.

**Fix**: route product images through the (future) asset pipeline with `sizes="(min-width: 720px) 720px, 100vw"`.

title: ghost-card: video: `kg-video-thumbnail-image-card` poster — multiple sizes lost
body:
Ghost stores video posters at multiple resolutions (matching image card srcset). Lost on Turndown.

**Fix**: when migrating, preserve poster URL; rely on image pipeline to generate variants. Add to video shortcode contract.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `admin_url`
body:
Some themes show a small admin link; some assets resolve through `@site.admin_url`. Not exposed in `SiteData`.

Fix: surface as optional config, default to `${site.url}/ghost/`.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `members_support_address` and `allow_self_signup`
body:
Themes that show "Contact support: {{@site.members_support_address}}" or branch on `@site.allow_self_signup` will get undefined.

Fix: surface both via config; document defaults.

title: ghost-compat: @site.lang vs @site.locale
body: Ghost themes use both. We currently set both to the same value via SiteData; document.

title: ghost-compat: AMP route (`amp.hbs` + `/<slug>/amp/` URL) not generated
body:
Ghost optionally renders AMP versions of posts via `amp.hbs`. Our route planner doesn't emit any AMP routes. Themes that ship `amp.hbs` (Casper does) won't have it rendered.

Fix: when `amp.hbs` exists, emit `/<slug>/amp/index.html` for each post; emit corresponding `<link rel=amphtml>` in canonical post head.

title: ghost-compat: Page `show_title_and_feature_image` defaults to true, Ghost defaults to true for new pages but admin can toggle
body:
We default true in `src/content/loader.ts:239`. Source uses `{{#match @page.show_title_and_feature_image}}` to decide whether to render the header. Verify that omitting the frontmatter key produces the right default.

Fix: add a test snapshot.

title: ghost-compat: Post `comment_id` absent — Ghost themes display comment counts via this field
body:
Comments are keyed by `comment_id`. Without it, even our stub `{{comments}}` can't be enhanced later.

Fix: add `comment_id` (default to `id`).

title: ghost-compat: Post `count.*` (signups, clicks, comments, conversions, positive/negative_feedback) absent
body:
Some themes display post engagement counters. Always 0 is fine for our static-build model, but the field must exist.

Fix: add `count: { signups: 0, clicks: 0, comments: 0, conversions: 0, positive_feedback: 0, negative_feedback: 0 }` to Post.

title: ghost-compat: Post `email_subject`, `email_only`, `send_email_when_published` absent
body:
Ghost themes don't usually render these, but some Casper-derived themes branch on `email_only` to skip rendering the body when the post was for email only.

Fix: add to Post model; map from frontmatter.

title: ghost-compat: Post `frontmatter` field absent — Ghost exposes the raw card-deck order, themes use it
body:
Ghost's Post.frontmatter is the structured Mobiledoc/Lexical. We don't expose anything. Most themes don't use it, but those that do (custom newsletter renderers) will fail.

Fix: out of scope; document.

title: ghost-compat: Site `lang` and `locale` are unified — but Ghost distinguishes them (locale e.g. `en-gb`, lang short `en`)
body:
`src/content/loader.ts:79` sets both to the same value: `locale: config.site.locale, lang: config.site.locale`. Themes that want `<html lang="en">` from `@site.lang` separately from `@site.locale` (`en-gb`) can't distinguish.

Fix: derive `lang` as the first BCP-47 segment of `locale`.

title: ghost-compat: Twitter card type is hard-coded `summary_large_image` — Ghost picks based on whether image exists
body:
`src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:35` always emits `summary_large_image`. Ghost emits `summary` when there's no image and `summary_large_image` when there is.

Fix: emit `summary` for no-image routes.

title: ghost-compat: `/feed/` URL alias for RSS not emitted
body:
Ghost serves RSS at `/rss/` AND historically `/feed/`. Themes/social-media-cards may link `/feed/`.

Fix: when emitting RSS, also write a copy or HTTP-style redirect at `/feed/index.html` → `/rss/`.

title: ghost-compat: `<link rel="alternate" hreflang>` not emitted for multi-locale sites
body:
Future-proofing: when Nectar supports multi-locale we should emit hreflang alternates. Out of scope today but worth filing.

Fix: track for multi-locale milestone.

title: ghost-compat: `@first` / `@last` / etc. exposed under foreach but Ghost also exposes them under built-in `{{#each}}` — verify Handlebars defaults
body:
Handlebars `{{#each}}` natively provides `@first`, `@last`, `@index`, `@key`. Ghost's foreach is documented as the preferred iteration helper but `{{#each}}` works too. We don't override `each`. Verify themes that use `{{#each posts}}` instead of foreach still get the loop variables.

Fix: tests.

title: ghost-compat: `@labs` context exposes empty object; Ghost exposes a map of beta-feature flags
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:137` sets `labs: {}`. Themes that branch on `{{@labs.newsletters}}` get falsy — usually fine. Document.

Fix: docs note that `@labs` is always empty.

title: ghost-compat: `@page` context populated only on page routes — Ghost populates it on every route
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:135` sets `page: route.kind === 'page' ? route.data.page : undefined`. Ghost's `@page` represents the page-specific metadata (title, slug, show_title_and_feature_image, etc.) and is undefined on non-page routes. OK.

But Source uses `{{@page.show_title_and_feature_image}}` only inside `#post` on page.hbs; works. Other themes may rely on global @page on non-page routes (e.g. error). Verify.

Fix: docs note: @page is page-only.

title: ghost-compat: `@site.locale` exposed as e.g. `en` while Ghost uses `en-gb` lower-cased — verify the canonical form
body:
Some themes set `<html lang="{{@site.locale}}">` directly. Ghost uses lower-case BCP-47 (`en`, `en-gb`). Our config accepts any string and passes through.

Fix: normalise to lower-case at load time; reject obviously-broken locales with a warning.

title: ghost-compat: `@site` exposed both as `@site` and `@blog` — verify `@blog` deprecated alias works in nested partials
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:131` sets both. Ghost deprecated `@blog` in 4.x but legacy themes still use it. OK.

Fix: tests confirm parity.

title: ghost-compat: `Bun.Glob` is used for content discovery — non-Bun runtimes break
body:
`src/content/loader.ts:157`, `src/theme/loader.ts:37`, `src/theme/assets.ts:11` use `new Bun.Glob(...)`. Node-only runtimes (which Ghost users may have) can't run Nectar. Not a Ghost-compat issue per se, but a portability gap affecting theme devs.

Fix: behind a runtime check, fall back to `fast-glob`.

title: ghost-compat: `body_class` does not include `hash-<slug>` for internal-tag slugs
body:
Ghost's body_class includes `tag-<slug>` AND, when a tag is internal (slug starts with `hash-`), often a `hash-…` class. Our `computeBodyClass` only adds `tag-<slug>`. Themes that style hidden hash-tags need both.

Fix: when on a tag route and `tag.visibility === 'internal'`, also push `hash-…`.

title: ghost-compat: `body_class` missing `error-template` on error pages and `members-template` on members area
body:
Real Ghost emits these. Since we don't have error or members routes (see separate gaps), this is downstream.

Fix: when error routes are added, include `error-template` and `error-<statusCode>` body classes.

title: ghost-compat: `content_type` override for custom routes not supported
body:
Ghost lets routes emit `text/xml` etc. for custom feeds. We emit only HTML. Themes that ship a custom JSON or XML feed in routes.yaml can't be replicated.

Fix: pass through to the emitter; set file extension and Content-Type appropriately.

title: ghost-compat: `meta_data` helper not implemented (referenced in docs as a data helper)
body:
Per Ghost docs there's a data helper `{{meta_data}}` that returns the full SEO blob (not just title/description) used for structured-data output. Used less today, but Casper-derived themes may reference it.

Fix: register a stub helper that returns an object with `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `og_*`, `twitter_*`.

title: ghost-compat: `og:locale` not emitted by ghost_head
body:
Ghost emits `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_US">` (Facebook expects underscored locales). Helpful for international shares.

Fix: emit og:locale from `@site.locale`, mapped to OG format.

title: ghost-compat: `reading_time` divisor (275 wpm) close to Ghost's (~275) — verify exact match
body:
Ghost uses 275 wpm. We use 275 (`src/content/markdown.ts:18`). OK, but image-time-bonus (Ghost adds 12s per image) is not implemented.

Fix: add image bonus to match Ghost's actual algorithm.

title: ghost-compat: `twitter_url` and `facebook_url` direct helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost also has `{{twitter_url}}` / `{{facebook_url}}` as standalone helpers (deprecated but used in old themes). We have only `social_url`.

Fix: register the deprecated aliases or document the requirement to use `social_url`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{!< layout}}` only finds layouts at the template-root path, not in `layouts/` subdir or as nested partials
body:
Our `LAYOUT_TEMPLATES` set in `src/theme/loader.ts:9` includes specific names. Layouts referenced from `{{!< name}}` are looked up in `theme.templates`. If a theme keeps layouts in a subdir (e.g. `layouts/default.hbs`), they're loaded under `layouts/default`, but the directive `{{!< default}}` won't find them.

Fix: also probe `theme.templates[\`layouts/${layout}\`]`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#match}}` `~` substring operator does not work for arrays
body:
Ghost lets you do `{{#match @custom.header_style "~" "Landing,Search"}}` — string-contains. Our `compare` function casts both to String and uses `.includes()`. Works for strings. But Ghost ALSO permits matching against an array — our impl forces toString which yields `"Landing,Search"` and substring-matches against that — actually mostly OK. Verify edge cases (`null`, undefined, numbers).

Fix: add tests.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#post}}` block helper does not reset scope when post is absent (e.g. on tag pages)
body:
Source's post.hbs wraps all content in `{{#post}}…{{/post}}`. On the post route, our `registerContextBlock` correctly picks `route.data.post`. But the helper falls back to `this.post`, and on a tag page `this` is the tag — there's no `.post` property — so it returns inverse. OK.

Fix: documented; tests confirm.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#post}}` on a paged post is not provided with prev/next context — themes calling `{{#prev_post}}` from inside need access
body: Inside `{{#post}}…{{/post}}`, themes do `{{#prev_post}}{{title}}{{/prev_post}}`. Since we never register `prev_post`/`next_post`, this is a dead helper. (See separate gap.)

title: ghost-compat: `{{#unless}}` chains with `{{else unless}}` not specifically tested
body:
Source uses chained `{{#unless}} … {{else unless …}}` patterns rarely, but Ghost themes commonly nest them. Handlebars supports natively; verify our setup doesn't break anything.

Fix: tests.

title: ghost-compat: `{{cancel_link}}` helper not implemented
body: Themes that render a portal `Cancel subscription` link use `{{cancel_link}}`. Absent from our helpers. Templates render empty. Fix: register a stub helper emitting `Cancel`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{encode}}` only does `encodeURIComponent` — Ghost also encodes for HTML attribute contexts
body:
Our impl `encodeURIComponent(String(value ?? ''))` is URL encoding only. Ghost docs say "Encode text to be safely used in a URL" — matches our behaviour. OK, but verify the precise edge cases (spaces become `%20`, not `+`).

Fix: add tests asserting behaviour against Ghost's examples.

title: ghost-compat: `{{excerpt}}` `characters=` truncation has no smart-word-boundary handling
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:30` slices at exact character count. Ghost soft-stops at the last word boundary, optionally appending `…`. Source uses `{{excerpt words="20"}}` (which we implement via `truncateWordsText`, OK) but `characters=` is naive.

Fix: when `characters` is set, slice and back up to the last whitespace.

title: ghost-compat: `{{img_url}} absolute=true` always returns site.url-resolved value — doesn't honour @site.url when @site has a custom CDN
body:
Ghost themes that serve images from a CDN set `@site.url` to the CDN host. Our `img_url` always resolves against `engine.content.site.url`. If a future config splits image CDN from site URL, we'd need separate logic.

Fix: add `[site.cdn_url]` config for image hosting.

title: ghost-compat: `{{input_email}}` does not emit Ghost's required class `gh-input` and `data-members-email` correctly with the surrounding form
body:
We emit `<input data-members-email type="email" required …>`. That's mostly right but misses `class="gh-input"` and the parent form context that Portal hooks into.

Fix: add the conventional class; document that without a Portal script the input is non-functional.

title: ghost-compat: `{{input_password}}` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's private/password-protected sites use `private.hbs` with `{{input_password}}` rendering the password form. Helper absent.

Fix: register as a stub (since we don't support private sites) that emits `<input type="password" name="password">`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{json}}` helper not implemented
body:
Per docs (utility): "Safely serialize values for inline JSON output." Used by themes embedding analytics or hydration payloads.

Fix: register `json` that JSON.stringify-encodes its argument and wraps in SafeString.

title: ghost-compat: `{{log}}` debug helper not implemented
body:
Ghost has `{{log foo}}` to console.log values in development. Useful for theme dev. Absent.

Fix: register `log` to print to stdout when `process.env.NECTAR_DEBUG`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{or}}` / `{{and}}` / `{{not}}` / `{{eq}}` are Nectar-only — Ghost doesn't ship these
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/flow.ts` registers `or`, `and`, `not`, `eq`. These are not Ghost helpers. Themes won't depend on them, but if a Nectar-tuned theme uses them and is later moved to Ghost, it breaks. Document as Nectar extensions.

Fix: docs note in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{partials}}` registration helper not implemented
body:
Documented utility: "Include chunks of reusable template code." The `{{partials}}` helper is shorthand for registering shared markup. Themes use it as a directive, but mostly they just call `{{> name}}` directly. Document and confirm whether action is needed.

Fix: document; usually no work required.

title: ghost-compat: `{{plural}}` does not handle Ghost's `count, plural, one, other` ICU style or pluralisation by locale
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/strings.ts:18` accepts `singular=`, `plural=`, `empty=` hashes, and substitutes `%`. Ghost's `{{plural}}` is similar (legacy) but newer themes use ICU via `{{t}}`. Document the limitation; tests show our helper works for English-only.

Fix: at minimum add a `dual=` hash and language-pack-aware switch.

title: ghost-compat: `{{price}}` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's `{{price}}` formats a price with currency and interval. Used in pricing UIs. Absent.

Fix: register a stub that formats `{{price tier.monthly_price}}` → `$N/mo`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{products}}` and `{{product}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Older Ghost theme API used "products" before "tiers". Casper-vintage themes still reference them. Helper absent.

Fix: alias to `{{tiers}}` / `{{tier}}` for back-compat.

title: ghost-compat: `{{reading_time}}` helper uses fixed strings, not localised via `{{t}}`
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:34` reads `options.hash.minute` and `options.hash.minutes` defaults `'1 min read'`. Source / Casper pass localised strings via `{{t "1 min read"}}` so it works in their flow. But callers that omit the hash get English. Ghost's default also is English, so OK — but document.

Fix: docs note.

title: ghost-compat: `{{search}}` utility helper not implemented
body:
Ghost has a `{{search}}` utility helper that outputs a pre-styled search button + icon (uses Ghost's hosted search SDK). Absent. Source uses its own `data-ghost-search` triggers, so unaffected, but Casper-derived themes may invoke `{{search}}`.

Fix: register a stub that outputs the equivalent button markup.

title: ghost-compat: `{{secondary_navigation}}` global accessor not exposed
body:
Ghost exposes `@site.secondary_navigation` (we have it) but also a bare `{{secondary_navigation}}` accessor pattern in some legacy themes. We don't register one.

Fix: low priority; document.

title: ghost-compat: `{{split}}` utility helper not implemented
body:
Documented: "Split a string into one or more iterable strings." Some themes use it to parse comma-separated config values.

Fix: register `split` that takes a string + separator and returns an array iterable by foreach.

title: ghost-compat: `{{tags}}` does not output `accent_color` field on tag objects, but Ghost themes read it
body:
Tag objects in Ghost expose `accent_color`. Our `Tag` model in `src/content/model.ts:43` lacks the field. Themes that render colored tag pills break.

Fix: add `accent_color: string | undefined` to Tag, read from frontmatter.

title: ghost-compat: `{{tier}}` and `{{tiers}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost themes can iterate tiers with `{{#tiers}}…{{/tiers}}` and `{{#tier}}…{{/tier}}` on the post. Absent in our helpers and in our `Post`/`Page` models.

Fix: model tiers (optional, gated on members enabled); register helpers.

title: ghost-compat: `{{total_members}}` and `{{total_paid_members}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost data helpers documented: "Outputs the number of members, rounded and humanised." Absent. Themes that show "Join 10k+ subscribers" will render empty.

Fix: register helpers; default to 0 with a config-overridable static number.

title: ghost-compat: `{{url absolute=true}}` on home returns `${site.url}/` — Ghost returns `${site.url}` (no trailing slash variance)
body:
Our `urlHelper` returns `ctx.url` then runs through `new URL(ctx.url, site.url)`. On the home route, `ctx.url` is `/` and URL resolution produces `${site.url}/`. Ghost is consistent with trailing slash but worth verifying on subpath setups.

Fix: tests for subpath + trailing slash edge cases.

title: ghost-compat: assets fingerprinting collides for files with the same name in different folders
body:
`src/theme/assets.ts:28` also stores `out.set(rel.replaceAll('\\', '/'), …)` — bare path. If two folders have `style.css`, the second overrides the first. Not commonly a problem in Source, but a real bug for themes with `light/style.css` + `dark/style.css`.

Fix: store only the canonical key; require theme code to use the full path.

title: ghost-compat: built `screen.css.map` and other sourcemap files are not gitignored or excluded from asset emission
body:
Source ships `assets/built/screen.css.map`. Our `loadThemeAssets` glob includes everything. We'd emit it to dist. Not a correctness issue but a privacy/bandwidth one.

Fix: skip `*.map` files in production emit (or behind a flag).

title: ghost-compat: drafts may be rendered via direct URL access — no preview token enforcement
body:
Drafts are filtered out at content load (`src/content/loader.ts:30` skips status:draft). But our import preserves the slug, so subsequent edits + re-import could leak. Document that drafts are hard-excluded from build.

Fix: docs note; consider an optional `[build].include_drafts` flag for staging.

title: ghost-compat: encode helper supports `:component` vs `:full` modes
body: We always use encodeURIComponent. Ghost has variants.

title: ghost-compat: feature_image attached to posts isn't surfaced via Ghost's `<meta>` image tags when only OG/Twitter overrides are set
body:
Our `computeMeta` in `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:81` checks `og_image → twitter_image → feature_image`. Good. But Ghost also picks up `feature_image` for og:image even when og_image is set (uses feature_image as fallback only when og_image absent). Verify the ordering matches.

Fix: tests.

title: ghost-compat: feed for emailed-only posts not excluded — Ghost hides them from RSS / sitemap
body:
Posts with `email_only: true` are hidden from web in Ghost. Since we don't model `email_only` (separate gap) we'd emit them. Once added, the RSS/sitemap emitters need to filter accordingly.

Fix: filter in RSS/sitemap/index routes when `email_only: true`.

title: ghost-compat: i18n active locale lookup is exact match — Ghost falls back from `en-GB` to `en`
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:7` does `engine.theme.locales[locale]`. If site locale is `en-GB` but theme only ships `en.json`, the lookup returns undefined and we silently use English as fallback. OK behaviour by accident, but no real cascade.

Fix: implement BCP-47 fallback chain (en-GB → en → default).

title: ghost-compat: i18n locale `context.json` is also a real file — but our loader expects strings only
body:
Source ships `locales/context.json` (developer guidance file, not a real translation). Our loader includes it as `context` locale, which is bogus. Themes that set `locale: "context"` (impossible but) get noise.

Fix: skip `context.json` and any non-locale-named JSONs.

title: ghost-compat: import-ghost doesn't migrate per-post `email_subject`, `email_only`, `send_email_when_published`
body:
Frontmatter generation in `src/ghost/import.ts:130` covers many fields but not these. Imported sites lose this state.

Fix: include them in `buildFrontmatter`.

title: ghost-compat: import-ghost loses `posts_meta` (per-post engagement metrics) and `posts_authors` ordering edge cases
body:
Sort order in `src/ghost/import.ts:117` uses `sort_order ?? 0` — null/undefined become 0 and may reorder authors. Per-post meta (posts_meta in newer exports) is ignored entirely.

Fix: read posts_meta if present; preserve original sort order via stable secondary key.

title: ghost-compat: kg-audio-card / kg-video-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost renders custom player UI (progress, controls). Markdown's `<audio>`/`<video>` works but won't pick up theme card styling.

Fix: detect audio/video URLs or HTML, wrap in `kg-audio-card`/`kg-video-card`.

title: ghost-compat: kg-button-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost's button card renders `<div class="kg-card kg-button-card"><a class="kg-btn kg-btn-accent">`. Markdown has no native button.

Fix: support a Markdown convention (e.g. `[Click](url){.button}`) → button card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-callout-card markup not emitted
body:
Themes have hover/colour styles tied to `.kg-callout-card`. Markdown blockquotes won't match.

Fix: support GFM-style alerts (`> [!NOTE]`) and emit `<div class="kg-callout-card kg-callout-card-blue">`.

title: ghost-compat: kg-file-card markup not emitted
body:
Inline file/attachment downloads use `<div class="kg-file-card">` with a download anchor and metadata. Markdown links to files lack the card wrapper.

Fix: detect file-extension links and wrap in card markup.

title: ghost-compat: kg-gallery-card markup not emitted
body: Galleries in Ghost emit nested `` with rows. Markdown-only sites have no gallery primitive. Fix: add a Markdown convention (e.g. table of images, or fenced ` ```gallery`) that emits the gallery markup.

title: ghost-compat: kg-header-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost's header card is a large hero block placed mid-post. No equivalent in our pipeline.

Fix: convention via fenced block or a kg-style HTML block; emit the card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-product-card markup not emitted
body:
Product card used by review themes. No equivalent.

Fix: out of scope unless explicitly requested; document.

title: ghost-compat: kg-signup-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost embeds signup CTAs mid-post via signup card. Our Markdown can't produce one. Themes lose mid-content lead capture.

Fix: introduce a fenced block (e.g. ` ```signup heading=…`) that emits the signup card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-toggle-card markup not emitted
body:
Accordion/collapsible content uses `<div class="kg-card kg-toggle-card">`. Markdown `<details>` exists but doesn't match Ghost classes — theme styling won't apply.

Fix: post-process `<details>`/`<summary>` into Ghost's kg-toggle-card.

title: ghost-compat: locales folder loaded from `theme/locales/*.json` only — Ghost also reads global `content/themes/<theme>/locales`
body:
Our `loadLocales` in `src/theme/loader.ts:80` reads only from the theme rootDir. Ghost loads from `content/data/translations/<locale>.json` as a global override. Site-level translation overrides not supported.

Fix: add an optional `content/translations/<locale>.json` loader and merge with priority over theme locales.

title: ghost-compat: navigation_secondary slug class not added to `<li>` — Ghost emits `nav-secondary` for secondary nav items
body:
Our `navigationHelper` outputs `nav-${slugify(label)}`. Doesn't differentiate primary vs secondary. Themes' CSS for `nav-secondary` won't apply.

Fix: when `type="secondary"`, prefix classes with `nav-secondary-`.

title: ghost-compat: pagination object missing `prev`/`next` integer fields under the conventional names
body:
We expose `prev` and `next` as integers (good), but Ghost also documents `prev_url` and `next_url` (we have these). However, themes also frequently iterate using `{{#if prev}}…{{/if}}` (just the integer). Currently if there's no prev, our value is `undefined` — handlebars treats that as falsy, OK. Verify with tests.

Fix: add tests that exercise both numeric and url forms.

title: ghost-compat: pagination.pages missing when posts.length === 0 (we coerce to 1)
body:
`planRoutes` in `src/build/routes.ts:118` does `if (pages.length === 0) pages.push([]);` to ensure at least one page. That means an empty site still emits `/`. But the resulting `PaginationInfo.pages = 1`, total = 0. Themes often guard `{{#if pagination.pages}}` which would render pagination on an empty site.

Fix: when there are zero posts, set `pages = 0` and skip pagination output. Or, mirror Ghost which doesn't emit pagination when there are 0 posts.

title: ghost-compat: partial names containing dots/underscores not normalised
body:
Our `normalizeName` in `src/theme/loader.ts:73` only converts `\\` to `/`. Names like `partials/post.card.hbs` become partial id `post.card` — Handlebars interprets the dot as path access (`{{> post.card}}` → look up `post` then `card`). Subtle bug for any theme using dot-namespacing.

Fix: documentation + linter warning; or rename dots to dashes in the partial id.

title: ghost-compat: partial registration name collides for partials whose path equals a template name
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:69` registers templates as partials by name. If a theme has `theme.partials['post']` AND `theme.templates['post']`, the latter overwrites the former. Source doesn't have this collision, but Casper-like layouts that ship both `partials/post.hbs` and `post.hbs` would silently break.

Fix: keep the partial namespace separate; prefix templates as e.g. `templates/post` when registered as fallback partials.

title: ghost-compat: partials with names like `components/header` only accessible with quotes — Handlebars requires `{{> "components/header"}}`
body:
Source uses `{{> "components/navigation"}}`. Our loader stores partials as `components/navigation`. This works because Handlebars accepts string-name partials. Verify edge cases (single quotes vs double quotes; case sensitivity).

Fix: tests.

title: ghost-compat: private.hbs and password-protected sites not supported
body:
Ghost users can password-protect their entire publication; theme renders `private.hbs` with `{{input_password}}` and `error` messages. We have no concept and no helper.

Fix: scope out, but document. Optionally support a build-time `[site.private]` config that pre-renders a static auth wall.

title:
ghost-compat: rendering pipeline doesn't strip leading `

` from custom_excerpt when source is HTML — Ghost outputs plaintext
body:
If a user puts HTML in `custom_excerpt`, themes that use it as a plain `

{{custom_excerpt}}

` will get nested `

` tags. Ghost coerces to plaintext. Fix: when reading `custom_excerpt` from frontmatter, strip HTML.

title: ghost-compat: tag/author archive pages don't expose `posts` aggregated by year/month — themes may render chronological archives
body:
Some themes build a per-year/month archive view via `{{#foreach posts}}` and `{{#has number="…"}}` on dates. We expose `posts` but no group_by accessor. Mostly a no-op gap.

Fix: optional future helper `{{group_by_year}}`.

title: ghost-compat: theme `package.json` `engines.ghost` is not checked
body:
Ghost enforces a `gscan` rule that the theme's engines.ghost matches the host's version. We ignore it. Themes declaring `"ghost": ">=5.0.0"` won't get any compatibility warning.

Fix: read engines.ghost and emit a CLI warning if the theme requires newer features than we support.

title: ghost-compat: theme `package.json` config `card_assets: true | false | [list]` only honoured as boolean
body:
Our `src/theme/pkg.ts:46` does `Boolean(cfg.card_assets)` — loses the array form (`card_assets: ["bookmark", "gallery"]`) which Ghost uses to selectively bundle card CSS/JS. Themes that opt in to specific cards will get either everything or nothing.

Fix: preserve the array form; when emitting `built/cards.css` (future), filter by the list.

title: ghost-compat: time helper missing (`{{time format="..."}}` deprecated alias for date)
body: Some older themes still use it. Alias.

title: ghost-compat: timezone `UTC` default but dayjs needs IANA names — verify common values work
body:
`src/render/helpers/date.ts:35` calls `dayjs(value).tz(timezoneName)`. `UTC` is fine, but the dayjs `timezone` plugin requires the IANA database; ensure it's loaded for all common values (Europe/Paris, America/New_York, etc.).

Fix: tests with non-UTC timezones; document loaded plugins.

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} `visibility="all|public|none|members|paid"` filter is partial
body:
Our `visibilityFilter` (`src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:211`) accepts `all` (always true) and equality match. It correctly handles `members` and `paid` because we set `obj.visibility` to those exact strings. But `visibility="none"` (display-only-hidden) is documented but not specially handled — `(obj.visibility ?? 'public') === 'none'` would never match because no item has visibility "none".

Fix: define `none` = filter out everything except items with visibility marker matching admin's `none`/`internal` visibility. Document the intent.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} ignores `include="authors,tags,count.posts"`
body:
Ghost's `include` attribute expands relationships and aggregate counts. We blindly accept whatever `include=` value but never branch on it — `count.posts` for tags/authors is already on the resolved Tag/Author objects, but the doc-blessed expectation is that without `include` those fields are not populated. Conversely, our objects always include them. Themes that test `{{#if count.posts}}` will see truthy where Ghost would see undefined. In practice this is fine, but record the divergence and add tests.

Fix: document this lossy compatibility — Nectar always populates everything; the `include` attribute is a no-op.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} resource `newsletters` not supported
body: Ghost added `newsletters` as a queryable resource. We don't model newsletters at all — themes querying `{{#get "newsletters"}}` will get an empty array with no signal. Same fix as tiers.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} does not support "members" or "subscriber" context
body:
Ghost supports membership-aware contexts. While `subscriber` was removed in newer versions, themes still ship using both `{{#is "members"}}` and the inverse pattern `{{^is "subscriber"}}`. Our implementation returns false for these unknown contexts.

Fix: even if we don't have members, recognise these names and resolve them deterministically (always false because no member context exists at build time) and document the behaviour.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} `timeago` accepts only `true`/'true' string, not Ghost's `timeago` (no value) idiom
body:
Ghost supports `{{date timeago="true"}}` and bare `{{date timeago=true}}`. We check `options.hash.timeago === true || options.hash.timeago === 'true'`. The `=true` (boolean from Handlebars) form is fine, but Handlebars sometimes coerces the string `'true'` for hash-without-value attributes. Verify both paths.

Fix: also accept the string `'true'` and any truthy.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} `timezone=` hash override not implemented
body:
Ghost lets themes override the global timezone with `{{date timezone="Europe/Paris"}}`. We always use `engine.content.site.timezone`. Per-helper override absent.

Fix: read `hash.timezone`, fall back to site default.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} doesn't gracefully handle invalid dates — emits 'Invalid Date'
body:
Themes may pass non-date data; Ghost emits an empty string or falls back. dayjs returns 'Invalid Date' as a literal string. Ghost themes that render `{{date custom_field}}` would show garbage.

Fix: check `dayjs(value).isValid()` and return empty string when invalid.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `<link rel="amphtml">` or AMP support
body:
Ghost emits an AMP link when AMP is enabled and `amp.hbs` exists. We don't generate AMP routes at all (see separate gap), and don't emit the link.

Fix: scope-out AMP; document the absence; ensure no `amphtml` reference accidentally leaks.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} returns plain string, not SafeString — theme HTML may be double-escaped via `&`
body:
Most uses are inside attribute values (`src="{{img_url …}}"`), which Handlebars will HTML-escape. That's fine. But if a theme uses `{{{img_url …}}}` (triple-stash) the value is unescaped — also fine. The risk is that the helper returns a plain string with `&` already in it (we don't escape, so OK). Trace and confirm.

Fix: write a test that asserts the literal string returned is a clean URL.

title: ghost-compat: {{social_url}} ignores `social_url="@site"` and `social_url="author"` positional targets
body:
Ghost's `{{social_url type="twitter"}}` reads from current scope, but you can also pass `{{social_url "@site" type="twitter"}}`. We ignore positional args. Themes rendering both author and site social blocks from one helper invocation can't switch.

Fix: read first positional arg as the lookup scope.

title: ghost-compat: {{social_url}} only supports a fixed list, missing `x`, `youtube_channel`, `github`, `mailto`
body:
Source/Casper authors may set X (formerly Twitter) handles, GitHub, etc. Our `SOCIAL_PATTERNS` covers twitter/facebook/linkedin/bluesky/mastodon/threads/tiktok/youtube/instagram only. Newer themes add `x` and `github`.

Fix: extend SOCIAL_PATTERNS; treat `x` and `twitter` interchangeably.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} doesn't expose the `{{lang}}` short-form alias documented by Ghost
body:
Ghost has `{{lang}}` (returns locale code) — we have it (`src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:16`). However the docs also show a helper called `translate` (utility category alias for `t`). We don't register `translate`.

Fix: register `translate` as an alias of `t`.

title: ghost-import: tags imported only if they have description/feature_image/meta_title
body: Otherwise auto-inferred. Importer should optionally emit all tags so users can edit them.

title: ghost-import: writes content with double newlines; not idempotent across runs
body: Body trailing newline + frontmatter trailing newline = inconsistent. Define a canonical output format.

title: good-first-issue: add `--quiet` / `--verbose` flags to all commands
body: Bind to logger threshold.

title: good-first-issue: add a `nectar version --check` to look up latest release
body: Lightweight network call to GitHub Releases; cached for 24h.

title: i18n: no RTL handling
body: When `locale` is `ar`/`he`, themes need `<html dir="rtl">`. Expose `{{dir}}` helper or auto-set `dir` attribute in default layouts. Document RTL-safe CSS patterns in the theme dev guide.

title: i18n: pluralisation in `t` helper is `%`-based, not ICU MessageFormat
body: Move to ICU when many locales are supported.

title: i18n: timezone treated as one global value
body: `[site].timezone` is one TZ, but multi-author/multi-locale blogs may want author-local published_at display. At minimum allow `{{date timezone="Asia/Tokyo"}}` override (per-call) — the dayjs helper currently ignores the hash.

title: infra: no CODEOWNERS
body: Add `.github/CODEOWNERS` so PRs touching `src/render/helpers/*` auto-request review from the theme-compat maintainer, PRs touching `src/ghost/import.ts` go to the migration maintainer, etc. Even a one-person project benefits from auto-assignment for future contributors.

title: infra: no GitHub repo settings as code (no `.github/settings.yml`)
body: Manage branch protection rules, required checks, default labels via `probot/settings`. Without it, when a maintainer joins, we'll have inconsistent enforcement (and `bun run check` will be bypassed on some PRs).

title: infra: no LICENSE in src/ headers; just root LICENSE file
body: Optional but nice for users copying snippets.

title: infra: no devcontainer / Gitpod / Codespaces config
body: Add `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` so a first-time contributor can open the repo in Codespaces and have `bun install` already done. Lowers the contribution bar significantly.

title: infra: no editor config / .editorconfig
body: Add `.editorconfig` (`indent_style = space`, `indent_size = 2`, `end_of_line = lf`, `insert_final_newline = true`, `charset = utf-8`) so Windows contributors don't accidentally land CRLF in `.hbs` test fixtures.

title: infra: no link checker for docs
body: Use lychee in CI.

title: infra: no spell check on docs/Markdown content
body: Add cspell GitHub Action over docs/ and example/content/.

title: members: build artefact — emit `dist/.nectar/portal-manifest.json` listing every rewritten selector
body: For debugging: when the portal adapter rewrites `[data-portal]` / `[data-members-form]` / `[data-ghost-search]` elements, record per-file counts in `dist/.nectar/portal-manifest.json`. Surface a summary in build output (`portal: rewrote 18 elements across 12 routes`).

title: members: gscan-compatible lint output for member-related theme fields
body: Ghost's `gscan` validator flags missing portal markup. If we add a `nectar theme lint` command (separate issue), include rules for: themes that read `@member.*` should warn that this is a static build; themes that include `data-portal` triggers without portal config should warn; themes that reference `{{recommendations}}` without `[[recommendations]]` should warn.

title: members: portal: no Bento (bentonow) adapter
body: Bento is a popular Ghost-adjacent ESP. Their embed form posts to `https://app.bentonow.com/<site-uuid>/subscribe`. Add adapter parallel to Buttondown/Beehiiv.

title: members: tiers: free-tier welcome page parity (`/welcome/free`)
body: Ghost auto-generates `/welcome/free/` and `/welcome/paid/` from the tier's `welcome_page_url`. Themes link to these from post-signup flows. For Nectar, accept `welcome_page_url` per `[[tiers]]` and emit those as actual content routes if the slug starts with `/welcome/`.

title: members: tiers: no helper to render a pricing-table partial
body: Most Ghost themes ship a hand-written pricing-table that calls `{{#get "tiers" filter="type:paid"}}…`. We could ship a built-in partial `nectar/tiers-table.hbs` (registered automatically) so users can drop `{{> nectar/tiers-table}}` into a page. Make styling theme-scoped via classes (`gh-tiers`, `gh-tier-card`).

title: migration: no reverse export (Nectar → Ghost JSON)
body: For Ghost-as-headless-CMS workflows, users want to push Nectar markdown back into a Ghost instance. Add `nectar export-ghost` that produces a valid `ghost-export.json` (db[0].data shape). Niche but unlocks evaluation pilots ("can I keep both running for a month?").

title: newsletter: `feature_image_caption` HTML stripped in email but kept on web — no policy doc
body: Per Ghost's docs, captions are HTML on web and plaintext-with-stripped-tags in email. Our render pipeline doesn't differentiate. Document the policy.

title: newsletter: no Postmark/Resend/SES emit-on-publish hook
body: Real workflow for Ghost-leavers: write Markdown, build site, also email subscribers. Add `[components.newsletter]` provider with API key + list ID, plus `nectar publish:email --post=<slug>` command that posts the rendered HTML to the provider. Out of scope for first cut — issue is to track demand.

title: newsletter: post `excerpt` for email-list teaser truncation defaults differ between web and email
body: Ghost uses different excerpt lengths in newsletter (~140 chars) vs web (~250). Currently we use `custom_excerpt || excerpt || plaintext.slice(0, 250)` everywhere. Document; add `[build].email_excerpt_length` (default 140) if/when email emit is added.

title: observability: no Sentry / error-reporter hook
body: Expose an opt-in `[telemetry.errors]` section that POSTs unhandled exceptions and `logger.error` calls to a user-provided DSN (Sentry, GlitchTip, custom). Lets agency users monitor their fleet of Nectar sites without us hosting anything.

title: observability: no error reporting integration (Sentry/Bugsnag)
body: Provide hooks; document configuration.

title: observability: no trace/build IDs
body: Every `build()` invocation should mint a UUID and prepend it to every log line. Lets you correlate output when running `nectar dev` (which spins many builds).

title: packaging: no AUR package for Arch users
body: Ghost has a non-trivial Arch following. Provide a `nectar-bin` AUR PKGBUILD that pulls the linux-x64/arm64 release binary. CI can push to AUR via aurpublish on tagged releases.

title: packaging: no Nix flake / package
body: Add `flake.nix` exposing `packages.<system>.nectar` so `nix run github:owner/nectar -- build` just works for power users. This also gives us a reproducible dev shell.

title: perf: config: zod parse runs per build start — fine but no schema reuse for dev
body:
loader.ts parses TOML then zod-validates per build. For dev/watch mode this is wasted CPU when config hasn't changed.

Fix: cache the validated config keyed by mtime of the nectar.toml. Trivial when watch lands.

title: perf: handlebars: noEscape:false redundant — it's the default
body:
engine.ts passes `{ noEscape: false }` to every hb.compile call. It's the default; passing the option does nothing but adds bytes per call.

Fix: drop the option (cosmetic; not a runtime perf win but cleans up the hot path).

title: perf: handlebars: theme.templates also registered as partials by bare name — collision risk
body:
engine.ts:69-71 registers every template as a partial under its bare name. For a theme with 50 templates and 100 partials, every render walks a 150-entry partials map. Not slow but inflates Handlebars internal state.

Fix: only register the templates that are actually invoked as partials (post, post-card, etc.). Determine via static scan of template ASTs, or expose as theme config.

title: perf: helpers/assets: linear scan over candidates per asset helper call
body:
assets.ts assetHelper iterates `[logical, 'assets/'+logical]` and Map.get for each. Map.get is O(1) so fine, but escapeHtml runs on every call even though asset URLs are already URL-safe.

Fix: precompute the asset's public URL once and store on the ThemeAsset; helper just returns the cached SafeString.

title: perf: helpers/blocks: foreach allocates new frame per item — minor
body:
blocks.ts foreach calls `engine.hb.createFrame(...)` per iteration. createFrame returns a new object with a hidden prototype link. For a homepage feed of 12 cards this is fine; for big-tag pages with 1000+ cards this is measurable allocation churn. Note: the prompt itself notes 'foreach allocates new frame per item; fine' — keep as-is unless profiling shows it.

Fix: only if a profile shows it as a hot allocation, reuse a single mutated frame object across iterations (semantically equivalent since Handlebars copies on render).

title: perf: helpers/content: post_class registered twice (engine + helper)
body:
engine.ts computePostClass writes `ctx.post_class` during buildContext, AND content.ts registers a `post_class` Handlebars helper that recomputes the same string. Whichever wins, one of them is wasted CPU + a confusing API.

Fix: pick one source of truth (recommend computing once at loader time and exposing as `post.post_class`); remove the duplicate helper.

title: perf: helpers/content: tags helper rebuilds <a> string per call
body:
content.ts tags helper allocates a fresh array of escaped anchors per invocation. Same tag list appears on every card of every paginated list for that post — duplicated work.

Fix: precompute `post.tags_html` (autolink=true) and `post.tags_text` (autolink=false) at loader time. Helper just selects.

title: perf: helpers/i18n: t helper rebuilt with capture per engine but lookup runs per call
body:
i18n.ts captures `locale`, `active`, `fallback` once at registration — good. But the helper still does an Object property lookup chain plus interpolate on every call. For a card grid with 50 t() invocations × 10k pages = 500k lookups.

Fix: precompile a Map<key, (hash) => string> at registration to skip the per-call dict lookup + branching.

title: perf: helpers/i18n: t helper recomputes string interpolation per call
body:
i18n.ts interpolate iterates `Object.entries(hash)` per `{{t}}` call. Same key/hash is invoked thousands of times across renders (e.g. 'By {authors}' in post cards). No memoization.

Fix: memoize `(key, hashStableString) → resolvedString`. Use JSON.stringify of hash entries as cache key (or sort keys for stability).

title: perf: helpers: every helper allocates a SafeString — escape cost
body: Many helpers wrap their return in `new engine.hb.SafeString(...)`. SafeString is a thin wrapper but invocation per render × per helper-call (often 20+ per page) adds up to ~hundreds of allocations per page. Fix: return raw strings when the content is already encoded; only use SafeString when truly necessary (e.g. helpers that emit / markup). Marginal but measurable.

title: perf: loader: existsSync inside async loadMarkdownDir — blocking
body:
loader.ts:159 calls `existsSync(dir)` synchronously. Doing this on the event loop per content dir × 4 dirs is fine here but the pattern is repeated across loadTheme, loadThemeAssets, etc.

Fix: prefer `await Bun.file(dir).exists()` or `fs.access` with try/catch — non-blocking.

title: perf: loader: prev/next assigned as full Post references — memory cycles
body:
loader.ts:40-41 assigns `current.next = resolvedPosts[i-1]; current.prev = resolvedPosts[i+1];`. Each post then holds a reference to its neighbor, which holds a reference back. For 10k posts this is fine but means dropping any single post requires walking the chain to GC. Serializing the graph (e.g. for cache) becomes circular.

Fix: store only slug + title in prev/next, or compute prev/next on demand from the sorted array index. Avoids deep nested cloning if you ever JSON.stringify a post.

title: perf: loader: resolveTagSlugs creates new Tag per missing slug — no dedupe across calls
body:
loader.ts resolveTagSlugs is called once per post and may mutate the shared tagMap. That's safe, but inside Promise.all execution (when parallelised per the parallel-readFile fix above) it would race. Even without parallelism, the per-post `slugs.map` allocates a new array even when post tagSlugs is empty.

Fix: early-return when slugs.length === 0 (returns a shared empty array). When parallelising, build tagMap from a serial second pass over already-loaded raw posts.

title: perf: loader: titleCase + slug.split etc allocated per slug per post
body:
loader.ts titleCase splits + maps + joins per tag/author slug per post that references it. Same slug shared across N posts → N allocations of the same string.

Fix: memoize titleCase(slug) → name in a Map, reuse.

title: perf: markdown: htmlToPlaintext runs 9 sequential .replace passes
body:
content/markdown.ts htmlToPlaintext does 9 chained .replace calls per post. For 10k posts that's 90k regex operations and lots of intermediate strings.

Fix: single state-machine pass that strips tags + decodes entities in one go. Or skip plaintext entirely when custom_excerpt is set (the only consumer).

title: perf: markdown: no syntax-highlighting cache
body:
renderMarkdown lacks a syntax highlighter (good — keeps it lean) but if/when one is added (shiki, highlight.js), the per-codeblock highlight cost is huge. Pre-emptive note: cache highlighted snippets by sha1(language+code).

Fix: when highlighting lands, add a `.nectar-cache/highlight/` directory keyed by content hash.

title: perf: output: featured image card has no loading attribute (eager) — fine, but inconsistent
body:
Featured card in dist/index.html does not have loading=lazy (good for LCP) but the next sibling row also lacks loading=lazy on the first item. Inconsistency suggests the template logic depends on theme state, not on viewport.

Fix: theme determines `lazyLoad` flag based on card index (first 1-2 cards eager, rest lazy). Render once per card.

title: perf: output: inline JSON-LD repeated across paginated index pages
body:
Every paginated /page/N/ embeds the same WebSite JSON-LD block (~150B). For 100 pages that's 15KB of identical bytes across the dist tree. Not huge but unnecessary.

Fix: emit JSON-LD once on / and rel=canonical the paginated pages to the root (or omit JSON-LD on paginated pages — search engines deduplicate via canonical anyway).

title: perf: output: loading=lazy on cards above the fold
body:
example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html related-posts cards use `loading=lazy` — but they're often within the viewport on tall desktop layouts. Mobile fine; desktop loses parallel fetches.

Fix: lazy only beyond the first row of cards (theme decides). Or use `loading=auto` (default) and let the browser heuristic decide based on viewport.

title: perf: output: sitemap escapeXml runs 5 replace passes per URL
body:
feeds.ts escapeXml chains 5 .replace() calls per URL. For 200k URLs that's 1M regex operations.

Fix: single regex `/[&<>"']/g` with a lookup table replacer. Or skip escaping for URLs entirely (URLs are already %-encoded so only `&` matters → single replace).

title: perf: render: buildCustom called per render — spread cost
body:
buildCustom in engine.ts is invoked inside buildRootData → invoked per render. It spreads `theme.pkg.customDefaults` and `config.theme.custom` every time. With ~30 custom fields × 10k renders this is wasted work.

Fix: build customDefaults merged with config.theme.custom once at engine creation, reference the cached object.

title: perf: render: buildRootData allocates fresh object per render
body:
engine.ts buildRootData (lines 127-139) constructs a new object including site/blog/config/custom every render. site, blog, config, custom are invariant per build — only `page` and `route` change.

Fix: precompute a frozen `engineRootDataBase` once and `{ ...base, route, page }` per render. Skips ~5 object allocations and re-evaluation of buildCustom (which itself spreads two records).

title: perf: render: computeBodyClass / computePostClass run per render
body:
engine.ts computes body_class and post_class strings per render. computePostClass iterates tags and concatenates strings each time — for a paginated index with 12 cards × 100 pages that's 1200 recomputations of the same class string.

Fix: precompute `post.post_class` once during loader.ts (resolvePostRelations) and just read it. Same for body_class per route kind — derive at planRoutes time.

title: perf: render: ghost_head string-concats large parts array per call
body:
ghostHeadHelper allocates a fresh `parts: string[]` and calls `.join('\n')` per render. For 10k routes that's 10k arrays + 10k joins. Combined with the rebuild cost above, this is measurable on cold builds.

Fix: build the meta tag block as a single template string (preserve newlines for readability) once helpers are registered. Or, with the memoization above, only the post-specific block needs to be recomputed.

title: perf: render: handlebars.create() per engine — fine; verify no global pollution
body:
engine.ts uses `Handlebars.create()` (not the global Handlebars) — good for isolation. But every helper is re-registered on every build invocation. For dev/watch mode this matters.

Fix: when serving in watch mode (future feature), reuse the engine across rebuilds; only re-register if theme files changed.

title: perf: render: layout splitLayout regex runs per render
body:
src/render/engine.ts renderRoute calls `splitLayout(template)` on every render even though the result is deterministic per template name. Regex execution + slice is cheap (~µs) but unnecessary multiplied by route count.

Fix: precompute `engine.templateSplits[name] = splitLayout(source)` once during createEngine.

title: perf: render: post.html injected as SafeString via {{{html}}} or content helper — re-escape risk
body:
content.ts content helper wraps post.html in SafeString. For a 50KB post that's a string copy (the SafeString wrapper is cheap but Handlebars may still traverse). Multiplied over 10k posts × 1 invocation per render this is hundreds of MB of string handling.

Fix: pre-wrap post.html as SafeString at loader time so engine.render never re-wraps. Or expose a raw-html marker that Handlebars skips.

title: perf: routes: defaultMeta string-concat per route — minor allocation churn
body:
routes.ts defaultMeta is called per route and allocates a fresh object literal each time. For 100k+ routes this is 100k object allocations.

Fix: precompute a frozen base meta, then `{ ...base, title, description, image }` per route. Marginal but visible in heap profiles.

title: perf: rss: <pubDate> creates a new Date per item per build
body:
feeds.ts:34 `new Date(post.published_at).toUTCString()` per item. Cheap, but redundant — could be precomputed on Post.
