Fix: precompute `post.published_at_rfc2822` at loader time.

title: perf: scale: bySlug Maps doubled storage for posts/pages/tags/authors
body:
loader.ts:63-68 builds bySlug maps that hold references to all posts/pages/tags/authors — doubling pointer storage. For 10k posts that's 10k extra pointers (small) but the map overhead is ~100 bytes/entry on V8/Bun.

Fix: lazy-build bySlug only when a helper needs it. Most templates use `{{#get "posts"}}` (array iteration) more than slug lookups.

title: perf: serve: Bun.serve uses Bun.file().exists() which stats per request
body:
cli/commands/serve.ts re-stats the filesystem on every request. For local dev this is fine; for any production-ish use case it's slow.

Fix: add an in-memory route → file map populated at startup from a recursive scan of distDir. Refresh on SIGHUP or fs.watch.

title: perf: serve: no Content-Encoding negotiation — always serves raw bytes
body:
Bun's Response from Bun.file does not auto-serve precompressed .br/.gz companions. Even if we add precompression (separate issue), serve.ts won't pick them up.

Fix: parse Accept-Encoding header, look for .br / .gz beside the requested file, serve with appropriate Content-Encoding header.

title: perf: theme: assets Map double-indexed (logical + bare) doubles map size
body:
theme/assets.ts:28 sets `out.set(rel.replaceAll('\\', '/'), out.get(logical)!)` — duplicating every entry. Memory cost is O(2 × assets) and lookup speed is unchanged.

Fix: keep one canonical key; have assetHelper try both keys in resolution.

title: perf: theme: locales loaded serially — same pattern
body:
loader.ts:85-99 loads each locale JSON in series. Likely <10 locales so impact is minor, but the pattern propagates.

Fix: Promise.all the JSON reads + parses.

title: performance: handlebars compile per partial registration; pre-compile once at engine init
body: Partial source is registered as raw string; Handlebars compiles lazily on first use. For sites with many partials, pre-compile to AOT.

title: recommendations: favicon fetching at build time
body: If a `[[recommendations]]` entry has no `favicon`, the helper currently emits nothing. Add a build step that fetches `<recommendation.url>/favicon.ico` (or HEAD-resolves it from `<link rel=icon>`) once at build time, caches to `.nectar/favicons/`, and emits a hashed asset URL. Gate behind `[components.recommendations] fetch_favicons = true` (default false because it's network I/O).

title: recommendations: no incoming-recommendations directory listing
body: Ghost has a global recommendations directory at ghost.org/explore — sites can opt in. Not feasible for SSG (it's a network). Document as out-of-scope; suggest users link to https://ghost.org/explore/ if they want directory presence.

title: render: CSS not purged/inlined
body: Source theme ships ~50KB of unused CSS. Optional purge step (purgecss).

title: render: JS asset bundling/minification not done
body: Source theme JS already minified; users with custom JS would want bun build pipeline.

title: render: `concat` does not honour `separator` when given Handlebars SafeString
body: concatHelper calls `String()` on each value. SafeString.toString() returns the wrapped string — fine. Edge case: when SafeString contains unescaped HTML, concat strips that information (downstream re-escapes). Mark concat result as SafeString when all inputs were SafeString.

title: render: `date` helper does not honour Ghost's `timeago` semantics (number unit)
body: Our timeago path calls `dayjs(value).fromNow()`. Verify it matches Ghost's output (e.g., "a day ago" vs "1 day ago"). Probably fine; needs a test.

title: render: `date` helper inflates timezone before tz plugin loaded if module ordering shifts
body: We `dayjs.extend(timezone)` at module top; rely on import side-effects. If tree-shaking removes the side-effect, `dayjs(value).tz(...)` throws. Add a runtime guard / test that `dayjs.tz` is defined.

title: render: `get` mutates input arrays via slice().sort() — fine, but does not deep-clone
body: If a downstream helper mutates a Post inside the block, it mutates our content graph. Freeze content models with `Object.freeze` after load, or deep-clone in `get`.

title: render: `is` helper's `paged` check uses route.data.pagination but list views without pagination still set page=1; correct
body: Verify that pages without pagination (single post route) don't return true for `{{#is "paged"}}`. Currently `route.data.pagination` is undefined for posts → ok, but for indexes with 1 page also ok. Add explicit test.

title: render: `meta_title` does not handle Ghost's title delimiters per route
body: Ghost themes commonly do `{{meta_title}} | {{@site.title}}` themselves. Our implementation falls back to title alone. That's fine, but `page=" (Page %)"` suffix only applies on listing routes. Verify across home/index/tag/author.

title: render: `pagination` helper does not include `page_url` blockParam style
body: Ghost exposes `{{#pagination}}{{this.page_url}}{{/pagination}}` block form. Implement.

title: render: `social_url` mastodon assumes mastodon.social when host omitted
body: Ghost themes pass `@user@host.tld` — we split correctly. But when only `@user` is given, defaulting to `mastodon.social` is presumptuous. Either return empty (require fully-qualified) or document.

title: render: `t` helper falls back to key when missing; but if active locale exists with empty string, returns empty
body: helpers/i18n.ts: lookup chain is active[key] ?? fallback[key] ?? key. An empty string is NOT nullish, so an intentionally empty translation (like Source theme's `"Featured": ""`) gets returned. That's actually correct Ghost behavior — but it surprises users. Document.

title: render: foreach with empty input falls through inverse but emits no `{{else}}` block separator
body: Sanity check: when items array is empty, we call `options.inverse(this)`. Verify this is exposed via `{{else}}` in foreach (Ghost docs say it is). Add a test.

title: render: missing helper — `data`
body: Ghost has a `{{#data}}` block for loading additional content. We don't implement it.

title: render: missing helper — `is_active`
body: Some Casper-style themes use this for nav highlighting. Implement as alias for our link_class.

title: render: missing helper — `lookup`
body: Ghost's `{{lookup obj key}}` does dynamic property access. Useful for theme writers.

title: render: missing helper — `raw`
body: Outputs raw HTML, similar to triple-mustache. Used in some themes.

title: render: no Subresource Integrity (SRI) for asset links
body: Emit `integrity="sha384-..."` on external scripts/stylesheets where applicable.

title: render: {{asset 'path'}} double-encodes URL when path contains %
body: helpers/assets.ts:assetHelper joins the base path then runs `escapeHtml` over the result. Filenames containing `&`, `'`, `"`, `<`, `>` are escaped, but a URL with `%20` is fine — however a path with literal `%` would get re-encoded by escapeHtml's `&` rule indirectly via `&`. Use proper URL handling (`encodeURI` once at construction) rather than HTML-escaping.

title: search: `gh-search` partial in Source theme leaks CSS classes that suggest portal-bound UI
body: Document that the Source theme's `.gh-search` button is a *trigger* — Nectar's responsibility is to bind it. Otherwise theme authors blame the theme for a non-functional button.

title: search: no `<search>` widget partial in built-in helpers
body: Expose a default search UI partial themes can include.

title: search: no fallback Lunr integration
body: For users who can't ship Pagefind binary.

title: security: TOML config parsed with no size cap (compression-bomb / OOM)
body: src/config/loader.ts:18-20 reads `nectar.toml` with `readFile` (no size limit) then `@iarna/toml` parses it. @iarna/toml is pure JS and reasonably robust, but it has no built-in depth or length cap, so a 500 MB `nectar.toml` will be loaded entirely into memory. Less a realistic remote attack and more a footgun for shared CI runners. Mitigation: stat the config file and refuse files over (say) 1 MB.

title: security: `get` helper allows themes to enumerate all content including drafts when status filter changes upstream
body: src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:127-135 slices `engine.content.posts` etc., which is the *resolved* list (drafts already removed). Good. But if a future feature exposes raw content (e.g. `nectar serve --drafts`) the same `get` helper would surface drafts to readers. Mitigation: add a unit test that asserts no draft is ever in `engine.content.posts`; document the invariant.

title: security: `joinUrl` in content/loader.ts uses `new URL(path, base)` and may accept exotic schemes
body: src/content/loader.ts:473-475: if `config.site.url` is `file:///etc/`, the resulting per-post URL is `file:///etc/...`. Combined with the no-validation issue above. Mitigation: validate site.url is http(s) at config load (already covered by separate finding) — keep both fixes consistent.

title: security: `link` helper emits attributes without quoting if hash values contain `"` (escaped) but accepts arbitrary `class` strings
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:42-47 emits `class=\"${escapeAttr(cls)}\"`. A theme passing `class=\"x onclick=alert(1)\"` — wait, that ends up inside `class="…"` so it stays as a class token. Safer than it looks, but document the contract.

title: security: `nectar new` writes file with title interpolated into frontmatter via JSON.stringify — OK but slug not validated
body: src/cli/commands/new.ts:20-25: title is JSON.stringified (safe for YAML quotes), but the derived slug comes only from `slugify(title, { strict: true })` — which is robust. The remaining concern is that `dest` is `join(cwd, baseDir, '${slug}.md')` and `cwd` is `process.cwd()`. If the user runs `nectar new` from outside the project root, content is created in the wrong place silently. Mitigation: detect project root by locating `nectar.toml` upward; refuse if not inside a project.

title: security: asset fingerprinted only for .css/.js/.mjs — fonts, images, source maps go un-fingerprinted
body: src/theme/assets.ts:33-36 fingerprints only `.css/.js/.mjs`. A theme that ships a `.map` source map for built JS will serve it at a stable path, leaking unminified source to readers. Mitigation: either skip emitting source maps in production builds or fingerprint them too; allow theme authors to declare extra fingerprintable extensions.

title: security: build emits absolute timestamps in HTML/RSS (privacy + reproducibility)
body: src/build/feeds.ts:34 emits `new Date(post.published_at).toUTCString()` and JSON-LD uses `ctx.published_at`/`ctx.updated_at`. These come from frontmatter (good — author-controlled, no host clock leakage). However, src/content/loader.ts:185-187 falls back to `new Date().toISOString()` when no date is present. That means a post without a date publishes the build time, which leaks the build server's clock and time zone to readers. Mitigation: refuse to build a post with no date, or use a fixed epoch fallback.

title: security: build process prints stack traces to stderr (information disclosure to CI logs)
body: src/cli/commands/build.ts:16 logs `err.stack ?? err.message`. Pipeline.ts:42 logs `Failed to render ${route.url}` before re-throwing. Stack traces in CI logs reveal absolute paths under `/home/runner/...` or `/Users/<name>/...`, sometimes including private package names. Public CI logs (e.g. Actions on open-source repos) become a fingerprinting surface. Mitigation: in non-debug mode log only the error message; gate stack output behind `NECTAR_DEBUG=1`.

title: security: bun.lock contains no `integrity` hashes (Bun stores SHA-512 in registry-meta only)
body: Bun's lockfile stores integrity hashes implicitly via tarball SHA-512 in the package entries (see `sha512-…` lines in bun.lock). This is good, but the project does not mention `--frozen-lockfile` anywhere. Document the requirement, and have CI fail if the lockfile drifts.

title: security: copyContentAssets silently swallows errors (catch {})
body: src/build/emit.ts:48 wraps the copy in `try { … } catch {}` to make `content/images` optional. This hides real errors (permission denied, ENOSPC, symlink loops). A symlink loop or unreadable file fails the copy without any user feedback, leaving a broken site. Mitigation: only swallow ENOENT on the root directory; log all other errors at warn level.

title: security: date helper formats untrusted format string via dayjs — limited but still concerning
body: src/render/helpers/date.ts:33 takes `options.hash.format` from the theme. dayjs format tokens are safe (no eval), but a theme can use this to render misleading dates (e.g. always show the year as 1970) to defraud readers, or hide updated_at to make stale content look fresh. Lower priority; documenting it so theme reviewers know.

title: security: draft filenames may still leak via output_dir if a previous build emitted them
body: src/build/pipeline.ts:26 deletes outputDir before each build, so within a single run drafts are not emitted. However, if the user uses `--no-clean` (not currently a flag, but a likely future addition) or copies dist between builds, old draft HTML persists. Today the main leak is via build logs: `logger.info` echoes counts but not filenames, but `logger.error` in pipeline.ts:42 prints `Failed to render ${route.url} (${route.template})` which can include draft slugs if a route plan ever includes them. Mitigation: never include drafts in route planning; document the clean-build behaviour; ensure `nectar check` does not print draft slugs unless verbose.

title: security: env var NECTAR_LOG_LEVEL has no whitelist — unknown values fall back to info silently
body: src/util/logger.ts:5 reads `process.env.NECTAR_LOG_LEVEL` and falls back if undefined or unrecognised. A typo silently disables the intended verbosity. Mitigation: warn on unknown values; this is minor but worth adding a test.

title: security: filter mini-DSL in `get` helper interpolates `{{...}}` against arbitrary context — potential injection between posts
body: src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:262-268 in `evaluateClause` substitutes `{{post.id}}` style references against the current render context. The substituted value is then used as a literal in field comparison. While the result is only used in equality checks (no eval), the helper allows a theme to read arbitrary context fields and use them to filter content. If a future change extends the DSL to support comparisons (`~`, `~^`, etc.) without escaping, this becomes a stored XSS / template-injection vector. Mitigation: cap interpolation to a small whitelist of fields (id, slug, primary_tag.slug), reject `{{ ... | ... }}` payloads, and unit-test the boundary.

title: security: foreach helper exposes `index/number/first/last` data on a frame derived from caller-controlled options.data
body: src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:23 calls `engine.hb.createFrame((options.data as Record<string, unknown> | undefined) ?? {})`. Because the helper is invoked from theme templates, a theme author can pass a custom `@data` and trigger Handlebars-prototype-pollution-like behaviour. Handlebars 4.7.7+ closed the known prototype access path; the casts here are safe in practice but the code should be defensive. Mitigation: use `Object.create(null)` for frames built from untrusted helper data, and pin handlebars to 4.7.8+ explicitly (already in package.json — keep it that way and watch for new CVEs).

title: security: ghost_head emits og:image / twitter:image without verifying scheme (open redirect-ish for SEO scrapers)
body: src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:31-41 emits image URLs from frontmatter directly into `<meta og:image content=\"…\">`. If feature_image is `javascript:` it is HTML-escaped (no XSS), but the scraper-side behaviour of pasting non-http URLs into og:image is undefined and may break link previews. Mitigation: validate http(s) scheme; fall back to site default.

title: security: handlebars `compile` is invoked at render time per route inside renderRoute (perf + DoS)
body: src/render/engine.ts:82-91 calls `engine.hb.compile(...)` on every route, instead of using the pre-compiled template stored in `engine.templates`. This wastes CPU but more importantly means an attacker-supplied template body (theme) is recompiled N times per build, multiplying any compile-time exponential parsing. Mitigation: compile once at theme-load and reuse. (Also a correctness bug: any helpers registered later are unavailable for the pre-compiled copy stored under `templates[name]`, but the runtime compile picks them up — confirm intent.)

title: security: navigation `slugify` for nav CSS class names is naive and can produce attacker-controlled class with `"`/`'`
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:71-76 strips `[^\w]+` from labels — Unicode word characters in JS `\w` *do not* include letters from non-Latin scripts in legacy mode, but the regex uses default mode which is ASCII-only, so it actually maps everything non-ASCII to `-`. That part is safe. The real bug is that the class name is then dropped into `class="nav-${slug}"` with no quoting — but since slugify already strips quotes and `<`/`>`, this is OK. Document the invariant in a code comment so a future refactor does not break it.

title: security: navigation pagination uses HTML-escaped but un-quoted role/aria attrs — minor
body: src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:28 emits `role="navigation" aria-label="Pagination"` literally. No XSS, but a future change inserting variables here without escaping would regress. Add a unit test that pagination output stays well-formed for unusual pagination data.

title: security: no rate limit or size limit on `nectar serve` responses (LAN attacker can request huge files)
body: src/cli/commands/serve.ts streams whatever `Bun.file(filePath)` returns, with no per-request size cap. A LAN attacker requesting `/big-asset.bin` repeatedly can saturate the developer's link. Lower priority since `serve` is documented as local-only. Mitigation: cap concurrent requests, document binding to 127.0.0.1.

title: security: regex `LAYOUT_DIRECTIVE` in src/render/layouts.ts uses `[\w-]+` for layout name — controlled, but a theme layout name with backslashes/spaces is silently rejected
body: Low priority: src/render/layouts.ts:1 only accepts alphanumerics, underscore, hyphen for layout names. Themes that use namespaced layouts (`layouts/post`) cannot reference them via `{{!< layouts/post}}`. Documented in code; not a security issue per se, but worth pinning down so future relaxation does not allow path traversal.

title: security: rendered HTML written with utf8 encoding but no BOM stripping — UTF-7 / encoding-confusion risk for old user agents
body: src/build/emit.ts:9 writes HTML without an explicit `<meta charset>`. The default theme emits `<meta charset=utf-8>` so this is fine in practice, but a custom theme that omits the meta tag relies on the HTTP `Content-Type` header — which static hosts may set incorrectly. Old browsers with charset auto-detection have historically been XSS-able via UTF-7 sniffing. Mitigation: document the requirement that themes include `<meta charset=utf-8>` and consider injecting it automatically if absent.

title: security: sha1 short prefix (10 chars) for asset cache-busting is collision-prone
body: src/theme/assets.ts:38-43 truncates SHA-1 to 10 hex chars. With ~10^12 buckets and a typical theme with <500 assets, collisions are improbable but not impossible — and SHA-1 itself is broken for collision resistance. The real risk is not security but cache poisoning: two distinct assets sharing a fingerprinted filename means a CDN serves the wrong content to readers after a rebuild. Mitigation: use SHA-256 truncated to 16 hex chars, or include the original path in the fingerprint to make collisions practically impossible.

title: security: sitemap entries use a hand-rolled escapeXml that misses control characters
body: src/build/feeds.ts:53-60 replaces only `& < > " '`. XML 1.0 forbids most ASCII control bytes; if a slug or URL ever contains them (rare but possible after future feature additions like custom permalinks), the produced sitemap.xml is invalid and may be rejected by search engines, or fed to a buggy XML parser. Mitigation: strip / encode `[\x00-\x08\x0B\x0C\x0E-\x1F]` before XML emission.

title: security: turndown's TurndownService is created at module scope and mutated implicitly (state leak across imports)
body: src/ghost/import.ts:8 keeps `turndown` as a module-level singleton. Turndown supports `addRule` mutation; if a future helper registers a rule for one import, it persists for the next. Currently no rules are added so the risk is theoretical. Mitigation: instantiate fresh per import call.

title: seo: No hreflang emitted even when site has multiple locales
body: Nectar config supports `site.locale` but there is no hreflang emission and no concept of translated routes. For multi-language readers this means duplicate-content penalties if the same content exists at multiple URLs. Fix: document multi-locale strategy (sub-path /ja/, /en/) and emit `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="…" href="…">` per available translation.

title: seo: RSS lacks pagination / <link rel="next">
body: Nectar only emits a single rss.xml capped at config.components.rss.items. For high-volume blogs, RFC 5005 (Feed Paging and Archiving) is the recognised pattern for paginated feeds. Fix: when posts.length > rss.items, emit an archive feed scheme (rss/2/, rss/3/) with `<atom:link rel="next">` between them.

title: seo: sitemap.xml is a single flat file — no sitemap-index, no per-kind sitemap
body: Ghost itself emits sitemap-pages.xml, sitemap-posts.xml, sitemap-tags.xml, sitemap-authors.xml and a sitemap.xml index. Nectar emits one flat sitemap.xml. For >50k URLs this will exceed the 50MB / 50,000 URL limit per the sitemaps.org spec. Fix: split feeds.ts into multiple sitemaps when post count exceeds threshold and emit a sitemap index file.

title: seo: sitemap.xml is missing newlines (single-line XML)
body: example/dist/sitemap.xml is a single long line. While valid XML, this makes manual inspection and grep-based diff review painful, and a few crawler bugs (historical) choked on lines >65535 chars. Fix: in feeds.ts emit one <url> per line with leading whitespace.

title: test: author block: falls back to `this.author` when not in route data
body:
Input: `{{#author}}{{name}}{{/author}}` inside foreach over posts; uses post.author.
Expected: post's author name.
Why: covers the `(this as Record).author` fallback branch.

title: test: authors: limit/from/to slicing
body:
Input: `{{authors limit=1}}` with 3 authors.
Expected: only the first.
Why: documented hash; gap pin.

title: test: body_class: ctx.body_class overrides defaults
body:
Input: ctx.body_class='custom'.
Expected: literal `custom` returned.
Why: explicit override path.

title: test: body_class: post template adds post tag tokens (gap)
body:
Input: on a post with tags news+sport.
Expected: includes `tag-news` and `tag-sport` (per Ghost docs Casper).
Why: gap pin.

title: test: concat: output should be a SafeString (gap)
body: Input: `{{concat "" "hi" ""}}` used inside `triple-stash` contexts. Expected: per Ghost docs the result should be inert HTML or wrapped in SafeString. Why: pin escaping contract; current impl returns plain string.

title: test: content: empty html returns empty SafeString
body:
Input: ctx without html.
Expected: empty output without throwing.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: date: accepts Date object positional argument
body:
Input: `{{date dateObj format="YYYY"}}` where dateObj is a JS Date.
Expected: year value.
Why: pin object-instance support.

title: test: date: empty ctx falls back to `new Date()`
body:
Input: `{{date}}` with no published_at/updated_at/created_at.
Expected: today's formatted date (within tolerance).
Why: covers default-now branch.

title: test: date: invalid date string yields `Invalid Date` (or empty)
body:
Input: `{{date "not-a-date"}}`.
Expected: dayjs invalid output; helper must not throw.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: encode: used to safely build mailto/twitter share URLs
body:
Input: `mailto:?subject={{encode title}}` with `title='Hi & welcome'`.
Expected: `Hi%20%26%20welcome`.
Why: documented usage pattern.

title: test: example-build.test runs full build per run — slow if invoked frequently
body: Move to a separate `test:e2e` script.

title: test: foreach: visibility="all" returns every item
body:
Input: `{{#foreach posts visibility="all"}}{{slug}}{{/foreach}}`.
Expected: nothing filtered.
Why: explicit `all` is documented; covers the early-return branch.

title: test: get: blockParams second name is pagination meta
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" as |posts pagination|}}{{pagination.resource}}{{/get}}`.
Expected: per Ghost docs pagination object includes page/limit/pages.
Why: pin gap; current impl exposes `{resource}` placeholder only.

title: test: get: include hash is accepted (no error)
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" include="tags,authors"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: helper does not throw; data still flows.
Why: Ghost API attribute; ensure parser does not reject the hash.

title: test: get: ordering by string field uses localeCompare
body:
Input: posts with mixed-case titles ordered `title asc`.
Expected: case-insensitive locale ordering.
Why: pin localeCompare branch.

title: test: has: arbitrary key falls through to context property comparison
body:
Input: `{{#has featured="true"}}yes{{/has}}` with `this.featured = true`.
Expected: `yes` (current impl does String comparison).
Why: covers default branch in switch.

title: test: has: empty hash → matched stays false → inverse runs
body:
Input: `{{#has}}A{{else}}B{{/has}}` (no attrs).
Expected: `B`.
Why: defensive coverage of no-op invocation.

title: test: img_url: extracts `url` from a generic media object
body:
Input: `{{img_url obj}}` where `obj = { url: 'https://cdn.example.com/x.png' }`.
Expected: `https://cdn.example.com/x.png`.
Why: fallback for objects with no feature/profile image; matches helper's third extractor branch.

title: test: is: unknown target string does not throw
body:
Input: `{{#is "banana"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: defensive coverage so a typo never crashes the page render.

title: test: link_class: respects custom activeClass hash
body:
Input: `{{link_class for="/x/" activeClass="is-active"}}`.
Expected: `is-active` when matching.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: match: 3-arg `~$` endsWith operator
body:
Input: `{{#match "foobar" "~$" "bar"}}yes{{/match}}`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: suffix match operator.

title: test: match: 3-arg `~^` startsWith operator
body:
Input: `{{#match "foobar" "~^" "foo"}}yes{{/match}}`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: prefix match operator.

title: test: social_url: missing handle returns empty
body:
Input: type=twitter but ctx.twitter undefined.
Expected: `''`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: social_url: unknown type returns empty
body:
Input: `{{social_url type="myspace"}}`.
Expected: `''`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: url: absolute accepts string 'true'
body:
Input: `{{url absolute="true"}}`.
Expected: same as boolean true.
Why: Ghost themes sometimes pass string literals.

title: test: url: empty ctx.url returns empty string
body:
Input: `{{url}}` with no `this.url`.
Expected: `''`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: url: secure flag forces https (gap pin)
body:
Input: `{{url secure=true}}` per Ghost docs.
Expected: https-prefixed canonical URL.
Why: Ghost documents `secure`; current impl ignores it — pin gap.

title: theme-loader: only string values are kept; numbers/booleans in locales dropped silently
body: Some themes store interpolation counters. Either keep all values or document the constraint.

title: theme-loader: partials cannot include / via ../ — by design — but no clear error
body: Document.

title: theme-loader: theme custom-settings group/visibility are parsed but unused
body: Once a UI exists they matter. Today, no UI. Capture and expose to a future admin component.

title: theme/alto: pswp lightbox partial inclusion in `{{#is "post, page"}}`
body: default.hbs:96 wraps pswp lightbox in `{{#is "post, page"}}`. Our is helper splits on comma — already supported. Add Alto-specific snapshot test that pswp appears only on post/page routes.

title: theme/biron: icon-sprite.hbs partial loaded once globally, expected `<symbol>` reuse
body: biron/default.hbs:25 invokes `{{> icon-sprite}}` once with all `<symbol>` definitions; biron/partials/icon.hbs then `<use xlink:href="#icon-name">`. Confirm Nectar's HTML emit step does not strip `<symbol>` / `<use>` elements or rewrite their hrefs.

title: theme/biron: {{{error.message}}} runtime error display
body: biron/partials/subscribe_form.hbs:14 reads `{{{error.message}}}`. Ghost set `error` in the rendering context when /subscribe POST failed. Nectar never sets `error`. The conditional `{{#if error}}` will be false → no rendering. Safe. Document.

title: theme/bulletin: @site.icon for site icon meta
body: home.hbs:38 reads `{{@site.icon}}`. Our SiteData has icon (model.ts:11) but config/loader.ts should populate it from config.theme.icon (or similar). Verify a default site icon round-trips.

title: theme/bulletin: feature_image_width custom select for post header layout
body: partials/article.hbs:2 toggles classes off `@custom.feature_image_width`. Default value 'Wide'. Confirm config-loader hands the string through unmodified and {{#match ... "Wide"}} fires.

title: theme/casper: {{t}} hash-key interpolation expects `{key}` braces (already supported), but t key registry lookup not validated against Casper's locales
body: Casper's locales/*.json keys are full English sentences with `{varname}` placeholders. Our t helper in src/render/helpers/i18n.ts substitutes `{key}` and falls back to the literal key when missing — good. However Casper's locales/de.json etc. have not been smoke-tested with Nectar's loader (src/theme/loader.ts). Add a test that runs Casper with a non-en locale and asserts placeholders render correctly.

title: theme/dawn: cover.hbs subscribe form `data-members-form` HTML structure expected
body: partials/cover.hbs:24-31 hand-rolls a subscribe form using `data-members-form` and `data-members-email`. Nectar's subscribe_form helper emits a different shape. Decide if Nectar should attempt to substitute or just leave the form (no JS to bind). Document that data-members-form-style forms are inert in static build.

title: theme/dawn: {{#match pagination.page 2}} when pagination object absent on home with single page
body: default.hbs (and `partials/content.hbs` in Dawn family) uses `{{#match pagination.page 2}}` to preload page 2. If `pagination` is undefined on the first home render, comparing `undefined === 2` is false — fine. But ensure pagination object is always provided with default {page:1,pages:1} (see Headline note).

title: theme/dawn: {{> "srcset"}} partial includes need access to outer ctx.feature_image
body: partials/srcset.hbs references `feature_image` directly, expecting the parent scope to have it. Our registerPartials registers partials as raw strings; Handlebars defaults to passing the current `this` into the partial, so this should work, but add a test asserting partials/srcset.hbs resolves feature_image when invoked from inside a foreach.

title: theme/ease: load-more button (`<button class="gh-loadmore">`) requires infinite-scroll JS
body: ease/index.hbs:52 and ease/tag.hbs:30 emit a `gh-loadmore` button. The shared-theme-assets script handles this on real Ghost. Nectar should document that load-more requires the shared-theme-assets JS to be vendored, or the button just sits inert.

title: theme/edition: comments helper hash params (title="" count=false) ignored
body: partials/comments.hbs:7 invokes `{{comments title="" count=false}}`. Our comments helper (src/render/helpers/content.ts:112) emits a static div regardless of hash. Accept and forward hash params (title, count) as data attributes for the comments client to consume.

title: theme/edition: custom 'Minimal' / 'Right thumbnail' feed_layout select values are case-sensitive in match
body: default.hbs/index.hbs use `{{#match @custom.feed_layout "Right thumbnail"}}`. Our match uses strict === comparison. Ensure that select-value strings preserve case from package.json (already do) and that no normalisation strips spaces or lowercases values during config load (src/config/loader.ts).

title: theme/edition: {{#get ... include="authors"}} include hash unused
body: partials/related-posts.hbs uses `include="authors"`. Our get helper ignores `include`; results already carry tags/authors because they're embedded in Post. Verify and add an assertion: the include hash is a no-op for Nectar (since relations are eagerly loaded) but should not crash.

title: theme/editorial: legacy theme contains inline gallery <script> inside post.hbs body
body: editorial/post.hbs:71-80 embeds a runtime `<script>` that walks `.kg-gallery-image`. This is rendered raw and depends on Koenig gallery card markup. Nectar should ensure gallery card HTML (from markdown content) matches `.kg-gallery-image > img[width][height]` shape; otherwise the inline script no-ops silently. Document Koenig card markup requirements.

title: theme/headline: tags.[3] array-index syntax in {{#if tags.[3]}}
body: home.hbs:56 uses `{{#if tags.[3]}}` (Handlebars array indexing). This is a Handlebars built-in; verify it works in Nectar's environment without registering any extra helper, and add a smoke test (Headline's secondary sections would crash otherwise).

title: theme/journal: {{^if @member.paid}} negated if on member.paid
body: journal/index.hbs:74 uses `{{^if @member.paid}}` (negated if). Handlebars resolves @member→undefined, accessing .paid still undefined → ^if renders the block. Verify that {{^if X}} on undefined nested path correctly renders the inverse branch.

title: theme/london: legacy theme, missing partials/icons referenced (avatar/ghost-logo/infinity etc.)
body: London's partials include icons like infinity.hbs and ghost-logo.hbs which Casper-derived themes never use, but they ship in the bundle. Verify Nectar's theme loader (src/theme/loader.ts) traverses all hbs files under partials/ regardless of whether referenced. No bug per se, but a confirmation test.

title: theme/solo: header_section_layout values like 'Typographic profile' need to round-trip through config
body: package.json custom.header_section_layout has 3 options including 'Typographic profile' (with space). config/loader.ts should preserve string verbatim for match comparisons in default.hbs.

title: theme/solo: post `{{#unless feature_image}}` fall-through layout — verify post_class CSS
body: Solo's post.hbs has a no-image fallback that re-uses gh-content. This depends on Casper-derived CSS classes (`.gh-canvas`, `.gh-content`). Nectar's source theme uses similar classes; ensure no Nectar-side CSS rewriting drops `.gh-` prefix.

title: theme/wave: `is_popup` context variable for subscribe form unsupported
body: default.hbs:92 reads `{{#if is_popup}}`. This is set by Ghost when rendering the subscribe iframe popup. Nectar should always treat is_popup=false, but make sure the variable is *defined* (not undefined → no crash) and document. Effect on Wave: extra `.popup` class never added — safe to ignore.

title: theme/wave: jQuery 3.3.1 CDN dependency baked into default.hbs
body: default.hbs:131 hard-codes a jQuery CDN URL. This is theme-side, not Nectar's problem, but if Nectar's CSP/SRI features ever bundle external scripts, ensure no rewriting of integrity hash. Document as 'theme limitation, leave untouched'.

title: theme/wave: {{#foreach authors}}{{> author-box}}{{/foreach}} expects author context with social fields
body: post.hbs:35-37 + partials/author-box.hbs reads twitter/facebook/linkedin/bluesky/mastodon/threads/tiktok/youtube/instagram. Our Author model (src/content/model.ts:20-41) covers all of these. Good. But `{{social_url type="twitter"}}` requires the context to be a single Author, not the post — verify foreach changes `this` correctly. Add test.

title: web-standards:
body: example/dist/hello-nectar/index.html lines 126-138 emit `

title: web-standards: No <meta name="color-scheme"> hint
body: Without `<meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">` browsers can't apply the correct default form/scrollbar colouring during the initial paint. Fix: emit color-scheme meta based on theme support.

title: web-standards: No preconnect / dns-prefetch for third-party domains
body: When themes embed Giscus, Disqus, Cloudinary, fonts.googleapis, etc., Nectar emits no `<link rel="preconnect">` hints. The optional component for comments should register required preconnects. Fix: extend the components system with a `headHints()` hook that returns array of <link rel=preconnect|dns-prefetch> elements.

title: web-standards: No subresource integrity on assets
body: example/dist/index.html lines 9-10 and 24 emit `<link rel="preload" as="style" href="/assets/built/screen.9d5b34cbe9.css">` and `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/built/screen.9d5b34cbe9.css">` without integrity= attributes. For first-party assets SRI is optional, but Nectar should at least produce SRI hashes alongside the fingerprinted filenames so they can be opted into. Fix: compute SHA-384 at fingerprint time in src/build/emit.ts (or wherever assets are versioned) and emit integrity="sha384-…" crossorigin="anonymous".

title: web-standards: no <meta name="referrer"> guidance
body: Nectar emits no `<meta name="referrer" content="strict-origin-when-cross-origin">` meta. For privacy-respecting blogs this should be the default. Fix: emit the meta from ghost-head.ts (configurable via [site].referrer_policy).

title: web-standards: viewport meta lacks viewport-fit=cover for safe-area on notched devices
body: example/dist/index.html line 7: `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">` — fine, but missing `viewport-fit=cover` means iOS Safari won't extend content into the safe-area on notched iPhones, and the theme's safe-area-inset-* CSS becomes a no-op. Fix: append `, viewport-fit=cover`.

title: webhooks: integration directory not modelled
body: Ghost has a `/ghost/api/integrations` admin concept (Zapier, Slack, etc.). Out of scope; document.

title: webhooks: no concept of build-completion or content-change webhook
body: Ghost admin sends webhooks on post.published etc. Nectar is a build tool — analogue is a post-build hook. Add `[hooks] post_build = "./scripts/notify-discord.sh"` (already covered partly by `cli: no build hooks` in self-audit?). Specifically for member-facing flow, recommend invoking the newsletter-send command from this hook.
